[F21] LIGHTING -- F21 covers inventions related to lighting, including lamps, light sources, illumination assemblies and control mechanisms for generating and directing light. It addresses conventional and emerging lighting technologies used in domestic, industrial, automotive and architectural applications.
[F21H] INCANDESCENT MANTLES -- **Definition:** This place covers:  **Glossary:** - Incandescent: Capable of emitting visible light when heated. - Incandescent mantle: A device comprising a lace-like hood or envelope of thin, porous, refractory material that glows when it is positioned in a flame or otherwise heated by combustion of a distinct combustible material. - Light source: The entity (e.g. mantle) that generates light for a lighting device or apparatus.
[F21H 1/00] Incandescent mantles -- F21H 1/00 relates to incandescent mantles, the fabric or ceramic structures that are heated by a flame to emit bright white light in portable gas lanterns and similar lighting devices. It includes mantle materials, designs, and methods for attaching them to burners.
[F21H 1/02] characterised by the material thereof -- The F21H 1/02 subclass includes incandescent mantles that are defined by the specific material from which the mantle is made, such as ceramic, metal-oxide or other heat-resistant compositions, and covers patents describing the mantle's material characteristics, preparation and performance.
[F21H 1/04] Suspension devices therefor  for attaching the mantle on the housing of a lamp  F21V19/06
[F21H 3/00] Manufacturing incandescent mantles -- The CPC subclass F21H 3/00 covers processes for producing incandescent mantles, the fabric-based envelopes that contain the combustible material in mantle lamps. It includes methods of shaping, impregnating, and treating the mantle material before assembly, focusing on manufacturing steps that affect the mantle's light output and durability.
[F21H 5/00] Solid incandescent bodies -- F21H 5/00 relates to solid incandescent bodies used as light-emitting elements in lamps or mantles. It encompasses the design, materials, shape and heating methods of solid filaments or rods that generate illumination by incandescence, covering both conventional and specialized applications.
[F21H 7/00] Other incandescent bodies -- F21H 7/00 covers incandescent bodies used for lighting that are not mantles, such as standard filaments, wire loops, or other heated elements that emit light by incandescence. It falls under the mechanical-engineering and lighting hierarchy, specifically within the broader category of incandescent mantles.
[F21K] NON-ELECTRIC LIGHT SOURCES USING LUMINESCENCE -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The following light sources per se:  **Glossary:** - Light source: The entity (e.g. light bulb, gas mantle, combustible charge) that generates light for a lighting device or apparatus. The light source of the lighting devices or lighting apparatus may be electrically powered (e.g. power is supplied by electric batteries, electric cells, electric generators, flexible cables for electric mains constructions), non-electrically powered (e.g. power is created by combustible fuels, triboluminescence) or a naturally occurring source of light (e.g. sunlight).
[F21K 2/00] Non-electric light sources using luminescence -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Light sources that emit light by effects other than thermal radiation and that do not use electric power for the generation of light, for example chemoluminescent, triboluminescent or thermoluminescent light sources.  Chemoluminescent light sources activated by an electric field.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Using a radioactive source to excite a material into luminescence -> G21H3/02 - Lamps in which a gas filling is excited to luminesce by an external electromagnetic field or by external corpuscular radiation -> H01J65/04 - Lamps in which a gas filling is excited to luminesce by radioactive material structurally associated with the lamp -> H01J65/06 - Lamps, other than those in which all the electrodes are within the vessel, in which a screen or coating is excited to luminesce by radioactive material located inside the vessel -> H01J65/08
[F21K 2/005] excited by infrared radiation using up-conversion -- This CPC subclass falls under mechanical engineering, lighting, non-electric light sources using luminescence and covers light sources that are stimulated by infrared radiation through up-conversion processes, where lower-energy infrared photons are converted to higher-energy visible emission.
[F21K 2/04] using triboluminescence -- F21K 2/04 covers illumination systems that produce light by triboluminescence, the emission of photons when a material is fractured, rubbed or otherwise mechanically stressed, without any electrical power. It includes devices or methods that harness this mechanically induced luminescence for lighting, signaling or visual effects in non-electric light-source applications.
[F21K 2/06] using chemiluminescence -- F21K 2/06 covers non-electric luminescent devices that generate light through chemiluminescent reactions, where chemical energy is directly converted to visible radiation without electrical power. Such devices include glow sticks, emergency illumination strips and other self-luminous products that rely on the oxidation of a luminescent compound.
[F21K 2/08] activated by an electric field, i.e. electrochemiluminescence -- The subclass F21K 2/08 covers light sources whose luminescence is initiated by an electric field, namely electrochemiluminescence, where an applied voltage drives electrochemical reactions that emit light. It includes devices, systems and methods that generate illumination through this field-activated chemical process.
[F21K 5/00] Light sources using charges of combustible material, e.g. illuminating flash devices -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Light sources, for example illuminating flash devices, in which light is generated by charges of explosive or combustible materials.
[F21K 5/02] ignited in a non-disrupting container, e.g. photo-flash bulb -- The code F21K 5/02 covers light sources that generate illumination by igniting a combustible charge inside a container that stays intact during the flash, such as photographic flash bulbs. These non-electric devices belong to the CPC class for non-electric luminescent sources and are used where a brief, intense light is required without disrupting the surrounding enclosure.
[F21K 5/023] Ignition devices in photo flash bulbs -- The classification F21K 5/023 covers ignition devices for photographic flash bulbs that contain a combustible charge, including heated filaments, spark gaps or other mechanisms that start the flash reaction. It falls under the CPC group for non-electric luminescent light sources within mechanical engineering.
[F21K 5/026] using mechanical firing, e.g. percussion of a fulminating charge -- The F21K 5/026 subclass of the CPC deals with non-electric luminous devices that generate light by mechanically igniting a combustible, fulminating charge, for example through percussion or impact in flash lamps. It is categorized under mechanical engineering, lighting, non-electric light sources using luminescence.
[F21K 9/00] Light sources using semiconductor devices as light-generating elements, e.g. using light-emitting diodes [LED] or lasers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative examples of subject matter classified in this place:  LED lamps  LED modules  **Glossary:** - LED: Light Emitting Diode - OLED: Organic Light Emitting Diode
[F21K 9/20] Light sources comprising attachment means -- F21K 9/20 covers semiconductor-based light sources, such as LEDs or laser diodes, that incorporate means for attaching the device to a support, housing, or other component. The classification includes fixtures, mounts, brackets, couplings and similar structures that enable installation or integration of the light-emitting element.
[F21K 9/23] Retrofit light sources for lighting devices with a single fitting for each light source, e.g. for substitution of incandescent lamps with bayonet or threaded fittings -- F21K 9/23 concerns retrofit light sources that replace traditional incandescent lamps in fixtures with a single bayonet or threaded fitting, using semiconductor emitters such as LEDs or lasers. The devices are engineered to fit existing sockets directly, allowing easy substitution without altering the lighting fixture.
[F21K 9/232] specially adapted for generating an essentially omnidirectional light distribution, e.g. with a glass bulb
[F21K 9/233] specially adapted for generating a spot light distribution, e.g. for substitution of reflector lamps -- F21K 9/233 covers lighting devices that use semiconductor light-generating elements such as LEDs or lasers and are specially designed to produce a spot-light distribution, intended as replacements for traditional reflector lamps.
[F21K 9/235] Details of bases or caps, i.e. the parts that connect the light source to a fitting -- This CPC subclass F21K 9/235 concerns the design and features of bases or caps that attach non-electric luminescent light sources such as LEDs or laser diodes to a lamp holder. It covers the mechanical aspects of the connector, including shape, material, and attachment methods.
[F21K 9/237] Details of housings or cases, i.e. the parts between the light-generating element and the bases -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Details of housings or cases, i.e. the parts between the light-generating element and the bases, of retrofit light sources for lighting devices with a single fitting for each light source, e.g. for substitution of incandescent lamps with bayonet or threaded fittings  Arrangement of components within housings or cases of retrofit light sources for lighting devices with a single fitting for each light source, e.g. for substitution of incandescent lamps with bayonet or threaded fittings  Illustrative examples of subject matter classified in this group, see figures below:  Figure 1. A cylindrical housing 10 has a first component unit 50 and a second component unit 70 therein. The housing 10 has therein a guide member 80 that is elongated in a direction from one-end 10a side of the housing 10 to the other-end 10b side thereof. The first and second component units respectively comprise notch sections 57, 75 that are different in shape and size from each other, and the first and second component units, when inserted in the housing 10 from the one-end 10a side, are movable along the guide member 80 in a state wherein the guide member 80 is positioned in the notch sections.  Figure 2. A cylindrical housing 16 is interposed between the heat dissipater 12 and the cap 17 and is flexible so that the heat dissipater 12 is tiltable relative to the cap 17.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Arrangement or mounting of circuit elements integrated in the light source -> F21K9/238
[F21K 9/238] Arrangement or mounting of circuit elements integrated in the light source -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Arrangement or mounting of circuit elements integrated in the retrofit light source for lighting devices with a single fitting for each light source, e.g. for substitution of incandescent lamps with bayonet or threaded fittings.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group, see figure below:  A circuit unit 50 has a circuit board 51 to which a plurality of electronic components 52 are mounted. The circuit unit 50 is housed in a case 70 in a manner such that the circuit board 51 is along the inner peripheral surface of the case 70.
[F21K 9/27] Retrofit light sources for lighting devices with two fittings for each light source, e.g. for substitution of fluorescent tubes -- F21K 9/27 relates to retrofit light sources that provide two fittings for each lamp, allowing a single unit to replace existing fixtures such as fluorescent tubes with semiconductor-based emitters like LEDs. The design enables easy conversion of traditional lighting devices to modern, more efficient light sources.
[F21K 9/272] Details of end parts, i.e. the parts that connect the light source to a fitting -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative examples of subject matter classified in this group, see figures below:  Figure 1. cap, and Figure 2. cap.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Arrangement or mounting of circuit elements integrated in the light source -> F21K9/278
[F21K 9/275] Details of bases or housings, i.e. the parts between the light-generating element and the end caps -- This subclass covers structural components that support and enclose the semiconductor light-emitting element, such as bases, housings, or intermediate parts between the diodes and the outer caps. It includes design features that affect thermal management, mechanical stability, and optical coupling.
[F21K 9/278] Arrangement or mounting of circuit elements integrated in the light source -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Arrangement or mounting of circuit elements integrated in the retrofit light source for lighting devices with two fittings for each light source, e.g. for substitution of fluorescent tubes  Illustrative examples of subject matter classified in this group, see figures below:  A power supply board 102 having a power supply circuit 106 for supplying lighting-up power to the LED, an insulating inner 103 for surrounding the power supply board, and a metallic heat sink 104 for improving heat radiation performance of the power supply board. The power supply circuit 106 is electrically connected to the heat sink through a common wire 107.
[F21K 9/60] Optical arrangements integrated in the light source, e.g. for improving the colour rendering index or the light extraction -- The subclass F21K 9/60 covers light-emitting devices, such as LEDs or laser diodes, that incorporate optical elements directly within the source to modify the emitted light, for example to boost colour-rendering index, shape the beam or improve light-extraction efficiency. It lies under non-electric luminescent light sources in mechanical engineering.
[F21K 9/61] using light guides -- F21K 9/61 relates to the incorporation of light guides in lighting fixtures that use semiconductor light sources such as LEDs or laser diodes. It addresses how the emitted light is distributed, coupled or transmitted through a light guide to achieve desired illumination patterns.
[F21K 9/62] using mixing chambers, e.g. housings with reflective walls -- This subclass covers semiconductor-based light sources such as LEDs or lasers that employ a mixing chamber-typically a housing with reflective walls-to homogenise the emitted light, shape the beam or improve illumination uniformity.
[F21K 9/64] using wavelength conversion means distinct or spaced from the light-generating element, e.g. a remote phosphor layer -- F21K 9/64 pertains to semiconductor-based light sources, such as LEDs or lasers, that use a wavelength-conversion component located separate from the emitting element, for example a remote phosphor layer that modifies the light colour or spectrum.
[F21K 9/65] specially adapted for changing the characteristics or the distribution of the light, e.g. by adjustment of parts -- F21K 9/65 covers semiconductor light sources such as LEDs or laser diodes that are specially adapted to modify their light characteristics or distribution, for example by adjusting lenses, reflectors, filters or other components to change intensity, colour, beam shape or pattern.
[F21K 9/66] Details of globes or covers forming part of the light source -- F21K 9/66 covers the design and construction of protective or shaping glass, ceramic or polymer envelopes that surround semiconductor light-generating elements in non-electric luminescent sources such as LEDs and laser diodes. It includes features of the globe, lens or cover that are integral to the light-emitting assembly.
[F21K 9/68] Details of reflectors forming part of the light source -- This subclass covers technical information concerning the design and characteristics of reflectors that are built into or form part of a non-electric light source using semiconductor emitters such as LEDs or lasers. It includes reflector shape, material, surface treatment, positioning and how they affect light distribution and efficiency.
[F21K 9/69] Details of refractors forming part of the light source -- F21K 9/69 concerns the design and configuration of refractive elements that are integral to semiconductor-based light sources such as LEDs or laser diodes. It addresses the shape, material, positioning and optical characteristics of lenses, prisms or other refractors that shape or direct the emitted light. The code falls under mechanical engineering, lighting, non-electric luminescent sources.
[F21K 9/90] Methods of manufacture -- F21K 9/90 pertains to manufacturing processes for semiconductor light-emitting devices such as LEDs and laser diodes. It covers methods including crystal growth, wafer handling, epitaxial layer formation, structuring, and packaging steps used to produce non-electric luminescent light sources.
[F21K 99/00] Subject matter not provided for in other groups of this subclass -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Light sources that are not provided for elsewhere in the CPC.
[F21L] LIGHTING DEVICES OR SYSTEMS THEREOF, BEING PORTABLE OR SPECIALLY ADAPTED FOR TRANSPORTATION -- **Definition:** This place covers:  **Glossary:** - light source: the entity (e.g. light bulb, fluorescent tube, gas mantle, combustible charge, luminescent element or semiconductor light generating element) that generates light for a lighting device - pocket lamps: lamps primarily intended to be held in a hand, or to fit in a pocket
[F21L 13/00] Electric lighting devices with built-in electric generators -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Electric lighting devices with solar cells -> F21L4/00
[F21L 13/02] with fluid drive -- F21L 13/02 covers electric lighting devices that incorporate a fluid-driven generator, such as a turbine, hydraulic motor or pneumatic engine, to produce power for the lamp. The entry applies to portable or transport-adapted lighting systems where the fluid drive provides the electrical energy needed for illumination.
[F21L 13/04] actuated by hand -- This subclass covers portable electric lighting devices that incorporate a built-in hand-cranked generator to supply power. The hand actuation converts mechanical energy into electricity for illumination.
[F21L 13/06] with mechanical drive, e.g. spring -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Flashlight having manual charging device
[F21L 13/08] by reciprocating pusher actuated by hand -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Electric power device for portable device with a piezo-converter  Portable signal light with a self-generator
[F21L 14/00] Electric lighting devices without a self-contained power source, e.g. for mains connection -- F21L 14/00 refers to electric lighting devices that do not contain their own power source, such as lamps and fixtures designed to be powered from an external mains supply, typically used in portable or transport-adapted lighting systems.
[F21L 14/02] capable of hand-held use, e.g. inspection lamps -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[F21L 14/023] having two or more, or different light sources -- F21L 14/023 pertains to portable or transport-adapted lighting devices that incorporate two or more light sources or combine different types of light sources, such as LEDs with incandescent or fluorescent emitters. These devices are powered from an external source rather than having an integrated power supply.
[F21L 14/026] having a linear light source -- F21L 14/026 covers electric lighting devices that lack an onboard power source and are designed for portable or transport-adapted use, specifically those employing a linear light source such as a tube or strip, typically connected to mains electricity.
[F21L 14/04] carried on wheeled supports -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[F21L 17/00] Non-electric lighting -- F21L 17/00 covers lighting apparatus that operates without electrical power, including devices that emit light by combustion of fuels, chemical reactions, or other non-electric means. Examples are candles, oil lamps, gas lanterns, and portable torches that rely on flame or phosphorescence.
[F21L 19/00] Lanterns, e.g. hurricane lamps or candle lamps -- This CPC subclass covers portable lighting devices such as lanterns, hurricane lamps and candle lamps that are designed for use away from fixed installations or for transport. It is part of the mechanical-engineering hierarchy under lighting devices or systems specially adapted for transportation.
[F21L 19/003] Fastening or lifting of the lamp glass  fastening of component parts of lighting devices  F21V17/00 -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Fastening of component parts of the lighting devices -> F21V17/00

The following image shows an example of an identical portable lighting system.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Systems employing both electric and non-electric light sources or exchangeable light sources -> F21L27/00
[F21L 21/00] Non-electric pocket lamps, e.g. lamps producing sparks -- This classification covers portable lighting devices that operate without electricity, such as pocket lamps that generate light by creating sparks using combustible material or mechanical action. It applies to non-electric pocket lamps specially adapted for transport.
[F21L 23/00] Non-electric hand-lamps for miners -- A non-electric hand-lamp intended for use by miners, typically a portable lighting device that operates without electrical power, such as a fuel-burning or chemically activated lamp, designed for rugged conditions and ease of transport in underground work.
[F21L 26/00] Non-electric portable lighting devices, or systems thereof, not provided for in groups -- F21L 26/00 covers non-electric portable lighting devices or systems not found in other groups, including chemical-light sticks, flame-based torches, gas-powered lamps and other manually operated illumination tools designed for transportable use.
[F21L 27/00] Lighting devices or systems, employing combinations of electric and non-electric light sources
[F21L 4/00] Portable electric lighting devices -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Electrical portable lighting devices being self-powered

[F21L 4/02] characterised by the provision of two or more light sources -- The subgroup pertains to portable electric lighting devices that incorporate two or more light sources, such as flashlights, headlamps or lanterns with multiple LEDs, providing combined illumination, redundancy or adjustable lighting modes.
[F21L 4/022] Pocket lamps -- F21L 4/022 covers portable electric lighting devices designed to be handheld or carried, such as pocket lamps, flashlights and similar battery-powered lamps intended for personal use while moving.
[F21L 4/025] the light sources being of different shape or type -- F21L 4/025 relates to portable electric lighting devices whose light sources differ in shape or type, encompassing systems that use varied geometries such as LEDs, filament lamps, or other uniquely shaped emitters within handheld or transport-adapted lighting apparatus.
[F21L 4/027] the light sources being a LED -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Pocket lamp, the light emitter of which being one or more LED

[F21L 4/045] Pocket lamps -- Pocket lamps are small, hand-held electric lighting devices designed for portability, typically powered by batteries or rechargeable cells. They are used for personal illumination in situations where larger fixtures are impractical, such as outdoor activities, emergencies, or confined spaces.
[F21L 4/06] with the light source coupled to the remainder of the device solely by cable -- **Definition:** This place covers:  For example, note the following figure showing head portion (8) attached to body part (16) by means of flexible connecting sheath comprising electrical wires (14).
[F21L 4/08] characterised by means for -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Portable lighting device provided with connectors to connect the device to external power supply in order to recharge the batteries, e.g. with built-in accumulators  Portable lighting device provided with accumulators (not shaped like conventional batteries) that can be dismounted in order to be recharged on an external recharging base  Portable lighting devices with solar cells integrated to the body of the lamp
[F21L 4/085] Pocket lamps -- Pocket lamps are small handheld electric lighting devices designed for personal, portable use. They typically incorporate batteries or rechargeable cells and may be shaped as flashlights, key-chain lights or other compact forms suitable for carrying in a pocket or bag. The classification covers their construction, power sources, and features that enable convenient illumination while on the move.
[F21S] NON-PORTABLE LIGHTING DEVICES -- **Definition:** This place covers:  **Glossary:** - Light source: The entity (e.g. light bulb, fluorescent tube, gas mantle or combustible charge) that generates light for a lighting device.
[F21S 10/00] Lighting devices or systems producing a varying lighting effect -- F21S 10/00 covers non-portable lighting devices or systems that create a varying lighting effect, such as dimming, color shifting, flashing, or patterned illumination. It applies to mechanisms, controls, and arrangements designed to alter light output over time or in response to signals, including smart or programmable fixtures used in architectural, industrial, or decorative lighting. This subclass falls under Mechanical Engineering → Lighting → Non-portable Lighting Devices in the Cooperative Patent Classification.
[F21S 10/002] using liquids, e.g. water -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group:  or  Decorative lamp with a lighted water container/reservoir.
[F21S 10/005] using light guides  light guides specially adapted for lighting devices  G02B6/0001 -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Light guides specially adapted for lighting devices -> G02B6/0001
[F21S 10/007] using rotating transparent or colored disks, e.g. gobo wheels -- This subclass includes lighting devices or systems that produce a varying effect by rotating transparent or colored disks, such as gobo wheels, to project patterns, textures or colors onto a scene. It is commonly used in stage, architectural or entertainment lighting to change the visual appearance dynamically.
[F21S 10/02] changing colors -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Lighting devices or systems producing a varying lighting effect using liquids -> F21S10/002 - Lighting devices or systems producing a varying lighting effect using rotating disks -> F21S10/007 - Lighting devices or systems producing a varying lighting effect simulating flames -> F21S10/04
[F21S 10/023] by selectively switching fixed light sources -- F21S 10/023 relates to non-portable lighting devices that create changing lighting effects by selectively turning fixed light sources on or off, or by dimming them, rather than moving the lamps. This includes systems that control individual LEDs or lamps to produce patterns, sequences or variable intensity for illumination, signage or decorative purposes.
[F21S 10/026] by movement of parts, e.g. by movement of reflectors or light sources  F21S10/007  takes precedence -- The F21S 10/026 subclass covers non-portable lighting apparatus that achieve changing illumination by moving components, such as rotating reflectors, shifting lenses, or relocating light sources. It falls under mechanical engineering, lighting, and non-portable lighting devices, and is subordinated to the broader F21S 10/007 group.
[F21S 10/04] simulating flames -- The F21S 10/04 subclass covers lighting devices or systems, typically non-portable, that create a visual effect resembling a flame. It includes technologies that simulate flickering, color shifting, and other flame-like characteristics for decorative or safety purposes.
[F21S 10/043] by selectively switching fixed light sources -- This subclass covers lighting devices or systems where fixed light sources are selectively switched to produce varying lighting effects. It includes methods for controlling individual lamps in a fixture to change illumination patterns, colors, or intensities without moving the sources.
[F21S 10/046] by movement of parts, e.g. by movement of reflectors or light sources -- F21S 10/046 covers non-portable lighting devices that achieve varying illumination by mechanically moving parts, such as rotating reflectors, shifting lenses, or relocating light sources. The movement changes the direction, pattern, or intensity of the light to produce dynamic lighting effects.
[F21S 10/06] flashing, e.g. with rotating reflector or light source -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Example: Rotating reflector flashlight  Example: Apparatus for generating light patterns responsive to audio frequency input signals
[F21S 10/063] for providing a rotating light effect -- The subclass F21S 10/063 covers lighting devices or systems that create a rotating light effect, such as beacons, headlights, or other non-portable lamps where the emitted light is made to move in a circular pattern to produce a dynamic visual signal. It falls under mechanical engineering, lighting, non-portable lighting devices, and specifically devices producing varying lighting effects.
[F21S 10/066] by selectively switching fixed light sources -- The class covers lighting installations that create changing illumination effects by individually controlling fixed light sources, such as turning them on or off or dimming them, rather than moving the sources. This method is used in signage, decorative fixtures and stage lighting to produce varied visual patterns.
[F21S 11/00] Non-electric lighting -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Example:Daylight deflecting device for use in double glass window for illuminating of building room  Example: Daylight illumination system
[F21S 11/002] characterised by the means for collecting or concentrating the sunlight, e.g. parabolic reflectors or Fresnel lenses -- The entry covers non-electric lighting devices that collect or concentrate sunlight, using elements such as parabolic reflectors or Fresnel lenses to focus solar energy for illumination. It is classified under mechanical engineering, lighting, non-portable lighting devices, CPC code F21S 11/002.
[F21S 11/005] with tracking means for following the position of the sun -- F21S 11/005 classifies non-electric lighting devices that incorporate mechanisms to track and follow the sun's position, enabling continuous illumination without electrical power. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering, lighting, and non-portable lighting devices in the Cooperative Patent Classification system.
[F21S 11/007] characterised by the means for transmitting light into the interior of a building -- This subclass covers non-electric lighting fixtures that transmit light from an external source into the interior of a building, such as windows, skylights, light tubes or reflective ducts. The classification is used for designs that rely on daylight or other ambient illumination without electrical power.
[F21S 13/00] Non-electric lighting devices or systems employing a point-like light source -- This CPC subclass covers non-electric lighting devices or systems that use a point-like light source, such as candles, torches, or flame lamps. It is situated under mechanical engineering → lighting → non-portable lighting devices.
[F21S 13/02] Devices intended to be fixed, e.g. ceiling lamp, wall lamp -- F21S 13/02 covers non-electric lighting devices that use a point-like light source and are designed to be permanently installed, such as ceiling lamps or wall-mounted fixtures. It falls under the hierarchy of Mechanical Engineering → Lighting → Non-portable lighting devices, specifically for fixed-position illumination systems.
[F21S 13/04] with a pendant -- F21S 13/04 refers to non-electric lighting devices that use a point-like light source and include a pendant element, such as a hanging or suspended lamp that receives illumination from a fixed source without electrical power. The classification covers mechanical arrangements for positioning and supporting the pendant.
[F21S 13/06] multi-branched, e.g. chandelier -- F21S 13/06 covers non-electric lighting fixtures with multiple branches, such as chandeliers, where a point-like light source is mounted on each arm for indoor illumination without electrical power.
[F21S 13/08] with suspension from a stretched wire -- F21S 13/08 pertains to non-electric lighting devices that use a point-like light source and are mounted by suspending the unit from a stretched wire, such as lanterns or torches hung from tensioned cords.
[F21S 13/10] with a standard, e.g. street lamp -- This subclass pertains to non-electric lighting devices that employ a point-like light source and are installed on a standard, for example traditional street lamps that rely on gas or oil combustion.
[F21S 13/12] Devices intended to be free-standing, e.g. table lamp, floor lamp -- This subclass of non-electric lighting covers free-standing devices such as table and floor lamps that incorporate a point-like light source and are not attached to a wall or ceiling. It falls under mechanical engineering, lighting, non-portable lighting devices.
[F21S 13/14] Lighting systems -- F21S 13/14 covers lighting systems that use non-electric, point-like light sources such as candles, lanterns, or chemical luminescence. The entry addresses the design, arrangement, and integration of these sources into fixtures or portable devices for illumination.
[F21S 15/00] Non-electric lighting devices or systems employing light sources not covered by main groups -- F21S 15/00 covers non-electric lighting devices or systems for non-portable use that employ light sources not classified in the main groups, such as candles, torches, gas flames, combustible or chemical emitters, and other mechanically generated illumination methods. It lies within the mechanical engineering field of lighting.
[F21S 19/00] Lighting devices or systems employing combinations of electric and non-electric light sources -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[F21S 19/005] Combining sunlight and electric light sources for indoor illumination -- Combining sunlight with electric light sources for indoor illumination refers to lighting systems that integrate natural daylight and artificial electric lighting to provide a blended illumination environment inside buildings. This includes fixtures or control schemes that collect and channel sunlight while supplementing it with electric light to maintain consistent light levels.
[F21S 2/00] Electric lighting -- F21S 2/00 classifies electric lighting within non-portable lighting devices, covering stationary fixtures that use electricity to produce illumination such as ceiling lights, wall lamps, recessed luminaires, and associated control or power-distribution components. It includes the design, mounting, and integration of electric light sources in buildings and other fixed installations. This entry falls under the hierarchy Mechanical Engineering → Lighting → Non-portable lighting devices.
[F21S 2/005] of modular construction -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Articulated light source assembly  **Glossary:** - Module: A self-contained assembly of light emitting devices that can be installed as a unit, for insertion in a housing in order to form a lighting device, e.g. for street lighting
[F21S 2217/00] Indexing codes associated with groups -- F21S 2217/00 relates to the use of indexing codes that identify and categorize groups of non-portable lighting devices, enabling efficient retrieval and analysis of patents within the cooperative classification system. It is situated in the hierarchy under mechanical engineering, lighting and non-portable lighting devices.
[F21S 2217/02] Fastening or lifting of lamp glass -- The CPC entry F21S 2217/02 addresses ways of fastening or lifting the glass component of non-portable lighting devices, covering mechanisms that secure, adjust or remove lamp glass in fixtures such as ceiling, wall or street luminaires. It includes clamps, hinges, lift-out systems and related mounting structures.

[F21S 4/00] Lighting devices or systems using a string or strip of light sources -- F21S 4/00 covers lighting devices or systems that employ a continuous string or strip of light sources, such as LED strips or filament strings, intended for non-portable applications. The classification relates to mechanical engineering solutions for distributing illumination along a linear element.

[F21S 41/00] Vehicle lighting devices specially adapted for vehicle exteriors -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Reversing lights -> F21S43/00
[F21S 41/10] characterised by the light source -- This subclass groups vehicle exterior lighting devices that are defined primarily by the type of light source employed, such as LEDs, HID, laser or other emitters. The classification distinguishes the fixture based on its light-generating element rather than shape or mounting.
[F21S 41/12] characterised by the type of emitted light -- The subclass pertains to vehicle exterior lighting devices that are distinguished by the type of light they emit, such as colour, spectrum or illumination characteristics, for example white, amber or coloured LEDs. It falls under non-portable lighting for road vehicles.
[F21S 41/125] Coloured light -- **Definition:** This place covers:  e.g. headlamps having more photoluminescent light sources which emit light of different colours when exited by laser beam. Emitted light then mixes in order to get (in, e.g. to weather) appropriate colour of (white) light.  Illustrative examples of subject matter classified in this place:  1.  2.

[F21S 41/135] Polarised -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Headlamps having polarising filters for reducing glare. Usually, LCD devices have polarising filters.
[F21S 41/14] characterised by the type of light source -- F21S 41/14 covers vehicle exterior lighting devices that are distinguished by the type of light source employed, such as LED, halogen, xenon or other technologies. The subclass groups designs where the light-source choice defines the function or performance of headlamps, turn signals, brake lights or other exterior illumination.

[F21S 41/30] characterised by reflectors -- This CPC subclass covers vehicle exterior lighting devices, such as headlamps or rear lamps, that use reflectors as a defining element to collect and direct the light source. The classification applies to designs where the reflector shape or arrangement is a principal functional feature of the lighting apparatus.
[F21S 41/32] Optical layout thereof -- F21S 41/32 concerns the optical layout of vehicle exterior lighting devices, covering the design and arrangement of reflectors, lenses and light-distribution elements in headlamps, tail lamps, turn signals and similar non-portable lighting fixtures mounted on vehicles.

[F21S 41/33] Multi-surface reflectors, e.g. reflectors with facets or reflectors with portions of different curvature -- Multi-surface reflectors with facets or mixed curvatures are used in vehicle exterior lighting to direct light more efficiently and achieve specific beam patterns. By combining flat and curved sections, they can concentrate, spread or shape illumination for signals, markers or rear-running lights.
[F21S 41/331] the reflector consisting of complete annular areas -- F21S 41/331 covers reflectors that are formed from uninterrupted annular sections, meaning the reflector consists of complete ring-shaped areas rather than segmented or partial pieces. This design is applied to vehicle exterior lighting devices such as headlights, tail lights, turn signals and other non-portable lighting systems. It falls under the CPC hierarchy for mechanical engineering, lighting, and non-portable lighting devices.

[F21S 41/37] characterised by their material, surface treatment or coatings -- This subclass covers vehicle exterior lighting devices that are distinguished by the material they are made of, the surface treatments applied, or any special coatings on their components. It includes lamps, reflectors, lenses and other exterior lighting parts where these material or coating features define the design.

[F21S 41/40] characterised by screens, non-reflecting members, light-shielding members or fixed shades -- The CPC subclass F21S 41/40 relates to vehicle exterior lighting devices that are specially adapted by using screens, non-reflecting elements, light-shielding members or fixed shades to control glare, direct illumination or protect the light source. It falls under mechanical engineering, lighting, non-portable lighting devices and vehicle lighting devices specially adapted for vehicle exteriors.

[F21S 4/15] the cables forming a grid, net or web structure -- F21S 4/15 covers lighting systems where the individual light sources are arranged on cables that are interconnected to form a grid, net or web structure, providing a flexible or modular support for the lamps. This sub-class is used for non-portable lighting devices that employ a string or strip of light sources held in a woven or meshed cable framework.

[F21S 41/60] characterised by a variable light distribution -- This subclass covers vehicle exterior lighting devices whose illumination pattern can be varied, such as adaptive headlights that adjust beam shape, intensity, or direction in response to speed, steering angle or driving conditions. The classification applies to systems that dynamically change light distribution to improve visibility while reducing glare.
[F21S 41/62] for adaptation between right-hand and left-hand traffic -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Stickers or other elements attached to the lens to change a right-hand traffic light distribution to a left-hand traffic light distribution, or vice versa.  Illustrative examples of subject matter classified in this place:  1a.  1b.  1c.  2a.  2b.
[F21S 41/63] by acting on refractors, filters or transparent cover plates -- F21S 41/63 covers vehicle exterior lighting systems that obtain a desired light effect by using refractive elements, colour or spectral filters, or transparent cover plates such as lenses, diffusers or coated glasses to shape, attenuate or colour the emitted beam.
[F21S 41/635] by moving refractors, filters or transparent cover plates -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative examples of subject matter classified in this place:  1a.  1b.  2a.  2b.  3a.  3b.
[F21S 41/64] by changing their light transmissivity, e.g. by liquid crystal or electrochromic devices -- This subclass covers vehicle exterior lighting systems that adjust illumination by varying the transmissivity of a window or lens, for example using liquid crystal or electrochromic elements. The technology enables dynamic control of brightness or colour without moving parts, suitable for adaptive headlights, tail lights, turn signals, or indicator panels.
[F21S 41/645] by electro-optic means, e.g. liquid crystal or electrochromic devices -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative examples of subject matter classified in this place:  1a.  1b.  1c.  2.
[F21S 41/65] by acting on light sources -- F21S 41/65 relates to vehicle exterior lighting devices in which the light source itself is controlled or altered to achieve the desired illumination. The subclass includes techniques such as adjusting lamp intensity, color, temperature or other properties by electrical, thermal or mechanical means.
[F21S 41/657] by moving light sources -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative examples of subject matter classified in this place:  1.  2.  3a.  3b.  4a.  4b.

[F21S 41/67] by acting on reflectors -- The F21S 41/67 group covers vehicle exterior lighting devices that achieve illumination or modify the light pattern by acting on reflectors, such as adjusting, shaping, or moving the reflector surfaces to control the direction and distribution of the emitted light. This includes mechanisms that alter reflector geometry or position to produce the desired beam characteristics.
[F21S 41/675] by moving reflectors -- **Definition:** This place covers:  e.g. DMDs, MEMS.  Illustrative examples of subject matter classified in this place:  1.  2.  3a.  3b.
[F21S 41/68] by acting on screens -- This subclass covers vehicle exterior lighting devices whose illumination is regulated or modified by a screen or display element, such as LCD or LED matrices, allowing the light intensity, color or pattern to be varied for signaling, adaptive illumination or aesthetic effects.
[F21S 41/683] by moving screens -- This classification covers vehicle exterior lighting systems that incorporate moving screens or shutters to modify the emitted light distribution. Examples include adaptive headlights that dynamically adjust beam shape or direction by shifting transparent or opaque panels, enhancing visibility while reducing glare.

[F21S 4/20] with light sources held by or within elongate supports -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Example: Flexible strip with multiple light emitting diodes  Example: Flexible illuminated device for simulating neon lighting  Example: Display cabinet illumination  Ex.: Under-shelf lighting system  Example: Strip of lights for handrail illumination  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - - Elongated LED (Light Emitting Diode) lamps with connectors for retrofitting -> F21K9/27
[F21S 4/22] flexible or deformable, e.g. into a curved shape -- The CPC subclass F21S 4/22 covers lighting devices that use a continuous string or strip of light sources and are designed to be flexible or deformable, allowing them to be bent or formed into curved shapes for installation on non-portable fixtures. This enables adaptable illumination on surfaces that are not flat, such as architectural trim or curved panels.
[F21S 4/24] of ribbon or tape form, e.g. LED tapes -- F21S 4/24 relates to non-portable lighting devices that employ a ribbon or tape form, such as LED tapes, where the light sources are arranged in a continuous strip for illumination.
[F21S 4/26] of rope form, e.g. LED lighting ropes, or of tubular form -- F21S 4/26 covers lighting devices or systems that use a rope-shaped or tubular string of light sources, such as LED lighting ropes employed for decorative or architectural illumination. The entry falls under mechanical engineering, lighting, non-portable lighting devices.

[F21S 43/00] Signalling devices specially adapted for vehicle exteriors, e.g. brake lamps, direction indicator lights or reversing lights -- Group F21S43/00 is impacted by reclassification into group F21S43/601 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[F21S 43/10] characterised by the light source -- F21S 43/10 covers vehicle exterior signalling lights such as brake, turn or reversing lamps where the particular type of light source-LED, halogen, discharge lamp, etc.-is the primary distinguishing feature of the device.
[F21S 43/13] characterised by the type of light source -- F21S 43/13 classifies non-portable vehicle exterior signalling devices, such as brake lamps, turn indicators and reversing lights, based on the type of light source they employ, distinguishing systems that use incandescent, halogen, LED or other illumination technologies.
[F21S 43/14] Light emitting diodes [LED] -- This class covers light emitting diodes used as light sources in vehicle exterior signalling devices such as brake lamps, turn indicators and reversing lights, including the design, arrangement and integration of LEDs for illumination and signalling.
[F21S 43/145] Surface emitters, e.g. organic light emitting diodes [OLED] -- F21S 43/145 covers surface-emitting light sources, notably organic light-emitting diodes, employed in vehicle exterior signalling devices such as brake lamps, direction indicators and reversing lights. The code groups these OLED or similar emitters within the non-portable lighting subclass of mechanical engineering.
[F21S 43/15] Strips of light sources -- This subclass covers linear arrays of light sources used in vehicle exterior signalling devices such as brake lamps, turn-indicator strips or reversing-light elements. It includes LED, electroluminescent or other strip-type modules mounted on the vehicle body to provide continuous or flashing illumination.
[F21S 43/16] Light sources where the light is generated by photoluminescent material spaced from a primary light generating element -- The subclass F21S 43/16 covers vehicle exterior signalling lights-such as brake, direction-indicator or reversing lamps-whose illumination is created by a photoluminescent material that is positioned apart from the primary light-emitting element, using the primary source to activate the phosphor and produce the visible signal.

[F21S 6/00] Lighting devices intended to be free-standing -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Electric lighting devices carried on wheeled supports without a self-contained power source -> F21L14/04
[F21S 6/001] candle-shaped -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative examples of subject matter classified in this place:  1.  Figure 1 illustrates a single candle-shaped lamp.  2.
[F21S 6/002] Table lamps, e.g. for ambient lighting -- F21S 6/002 covers table lamps intended for ambient lighting, which are free-standing devices placed on surfaces such as desks, tables or nightstands. The code belongs to the mechanical engineering section, under lighting and non-portable lighting devices.
[F21S 6/003] for task lighting, e.g. for reading or desk work, e.g. angle poise lamps -- This class covers free-standing, non-portable lighting devices designed for task illumination such as reading or desk work, exemplified by angle-poise lamps.
[F21S 6/004] with a lamp housing in direct contact with the floor or ground -- The subclass F21S 6/004 refers to free-standing, non-portable lighting devices whose lamp housing rests directly on the floor or ground, without a separate base or pedestal. It includes floor lamps, torchières and similar fixtures designed to be placed directly on a surface.
[F21S 6/005] with a lamp housing maintained at a distance from the floor or ground via a support, e.g. standing lamp for ambient lighting -- This subclass covers free-standing lighting devices whose lamp housing is kept away from the floor by a supporting structure, such as floor lamps used to provide ambient illumination.
[F21S 6/006] for direct lighting only, e.g. task lighting -- This subclass covers free-standing lighting appliances that provide direct illumination, such as task lamps, and excludes indirect or ambient lighting. It is placed under non-portable lighting devices in the mechanical engineering section.
[F21S 6/007] for indirect lighting only, e.g. torchiere with reflector bowl directed towards ceiling -- A free-standing, non-portable lighting device that provides only indirect illumination, typically by using a reflector bowl that directs light upward toward the ceiling, such as a torchiere floor lamp. This subclass covers designs where the primary purpose is to bounce light off the ceiling rather than direct it onto objects.
[F21S 6/008] with a combination of direct and indirect lighting -- F21S 6/008 pertains to free-standing lighting fixtures that provide both direct and indirect illumination, meaning the device emits light straight onto a target while also distributing light by reflection or diffusion to create ambient lighting.

[F21S 8/006] Solar simulators, e.g. for testing photovoltaic panels -- F21S 8/006 covers solar simulators, devices that reproduce the spectral, spatial and intensity characteristics of sunlight for the purpose of testing or evaluating photovoltaic panels or other solar energy converters. These systems are designed for fixed installation and provide controlled illumination to assess performance under standardized conditions.
[F21S 8/02] of recess-mounted type, e.g. downlighters -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Lighting devices intended for fixed installation specially adapted for vehicles -> F21S41/00 - F21S45/00
[F21S 8/022] intended to be recessed in a floor or like ground surface, e.g. pavement or false floor -- A lighting device designed for fixed installation that is mounted flush with a floor, pavement or false-floor surface, so the light source is recessed within the ground rather than projecting outward. It belongs to the non-portable lighting group under mechanical engineering.
[F21S 8/024] intended to be recessed in a wall or like vertical structure, e.g. building facade -- F21S 8/024 covers fixed, non-portable lighting fixtures that are installed recessed in walls, façades or other vertical structures. The category includes concealed or built-in lamps, downlights and façade luminaires designed to sit within cavities or behind surfaces for a flush appearance. It is part of the broader mechanical-engineering class for lighting devices intended for permanent installation.
[F21S 8/026] intended to be recessed in a ceiling or like overhead structure, e.g. suspended ceiling -- F21S 8/026 classifies fixed lighting fixtures that are installed recessed in a ceiling or similar overhead structure, such as a suspended ceiling. These devices are intended to be mounted flush with the surface to provide concealed illumination.

[F21S 8/033] the surface being a wall or like vertical structure, e.g. building facade -- This subclass covers non-portable lighting fixtures designed for permanent mounting on walls or similar vertical structures, such as building façades, where the lighting surface is the wall itself. [F21S 8/035] by means of plugging into a wall outlet, e.g. night light -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Lighting devices which are intended to be plugged into a power outlet  Example: Night light
[F21S 8/036] by means of a rigid support, e.g. bracket or arm -- The subclass F21S 8/036 pertains to lighting devices intended for fixed installation that are mounted by means of a rigid support such as a bracket or arm. [F21S 8/037] for mounting in a corner, i.e. between adjacent walls or wall and ceiling -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Example: Indirect wall mounted lighting apparatus
[F21S 8/038] intended to be mounted on a light track  suspended from a light track  F21S8/066 ; details of supporting elements displaceable along a guiding element  F21V21/34 -- This subclass covers fixed, non-portable lighting fixtures that are designed to be mounted on a light track suspended from a support. The devices are intended to attach to a hanging track and may include supporting elements that can move along the guiding track.
[F21S 8/04] intended only for mounting on a ceiling or the like overhead structures -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Lighting device essentially made of an elongated section for suspending at least one linear light source -> F21S8/033
[F21S 8/043] mounted by means of a rigid support, e.g. bracket or arm -- This subgroup concerns fixed-installation lighting fixtures that are secured by a rigid support such as a bracket, arm or similar structure, rather than being freely suspended.
[F21S 8/046] having multiple lighting devices, e.g. connected to a common ceiling base -- This subclass covers lighting arrangements that incorporate two or more individual luminaires mounted together on a shared support such as a common ceiling base or grid, allowing the devices to be installed as a single unit and often controlled collectively. It applies to fixed, non-portable lighting systems where multiple fixtures are connected to the same mounting structure.
[F21S 8/06] by suspension -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Example: Hanging light fixture
[F21S 8/061] with a non-rigid pendant, i.e. a cable, wire or chain -- F21S 8/061 covers fixed lighting fixtures that are suspended by a flexible element such as a cable, wire or chain. The classification applies to non-portable lighting devices intended for permanent installation, where the pendant allows the fixture to be positioned or adjusted without a rigid mounting. It is part of the mechanical-engineering hierarchy under lighting and non-portable lighting devices.
[F21S 8/063] with a rigid pendant, i.e. a pipe or rod -- These are non-portable lighting fixtures intended for fixed installation that are suspended by a rigid pendant such as a pipe or rod, providing a stable support for the luminaire. The rigid pendant may be integrated with the fixture or separate, allowing precise positioning and stability in commercial or residential lighting setups.
[F21S 8/065] multi-branched, e.g. a chandelier -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Example: Ceiling chandelier
