[F21S 8/08] with a standard -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Free standing lighting devices with a lamp housing distant from the floor or the ground via a support, e.g. standing lamp for ambient light -> F21S6/005 - Spring-loaded poles fixed at both ends -> F21V21/13
[F21S 8/081] of low-built type, e.g. landscape light -- F21S 8/081 pertains to low-built fixed lighting fixtures such as landscape lights, ground-level or wall-mounted luminaires installed permanently in outdoor areas. These devices are designed for permanent placement on posts, walls or directly on the ground to illuminate pathways, gardens, lawns or other decorative outdoor spaces.
[F21S 8/083] of bollard type, i.e. with lighting fixture integrated into the standard or mounted on top of it and having substantially the same diameter
[F21S 8/085] of high-built type, e.g. street light -- The CPC subclass F21S 8/085 covers non-portable lighting devices that are installed in a fixed position and have a high-built structure, such as street lights and other elevated luminaires designed for permanent mounting. It falls under mechanical engineering, lighting, and non-portable lighting devices, specifically for fixtures intended for fixed installation.
[F21S 8/086] with lighting device attached sideways of the standard, e.g. for roads and highways -- F21S 8/086 covers fixed, non-portable lighting installations where the light source is mounted sideways relative to the standard support, such as luminaires placed on the side of posts or poles for roadways and highways, providing illumination from a lateral position rather than directly overhead.
[F21S 8/088] with lighting device mounted on top of the standard, e.g. for pedestrian zones -- The code F21S 8/088 classifies fixed lighting installations that incorporate a light source mounted on the upper part of a pole or standard, typical for pedestrian zones and similar public areas. It is a subcategory of non-portable lighting devices within mechanical engineering.
[F21S 9/00] Lighting devices with a built-in power supply -- This class covers lighting fixtures that incorporate their own power source, such as integrated batteries, fuel cells, or generators, enabling operation without external wiring. It includes self-contained lamps, emergency lights and similar devices where the built-in supply is a defining feature rather than the light-emitting element.
[F21S 9/02] the power supply being a battery or accumulator -- F21S 9/02 classifies non-portable lighting devices that contain an integrated power source, specifically a battery or accumulator, instead of relying on external supply. It is situated under mechanical engineering → lighting → non-portable lighting devices → lighting devices with a built-in power supply.
[F21S 9/022] Emergency lighting devices -- This class refers to fixed lighting units designed for emergency use that contain an integral power source such as a battery or capacitor, allowing them to operate when the normal supply is lost. The devices are installed in buildings, tunnels or similar structures to provide illumination during power outages.
[F21S 9/024] using a supplementary light source for emergency lighting -- This subclass covers devices that add a secondary light source to provide illumination during emergencies, supplementing the main lighting system. It includes arrangements that automatically activate a backup lamp when the primary power fails, ensuring continued visibility.
[F21S 9/026] rechargeable by using wind power, e.g. using wind turbines -- This subclass covers non-portable lighting devices that contain an integrated power source and can be recharged using wind energy, such as built-in wind turbines or other wind-driven generators. The classification is applied to lamps, flashlights, or similar lights where wind power is the primary recharging method.
[F21S 9/028] rechargeable by using hydropower, e.g. using water powered turbines -- This subclass relates to non-portable lighting devices that contain an integrated power supply which can be recharged by harvesting energy from moving water, for example using water-driven turbines. It covers systems where the internal battery or storage is replenished through hydropower.
[F21S 9/03] rechargeable by exposure to light -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[F21S 9/032] the solar unit being separate from the lighting unit -- This CPC subclass concerns non-portable lighting devices that incorporate a built-in power supply, specifically where the solar unit is provided as a separate component from the lighting unit. It applies to arrangements in which the photovoltaic collector and the lamp are distinct but together supply electricity to the fixture.
[F21S 9/035] the solar unit being integrated within the support for the lighting unit, e.g. within or on a pole -- F21S 9/035 covers lighting fixtures in which the solar power module is built into the fixture's support structure, such as being embedded in or attached to a pole, providing an integrated renewable energy source for the non-portable lighting device.
[F21S 9/037] the solar unit and the lighting unit being located within or on the same housing -- F21S 9/037 covers non-portable lighting devices in which a solar-energy collection unit and the illumination unit are integrated within a single housing or mounted together, allowing the lamp to operate from the solar-generated power without separate external storage. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering, lighting, and non-portable lighting devices with built-in power supplies.
[F21S 9/04] the power supply being a generator -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[F21S 9/043] driven by wind power, e.g. by wind turbines -- This subgroup covers non-portable lighting apparatus that integrate their own wind-energy generator, such as small turbines or wind-driven mechanisms, to power the light source without external electricity. It includes lamps, streetlights and other fixtures where wind motion is converted directly into electrical energy for illumination.
[F21S 9/046] driven by hydropower, e.g. by water powered turbines -- The subclass F21S 9/046 covers non-portable lighting devices that contain their own power source and are powered by hydropower, for example using water-driven turbines to generate electricity for the lamp.
[F21V] FUNCTIONAL FEATURES OR DETAILS OF LIGHTING DEVICES OR SYSTEMS THEREOF -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Functional features or details of lighting devices or systems of lighting devices, for example:  Aspects related to light emission or distribution:  Aspects not related to light emission or distribution, e.g. fittings:  Lighting devices combined with articles having a primary function unrelated to lighting that are not otherwise provided for.  **Glossary:** - Light source: The entity (e.g. light bulb, fluorescent tube, gas mantle or combustible charge) that generates light for a lighting device.
[F21V 1/00] Aspects related to light emission or distribution -- F21V 1/00 pertains to features of lighting devices that involve the generation, control, or distribution of light, covering methods and arrangements for emitting or directing light within a system. It addresses functional aspects of how light is produced and dispersed, falling under mechanical engineering and lighting categories.
[F21V 1/02] Frames -- F21V 1/02 covers the structural frames that support, enclose or mount lighting elements or assemblies, including the mechanical frames, brackets, housings and supporting skeletons that define the geometry and stability of a light-emitting device or distribution system.
[F21V 1/04] rigid -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Adjustable frame of a shade for light source -> F21V1/08
[F21V 1/06] foldable or collapsible -- F21V 1/06 refers to lighting devices or systems that are designed to be folded or collapsed, enabling compact storage or easy transport. The classification covers mechanisms, hinges, and structural features that allow the light source or its housing to be reduced in size while retaining functionality.
[F21V 1/08] adjustable -- F21V 1/08 covers lighting devices or systems that incorporate adjustable features, allowing the direction, intensity, or distribution of emitted light to be varied by the user or automatically.
[F21V 1/10] Rotating shades -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.  Rotating glass cover or globe or shade or fins due to heat of a light source
[F21V 11/00] Screens not covered by groups -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Cooling arrangements for screens not covered by groups -> F21V1/00 , F21V3/00 , F21V7/00 or F21V9/00 , F21V29/502
[F21V 11/02] using parallel laminae or strips, e.g. of Venetian-blind type -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Screens using crossed laminae or strips -> F21V11/06
[F21V 11/04] adjustable -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Adjustable lamellae  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[F21V 11/06] using crossed laminae or strips -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[F21V 11/065] adjustable -- The F21V 11/065 subclass covers lighting devices that incorporate screens, diffusers or similar elements whose position or orientation can be varied to modify the illumination. It includes mechanisms that allow adjustment of the screen angle, tilt, height or shape, thereby changing light distribution or intensity. This class applies to functional features of lighting systems not covered by other more specific groups.
[F21V 11/08] using diaphragms containing one or more apertures -- The code covers lighting devices that employ diaphragms with one or more openings to shape, direct or modulate the emitted light. Such diaphragms may be movable or fixed and are used to adjust illumination patterns, intensity or beam spread in various applications.
[F21V 11/10] of iris type -- F21V 11/10 classifies lighting devices that incorporate an iris-type mechanism, meaning a system of overlapping leaves or blades that can be opened or closed to control the amount of light emitted or to shape the light beam. The entry covers functional features of such adjustable apertures that are not covered by more specific groups within the lighting subsection of mechanical engineering.
[F21V 11/12] of slot type -- F21V 11/12 classifies lighting devices that incorporate a slot-type screen, covering designs where light is emitted through narrow, elongated apertures rather than conventional panes or lenses. It applies to mechanical engineering solutions that modify beam shape, distribution or glare control by using such slot openings.
[F21V 11/14] with many small apertures -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.  Polymeric light control film with holes on an elongated light source
[F21V 11/16] using sheets without apertures, e.g. fixed -- F21V 11/16 concerns lighting devices that employ solid sheets or panels with no openings, such as fixed diffusers or covers that shape or distribute light. The sheets are integral to the system and are not perforated.
[F21V 11/18] movable, e.g. flaps, slides -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.  Light-shielding plates for stage projectors
[F21V 11/183] pivotable -- F21V 11/183 covers lighting devices that incorporate a pivotable element, allowing the lamp or reflector to be rotated or angled relative to the housing for directional illumination. The classification is used for mechanisms that provide adjustability of the light beam without requiring separate mounting brackets.
[F21V 11/186] slidable
[F21V 1/12] Composite shades -- Composite shades are multi-material or multi-layer coverings for lighting devices that combine transparent, translucent or opaque elements to control, diffuse or direct emitted light. They may integrate diffusers, reflectors or protective layers to achieve specific illumination patterns, color or glare reduction while providing structural stability.
[F21V 1/14] Covers for frames -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Shades characterised by the material  Stretched fly attached to a ceiling frame  A stretched fly attached to a ceiling frame
[F21V 1/143] The cover being attached to a supporting lampshade frame -- F21V 1/143 covers lighting devices where the cover is affixed directly to the structural frame of a lampshade. It relates to designs that integrate the protective or decorative cover with the supporting frame, influencing light distribution and aesthetics.
[F21V 1/146] Frameless shades -- F21V 1/146 classifies lamp shades that have no surrounding frame, describing their construction, mounting methods and material characteristics that influence how light is emitted and distributed from the source. It addresses design features that affect illumination quality and directional control without a supporting rim or cage.
[F21V 1/16] characterised by the material -- F21V 1/16 covers lighting devices or systems where the choice of material - such as glass, polymer, ceramic or semiconductor - defines their functional features, influencing light generation, shaping, transmission or protection. The classification is used for inventions whose performance or design is determined primarily by material properties rather than geometry or electrical aspects.
[F21V 1/17] the material comprising photoluminescent substances -- This subclass covers lighting devices whose bodies or components are made from materials that contain photoluminescent substances, which absorb energy and re-emit visible light, such as phosphors, glow-in-the-dark paints, or luminescent coatings. It includes any structural element designed to generate illumination through photoluminescence rather than conventional electric discharge.
[F21V 1/18] the material being paper
[F21V 1/20] the material being glass -- F21V 1/20 covers lighting devices or systems in which the principal material is glass, including the use of glass for lenses, covers, reflectors or envelopes that affect light emission and distribution. The subclass addresses glass composition, shape and optical properties as they relate to the functional features of the lighting apparatus.
[F21V 1/22] the material being plastics -- F21V 1/22 concerns lighting apparatus whose structural or optical parts are made of plastic, focusing on how plastic housings, lenses, reflectors or diffusers influence light generation, transmission or distribution. The subclass captures inventions where the use of plastic material is a defining functional feature of the lighting device.
[F21V 1/24] the material being metal
[F21V 1/26] Manufacturing shades -- This subclass covers the manufacturing processes for shading components used in lighting devices, such as diffusers, reflectors, filters and other light-modulating elements. It includes methods for shaping, treating, assembling and finishing these shades to control light emission, distribution or glare.
[F21V 13/00] Producing particular characteristics or distribution of the light emitted by means of a combination of elements specified in two or more of main groups -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Controlling the distribution of the light emitted by adjustment of elements -> F21V14/00
[F21V 13/02] Combinations of only two kinds of elements -- F21V 13/02 covers lighting devices that achieve specific light characteristics or distribution by using a combination of exactly two different types of elements, such as lamps, reflectors, lenses or filters, drawn from two or more main groups. The focus is on how these paired elements interact to shape the emitted light.
[F21V 13/04] the elements being reflectors and refractors -- F21V 13/04 concerns lighting devices that obtain particular light characteristics or distribution by employing a combination of reflectors and refractors. It falls within the functional features or details of lighting systems category of the mechanical engineering section.
[F21V 13/045] for portable lighting devices -- F21V 13/045 pertains to portable lighting devices whose light output or distribution is shaped by combining elements from two or more main groups, such as multiple light sources, reflectors, lenses or filters. The entry addresses functional features that produce particular characteristics of the emitted light.
[F21V 13/06] a reflector being rotatable -- F21V 13/06 relates to lighting devices that achieve specific light characteristics or distribution by employing a reflector that can be rotated, allowing the beam pattern or intensity to be adjusted. The subclass covers any arrangement where the rotatable reflector is a functional element for controlling emitted light.
[F21V 13/08] the elements being filters or photoluminescent elements and reflectors -- This CPC subclass F21V 13/08 covers lighting devices where the light output is modified using filters, photoluminescent materials and reflectors acting together to shape colour, intensity or beam pattern. It relates to configurations that combine two or more of these elements to achieve a specific spectral or spatial distribution of the emitted light.
[F21V 13/10] the elements being reflectors and screens -- This subclass covers lighting devices whose functional elements consist of reflectors combined with screens, used to shape, direct or modify the light output in order to achieve specific illumination characteristics or distribution.
[F21V 13/12] Combinations of only three kinds of elements -- This CPC subclass deals with lighting arrangements that attain particular light qualities or distribution by using a combination of exactly three distinct element types, such as sources, reflectors, lenses, filters, or diffusers. It applies when the functional effect results from integrating three of the main element groups defined in the broader F21V category.
[F21V 13/14] the elements being filters or photoluminescent elements, reflectors and refractors -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[F21V 14/00] Controlling the distribution of the light emitted by adjustment of elements -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Reflectors with provision for adjusting the curvature -> F21V7/16 - Light filters or the like with provision for controlling the colour or intensity -> F21V9/40 - Screens using adjustable parallel laminae or strips -> F21V11/04 - Screens using iris-type diaphragms -> F21V11/10 - Screens using movable sheets without apertures -> F21V11/18 - Adjustable mountings for lighting devices -> F21V21/14
[F21V 14/003] by interposition of elements with electrically controlled variable light transmissivity, e.g. liquid crystal elements or electrochromic devices -- The subclass covers lighting systems that regulate light distribution by inserting elements whose transparency can be changed electrically, such as liquid-crystal panels or electrochromic devices, allowing dynamic control of the emitted illumination.
[F21V 14/006] by means of optical elements, e.g. films, filters or screens, being rolled up around a roller -- The code F21V 14/006 falls under mechanical engineering, lighting, and functional features of lighting devices that control light distribution. It covers systems that adjust illumination using optical elements such as films, filters or screens that are wound around a roller
[F21V 14/02] by movement of light sources -- The CPC subclass F21V 14/02 covers lighting devices where the distribution of emitted light is controlled by physically moving the light-source. It includes mechanisms that adjust the position or orientation of lamps or LEDs to shape illumination patterns.
[F21V 14/025] in portable lighting devices -- The class covers portable lighting devices that incorporate adjustable components-such as movable reflectors, lenses, diffusers or other optical elements-to modify how the emitted light is distributed. It includes mechanisms that let the user change beam shape, direction or intensity by altering the position or configuration of these elements.
[F21V 14/04] by movement of reflectors -- F21V 14/04 covers lighting devices that control the distribution of emitted light by physically moving reflectors. The adjustment of reflector position alters the direction or shape of the light beam, allowing functions such as beam steering, focus change, or pattern modulation in lamps, headlights and spotlights.
[F21V 14/045] in portable lighting devices -- F21V 14/045 covers portable lighting devices that manage how light is spread by moving or modifying components such as lenses, reflectors, diffusers or shutters. The classification applies to any mechanism that adjusts the direction, shape or intensity of the emitted light to achieve a desired illumination pattern. It is a subcategory of functional features of lighting systems within mechanical engineering.
[F21V 14/06] by movement of refractors -- F21V 14/06 covers lighting devices in which the light distribution is regulated by moving refracting elements. The classification applies to mechanisms that adjust prisms, lenses or other refractors to steer or spread emitted light for desired illumination patterns.
[F21V 14/065] in portable lighting devices -- F21V 14/065 covers portable lighting devices in which the distribution of emitted light is varied by adjusting elements such as reflectors, lenses, diffusers or movable shields. The class includes hand-held lamps, flashlights and other battery-powered fixtures that incorporate mechanisms for changing beam shape or direction.
[F21V 14/08] by movement of the screens -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Changing characteristic or distribution of light by movement of screens or filters
[F21V 14/085] in portable lighting devices -- The CPC subclass F21V 14/085 covers portable lighting devices whose light distribution is regulated by adjusting elements such as reflectors, lenses, shutters or other optical components. It falls under mechanical engineering → lighting → functional features of lighting devices, focusing on methods for shaping or directing emitted light in handheld or portable applications.
[F21V 15/00] Aspects not related to  light emission or distribution, e.g. fittings -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Protecting lighting devices from damage due to external interactions, e.g. anti-vandalism devices, cages  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Protecting lighting devices from thermal damage -> F21V29/00 - Gas-tight or water-tight arrangements -> F21V31/00
[F21V 15/005] Measures against vandalism, stealing or tampering  F21V15/02 ,  F21V15/04  take precedence -- F21V 15/005 pertains to safety measures applied to lighting fittings and systems to prevent vandalism, theft or tampering. It covers protective designs, locks, enclosures or other structural features that deter unauthorized access or damage, excluding aspects of light emission.
[F21V 15/01] Housings, e.g. material or assembling of housing parts -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Cages for protecting lighting devices from damage -> F21V15/02 - Housings forming signs or letters -> G09F13/0404
[F21V 15/012] Housings with variable shape or dimensions, e.g. by means of elastically deformable materials or by movement of parts forming telescopic extensions of the housing body -- This classification covers housings for lighting devices that can change shape or size, using flexible materials or telescopic components, allowing adjustment of the enclosure without affecting light output. It includes designs where the housing expands, contracts, or deforms to adapt to mounting conditions or aesthetic requirements.
[F21V 15/013] the housing being an extrusion -- This CPC subclass deals with lighting devices where the housing is formed by an extrusion process; it covers extruded housings for lamps, luminaires, or components, focusing on manufacturing aspects rather than light emission.
[F21V 15/015] Devices for covering joints between adjacent lighting devices -- F21V 15/015 pertains to devices that cover the joints between adjacent lighting fixtures, such as trim pieces, gaskets or sealing elements that hide gaps and protect the junction. It addresses mechanical solutions for joining lighting units without influencing light emission or distribution.
[F21V 15/02] Cages -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.

[F21V 17/00] Fastening of component parts of lighting devices, e.g. shades, globes, refractors, reflectors, filters, screens, grids or protective cages -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Fastening of light sources or light holders -> F21V19/00
[F21V 17/002] with provision for interchangeability, i.e. component parts being especially adapted to be replaced by another part with the same or a different function  F21V17/10  takes precedence -- This CPC subclass deals with fastening of lighting device components such as shades, globes, reflectors, filters, screens, grids or protective cages where the parts are specially adapted for interchangeability, allowing them to be replaced by another part with the same or a different function.
[F21V 17/005] with keying means, i.e. for enabling the assembling of component parts in distinctive positions, e.g. for preventing wrong mounting -- This CPC subclass concerns fastening of lighting-device parts such as shades, globes, reflectors or filters by means that include keying features, enabling the components to be assembled only in designated positions and thus preventing incorrect mounting.
[F21V 17/007] with provision for shipment or storage -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Components of lighting devices intended to be easily dismounted or with reduced dismounted volume for shipment or storage

[F21V 17/10] characterised by specific fastening means or way of fastening -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Fastening of component parts of lighting devices with provision for adjustment -> F21V17/02 - Fastening of component parts of lighting devices, the fastening being onto or by the light source -> F21V17/04 - Fastening of component parts of lighting devices, the fastening being onto or by the lampholder -> F21V17/06 - Fastening of component parts of lighting devices, onto the supporting or suspending arrangements of the lighting device, e.g. power cords, standards -> F21V17/08
[F21V 17/101] permanently, e.g. welding, gluing or riveting -- F21V 17/101 covers permanent fastening methods, such as welding, gluing or riveting, used to join component parts of lighting devices including shades, globes, refractors, reflectors, filters, screens, grids or protective cages.
[F21V 17/102] using gravity or suction -- This subclass relates to fastening the parts of lighting devices-such as shades, globes, reflectors, filters or protective cages-by using gravity or suction instead of conventional mechanical fasteners. It covers designs where the weight of the component or a pressure differential holds elements in place.
[F21V 17/104] using feather joints, e.g. tongues and grooves, with or without friction -- This subclass covers methods of attaching parts of lighting fixtures such as shades, globes or reflectors by using feather joints, for example tongue-and-groove connections, whether they rely on friction or not.
[F21V 17/105] using magnets -- F21V 17/105 concerns lighting devices where magnets are employed to fasten component parts such as shades, globes, reflectors, filters, screens, grids or protective cages, enabling detachable or adjustable assembly without screws or adhesives. It falls under functional features of lighting devices within mechanical engineering.
[F21V 17/107] using hinge joints -- F21V 17/107 covers fastening of lighting device components such as shades, globes, reflectors or protective cages by means of hinge joints. It is classified under mechanical engineering, lighting, functional features or details of lighting devices or systems.
[F21V 17/108] using hook and loop-type fasteners -- The code F21V 17/108 covers fastening methods for lighting-device components that employ hook-and-loop type fasteners. It applies to the attachment of parts such as shades, globes, reflectors, filters, screens, grids or protective cages without screws or adhesives.
[F21V 17/12] by screwing -- The subclass concerns fastening parts of lighting devices-such as shades, globes, reflectors, filters, screens, grids or protective cages-by means of a screw connection. It classifies methods where threaded elements are used to secure these components within lighting assemblies.
[F21V 17/14] Bayonet-type fastening -- F21V 17/14 pertains to bayonet-type fastenings employed to secure component parts of lighting devices, such as shades, globes, refractors, reflectors, filters, screens, grids or protective cages, within the broader class of functional features of lighting systems.
[F21V 17/16] by deformation of parts -- The F21V 17/16 subclass covers fastening of lighting-device components-such as shades, globes, reflectors, filters, screens, grids or protective cages-by deforming the parts themselves, for example by bending, crimping or swaging to lock the elements together without separate fasteners.
[F21V 17/162] the parts being subjected to traction or compression, e.g. coil springs -- This CPC subclass covers fastening elements used in lighting devices where the parts experience tensile or compressive loads, such as coil springs that hold shades, globes, reflectors, filters, screens, grids or protective cages in place. It falls under Mechanical Engineering → Lighting → Functional features of lighting devices → Fastening of component parts.
[F21V 17/164] the parts being subjected to bending, e.g. snap joints -- This subclass covers fastening of lighting device components by bending the parts so that they interlock, such as snap-joint connections of shades, globes, reflectors, filters, screens or protective cages. It pertains to designs where the parts themselves are deformed to create a secure attachment without additional hardware.
[F21V 17/166] the parts being subjected to torsion, e.g. spiral springs -- F21V 17/166 covers fastening elements of lighting devices that are subjected to torsional forces, such as spiral springs used to secure shades, globes, reflectors or other components. The classification applies to any part that rotates or twists to provide holding or adjustment functions within the lighting assembly.
[F21V 17/168] the parts being resilient rings acting substantially isotropically, e.g. split rings -- This subclass (F21V 17/168) concerns fastening components of lighting devices with resilient, substantially isotropic rings such as split-rings, used to secure shades, globes, refractors, reflectors, filters, screens, grids or protective cages.
[F21V 17/18] Latch-type fastening, e.g. with rotary action -- F21V 17/18 classifies latch-type fastening mechanisms, especially those that operate with rotary motion, for securing parts of lighting devices such as shades, globes, reflectors, filters, screens or protective cages. It falls under Mechanical Engineering → Lighting → Functional features or details of lighting devices or systems.
[F21V 17/20] by toggle-action levers -- F21V 17/20 relates to the fastening of parts in lighting devices such as shades, globes, reflectors or filters by means of toggle-action levers, detailing how these levers secure components together within the overall mechanical engineering of lighting systems.
[F21V 19/00] Fastening of light sources or lamp holders -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Special means for attaching the candle to the candle holder -> F21V35/003 - Fastening electric light sources in vehicle headlamps -> F21S41/19 - Fastening electric light sources in vehicle signal lamps -> F21S43/19 - Fastening electric light source solely by the coupling device -> H01R33/00
[F21V 19/0005] of sources having contact pins, wires or blades, e.g. pinch sealed lamp  F21V19/001  takes precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Fastening of removably mounted LEDs; Fastening of removably mounted PCBs holding light sources -> F21V19/001
[F21V 19/001] the light sources being semiconductors devices, e.g. LEDs -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Fastening of light sources or lampholders: Details of lamp holders, terminals or connectors for vehicle headlamps -> F21S41/192 - Fastening of light sources or lampholders: Details of lamp holders, terminals or connectors for vehicle rear lights -> F21S43/195
[F21V 19/0015] Fastening arrangements intended to retain light sources -- This CPC subclass (F21V 19/0015) covers fastening arrangements that are specifically designed to retain light sources or lamp holders, including clips, brackets, screws, spring-loaded devices and other mechanical means that secure bulbs within fixtures. It falls under mechanical engineering → lighting → functional features or details of lighting devices or systems thereof.
[F21V 19/002] the fastening means engaging the encapsulation or the packaging of the semiconductor device -- The code covers fastening components that engage the encapsulation or packaging of semiconductor devices used in lighting, such as clips, brackets or mounting tabs that hold an LED or other chip within its mold or housing. These means secure the light source to the lamp holder or module.
[F21V 19/0025] the fastening means engaging the conductors of the light source, i.e. providing simultaneous fastening of the light sources and their electric connections -- This subclass concerns fastening devices that simultaneously secure a light source and engage its electrical conductors, providing a combined mechanical mounting and electrical connection for lamps or lamp holders.
[F21V 19/003] Fastening of light source holders, e.g. of circuit boards or substrates holding light sources -- The subclass F21V 19/003 deals with mechanical means for attaching light-emitting components to supporting structures such as circuit boards, substrates or lamp holders. It covers fasteners, clips, adhesives, soldered joints and similar securing arrangements that hold the light source in place within a lighting device.
[F21V 19/0035] the fastening means being capable of simultaneously attaching of an other part, e.g. a housing portion or an optical component -- F21V 19/0035 defines fastening means that can secure a light source or lamp holder while simultaneously attaching an additional component, such as a housing portion or an optical element, to the lighting device.
[F21V 19/004] by deformation of parts or snap action mountings, e.g. using clips -- The entry F21V 19/004 refers to fastening light sources or lamp holders by deforming parts or using snap-action mountings such as clips, which secure the lamp without separate fasteners. This functional feature falls under mechanical engineering, lighting, and details of lighting devices.
[F21V 19/0045] by tongue and groove connections, e.g. dovetail interlocking means fixed by sliding -- The entry covers fastening of light sources or lamp holders by means of tongue-and-groove or dovetail connections that lock when slid into place. It applies to devices where a sliding interlock secures the lamp or holder within a lighting system. This falls under mechanical engineering, lighting functional features, and specifically fasteners for lamps.
[F21V 19/005] by permanent fixing means, e.g. gluing, riveting or embedding in a potting compound -- F21V 19/005 is a CPC subclass that deals with permanently fixing light sources or lamp holders to a support, for example by gluing, riveting or embedding in a potting compound. The code falls under mechanical engineering, lighting, functional features or details of lighting devices, fastening of light sources or lamp holders. It is used to classify patents that describe such permanent fixing means.
[F21V 19/0055] by screwing -- The subclass F21V 19/0055 covers fastening methods for light sources or lamp holders that rely on a screw connection. It pertains to mechanical engineering of lighting devices where the light source is secured by rotating the holder onto a threaded socket.
[F21V 19/006] of point-like light sources, e.g. incandescent or halogen lamps, with screw-threaded or bayonet base  of sources having contact pins, wires or blades  F21V19/0005 ; of LEDs or sources mounted on printed-circuit board  F21V19/001 -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Fastening of light sources or lampholders of sources having contact pins, wires or blades -> F21V19/0005 - Fastening of removably mounted LEDs; Fastening of removably mounted PCBs holding light sources -> F21V19/001
[F21V 19/0065] at least one conductive element acting as a support means, e.g. spring-mounted contact plate in a bayonet base -- The CPC subclass F21V 19/0065 covers lighting devices that secure a light source using a conductive support element, for example a spring-mounted contact plate that also provides electrical contact in a bayonet-type lamp base.
[F21V 19/007] the support means engaging the vessel of the source -- F21V 19/007 deals with the support structures that engage the vessel of a light source, such as brackets, clips or holders that secure lamps, LEDs or other emitters within a lighting system. It falls under mechanical engineering → lighting → functional features of lighting devices, specifically addressing the fastening of light sources or lamp holders.
[F21V 19/0075] of tubular light sources, e.g. ring-shaped fluorescent light sources -- F21V 19/0075 relates to the means of fastening tubular light sources, such as ring-shaped fluorescent lamps, within lighting apparatus. It covers mounting, retaining or securing arrangements designed for these tube-type emitters in lighting systems.
[F21V 19/008] of straight tubular light sources, e.g. straight fluorescent tubes, soffit lamps -- The CPC subclass F21V 19/008 falls under mechanical engineering → lighting → functional features or details of lighting devices, and it specifically addresses means for fastening straight tubular light sources such as straight fluorescent tubes or soffit lamps.
[F21V 19/0085] at least one conductive element acting as a support means, e.g. resilient contact blades, piston-like contact
[F21V 19/009] the support means engaging the vessel of the source -- F21V 19/009 covers support structures that engage the vessel of a light source, such as clips, brackets or other fastening means that hold bulbs, LEDs or similar emitters in place within a lighting system.
[F21V 19/0095] of U-shaped tubular light sources, e.g. compact fluorescent tubes
[F21V 19/02] with provision for adjustment, e.g. for focusing -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Details regarding the mechanism for the adjustment of the light source or lampholder
[F21V 19/04] with provision for changing light source, e.g. turret -- F21V 19/04 pertains to lighting devices that include a mechanism for changing the light source, such as a turret-type holder. It covers fastening arrangements that enable quick or repeated replacement of lamps or lamp holders within luminaires.
[F21V 19/047] by using spare light sources comprised in or attached to the lighting device and being intended to replace a defect light source by manual mounting -- This subclass describes lighting fixtures that include spare light sources or lamp holders attached to the device, allowing a user to replace a failed lamp by manually mounting the spare, thus providing a built-in replacement capability.
[F21V 19/06] Fastening incandescent mantles or other incandescent bodies to lamp parts -- The code F21V 19/06 covers ways of securing incandescent mantles or other glowing bodies to parts of a lamp, focusing on the fastening mechanisms that attach the light source to its holder or structure within lighting devices.

[F21V 21/041] Mounting arrangements specially adapted for false ceiling panels or partition walls made of plates  F21V21/047  takes precedence -- F21V 21/041 covers mounting arrangements specially adapted for false-ceiling panels or partition walls made of plates, designed to support, suspend or attach lighting devices to these surfaces. It details fasteners, brackets and support structures tailored for plate-type ceilings and walls, and is superseded by the more specific subclass F21V21/047.
[F21V 21/042] using clamping means, e.g. for clamping with panel or wall -- The classification covers arrangements that support, suspend or attach lighting devices by means of clamping onto a surface such as a panel or wall, including mechanisms that secure fixtures without permanent fasteners. It pertains to functional features of lighting devices within mechanical engineering.
[F21V 21/043] actuated by screwing -- This subclass covers lighting devices whose supporting, suspending or attaching arrangement is operated by a screw action, for example fixtures that are mounted, adjusted or secured by turning a screw to engage the fixture with a base or bracket. It pertains to the functional feature of actuation by screwing.
[F21V 21/044] with elastically deformable elements, e.g. spring tongues -- The CPC class F21V 21/044 covers supporting, suspending, or attaching arrangements for lighting devices that incorporate elastically deformable elements such as spring tongues to secure the lamp and allow flexible mounting. This falls under mechanical engineering, lighting functional features.
[F21V 21/045] being tensioned by translation of parts, e.g. by pushing or pulling -- The code F21V 21/045 pertains to lighting devices whose supporting or suspending structures are tensioned by moving parts-such as pulling or pushing components-to create tensile force. It falls under mechanical engineering, lighting, and functional features of lighting systems dealing with attachment arrangements.
[F21V 21/046] being tensioned by rotation of parts -- This subgroup covers lighting supports, suspensions, or mounts that achieve tension through the rotational movement of components, such as twisted cords or screw-driven mechanisms that tighten a fixture in place.
[F21V 21/047] Mounting arrangements with fastening means engaging the inner surface of a hole in a ceiling or wall, e.g. for solid walls or for blind holes -- Mounting arrangements that secure lighting fixtures by using fastening elements that grip the inner surface of a hole in a ceiling or wall, suitable for solid or blind holes, enabling the device to be attached without external brackets. This subclass covers designs where the fastening means engage the hole's interior to support, suspend, or attach the lighting apparatus.

[F21V 21/049] Mounting arrangements for attaching lighting devices to the ceiling, the lighting devices being recessed in a false or stretched ceiling -- This CPC subclass defines mounting arrangements that support, suspend, or attach recessed lighting devices to false or stretched ceilings. They are used in outdoor lighting installations such as roadway or area illumination where a secure, ground-based attachment is required.

[F21V 2200/00] Use of light guides, e.g. fibre optic devices, in lighting devices or systems -- This subclass pertains to lighting apparatuses or systems that incorporate light guides, such as fibre-optic devices, to transmit, distribute or shape illumination. It includes designs where light is carried through guide elements to target areas, improving control over light delivery and enabling flexible lighting configurations.
[F21V 2200/10] of light guides of the optical fibres type -- F21V 2200/10 covers the incorporation of light guides made from optical-fibres or similar fibre-optic devices into lighting products or systems, detailing how the fibres transmit, distribute or shape illumination. It applies to any mechanical-engineering lighting design that uses such guided-light technology to achieve specific functional lighting effects.
[F21V 2200/13] the light being emitted at the end of the guide -- This CPC subclass concerns lighting arrangements in which light is transmitted through a light guide such as an optical fiber and emitted at the distal end of the guide. It covers devices that use the guide to direct illumination to a specific point or surface, including the characteristics of the emitted light.
[F21V 2200/15] the light being emitted along at least a portion of the outer surface of the guide -- This CPC subgroup covers lighting configurations where light travels within a guide such as a fibre-optic element and is emitted from at least part of the guide's outer surface. It includes designs that deliberately radiate light along the guide's exterior to illuminate an area or object.
[F21V 2200/17] characterised by the admission of light into the guide -- This classification covers lighting devices or systems that incorporate light guides such as fibre-optic elements, focusing on the feature where light is admitted into the guide. It is relevant for designs that channel external illumination into the optical conduit as part of the device's functional architecture.
[F21V 2200/20] of light guides of a generally planar shape -- The subclass covers lighting devices that employ light guides having a generally planar shape, such as flat waveguides or fiber-optic plates used to spread illumination across a surface. It relates to the structural design and integration of these planar optical elements within lighting systems.
[F21V 2200/30] of light guides doped with fluorescent agents -- This CPC subclass covers lighting devices that incorporate light guides, such as fiber-optic elements, which are infused with fluorescent materials to modify or enhance the emitted light. It addresses the use of doped light guides to achieve colour conversion, spectral shifting, or improved illumination efficiency within lighting systems.
[F21V 2200/40] of hollow light guides -- F21V 2200/40 covers the use of hollow light guides, such as fibre-optic or similar wave-guiding structures, within lighting devices or systems. It relates to functional features of lighting engineering where light is transmitted through an internal cavity to direct illumination, typically improving distribution, safety, or design flexibility. This classification falls under mechanical engineering, lighting, and details of lighting device functions.
[F21V 23/00] Arrangement of electric circuit elements in or on lighting devices -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Electric circuits per se -> H05B39/00
[F21V 23/001] the elements being electrical wires or cables -- This CPC subclass concerns the arrangement of electric circuit elements within lighting devices, specifically when those elements consist of electrical wires or cables. It covers how wires and cables are positioned, routed or integrated in lamps, luminaires or related lighting systems to achieve functional or structural objectives.
[F21V 23/002] Arrangements of cables or conductors inside a lighting device, e.g. means for guiding along parts of the housing or in a pivoting arm -- This CPC subclass F21V 23/002 pertains to the arrangement of cables or conductors inside lighting devices, including means for guiding them along parts of the housing or within a pivoting arm to protect and organise electrical connections.
[F21V 23/003] the elements being electronics drivers or controllers for operating the light source, e.g. for a LED array -- This subclass covers the arrangement of electronic drivers or controllers that power light sources, such as LED arrays, within lighting devices. It includes circuits that regulate voltage, current, and dimming functions to ensure proper operation of the light source.
[F21V 23/004] arranged on a substrate, e.g. a printed circuit board -- This subclass concerns lighting devices in which the electric circuit elements are integrated onto a substrate such as a printed circuit board, forming part of the lamp or fixture. It addresses the layout and mounting of these components on the substrate to achieve functional and compact lighting solutions.
[F21V 23/005] the substrate is supporting also the light source -- F21V 23/005 covers lighting devices in which the substrate serves both as a mechanical support and as a holder for the light source, integrating the source directly onto the supporting material. It relates to the arrangement of electric circuit elements on or within the substrate of the lighting apparatus.
[F21V 23/006] the substrate being distinct from the light source holder -- This CPC subclass defines lighting devices in which the substrate that carries the electric circuit is separate from the holder that secures the light source. It covers arrangements where the substrate is distinct, allowing independent material or mounting choices for the circuit board and the lamp holder.
[F21V 23/007] enclosed in a casing -- F21V 23/007 relates to lighting devices in which the electric circuit elements are positioned inside an enclosed housing. It classifies functional details of lighting systems that involve arranging the circuitry within a casing, covering the integration of components in a protective shell. This entry falls under mechanical engineering → lighting → functional features or details of lighting devices or systems thereof.
[F21V 23/008] the casing being outside the housing of the lighting device -- F21V 23/008 classifies lighting devices in which the protective casing is positioned outside the main housing, separating the external enclosure from the internal components. This arrangement concerns the layout of electric circuit elements on or within the device, emphasizing the external placement of the casing as a functional feature in the lighting system.
[F21V 23/009] the casing being inside the housing of the lighting device -- This CPC subclass deals with lighting fixtures where the protective casing that contains the electric circuit is located inside the outer housing of the lamp. It covers designs that integrate the circuit enclosure within the device's body to influence thermal, mechanical or aesthetic aspects.
[F21V 23/02] the elements being transformers, impedances -- F21V 23/02 covers lighting devices in which electric circuit elements such as transformers or impedances are arranged inside or on the device. It belongs to the functional-feature subclass of lighting systems within mechanical engineering. The group addresses how these components are configured for power handling or signal conditioning.
[F21V 23/023] Power supplies in a casing  F21V23/003  takes precedence -- This classification covers the integration of power-supply components within the housing of a lighting apparatus, detailing their placement and interconnection inside the lamp or luminaire casing. It relates to functional features of lighting devices concerning the arrangement of electric circuit elements.
[F21V 23/026] Fastening of transformers or ballasts -- It covers methods and structures for securing transformers or ballasts within lighting fixtures, such as brackets, clips, screws, adhesives or other fasteners that provide mechanical stability and proper electrical integration. The code belongs to the functional-feature subgroup dealing with the arrangement of electric circuit elements in or on lighting devices.
[F21V 23/04] the elements being switches -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Electric means varying continuously or discontinuously the light intensity, e.g. ON/OFF switch, potentiometer, dimmer,...etc
[F21V 23/0407] for flashing -- F21V 23/0407 classifies lighting devices whose electric circuit elements are arranged to generate a flashing operation, indicating the specific configuration of the circuitry that produces intermittent light output.
[F21V 23/0414] specially adapted to be used with portable lighting devices -- This CPC subclass covers arrangements of electrical circuit elements incorporated into lighting devices that are designed for portable use, such as flashlights, battery-powered lamps, and other handheld illumination units. It includes specific adaptations of the circuit layout, power source integration, and control components to suit the size, weight, and handling requirements of portable lighting.
[F21V 23/0421] the switch being part of, or disposed on the tail cap portion thereof -- F21V 23/0421 refers to lighting devices in which the electric switch is built into or mounted on the tail-cap portion of the lamp, representing a specific arrangement of circuit elements on the rear end of the light source. This subclass falls under functional features of lighting devices, focusing on the integration of switching components within the tail-cap assembly.
[F21V 23/0428] the switch being part of, or disposed on the lamp head portion thereof -- F21V 23/0428 classifies lighting devices in which the arrangement of electric-circuit elements includes a switch that is integral to, or mounted on, the lamp-head portion of the fixture, covering designs where the control element forms part of the lamp itself.
[F21V 23/0435] activated by remote control means -- F21V 23/0435 covers lighting devices whose electric circuit elements are arranged so that the light source can be turned on, off or dimmed by a remote control signal. It includes systems using infrared, radio-frequency or other wireless commands to activate the lighting.
[F21V 23/0442] activated by means of a sensor, e.g. motion or photodetectors -- The code F21V 23/0442 relates to lighting devices where the electric circuit elements are arranged in or on the lamp and the device is switched on by a sensor such as a motion detector or photodetector. It covers the functional integration of sensor-triggered control within the lighting system.
[F21V 23/045] the sensor receiving a signal from a remote controller -- F21V 23/045 pertains to the arrangement of electric circuit elements in or on lighting devices, focusing on sensors that receive signals from remote controllers. This classification captures how such sensors are integrated within the lighting system's circuitry.
[F21V 23/0457] the sensor sensing the operating status of the lighting device, e.g. to detect failure of a light source or to provide feedback to the device -- This code refers to sensors built into lighting devices that monitor their operating status, for example detecting a lamp failure or supplying feedback to the control system, and is classified under mechanical engineering, lighting, functional features of lighting devices, and the arrangement of electric circuit elements.
[F21V 23/0464] the sensor sensing the level of ambient illumination, e.g. dawn or dusk sensors -- The CPC subgroup F21V 23/0464 covers lighting devices in which the electric circuit arrangement includes a sensor that detects ambient illumination, such as dawn-or-dusk light level detectors. It falls under mechanical engineering → lighting → functional features or details of lighting devices or systems thereof.
[F21V 23/0471] the sensor detecting the proximity, the presence or the movement of an object or a person -- F21V 23/0471 refers to the design of electric circuit elements in lighting devices that incorporate a sensor to detect the proximity, presence or movement of an object or person, enabling the light to respond automatically to such stimuli. It is classified within mechanical engineering → lighting → functional features or details of lighting devices or systems thereof.
[F21V 23/0478] by means of an image recording device, e.g. a camera -- The subgroup addresses lighting devices whose electric circuit arrangement is controlled or configured using an image-recording device such as a camera, allowing visual feedback to adjust illumination, positioning or monitoring functions. It relates to integrating cameras into the circuitry of lamps or lighting systems for adaptive or responsive operation.
[F21V 23/0485] the sensor sensing the physical interaction between a user and certain areas located on the lighting device, e.g. a touch sensor -- The classification covers sensors that detect a user's physical interaction with specific zones on a lighting device, such as touch or proximity sensors integrated into the fixture. It relates to the arrangement of electric circuit elements within or on lighting apparatuses.
[F21V 23/0492] the sensor detecting a change in orientation, a movement or an acceleration of the lighting device, e.g. a tilt switch -- This CPC subclass covers lighting devices that incorporate an orientation-sensing circuit element, such as a tilt switch, which detects changes in tilt, motion or acceleration and modifies the device's operation.
[F21V 23/06] the elements being coupling devices -- The F21V 23/06 subclass relates to lighting devices in which the arrangement of the electric circuit elements serves as coupling devices, meaning the components act as connectors or interfaces that join parts of the circuit within the lamp or its housing. It covers designs where these coupling functions are integrated in the fixture or its surrounding structure.
[F21V 25/00] Safety devices structurally associated with lighting devices -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Electromagnetic shielding of the lighting device  Intumescent material associated with lighting device
[F21V 25/02] coming into action when lighting device is disturbed, dismounted, or broken
[F21V 25/04] breaking the electric circuit -- F21V 25/04 classifies safety devices that are built into lighting equipment and function to interrupt the electric circuit. It covers circuit-breaking mechanisms integrated in lamps, luminaires or related systems that disconnect power when a fault, overload or hazardous condition is detected.
[F21V 25/06] feeding a quenching fluid to the light source -- This subclass deals with the provision of a quenching fluid to a light source, typically in safety-related lighting such as lamps or fixtures that incorporate a fluid to suppress arcs or extinguish the discharge. It covers mechanisms for delivering, controlling, or circulating the fluid during operation or failure conditions.
[F21V 25/08] cutting the incandescent filament -- The code F21V 25/08 covers safety features that physically break or cut the incandescent filament in a lamp. It applies to designs where filament rupture is used to stop current flow or prevent overheating, often as a built-in protective mechanism within the light-fixture assembly.
[F21V 25/10] coming into action when lighting device is overloaded, e.g. thermal switch -- The F21V 25/10 subclass concerns safety mechanisms that are integral to lighting equipment and are triggered when the device is overloaded, such as thermal switches that interrupt power or shut off the lamp to prevent damage or fire. It falls under functional details of lighting devices within the mechanical engineering domain.
[F21V 25/12] Flameproof or explosion-proof arrangements -- F21V 25/12 covers safety devices integrated with lighting equipment that are designed to prevent ignition in flammable or explosive atmospheres. It includes flame-proof enclosures, explosion-proof seals, and other protective arrangements that keep sparks, hot surfaces or flames from contacting hazardous gases, vapours or dust while the lamp or luminaire operates. This subclass is used for lighting products intended for use in mines, chemical plants, oil-refining facilities and similar dangerous environments.
[F21V 25/125] using intumescent material, i.e. using materials which swells up as a result of heat exposure -- The code F21V 25/125 covers safety devices that are built into lighting equipment and employ intumescent material, which expands when exposed to heat. It relates to designs where the swelling material provides fire-protective functions, such as sealing gaps or shielding components, to enhance the safety of the lighting system.

[F21V 29/50] Cooling arrangements -- F21V 29/50 concerns cooling arrangements for lighting devices, detailing methods and structures that remove or dissipate heat to protect lamps and components from thermal damage. It includes heat sinks, airflow channels, liquid cooling circuits and other thermal management features integrated into luminaires.
[F21V 29/502] characterised by the adaptation for cooling of specific components -- The subclass covers lighting devices that incorporate specific cooling adaptations for components such as LEDs, reflectors, power supplies or drivers, using heat sinks, vents, liquid or air flow channels, thermally conductive materials or other heat-dissipation features to prevent thermal damage. It focuses on design measures that protect the lighting system from overheating.
[F21V 29/503] of light sources -- This classification covers measures and structures for preventing overheating in lighting equipment. It includes heat-sinks, thermal barriers, ventilation and other design features that keep light sources within safe temperature limits, thereby extending lifespan and safety.
[F21V 29/504] of refractors -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Refractors for light sources being designed to facilitate cooling.

[F21V 29/57] characterised by control arrangements -- F21V 29/57 covers lighting devices that include control arrangements to protect against thermal damage. These arrangements may use temperature sensors, thermostats or control circuits that limit power or shut off the source when overheating is detected. The focus is on functional features that safeguard lamps and fixtures from heat-related failure.
[F21V 29/58] characterised by the coolants -- The F21V 29/58 subclass covers lighting devices that incorporate coolants to protect the system from heat-induced damage. It includes any functional features or details in which the coolant is integral to the design, such as liquid, gas or phase-change materials that dissipate or absorb thermal energy during operation.
[F21V 29/59] with forced flow of the coolant -- F21V 29/59 covers lighting devices that use a forced coolant flow to prevent overheating. The sub-category includes arrangements where a pump, fan or similar mechanism actively circulates liquid or gas around the light source or heat-generating components, thereby protecting the device from thermal damage.
[F21V 29/60] characterised by the use of a forced flow of gas, e.g. air -- This subclass covers lighting apparatus that avoid overheating by directing a forced stream of gas such as air over the light source. The gas flow actively removes heat, improving durability and performance of the device.
[F21V 29/61] characterised by control arrangements -- F21V 29/61 includes lighting devices that use dedicated control arrangements-such as temperature sensors, protective circuits or logic-to detect overheating and automatically reduce power or shut off the source, thereby preventing thermal damage to the lamp or emitter.

[F21V 29/67] characterised by the arrangement of fans -- The code F21V 29/67 classifies lighting devices that incorporate a specific arrangement of fans to provide cooling and protect the equipment from thermal damage. It pertains to functional features of lighting systems where the fan layout is the defining element for heat dissipation. This entry falls under mechanical engineering, lighting, and functional details of lighting devices.
[F21V 29/673] the fans being used for intake -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fans arranged in such a way that the warm air is moved away from the heat source after the air was heated by the heat source, i.e. the fans are arranged downstream from the light/heat sources in respect of the air flow. [F21V 29/71] using a combination of separate elements interconnected by heat-conducting means, e.g. with heat pipes or thermally conductive bars between separate heat-sink elements -- The classification covers methods for shielding lighting devices against overheating by coupling multiple separate components through heat-conducting elements. It includes arrangements such as heat pipes or thermally conductive bars that link distinct heat-sink units, allowing heat to be transferred away from the light source and distributed across the system.

[F21V 29/76] with essentially identical parallel planar fins or blades, e.g. with comb-like cross-section -- The code F21V 29/76 classifies lighting devices that use a series of essentially identical parallel planar fins or blades, often with a comb-like cross-section, to improve heat dissipation and protect the source from thermal damage. This functional feature belongs to the broader category of mechanical engineering solutions for thermal management in lighting systems.

[F21V 29/86] Ceramics or glass -- F21V 29/86 covers the use of ceramic or glass materials in lighting devices to shield them from heat damage, such as protective covers, envelopes, or heat-resistant windows that prevent overheating of the light source.

[F21V 3/00] Globes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Globes for street lighting devices  Glass cover for automotive vehicles  Globes, bowls, cover glasses characterised by their shapes  Globes, bowls, cover glasses characterised by material, coatings or surface treatments  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - With refracting properties -> F21V5/00 - With reflecting properties -> F21V7/00
[F21V 3/02] characterised by the shape -- F21V 3/02 covers patents that focus on the shape of lighting globes, i.e., the overall geometric form of a bulb or similar enclosure. The classification addresses how the globe's contour influences light distribution, mounting, heat dissipation or aesthetic appearance, without regard to internal lamp technology.
[F21V 3/023] Chinese lanterns; Balloons -- F21V 3/023 refers to Chinese lanterns and balloons, which are decorative lighting items that employ an inflated paper or fabric envelope, often filled with hot air or gas, to hold a light source and produce illumination.
[F21V 3/026] being inflatable -- The CPC group F21V 3/026 classifies lighting globes whose envelope is designed to be inflatable, meaning the lamp housing can be expanded by air or gas to assume its shape. This functional feature is applied to create flexible, portable or shape-changing light sources.
[F21V 3/04] characterised by materials, surface treatments or coatings -- F21V 3/04 pertains to lighting globes whose distinguishing feature is the material they are made from or the surface treatment applied, such as reflective, anti-corrosion, anti-UV or other functional coatings. The classification covers the selection of substrates, metallisation, plating, enamelling, or polymeric layers that affect light output, durability, heat management or aesthetic qualities of the lamp.
[F21V 3/049] Patterns or structured surfaces for diffusing light, e.g. frosted surfaces -- F21V 3/049 is a subclass within the lighting section for globe-type lamps, covering globes that incorporate patterned or structured surfaces-such as frosted coatings-to scatter light uniformly and reduce glare. This classification addresses the design of surface textures that act as diffusers in lighting devices.
[F21V 3/06] characterised by the material -- F21V 3/06 covers globes for lighting devices where the claim is defined by the material of the globe, such as glass, plastic, quartz or ceramic, rather than its shape or electrical features. This subgroup falls under functional features of lighting devices in mechanical engineering, focusing on the type of material used for the light-emitting enclosure.
[F21V 3/061] the material being glass -- This subclass covers lighting globes whose envelope or cover is made of glass, specifying the type, thickness, and optical properties of the glass used. It addresses functional aspects of lighting devices where glass is the material of the globe.
[F21V 3/0615] the material diffusing light, e.g. translucent glass -- F21V 3/0615 pertains to lighting devices whose light-diffusing element is made of a translucent material such as glass, describing the use of this material to scatter or soften emitted light in globes and similar fixtures.
[F21V 3/062] the material being plastics -- F21V 3/062 covers lighting globes whose envelope is made of plastic material, detailing the various types of plastics used, their optical and thermal properties, and manufacturing methods. It is applied to designs where the globe's rigidity, transparency, or heat-resistance is achieved through polymer compositions.
[F21V 3/0625] the material diffusing light, e.g. translucent plastics -- F21V 3/0625 concerns the material that diffuses light in lighting globes, for example translucent plastics, used to produce uniform illumination. It falls under Mechanical Engineering → Lighting → Functional features or details of lighting devices or systems thereof → Globes.
[F21V 3/063] comprising air or water bubbles, e.g. foamed materials -- This subgroup covers lighting globes that contain air or water bubbles, such as foamed glass or plastic spheres, which are used to diffuse light, reduce weight, improve thermal performance or enhance structural strength. It includes devices where the bubble-filled material forms part of the envelope or interior of the lamp.
[F21V 3/08] the material comprising photoluminescent substances -- This subclass covers lighting globes whose material itself contains photoluminescent substances such as phosphors or glow-in-the-dark compounds that absorb energy and re-emit visible light. It includes the selection, formulation and incorporation of these luminescent materials into the construction of the device.
[F21V 3/10] characterised by coatings -- F21V 3/10 covers lighting globes whose defining feature is a coating applied to the lamp or its components, such as anti-reflective, protective, phosphor or thermal-management layers. The classification is used for patents that disclose how such coatings modify the performance, durability or light output of the globe. It falls within the hierarchy of mechanical engineering, lighting, functional features of lighting devices, and specifically globes.
[F21V 31/00] Gas-tight or water-tight arrangements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Sealing of lighting device housings
[F21V 31/005] Sealing arrangements therefor -- The F21V 31/005 subclass covers sealing arrangements that make lighting devices or systems gas-tight or water-tight. It includes designs for gaskets, O-rings, welds and other methods used to prevent ingress of fluids or gases in lamps, fixtures and related components.
[F21V 31/03] with provision for venting -- The code F21V 31/03 covers lighting assemblies that are sealed against gas or water ingress yet incorporate a vent to release pressure or moisture. It applies to luminaire housings, lanterns or similar devices where the enclosure is gas-tight or water-tight but includes a provision for venting, often to prevent condensation or pressure buildup in outdoor or hazardous-environment installations.
[F21V 31/04] Provision of filling media -- **Definition:** This place covers:  LED illuminating device with a transparent epoxy resin filling the shell to sealedly envelope the printed circuit board
[F21V 3/12] the coatings comprising photoluminescent substances -- The subgroup relates to lighting globes whose surfaces are coated with photoluminescent materials that absorb ambient light and re-emit it in darkness, creating a glow-in-the-dark effect. It includes phosphorescent paints, powders or films applied to lamps or fixtures for after-glow or safety illumination.

[F21V 33/0088] Ventilating systems  lighting fixtures combined with outlets for air treatment systems  F24F13/078 -- F21V 33/0088 covers lighting fixtures that are integrated with outlets for air-treatment or ventilating systems, such as ceiling lights that also serve as supply or exhaust vents.
