[F21V 35/00] Candle holders -- F21V 35/00 refers to candle holders, a functional feature of lighting devices within mechanical engineering. It covers designs and structures that support or mount candles, including bases, holders, and related accessories.
[F21V 35/003] Special means for attaching the candle to the candle holder -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Fastening of light sources or lampholders -> F21V19/00
[F21V 35/006] Drop catchers; Shade holders -- The F21V 35/006 subgroup defines accessories that catch wax drips and support shades in candle-holder assemblies. It covers drop catchers, shade brackets and similar functional elements that keep candles clean and secure the attached light-shading components.
[F21V 36/00] Arrangements of mantles or other incandescent bodies on burners -- F21V 36/00 deals with the design and positioning of mantles, glowers or other incandescent bodies on burners, describing how they are arranged to control heat distribution, light output or durability. The classification includes various configurations such as concentric, spaced or integrated placement within gas or fuel burners used for heating or illumination.
[F21V 36/02] in ceiling lamps -- F21V 36/02 designates the specific arrangement of mantles or other incandescent bodies on burners when used in ceiling lamps, covering functional design details of such lighting devices within the broader mechanical-engineering and lighting categories.
[F21V 37/00] Details of lighting devices employing combustion as light source, not otherwise provided for -- F21V 37/00 covers specific design elements of lighting apparatus that generate light through combustion, such as candles, oil lamps, gas burners or other flame-based sources, when the features are not covered by any other more specific subclass. It addresses the construction, mounting, control and safety aspects unique to these fire-based lighting devices.
[F21V 37/0004] using liquid fuel -- F21V 37/0004 covers lighting devices that produce illumination by combusting liquid fuel, such as oil lamps or torches, when the specific combustion method is not captured by other CPC subclasses. It falls within functional features of lighting systems that employ combustion as the light source.
[F21V 37/0008] Fuel containers -- This subclass defines fuel containers for lighting devices that generate light by combustion and are not classified elsewhere. It covers the design, structure and integration of reservoirs that store the combustible fuel used in lamps, torches, lanterns or similar systems. The focus is on details of the container itself rather than the overall lighting apparatus.
[F21V 37/0012] Filling or level checking -- F21V 37/0012 covers methods and devices for checking the filling level of lighting fixtures that use combustion as the light source, such as oil lamps or gas lights, ensuring proper fuel supply and safe operation.
[F21V 37/0016] Fastening of the container to other parts of the lamp -- The entry F21V 37/0016 covers the means of attaching the fuel or flame-containing vessel to other lamp parts in combustion-based lighting devices, such as brackets, clips, threads, screws or adhesives that secure the container within the overall lamp assembly.
[F21V 37/002] Wicks -- F21V 37/002 designates the wick component of lighting devices that generate light by combustion, such as oil lamps and candles. It addresses functional features of flame-based lighting systems, specifically the element that supplies fuel to the flame for illumination.
[F21V 37/0025] vegetal  F21V37/0033  takes precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Combination of mineral and vegetal -> F21V37/0033
[F21V 37/0029] mineral  F21V37/0033  takes precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Combination of mineral and vegetal -> F21V37/0033
[F21V 37/0033] combination of vegetal and mineral -- This subclass covers lighting devices that use a combustion light source formed from a mixture of plant-derived (vegetal) and mineral materials, such as blended fuels of wood and coal, applied in illumination apparatuses that are not classified elsewhere in the CPC.
[F21V 37/0037] Cleaning devices -- The subclass pertains to cleaning mechanisms incorporated in lighting devices that employ combustion as the light source, such as wick lamps, gas burners or candles, where the cleaning function is not otherwise classified. It includes features for removing soot, trimming wicks, cleaning lenses or other components of the flame-based illumination system.
[F21V 37/0041] Fuel supply -- The F21V 37/0041 entry concerns the fuel-supply arrangement for lighting devices that generate illumination through combustion. It includes the means of storing, regulating, transporting and delivering combustible material-such as gas lines, valves, pumps, burners and control mechanisms-to the light source.
[F21V 37/0045] using hydrostatic pressure or weights -- F21V 37/0045 addresses lighting devices that generate light by combustion and are operated or regulated through hydrostatic pressure or the use of weights. It includes systems where pressure-driven fuel delivery or weight-biased mechanisms control the flame or mantle to produce illumination.
[F21V 37/005] using air or gas pressure -- F21V 37/005 covers lighting devices that employ combustion as the light source and are operated or controlled by air or gas pressure, such as pressure-fed gas lamps or flame-jet illuminators. The subclass addresses functional features where pressure delivers fuel or regulates the flame, distinguishing these devices from other combustion-based lighting technologies.
[F21V 37/0054] Controlling means, e.g. floaters -- F21V 37/0054 falls under mechanical engineering, lighting, functional features of lighting devices that use combustion. It covers controlling mechanisms such as floaters that regulate the flame or fuel flow in combustion-based lamps.
[F21V 37/0058] Reflectors, cover glasses, chimneys; Smoke-removing devices; Preheaters -- F21V 37/0058 pertains to parts of lighting devices that employ combustion as the light source, specifically reflectors, cover glasses, chimneys, smoke-removing devices and preheaters, covering their design and integration within such systems.
[F21V 37/0062] Reflectors -- Reflectors are components that direct or shape light emitted by a combustion-based source within a lighting device, improving illumination efficiency, distribution, and intensity for applications such as lamps, lanterns, and torches.
[F21V 37/0066] Chimneys -- The F21V 37/0066 subclass pertains to chimneys incorporated in lighting devices that use combustion as the light source. It covers the design, construction and functional features of chimney elements that channel flame, enhance air flow and improve illumination where no more specific provision applies.
[F21V 37/007] Globs -- F21V 37/007 refers to globs, i.e., discrete masses of combustible material used as a light source in lighting devices that burn to produce illumination, where the design details of such combustion-based lighting are not covered by other specific categories.
[F21V 37/0075] Fastening or safety devices for reflectors, cover glasses or chimneys -- This subclass covers devices that fasten or protect components such as reflectors, cover glasses or chimneys in lighting systems that use combustion as the light source, focusing on mechanical fastening or safety mechanisms that ensure secure attachment and fire-resistant operation of these parts.
[F21V 37/0079] Fastening of chimneys  F21V37/0087  takes precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Devices for lifting chimneys or cover glasses -> F21V37/0087
[F21V 37/0083] Fastening of cover glasses  F21V37/0087  takes precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Devices for lifting chimneys or cover glasses -> F21V37/0087
[F21V 37/0087] Devices for lifting chimneys or cover glasses -- This subclass concerns mechanisms that raise or lower the chimney or protective glass covering of a combustion-powered lighting unit, allowing access for service, cleaning or adjustment of the flame.
[F21V 37/0091] Protections against shocks -- This subclass covers protective measures designed to guard combustion-based lighting devices from mechanical impacts or vibrations, such as cushioning mountings, shock-absorbing housings, or structural reinforcements that prevent damage to the flame source or surrounding components. It addresses designs that mitigate sudden forces that could disrupt operation or cause safety hazards.
[F21V 37/0095] Night lamps; Votive lamps -- Night lamps and votive lamps are small lighting devices that produce illumination by combustion, usually a wick drawing fuel from a reservoir and protected by a modest housing. They provide low-level, continuous or intermittent light for decorative, ceremonial or practical nighttime use, and are classified separately because they employ a flame as the light source rather than electric or other technologies.
[F21V 37/02] Special adaptation for protection against draughts -- F21V 37/02 covers adaptations to combustion-based lighting devices that shield the flame from air currents, such as wind guards, protective enclosures or baffles designed to reduce drafts and maintain stable illumination.
[F21V 5/00] Refractors for light sources -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Cooling arrangements for refractors -> F21V29/504
[F21V 5/002] using microoptical elements for redirecting or diffusing light -- This CPC subclass F21V 5/002 covers refracting elements for light sources that use micro-optical components to redirect or diffuse emitted light, such as micro-lenses or diffractive structures integrated into lamps, reflectors or LED packages. It resides in the hierarchy mechanical engineering-lighting-functional features of lighting devices.
[F21V 5/003] using holograms -- F21V 5/003 covers lighting devices in which holographic elements are employed as refractors to shape or redirect light from a source. The classification applies to systems that use holograms instead of conventional lenses or mirrors to achieve beam control, diffusion or focusing.
[F21V 5/004] using microlenses -- This subgroup deals with lighting systems that employ microlens arrays or individual microlenses to refract light from a source, adjusting beam shape, intensity distribution or focusing at micro scale. It includes designs where microlenses replace or supplement traditional refractors to improve efficiency or create patterned illumination.
[F21V 5/005] using microprisms -- F21V 5/005 covers refracting elements for lamps that use microprisms to shape or redirect light. The microprisms are small prism-like structures integrated into the refractor to enhance beam uniformity, increase efficiency, or control glare. This falls under functional features of lighting devices.
[F21V 5/006] applied to portable lighting devices -- F21V 5/006 is a CPC subclass under mechanical engineering → lighting → functional features of lighting devices, specifically for refractors used in portable lighting devices. It covers lenses, reflectors and other light-redirecting elements that are incorporated into handheld lamps, flashlights, head-lamps and similar mobile light sources.
[F21V 5/007] Array of lenses or refractors for a cluster of light sources, e.g. for arrangement of multiple light sources in one plane  combination of two or more refractors  F21V5/008 -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Combination of two or more refractors -> F21V5/008
[F21V 5/008] Combination of two or more successive refractors along an optical axis -- The class relates to lighting devices that employ two or more refracting elements positioned consecutively along a common optical axis, such as stacked lenses or prisms, to shape, focus or otherwise modify the emitted beam.
[F21V 5/02] of prismatic shape -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Refractor of lens shape -> F21V5/04
[F21V 5/04] of lens shape -- The subclass addresses the geometric shape of lenses used as refractors in lighting devices, covering spherical, aspherical, conical or other lens-shapes that direct and shape the light emitted from a source.
[F21V 5/041] Ball lenses -- Ball lenses are small spherical glass or plastic lenses that act as refractors, concentrating or diffusing light from a source. Their geometry enables simple, compact optical elements used in LED lamps, fiber optics and other lighting devices to shape beam patterns. The CPC subclass F21V 5/041 covers such ball lenses as functional features of lighting systems.
[F21V 5/043] the lens having cylindrical faces, e.g. rod lenses, toric lenses -- F21V 5/043 classifies cylindrical-faced lenses used as refractors in lighting devices, such as rod lenses and toric lenses. These components reshape or focus light beams and are employed to produce elongated or astigmatic illumination patterns in lamps, projectors or optical systems.
[F21V 5/045] the lens having discontinuous faces, e.g. Fresnel lenses -- F21V 5/045 covers lenses whose surfaces are broken into separate zones or steps, such as Fresnel lenses, used as refracting elements in lighting devices. These discontinuous-face lenses focus or spread light while reducing material thickness and weight.
[F21V 5/046] the lens having a rotationally symmetrical shape about an axis for transmitting light in a direction mainly perpendicular to this axis, e.g. ring or annular lens with light source disposed inside the ring -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[F21V 5/048] the lens being a simple lens adapted to cooperate with a point-like source for emitting mainly in one direction and having an axis coincident with the main light transmission direction, e.g. convergent or divergent lenses, plano-concave or plano-convex lenses -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[F21V 5/06] Hanging lustres for chandeliers -- F21V 5/06 covers hanging lustres used as chandeliers, specifically designs where the luminaire is suspended and incorporates refracting elements to distribute light. The class pertains to decorative lighting fixtures that employ prisms, lenses or other optical features to enhance illumination and aesthetic effect.
[F21V 5/08] producing an asymmetric light distribution -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[F21V 5/10] comprising photoluminescent material -- F21V 5/10 covers lighting refractors that incorporate photoluminescent material, which absorbs energy and re-emits light to augment illumination, improve light distribution, or create visual effects in lighting fixtures.
[F21V 7/00] Reflectors for light sources -- F21V 7/00 covers reflectors used with light sources, defining the shape, surface treatment, and material of components that direct or shape emitted light. It includes designs such as parabolic, elliptical, or compound-reflector geometries and may address integration with lamps, LEDs, or other emitters to achieve specific beam patterns or illumination efficiencies. This subclass falls under functional features of lighting devices within mechanical engineering.
[F21V 7/0008] providing for indirect lighting -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The lighting devices emitting light towards the ceiling and not toward the surface of interest to be lit  Lighting devices, the light beams of which are not being directed directly toward the aim, e.g. by means of reflectors  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[F21V 7/0016] on lighting devices that also provide for direct lighting, e.g. by means of independent light sources, by splitting of the light beam, by switching between both lighting modes -- F21V 7/0016 covers lighting devices that furnish both direct illumination and indirect lighting, for example by employing separate light sources, splitting the emitted beam, or switching between the two modes. It belongs to the hierarchy of mechanical engineering → lighting → functional features of lighting devices → reflectors for light sources.
[F21V 7/0025] Combination of two or more reflectors for a single light source  array of reflectors for a cluster of light sources  F21V7/0083 -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Array of reflectors for a cluster of light sources -> F21V7/0083
[F21V 7/0033] with successive reflections from one reflector to the next or following -- The F21V 7/0033 classification covers lighting devices that employ a series of reflectors arranged so that light reflected from one surface is directed onto the next reflector, creating successive reflections that enhance illumination or beam shaping. This feature applies to reflectors designed for coordinated, multi-stage light redirection within a lighting system.
[F21V 7/0041] for avoiding direct view of the light source or to prevent dazzling -- F21V 7/0041 pertains to reflectors for light sources that are configured to hide the lamp from direct view or to limit glare, achieved by shaping or arranging the reflective surfaces so observers cannot see the light source directly.
[F21V 7/005] with an elongated shape to cooperate with linear light sources -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Elongated reflective surface with its axis parallel to the axis of an elongated light emitter.  Plurality of reflective surfaces along an axis parallel to the axis of one elongated light emitter.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Plurality of reflective surfaces along an axis parallel to the axis of an light source formed of a plurality of light emitters, each light emitter corresponding to an individual reflective surface, i.e. array of light sources -> F21V7/0083
[F21V 7/0058] adapted to cooperate with light sources of shapes different from point-like or linear, e.g. circular light sources -- F21V 7/0058 covers reflectors designed to work with light sources that are not point-like or linear, such as circular or other shaped emitters. These reflectors are shaped or configured to match the geometry of the source for improved light collection or distribution.
[F21V 7/0066] specially adapted to cooperate with point like light sources; specially adapted to cooperate with light sources the shape of which is unspecified  F21V7/16  -  F21V7/30  take precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Construction of reflector with provision for adjusting the curvature -> F21V7/16 - Construction of reflector with provision for folding or collapsing -> F21V7/18 - Reflectors characterised by materials, surface treatments or coatings, e.g. dichroic reflectors -> F21V7/22 - F21V7/30 - Reflector constructed to facilitate cooling -> F21V29/505
[F21V 7/0075] for portable lighting devices -- The code F21V 7/0075 pertains to reflectors intended for portable lighting devices such as flashlights, lanterns and other handheld sources. It covers designs where the reflector directs or shapes the emitted light, including specific configurations or mounting arrangements suited to mobile use.

[F21V 7/04] Optical design -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Reflector for which the surface is defined by mathematical formulae.
[F21V 7/041] with conical or pyramidal surface -- F21V 7/041 covers reflectors for light sources that incorporate a conical or pyramidal surface to shape and direct the emitted beam. The geometry focuses or spreads light more efficiently than flat surfaces, and is used in lamps, projectors and automotive lighting where precise beam control is required.
[F21V 7/043] with cylindrical surface -- This CPC subclass F21V 7/043 pertains to reflectors for light sources that include a cylindrical surface, a design element affecting light distribution and efficiency within lighting devices. Such cylindrical surfaces are used to shape, focus or spread emitted light in various illumination applications.
[F21V 7/045] with spherical surface -- F21V 7/045 covers light-source reflectors that incorporate a spherical surface, meaning the reflector's shape is part of a sphere or includes a segment of a sphere to direct illumination. This sub-class falls under functional features of lighting devices, specifically within reflectors for light sources.
[F21V 7/046] with involute curvature -- F21V 7/046 concerns reflectors for light sources whose reflecting surface is formed with an involute curvature, a shape generated by unwinding a curve to produce a profile that directs light in a controlled manner. Such designs are used in lamps and luminaires to achieve specific beam patterns.
[F21V 7/048] with facets structure -- F21V 7/048 covers reflectors for light sources that incorporate a faceted structure to shape and direct the emitted light. The classification is used for functional features of lighting devices within the mechanical-engineering lighting hierarchy, focusing on designs where multiple flat or angled surfaces form the reflector.
[F21V 7/05] plane
[F21V 7/06] with parabolic curvature -- F21V 7/06 covers lighting reflectors that incorporate a parabolic curvature, meaning the reflective surface is shaped like a paraboloid to direct emitted light toward a focal region. This design is used in headlamps, spotlights and other illumination devices to concentrate light efficiently.
[F21V 7/07] with hyperbolic curvature -- F21V 7/07 covers reflectors used in lighting devices that employ a hyperbolic shape to direct or focus light. The hyperbolic curvature is chosen to improve light distribution or concentration, often used in lamps, projectors, or optical systems.
[F21V 7/08] with elliptical curvature -- F21V 7/08 designates reflectors for light sources that incorporate an elliptical curvature. It falls under mechanical engineering, lighting, and functional features of lighting devices, covering reflector shapes where the elliptical surface is used to direct or concentrate emitted light.

[F21V 7/10] Construction -- F21V 7/10 covers the structural design and manufacturing details of reflectors used in lighting devices. It includes the shape, mounting methods, material choices and assembly features that direct light from a source.
[F21V 7/16] with provision for adjusting the curvature -- This subclass pertains to reflectors for light sources that incorporate a means of changing their curvature, allowing the shape and direction of the reflected beam to be altered to meet different illumination requirements. The adjustment mechanism may be manual or automatic and is applicable to various lighting technologies such as lamps and LEDs.
[F21V 7/18] with provision for folding or collapsing -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative examples of subject matter classified in this group.
[F21V 7/22] characterised by materials, surface treatments or coatings, e.g. dichroic reflectors -- This CPC subclass F21V 7/22 covers reflectors for light sources that are distinguished by the materials they are made from or by surface treatments and coatings, for example dichroic layers, which modify reflectivity and spectral characteristics.
[F21V 7/24] characterised by the material -- F21V 7/24 is a subclass within the Cooperative Patent Classification for lighting reflectors that distinguishes the devices by the material from which the reflector is made. It covers reflectors constructed of metals, polymers, glass, ceramics or composite materials, focusing on how the material influences reflective performance, durability and manufacturing methods.
[F21V 7/26] the material comprising photoluminescent substances -- This group relates to reflectors for lamps whose construction uses a material that includes photoluminescent substances. These substances store energy from incident light and re-emit it, improving light distribution or providing glow-in-the-dark effects.
[F21V 7/28] characterised by coatings -- The subclass F21V 7/28 pertains to reflectors for light sources that are distinguished by one or more coating layers applied to the reflecting surface, such as reflective, diffusing, absorptive or protective coatings, which alter the reflector's optical performance, durability or heat management.
[F21V 7/30] the coatings comprising photoluminescent substances -- This CPC subclass addresses coatings that incorporate photoluminescent substances applied to reflectors in lighting devices. It covers the formulation, application and properties of such luminescent coatings used to enhance or modify the light output. The subclass is nested under mechanical engineering, lighting, functional features of lighting devices, specifically reflectors for light sources.
[F21V 9/00] Elements for modifying spectral properties, polarisation or intensity of the light emitted, e.g. filters -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Coloured shades -> F21V1/00 - Cooling arrangements for light filters -> F21V29/502
[F21V 9/02] for simulating daylight -- **Definition:** This place covers:  False windows are also covered by this subgroup  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Light filters for filtering out infrared radiation -> F21V9/04 - Light filters for filtering out ultraviolet radiation -> F21V9/06

[F21V 9/06] for filtering out ultraviolet radiation -- This group covers components integrated in lighting devices that filter or block ultraviolet radiation, such as UV-absorbing filters, glasses, coatings or other elements applied to lamps, luminaire housings or optical systems to reduce or eliminate UV output.
[F21V 9/08] for producing coloured light, e.g. monochromatic -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Elements with provision for controlling spectral properties, e.g. colour, or intensity -> F21V9/40
[F21V 9/083] for portable lighting devices -- F21V 9/083 relates to portable lighting devices that include elements such as filters or other components for altering the spectral characteristics, polarisation or intensity of the light they emit. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering → lighting → functional features of lighting systems.
[F21V 9/12] with liquid-filled chambers -- A lighting device that uses a liquid-filled chamber as an element to alter the spectral characteristics, polarization or intensity of the emitted light, falling under functional features for modifying light properties.
[F21V 9/14] for producing polarised light -- F21V 9/14 relates to components that generate or convert light into a defined state of polarization, such as polarising filters, birefringent plates or other devices that produce polarized light for lighting applications. These elements are used to control glare, improve contrast or tailor the optical performance of lamps, LEDs and other illumination systems.
[F21V 9/20] Dichroic filters, i.e. devices operating on the principle of wave interference to pass specific ranges of wavelengths while cancelling others -- F21V 9/20 concerns dichroic filters, optical elements that employ thin-film interference to transmit selected wavelength ranges while rejecting others, thereby adjusting the colour, polarisation or intensity of light in lighting devices.
[F21V 9/30] Elements containing photoluminescent material distinct from or spaced from the light source -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Shades containing photoluminescent material -> F21V1/17 - Globes, bowls or cover glasses containing photoluminescent material -> F21V3/08 , F21V3/12 - Refractors containing photoluminescent material -> F21V5/10 - Reflectors containing photoluminescent material -> F21V7/26 , F21V7/30 - Elements with provision for controlling the spectral properties or intensity containing photoluminescent material -> F21V9/40
[F21V 9/32] characterised by the arrangement of the photoluminescent material -- F21V 9/32 pertains to lighting devices whose spectral output is adjusted by the specific placement of photoluminescent material within the system. The classification covers arrangements such as coatings, layers or embedded particles that alter colour, intensity or polarisation of the emitted light through fluorescence or phosphorescence.
[F21V 9/35] at focal points, e.g. of refractors, lenses, reflectors or arrays of light sources -- This subclass concerns devices or elements positioned at the focal point of refractors, lenses, reflectors or arrays of light sources that modify the spectral characteristics, polarization or intensity of the emitted light, such as filters or other optical modifiers.
[F21V 9/38] Combination of two or more photoluminescent elements of different materials -- The subclass defines lighting components that combine two or more photoluminescent elements made from different materials, such as phosphors or quantum dots, to achieve desired spectral, colour or intensity characteristics. These combinations are used in lamps, displays or other illumination systems to tailor light output by mixing emissions from distinct luminescent substances.
[F21V 9/40] with provision for controlling spectral properties, e.g. colour, or intensity -- F21V 9/40 covers lighting devices that include means for adjusting the spectral characteristics of the emitted light, such as colour, polarisation or intensity. It pertains to elements like filters or other components that enable on-demand modification of the light's spectrum or brightness, falling under functional features of lighting systems.
[F21V 9/45] by adjustment of photoluminescent elements -- F21V 9/45 covers lighting devices whose output is altered by adjusting photoluminescent elements such as phosphors or other luminescent materials. The adjustment changes the spectral distribution, colour or intensity of the emitted light, providing a means to tailor illumination properties within a lighting system.
[F21V 99/00] Subject matter not provided for in other groups of this subclass -- F21V 99/00 covers any lighting device or system feature that does not fall within the more specific groups defined in subclass F21V, such as unusual or unconventional configurations, materials, or controls that are not otherwise classified in the subclass. This catch-all group is used for novel or miscellaneous functional aspects of lighting that lack a dedicated subclass heading.
[F21W] INDEXING SCHEME ASSOCIATED WITH SUBCLASSES -- F21W covers indexing schemes that organize and reference the various subclasses of the lighting sector within the mechanical engineering group. It defines systematic numbering or labeling methods used to locate and classify lighting inventions in the CPC.
[F21W 2102/00] Exterior vehicle lighting devices for illuminating purposes -- F21W 2102/00 falls under mechanical engineering and lighting and pertains to exterior vehicle lighting devices intended for illumination. It includes components such as headlights, taillights, turn signals, fog lamps and other outward-facing light sources that enhance vehicle visibility and convey signaling information.
[F21W 2102/10] Arrangement or contour of the emitted light -- This CPC subclass deals with how the light emitted by exterior vehicle lighting devices is arranged or shaped, covering beam patterns, cut-offs and distribution contours that define illumination zones for headlights, turn signals or other road-visible lamps.

[F21W 2103/00] Exterior vehicle lighting devices for signalling purposes -- F21W 2103/00 designates exterior lighting devices on vehicles that are intended specifically for signalling functions, such as turn indicators, hazard flashers, and other warning lights. It falls within the mechanical-engineering hierarchy under lighting, grouping technologies that provide visual communication to other road users.
[F21W 2103/10] Position lights -- F21W 2103/10 Position lights are exterior vehicle lighting devices used to indicate a vehicle's presence, orientation and movement, usually mounted on the front, rear or sides to show its heading and operation in darkness or reduced visibility.
[F21W 2103/15] Side marker lights -- F21W 2103/15 designates side marker lights, which are low-intensity lamps mounted on the sides of a vehicle to increase its visibility to other road users, especially in low-light conditions. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering, lighting, exterior vehicle lighting devices for signalling purposes within the CPC system used by the EPO and USPTO.
[F21W 2103/20] Direction indicator lights -- The CPC subclass F21W 2103/20 covers direction indicator lights, which are exterior vehicle lighting devices used to signal planned turning or lane changes. These lights are typically integrated into vehicle body panels and flash to warn other road users of the vehicle's intended direction.
[F21W 2103/25] for rear-view mirrors -- The subclass F21W 2103/25 in the CPC covers lighting devices mounted on or integrated with rear-view mirrors of vehicles for signalling purposes such as turn signals or brake lights. It belongs to the exterior vehicle lighting devices for signalling group within the mechanical engineering lighting section.
[F21W 2103/30] Hazard lights -- F21W 2103/30 covers hazard lights, which are exterior vehicle lighting devices used to signal a vehicle's emergency or abnormal condition, typically by flashing all forward and rear lights simultaneously. These devices fall under the mechanical engineering lighting subclass for signalling purposes.
[F21W 2103/35] Brake lights -- The CPC subclass F21W 2103/35 covers brake lights, which are exterior vehicle lighting devices used to signal deceleration or stopping. This falls within the mechanical engineering domain under lighting and specifically the indexing scheme for exterior vehicle signalling lights.
[F21W 2103/40] Rear fog lights -- The code F21W 2103/40 designates rear fog lights, a type of exterior vehicle lighting device used for signalling in adverse visibility conditions. It falls under the mechanical-engineering hierarchy, within the lighting sector and the indexing scheme for exterior vehicle lighting devices for signalling purposes.
[F21W 2103/45] Reversing lights -- The CPC subclass F21W 2103/45 defines reversing lights, the rear-mounted illumination devices that activate when a vehicle is driven backward to signal its reversing motion. It is part of the exterior vehicle lighting devices for signalling purposes, nested under mechanical engineering and lighting categories.
[F21W 2103/50] Registration plate lights -- Registration plate lights are exterior vehicle lighting devices that illuminate the vehicle's license plate to improve visibility in low-light conditions and meet legal requirements. They are typically mounted on the front or rear of a vehicle and may be powered by the vehicle's electrical system or incorporate LED or incandescent sources.
[F21W 2103/55] Daytime running lights [DRL] -- F21W 2103/55 designates exterior vehicle lighting devices used for signalling that operate as daytime running lights, a safety feature that automatically illuminates a vehicle's front or rear during daylight. The subclass covers the technical aspects of DRL systems, including their placement, light output, power control and integration with vehicle lighting architecture.
[F21W 2103/60] Projection of signs from lighting devices, e.g. symbols or information being projected onto the road -- F21W 2103/60 refers to vehicle lighting systems that project symbols, messages or other signs onto the road surface, such as lane markings, warnings or informational graphics, to assist drivers. It falls under exterior vehicle lighting devices used for signalling purposes.
[F21W 2104/00] Exterior vehicle lighting devices for decorative purposes -- F21W 2104/00 pertains to exterior vehicle lighting devices employed mainly for decorative effect, such as ornamental LEDs, light-up logos, accent strips and other aesthetic illumination fitted to a vehicle's body. The entry falls under the mechanical engineering - lighting hierarchy in the CPC system.
[F21W 2105/00] Combinations of lighting devices covered by codes -- F21W 2105/00 in the Cooperative Patent Classification covers arrangements that combine multiple lighting devices, such as LEDs, lamps, or displays, within a single unit or system. It includes the integration of different light sources, controls, or optical elements to achieve combined functionality and methods for assembling or coordinating these components.
[F21W 2106/00] Interior vehicle lighting devices -- CPC class F21W 2106/00 covers interior lighting devices for motor vehicles, including dome lights, reading lamps, footwell or instrument-panel illumination and the associated control or mounting arrangements. This subclass falls under Mechanical Engineering → Lighting.
[F21W 2107/00] Use or application of lighting devices on or in particular types of vehicles -- The CPC subclass F21W 2107/00 covers inventions that apply lighting devices to or within specific kinds of vehicles, such as cars, trucks, aircraft, ships or bicycles. It includes methods, fixtures, controls and integration techniques for vehicle-mounted illumination, addressing functional and safety aspects of vehicle lighting.
[F21W 2107/10] for land vehicles -- This CPC subclass relates to the use or application of lighting devices on land vehicles, covering systems such as headlights, taillights, signal lamps, and interior or auxiliary illumination designed specifically for cars, trucks, buses and similar road-going machines.
[F21W 2107/13] for cycles -- F21W 2107/13 designates lighting devices that are specifically adapted for use on cycles, including headlamps, taillights, reflectors and similar equipment mounted on bicycles or similar two-wheel vehicles. The subclass falls under mechanical engineering, lighting, and addresses the application of illumination systems to particular vehicle types.
[F21W 2107/17] for motorcycles -- This CPC subclass F21W 2107/17 covers lighting devices that are used on motorcycles, including any installations, mounting, or integration of lights specific to motorcycle vehicles. It falls under mechanical engineering, lighting, indexing scheme associated with subclasses, and pertains to the use or application of lighting devices on particular types of vehicles.
[F21W 2107/20] for water vehicles -- F21W 2107/20 designates the use or application of lighting devices on water vehicles, covering onboard illumination such as navigation lights, deck lights, and interior lighting. It falls under mechanical engineering, specifically the lighting category, and indexes subclasses related to vehicle-specific lighting installations.

[F21Y] INDEXING SCHEME ASSOCIATED WITH SUBCLASSES -- F21Y is a mechanical-engineering lighting subclass that groups documents according to an indexing scheme linking related sub-classes. It serves as a cross-reference tool within the Cooperative Patent Classification system, helping examiners locate and compare similar lighting technologies.
[F21Y 2101/00] Point-like light sources -- The code designates devices that generate illumination from a source that can be treated as a point, such as LEDs, laser diodes or other miniature emitters used for lighting, signaling or display purposes. It covers arrangements where the emitter's size is negligible relative to the optical system.
[F21Y 2103/00] Elongate light sources, e.g. fluorescent tubes -- The CPC subclass F21Y 2103/00 covers elongated lighting devices, primarily linear sources such as fluorescent tubes and other long, slender emitters used for illumination. It includes designs, constructions, and mounting arrangements specific to these rod-like light sources within mechanical engineering lighting applications.
[F21Y 2103/10] comprising a linear array of point-like light-generating elements -- F21Y 2103/10 covers lighting arrangements that use a linear series of discrete point-like emitters, such as LEDs, positioned in a line to form an elongated light source like a fluorescent-tube style fixture. The subclass addresses the geometry and distribution of these point sources within a linear array.
[F21Y 2103/20] of polygonal shape, e.g. square or rectangular -- The CPC subclass F21Y 2103/20 covers elongated light sources that have a polygonal cross-section such as square or rectangular tubes, typically fluorescent or other linear lamps. It is placed under mechanical engineering → lighting → elongated light sources.
[F21Y 2103/30] curved -- F21Y 2103/30 covers elongated light sources such as fluorescent tubes that are manufactured or shaped with a curved form. The classification falls under mechanical engineering lighting, specifically the indexing scheme for subclasses dealing with non-straight illumination elements.
[F21Y 2103/33] annular -- The CPC subclass F21Y 2103/33 falls under Mechanical Engineering → Lighting → Indexing scheme associated with subclasses for elongate light sources such as fluorescent tubes. It specifically designates annular light sources, i.e., ring-shaped lamps that emit light around a central void. This classification is used to catalogue patents concerning the structure, manufacturing or use of such circular tubular lighting elements.
[F21Y 2103/37] U-shaped -- F21Y 2103/37 designates U-shaped elongate light sources, such as fluorescent tubes formed into a U configuration for compact illumination in fixtures. The classification falls under mechanical engineering lighting and groups devices whose curved geometry enhances light distribution while retaining the characteristics of elongated lamps.
[F21Y 2105/00] Planar light sources -- F21Y 2105/00 is a CPC subclass that groups patents for planar light sources, such as flat LED panels, backlights or sheet-type emitters, used in displays, illumination and signage. It falls under the mechanical-engineering branch, specifically the lighting field, and is part of the indexing scheme that organizes related subclasses.
[F21Y 2105/10] comprising a two-dimensional [2D] array of point-like light-generating elements -- This subclass covers lighting devices that incorporate a planar arrangement of many point-like emitters organized in a two-dimensional array. The configuration provides uniform or patterned illumination across a flat surface and is used in displays, backlights and advanced lighting panels.
[F21Y 2105/12] characterised by the geometrical disposition of the light-generating elements, e.g. arranging light-generating elements in differing patterns or densities -- The subclass F21Y 2105/12 pertains to planar light sources whose performance is defined by the geometric arrangement of the light-generating elements, such as varying patterns, densities, or spacing of LEDs or other emitters to achieve specific illumination characteristics.
[F21Y 2105/14] characterised by the overall shape of the two-dimensional [2D] array -- This CPC subclass covers planar light sources whose distinguishing feature is the overall shape of the two-dimensional array of emitters or optical elements, such as rectangular, circular or other configured layouts. It is applied to inventions where the geometry of the 2D array defines the lighting device.
[F21Y 2105/16] square or rectangular, e.g. for light panels -- F21Y 2105/16 refers to planar light sources whose emitting surface is square or rectangular, such as light panels used in illumination fixtures. It is classified under Mechanical Engineering → Lighting → Planar Light Sources and covers devices designed for uniform light distribution from a flat, orthogonal geometry.
[F21Y 2105/18] annular -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Planar two-dimensional arrangements of point-like light-generating elements having an outside perimeter shape other than square or rectangular, including but not limited to: circular, elliptical, triangular, pentagonal, and the like.
[F21Y 2107/00] Light sources with three-dimensionally disposed light-generating elements -- A light source whose light-generating elements (such as LEDs, lamps or electro-luminescent devices) are positioned in a three-dimensional arrangement rather than a flat or linear layout, forming volumetric, stacked or distributed arrays that emit illumination in multiple directions. The classification covers designs where the spatial disposition of emitters is integral to the device's lighting functionality.
[F21Y 2107/10] on concave supports or substrates, e.g. on the inner side of bowl-shaped supports -- This CPC subclass concerns light sources whose light-generating elements are arranged three-dimensionally on concave supports or substrates, such as the inner surface of bowl-shaped holders. It falls under Mechanical Engineering → Lighting → Indexing scheme for three-dimensional light-generating element arrangements.
[F21Y 2107/20] on convex supports or substrates, e.g. on the outer surface of spheres
[F21Y 2107/30] on the outer surface of cylindrical surfaces, e.g. rod-shaped supports having a circular or a polygonal cross section -- This CPC subclass covers lighting devices in which the light-generating elements are arranged on the outer surface of cylindrical or rod-shaped supports, which may have a circular or polygonal cross-section. It pertains to three-dimensional placement of emitters on such surfaces.
[F21Y 2107/40] on the sides of polyhedrons, e.g. cubes or pyramids -- This subclass pertains to light-emitting devices whose active elements are arranged on the faces of three-dimensional polyhedral bodies such as cubes, pyramids or prisms. It includes lamps, LEDs or other sources that are mounted on the sides of these shapes to provide omni-directional illumination.
[F21Y 2107/50] on planar substrates or supports, but arranged in different planes or with differing orientation, e.g. on plate-shaped supports with steps on which light-generating elements are mounted -- This CPC subclass covers light sources whose light-generating elements are mounted on planar substrates or supports but positioned in different planes or with varying orientations, such as stepped or angled sections on a plate-shaped support.
[F21Y 2107/60] on stacked substrates -- F21Y 2107/60 classifies lighting apparatus in which light-generating elements are arranged on multiple stacked substrates, creating a three-dimensional light source. The entry covers designs, manufacturing methods and configurations that employ substrate stacking to improve illumination uniformity, efficiency or colour blending.
[F21Y 2107/70] on flexible or deformable supports or substrates, e.g. for changing the light source into a desired form -- F21Y 2107/70 relates to lighting devices that embed three-dimensionally arranged light-generating elements on flexible or deformable supports or substrates, enabling the light source to be shaped or reconfigured into a desired form. It belongs to the mechanical engineering class for lighting.
[F21Y 2107/80] on articulated supports or substrates -- This CPC subclass falls under mechanical engineering lighting and covers light sources whose light-generating elements are arranged three-dimensionally and are mounted on articulated supports or substrates that can be adjusted, positioned or articulated to direct illumination.
[F21Y 2107/90] on two opposite sides of supports or substrates -- The classification F21Y 2107/90 covers lighting devices in which the light-generating elements are arranged three-dimensionally and positioned on opposite faces of a support or substrate, such that illumination is produced from both sides simultaneously. This applies to LEDs, laser diodes or other emitters mounted on the two sides of a single carrier to provide bidirectional lighting.
[F21Y 2109/00] Light sources with light-generating elements disposed on transparent or translucent supports or substrates -- This CPC subclass covers lighting devices where light-generating elements such as LEDs are mounted on transparent or translucent supports or substrates, allowing the support itself to emit light and enabling backlit displays, signage and architectural lighting. It falls under mechanical engineering → lighting and is part of the indexing scheme associated with subclasses.
[F21Y 2111/00] Light sources of a form not covered by groups
[F21Y 2113/00] Combination of light sources -- F21Y 2113/00 covers arrangements that combine two or more distinct light-emitting sources within a single lighting device or system, such as mixing LEDs with lamps or incorporating different wavelengths for enhanced illumination. The classification falls under mechanical engineering lighting and is used to index patents that integrate multiple light sources to achieve specific performance or functional goals.
[F21Y 2113/10] of different colours -- The CPC subclass F21Y 2113/10 covers lighting arrangements that combine light sources of different colours, such as mixed-colour LEDs or lamps, to achieve specific colour effects, colour rendering or visual signalling.
[F21Y 2113/13] comprising an assembly of point-like light sources -- F21Y 2113/13 pertains to lighting arrangements that incorporate an assembly of point-like light sources, such as LEDs or bulbs, mounted together to produce a combined illumination effect. The classification is used for mechanical-engineering applications where multiple discrete emitters are integrated into a single unit.
[F21Y 2113/17] forming a single encapsulated light source -- F21Y 2113/17 refers to the manufacture of a single encapsulated light source that contains multiple light-emitting elements combined within one package, enabling integrated lighting solutions. It falls under mechanical engineering, lighting, and the indexing scheme for combinations of light sources.
[F21Y 2113/20] of different form -- F21Y 2113/20 covers lighting assemblies that combine light sources of different form, meaning the individual emitters have distinct shapes or geometries. It includes devices where, for example, tubular LEDs are integrated with planar or filament-type sources to achieve specific optical or aesthetic effects.
[F21Y 2113/30] of visible and non-visible spectrum -- F21Y 2113/30 classifies lighting apparatus that combines visible light with non-visible spectral ranges such as infrared, ultraviolet or other wavelengths. It covers devices where the different sources are integrated to provide mixed-spectrum illumination, signalling or functional effects within a single system.
[F21Y 2115/00] Light-generating elements of semiconductor light sources -- The F21Y 2115/00 subclass covers the light-generating elements of semiconductor light sources, such as LED chips, laser-diode structures and other photon-emitting semiconductor devices. It includes the semiconductor material, active layers, and any packaging that directly produces light, and is used to classify patents dealing with the core optoelectronic components that emit illumination.
[F21Y 2115/10] Light-emitting diodes [LED] -- F21Y 2115/10 classifies light-emitting diodes, semiconductor devices that generate light when an electric current passes through them, used in a wide range of illumination and display applications within the mechanical-engineering lighting field.
[F21Y 2115/15] Organic light-emitting diodes [OLED] -- F21Y 2115/15 covers organic light-emitting diodes, a type of semiconductor light source in which organic thin-film materials generate light when an electric current passes through them. The classification groups OLED devices, their structures and manufacturing methods, distinguishing them from other solid-state lighting technologies within the mechanical-engineering lighting sector.
[F21Y 2115/20] Electroluminescent [EL] light sources -- F21Y 2115/20 covers electroluminescent light sources, which produce light by applying an electric field to phosphor layers and are commonly used in thin-film displays, backlights and signage. The classification is part of the CPC group for light-generating elements of semiconductor light sources within the mechanical engineering - lighting sector.
[F21Y 2115/30] Semiconductor lasers -- F21Y 2115/30 covers semiconductor lasers, which are light-generating elements used in semiconductor light sources such as laser diodes. The subclass pertains to devices that emit coherent light through stimulated emission in a semiconductor material, applied in communications, sensing, and material processing. It falls under mechanical engineering, lighting, light-generating elements of semiconductor light sources in the CPC.
[F22] STEAM GENERATION -- F22 covers the generation of steam, including boilers, steam generators, and related equipment and processes used to produce steam for power, heating, or industrial applications. It addresses the design, construction, and operation of devices that convert water into vapor under pressure. The subclass groups technologies that control steam production, heat exchange, and associated safety features.
[F22B] METHODS OF STEAM GENERATION -- **Definition:** This place covers:  This subclass covers general aspects of, or methods for, steam generation. Methods of steam generation characterised by the form of heating method, constructional features of steam boilers, control systems for steam boilers and all component parts or details of steam boilers are covered. Thereby this subclass is limited in only methods of, or apparatus for, the generation of steam under pressure for heating or power purposes.  **Glossary:** - once-through boiler: type of boiler in which water is input at one side, and steam is extracted from the other side of the flow path - forced-flow boiler: type of boiler in which a pump ensures flow - forced-once-through boiler: combination of a once-through and a forced-flow boiler (a pump ensures flow of a boiler in which water is input at one side, and steam is extracted from the other side of the flow path) - forced-circulation boiler: type of boiler in which recirculation is achieved by a circulation pump - natural-circulation boiler: type of boiler in which the circulation is achieved by the difference in density of the heated water in the boiler causing convection currents
[F22B 1/00] General aspects of, or methods for, steam generation -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods of steam generation
[F22B 1/003] using combustion of hydrogen with oxygen -- This subgroup covers processes that generate steam by burning hydrogen in the presence of oxygen, distinguishing it from conventional fuel combustion. It includes direct hydrogen-oxygen combustion systems for producing high-temperature steam in boilers or turbines.
[F22B 1/006] using solar heat -- The subclass F22B 1/006 covers methods of producing steam that employ solar energy as the heat source, addressing the conversion of solar radiation into the thermal input needed for steam generation in mechanical engineering applications.
[F22B 1/02] by exploitation of the heat content of hot heat carriers -- F22B 1/02 relates to methods of producing steam that utilise the heat content of hot heat carriers such as oil, molten salts, steam or other heated media. The subclass includes waste-heat recovery, heat-exchange and regeneration processes where the carrier's thermal energy is transferred to water to generate steam.
[F22B 1/021] with heating tubes in which flows a non-specified heating fluid  for nuclear reactors  F22B1/023 -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Steam generation methods in nuclear reactors with heating tubes, as long as they are not classified according to a specified heating fluid, in another group -> F22B1/023
[F22B 1/023] with heating tubes for nuclear reactors, as long as they are not classified according to a specified heating fluid, in another group -- F22B 1/023 covers steam-generation methods that use heating tubes specifically for nuclear reactors, provided the tubes are not classified according to a particular heating fluid. The entry applies when the heating fluid does not determine placement in another subgroup.
[F22B 1/025] with vertical U shaped tubes carried on a horizontal tube sheet -- This subclass covers boilers in which the heating tubes are arranged as vertical U-shaped elements mounted on a horizontal tube sheet, allowing water to circulate through the curved legs for steam generation.
[F22B 1/026] with vertical tubes between two horizontal tube sheets -- This subclass covers steam generators that employ vertical tubes mounted between two horizontal tube sheets, a layout typical of fire-tube boilers and related devices. The arrangement creates a compact heat-exchange path and promotes uniform water circulation.
[F22B 1/028] Steam generation using heat accumulators  F22B27/14  takes precedence -- F22B 1/028 covers methods of generating steam that employ heat accumulators-devices that store thermal energy and release it to produce steam on demand, often improving efficiency or allowing operation independent of immediate heat sources. This subclass is superseded by the more specific F22B 27/14, which details particular accumulator designs and applications.
[F22B 1/04] the heat carrier being hot slag, hot residues, or heated blocks, e.g. iron blocks -- F22B 1/04 covers steam-generation methods that use solid heat carriers such as hot slag, other hot residues or heated blocks, for example iron blocks, to transfer thermal energy to water. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering, steam generation and methods of steam generation, focusing on alternative heat sources rather than conventional combustion.
[F22B 1/06] the heat carrier being molten -- F22B 1/06 relates to steam generators in which the heat-carrying medium is a molten substance such as molten salt, metal or alloy. The classification covers methods where this molten heat carrier transfers thermal energy to water to produce steam.
[F22B 1/063] for metal cooled nuclear reactors -- This subclass concerns methods of producing steam in nuclear reactors that use a liquid metal as the coolant, describing how heat is transferred from the reactor core to a steam-generating circuit, including design features, operating parameters and integration of metal-cooled systems.
[F22B 1/066] with double-wall tubes having a third fluid between these walls, e.g. helium for leak detection -- The entry covers steam-generation systems that employ double-wall tubes in which a third fluid, such as helium, circulates between the inner and outer walls to detect leaks, monitor tube condition, and enhance safety and efficiency.
[F22B 1/08] the heat carrier being steam -- F22B 1/08 covers the use of steam as the heat-carrying medium in steam generation systems, describing methods where steam itself transports thermal energy to a working fluid or process.
[F22B 1/10] released from heat accumulators -- Techniques for releasing steam that has been stored in heat accumulators, describing processes that draw off the accumulated thermal energy for use in steam generation. This includes methods of transferring heat from the accumulator to the working fluid and controlling the discharge of steam to meet system demands.
[F22B 11/00] Steam boilers of combined fire-tube type and water-tube type, i.e. steam boilers of fire-tube type having auxiliary water tubes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  All combinations of the steam boilers of fire-tube type with steam boilers of water-tube type consisting of a fire-tube boiler with auxiliary water tubes or a water-tube boiler having auxiliary fire tubes in a water or steam containing vessel. This place also covers boilers with fire tubes being in upright and horizontal arrangement.
[F22B 11/02] the fire tubes being in upright arrangement -- The F22B 11/02 subclass covers steam boilers that combine fire-tube and water-tube sections, specifically those where the fire tubes are installed in an upright, vertical orientation. This arrangement pertains to the methods of steam generation within mechanical engineering, focusing on designs that integrate auxiliary water tubes with fire-tube heating elements.
[F22B 11/04] the fire tubes being in horizontal arrangement -- The F22B 11/04 subclass refers to steam boilers that combine fire-tube and water-tube construction, specifically where the fire tubes are arranged in a horizontal position. This horizontal layout influences the flow of combustion gases and heat transfer within the boiler, distinguishing it from designs with vertical or inclined fire tubes.
[F22B 1/12] produced by an indirect cyclic process -- F22B 1/12 covers steam generation methods in which water is heated indirectly through a separate fluid or medium within a cyclic system rather than by direct contact with the heat source. These indirect cyclic processes usually employ heat exchangers or regenerative cycles to produce steam.
[F22B 1/123] Steam generators downstream of a nuclear boiling water reactor -- F22B 1/123 covers steam generators that are placed downstream of a nuclear boiling water reactor, focusing on how the reactor's heat is transferred to a secondary circuit to produce steam. The classification pertains to methods and arrangements for steam generation in nuclear power plants.
[F22B 1/126] Steam generators of the Schmidt-Hartmann type -- F22B 1/126 relates to Schmidt-Hartmann steam generators, a class of heat-exchange apparatus that produces steam by directing water through a matrix of heated tubes or plates. The design is employed in power-generation and industrial plants for compact, efficient steam generation.
[F22B 1/14] coming in direct contact with water in bulk or in sprays -- The entry covers steam-generation methods where water contacts the heating source directly, either as a bulk flow or as dispersed sprays, allowing heat transfer without intervening surfaces. It includes designs such as immersion heaters, direct-contact boilers and spray-type generators that rely on the water-to-heat interface for efficient steam production.
[F22B 1/143] in combination with a nuclear installation -- F22B 1/143 pertains to methods of steam generation that are integrated with a nuclear installation, describing how the heat from a nuclear reactor is used to produce steam for industrial or power-generation purposes. It covers arrangements and processes that combine nuclear energy sources with boiler systems to create steam efficiently.
[F22B 1/146] Loffler boilers -- F22B 1/146 designates Loffler boilers, a type of fire-tube steam generator in which water circulates through tubes that are directly exposed to combustion gases, allowing rapid heat transfer and the production of saturated or superheated steam. These boilers are used where compact, efficient steam generation is required, often in industrial or marine applications.
[F22B 1/16] the heat carrier being hot liquid or hot vapour, e.g. waste liquid, waste vapour -- F22B 1/16 relates to steam-generation methods that employ a hot liquid or hot vapour as the heat carrier, for example waste liquids or waste vapours, to transfer heat to the working fluid and produce steam.
[F22B 1/162] in combination with a nuclear installation -- F22B 1/162 covers methods and apparatus for generating steam that are specifically integrated with, or used in conjunction with, a nuclear installation. It addresses the coupling of steam-generation systems to nuclear reactors for power production or process heat applications.
[F22B 1/165] using heat pipes
[F22B 1/167] using an organic fluid -- The subclass F22B 1/167 covers methods of steam generation that employ an organic fluid as the working or heat-transfer medium, such as in organic Rankine cycles or processes where organic liquids are boiled or evaporated to produce steam for power or heating applications.
[F22B 1/18] the heat carrier being a hot gas, e.g. waste gas such as exhaust gas of internal-combustion engines -- F22B 1/18 covers steam-generation methods that employ a hot gas as the heat carrier, for example using waste gases such as the exhaust from internal-combustion engines instead of direct fuel combustion.
[F22B 1/1807] using the exhaust gases of combustion engines -- F22B 1/1807 covers methods that recover heat from the exhaust gases of internal-combustion engines to produce steam, typically by passing the gases through a heat-exchange or boiler system. The approach improves overall energy efficiency by utilizing waste heat that would otherwise be lost.
[F22B 1/1815] using the exhaust gases of gas-turbines -- The CPC subclass F22B 1/1815 relates to steam-generation methods that use the exhaust gases from gas-turbines as a heat source. It covers arrangements where turbine exhaust is directed to a boiler or heat-exchanger to produce steam for power cycles or process heating.
[F22B 1/1823] for gas-cooled nuclear reactors -- This CPC subclass is part of mechanical engineering, steam generation, and deals with general aspects of steam generation for gas-cooled nuclear reactors. It covers techniques that produce steam by using heat from the core of a gas-cooled reactor, including heat-transfer arrangements and system configurations.
[F22B 1/183] in combination with metallurgical converter installations -- These provisions relate to steam-generation methods that are integrated with metallurgical converter units, such as using the heat, gases or exhaust of a converter to produce steam for power or process purposes. The classification covers arrangements where the steam plant and converter are combined in a single installation to improve energy efficiency.
[F22B 1/1838] the hot gas being under a high pressure, e.g. in chemical installations -- This CPC subclass relates to steam-generation methods that employ hot gas under high pressure, such as in chemical installations, describing how the high-pressure hot gas is used to produce steam.
[F22B 1/1846] the hot gas being loaded with particles, e.g. waste heat boilers after a coal gasification plant -- This subclass concerns steam-generation methods in which the hot gas is loaded with solid particles, for example in waste-heat boilers that receive particle-laden gas from a coal-gasification plant. It addresses the utilization and handling of such particulate-laden gases to enhance heat recovery.
[F22B 1/1853] coming in direct contact with water in bulk or in sprays -- F22B 1/1853 covers methods where the working fluid contacts water directly, either as a bulk liquid or as a spray, during steam generation. This includes designs that introduce water into the heating zone without intermediate heat exchangers, enabling direct heat transfer for evaporation.
[F22B 1/1861] Waste heat boilers with supplementary firing -- The code covers boilers that recover waste heat from industrial processes and incorporate an auxiliary fuel-firing system to boost steam production, enhancing overall thermal efficiency and allowing better load control.
[F22B 1/1869] Hot gas water tube boilers not provided for in  F22B1/1807  -  F22B1/1861 -- F22B 1/1869 covers hot-gas water-tube boilers that are not classified in the preceding groups F22B 1/1807 to F22B 1/1861. These boilers use combustion gases that flow directly through water-filled tubes, generating steam without the separate fire-tube arrangement typical of other designs, and are applied where high-pressure steam production and rapid heating are required.
[F22B 1/1876] the hot gas being loaded with particles, e.g. dust -- This subclass covers methods in which a hot gas employed for steam generation is loaded with solid particles, such as dust, to influence heat transfer, combustion behavior, or downstream processing. The particles may be introduced into the gas stream before, during, or after heat exchange to improve efficiency or achieve specific functional effects.
[F22B 1/1884] Hot gas heating tube boilers with one or more heating tubes -- This CPC subclass covers boilers that generate steam by passing hot gases through one or more heating tubes arranged inside the boiler, where the tubes serve as the primary heat-exchange element. It falls under methods of steam generation in mechanical engineering.
[F22B 1/1892] Systems therefor not provided for in  F22B1/1807  -  F22B1/1861 -- F22B 1/1892 covers steam-generation systems that are not specifically described in the preceding groups F22B 1/1807 to F22B 1/1861. It applies to novel or unconventional arrangements for producing steam, such as unique boiler layouts, hybrid heating cycles, or integrated heat-recovery configurations that fall outside the earlier defined methods.
[F22B 1/20] using heat evolved in a solution absorbing steam -- F22B 1/20 covers methods of steam generation that make use of the heat released when a solution absorbs steam, employing that released energy to produce additional steam or to improve overall efficiency. It falls under the mechanical-engineering field, specifically within steam-generation techniques and general aspects of steam generation.
[F22B 1/22] using combustion under pressure substantially exceeding atmospheric pressure -- F22B 1/22 covers steam generation techniques that use combustion performed at pressures substantially above atmospheric, allowing direct production of high-pressure steam in boilers or similar devices. This approach can improve thermal efficiency and is employed in supercritical and other high-pressure power cycles.
[F22B 1/24] Pressure-fired steam boilers, e.g. using turbo air compressors actuated by hot gases from boiler furnace -- F22B 1/24 concerns pressure-fired steam boilers in which the furnace's hot gases drive turbo-air compressors that supply combustion air, generating steam under pressure. The code covers designs where the boiler's own heat source powers the compressor, improving efficiency and eliminating separate drive units.
[F22B 1/26] Steam boilers of submerged-flame type, i.e. the flame being surrounded by, or impinging on, the water to be vaporised -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Steam boilers of the submerged-flame type, i.e. the flame being surrounded by, or impinging on, the water to be vaporised, e.g. water in sprays.
[F22B 1/265] the water being in bulk -- F22B 1/265 covers steam-generation methods that use water in bulk form, such as drawing feedwater directly from lakes, reservoirs or other large bodies of water without treating it as a separate stream. It relates to the design of boilers or steam generators where a bulk water supply is an integral part of the process.
[F22B 1/28] in boilers heated electrically -- F22B 1/28 covers boilers that are heated by electrical energy instead of combustion, including resistive, induction or arc heating methods used to produce steam. It relates to the design, operation and control of electrically powered steam generators within the broader field of mechanical engineering and steam generation.
[F22B 1/281] other than by electrical resistances or electrodes -- F22B 1/281 concerns methods of producing steam that do not rely on electrical resistances or electrodes, covering alternative heating techniques such as combustion, solar radiation, chemical reactions or other non-electrical energy sources. It falls under the general aspects of steam-generation methods in mechanical engineering.
[F22B 1/282] with water or steam circulating in tubes or ducts -- F22B 1/282 covers methods of generating steam in which water or steam is made to flow through tubes, ducts or similar passages, allowing heat transfer and circulation within the boiler system. It pertains to designs that employ internal conduit networks for efficient steam production.
[F22B 1/284] with water in reservoirs -- The code F22B 1/284 pertains to steam-generation techniques that employ water stored in reservoirs prior to heating, covering the handling, supply and management of reservoir water for producing steam.
[F22B 1/285] the water being fed by a pump to the reservoirs -- The F22B 1/285 subgroup concerns the feeding of water to steam-generation reservoirs by means of a pump, including the arrangement, placement and control of pumps that deliver feedwater to boiler tanks or drums. It addresses the practical aspects of moving water into the reservoirs that store water for steam production.
[F22B 1/287] with water in sprays or in films -- F22B 1/287 covers steam-generating techniques in which water is introduced as fine sprays or thin films to promote rapid evaporation. This approach is used in boilers and heat-exchange systems to improve heat transfer efficiency and control of steam production. It falls under general methods of steam generation.
[F22B 1/288] Instantaneous electrical steam generators built-up from heat-exchange elements arranged within a confined chamber having heat-retaining walls -- The entry covers instantaneous electrical steam generators that are assembled from heat-exchange elements placed inside a sealed chamber whose walls retain heat. It focuses on the design of such compact generators for rapid steam production.
[F22B 1/30] Electrode boilers -- Electrode boilers are steam generators that produce steam by passing an electric current through water electrodes, heating the water through electrical resistance rather than combustion. They are used for low- to medium-pressure steam in industrial and utility applications, offering rapid start-up, high efficiency and the ability to operate without fuel storage.
[F22B 13/00] Steam boilers of fire-box type, i.e. boilers where both combustion chambers and subsequent flues or fire tubes are arranged within the boiler body -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Boilers in which combustion of fuel is performed inside a chamber called a fire box, including fire tubes of flues attached to the wall of the fire-box that carry the hot gaseous products of combustion through the boiler water, heating it, before the gaseous products escape to the atmosphere. Both the fire-box and fire tubes are built-in in the boiler body. This place covers locomotive boilers, fire-box boiler with flues other than fire tubes and component parts of said boilers.
[F22B 13/005] with flues, other than fire tubes -- F22B 13/005 refers to fire-box type steam boilers that employ flues rather than fire tubes to convey hot gases through the boiler water space. The flues are larger passages that allow combustion gases to flow and transfer heat without the narrow tubes typical of fire-tube designs. This subclass deals with boiler arrangements where the flues are the primary means of heat exchange.
[F22B 13/02] mounted in fixed position with the boiler body disposed upright -- This CPC subclass covers fire-box type steam boilers that are permanently installed in a fixed position with the boiler body standing upright, integrating the combustion chamber and fire tubes within the same vessel. It pertains to stationary designs where the boiler is not intended to be moved.
[F22B 13/023] with auxiliary water tubes inside the fire-box, e.g. vertical tubes  F22B13/10  takes precedence -- This subclass pertains to fire-box type steam boilers that incorporate auxiliary water tubes inside the fire-box, such as vertical tubes, to enhance heat absorption and steam production. It falls under methods of steam generation within the broader mechanical engineering category.
[F22B 13/026] the tubes being in substantially horizontal arrangement -- This subclass deals with fire-box type steam boilers in which the combustion chamber and the fire tubes are housed within the boiler shell, and the tubes are arranged essentially horizontally. The horizontal tube layout affects the flow of combustion gases and heat transfer to the water surrounding the tubes, distinguishing it from vertical or inclined tube configurations within the same boiler family.
[F22B 1/303] with means for injecting or spraying water against electrodes or with means for water circulation -- F22B 1/303 covers steam generators that incorporate devices for injecting or spraying water against the electrodes or that provide water circulation within the generator. This subcategory falls under methods of steam generation in mechanical engineering, focusing on techniques that improve heat transfer and control of the water-to-steam conversion process.
[F22B 13/04] mounted in fixed position with the boiler body disposed substantially horizontally -- This subgroup covers fire-box type steam boilers in which the boiler is permanently installed and the main body lies essentially horizontal rather than inclined or vertical. The classification is used for designs where the combustion chamber and fire tubes are integrated within a horizontally positioned pressure vessel.
[F22B 1/306] with at least one electrode permanently above the water surface -- This CPC subclass pertains to steam generators in which at least one electrode is permanently positioned above the water surface, allowing the electrode to heat the water by electrical discharge while remaining exposed to the vapor space. It covers methods and apparatuses that employ this configuration for efficient steam production.
[F22B 13/06] Locomobile, traction-engine, steam-roller, or locomotive boilers -- F22B 13/06 relates to fire-box type steam boilers employed in locomobiles, traction engines, steam rollers and locomotives, where the combustion chamber and the subsequent flues or fire tubes are incorporated within the boiler body.
[F22B 13/065] Combination of low- and high-pressure locomotive boilers -- F22B 13/065 deals with locomotive steam boilers that integrate both low-pressure and high-pressure sections within a fire-box type arrangement. The subclass covers designs where a low-pressure boiler supplies steam to a high-pressure unit or where the two pressure zones are combined in a single vessel to improve efficiency and power output.
[F22B 13/08] without auxiliary water tubes inside the fire-box -- F22B 13/08 refers to fire-box type steam boilers that generate steam without auxiliary water tubes inside the fire-box. It covers designs where water directly contacts the combustion chamber, eliminating internal water tubes for heat exchange, and falls under methods of steam generation within mechanical engineering.
[F22B 13/10] with auxiliary water tubes inside the fire-box -- F22B 13/10 refers to fire-box type steam boilers that include auxiliary water tubes inside the fire-box, adding heating surface and enhancing heat transfer efficiency.
[F22B 13/12] the auxiliary water tubes lining the fire-box -- F22B 13/12 relates to fire-box type steam boilers that incorporate auxiliary water tubes lining the fire-box, adding heating surface and protecting the fire-box walls. The tubes improve heat transfer, allowing higher pressure operation and more efficient steam generation.
[F22B 13/14] Component parts thereof -- F22B 13/14 refers to the individual components of fire-box type steam boilers, in which the combustion chamber and the fire tubes are placed inside the boiler shell. The classification covers parts such as the fire-box, tube plates, stays and associated fittings used in these integrated boiler designs.
[F22B 13/145] Firebox thermosiphons -- Firebox thermosiphons are passive heat-exchange devices installed in fire-box type steam boilers that use natural water circulation driven by temperature differences to transfer heat from the combustion chamber to the boiler water, improving steam generation efficiency without the need for pumps.
[F22B 13/16] Stay-bolt connections, e.g. rigid connections -- This CPC subclass deals with the design and attachment of stay-bolts used to reinforce fire-box type steam boilers, emphasizing rigid connections that secure the stays to the boiler shell or other structural parts.
