It falls under methods of steam generation for mechanical engineering.
[F22B 13/18] Flexible connections, e.g. of ball-and-socket type -- F22B 13/18 relates to flexible joints used in fire-box type steam boilers, such as ball-and-socket connections that permit relative movement between boiler parts while keeping a sealed path for steam and gases. These connections accommodate thermal expansion, vibration or slight misalignment of the combustion chamber and fire tubes.
[F22B 15/00] Water-tube boilers of horizontal type, i.e. the water-tube sets being arranged horizontally -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Details and constructional features of water-tube boilers in which water circulates in tubes heated externally and characterised by strictly horizontal arranged water tubes or water tube walls.
[F22B 17/00] Water-tube boilers of horizontally-inclined type, i.e. the water-tube sets being inclined slightly with respect to the horizontal plane -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Details, constructional features and component parts of water-tube boilers in which water circulates in tubes heated externally and characterised by horizontally-inclined water tubes or water tube walls.
[F22B 17/02] built-up from water-tube sets in abutting connection with two header boxes in common for all sets, e.g. with flat header boxes
[F22B 17/025] with combined inlet and outlet header boxes, e.g. connected by U-tubes or Field tubes -- This subclass concerns horizontally-inclined water-tube boilers in which the inlet and outlet header boxes are combined, typically linked by U-tubes or field tubes that serve both feed and exhaust functions. The arrangement simplifies the tube circuit and enables a compact boiler design.
[F22B 17/04] the water-tube sets being inclined in opposite directions, e.g. crosswise -- This subclass concerns water-tube boiler arrangements in which the tube banks are tilted slightly from the horizontal and mounted so that they slope in opposite directions, for example in a crosswise layout. It is part of the methods of steam generation group within mechanical engineering.
[F22B 17/06] the water-tube sets being bent angularly -- F22B 17/06 covers water-tube boiler sets that are inclined slightly from horizontal and bent at an angle, i.e. horizontally-inclined water-tube boilers with angular bends.
[F22B 17/08] the water-tube sets being curved -- F22B 17/08 relates to water-tube boilers of the horizontally-inclined type in which the water-tube assemblies are curved rather than straight. The curvature is used to accommodate the slight inclination of the boiler and to improve steam-water separation and flow. This subclass falls under methods of steam generation within mechanical engineering.
[F22B 17/10] built-up from water-tube sets in abutting connection with two sectional headers each for every set, i.e. with headers in a number of sections across the width or height of the boiler -- This subclass concerns horizontally-inclined water-tube boilers whose tube bundles are assembled in abutting connection and provided with two sectional headers for each set, the headers being divided into multiple sections across the boiler's width or height to distribute steam and water.
[F22B 17/105] with tubes in series flow arrangement -- F22B 17/105 refers to water-tube boilers whose tube banks are arranged in a horizontally-inclined configuration and where the tubes are connected so that the working fluid passes through them in series. This subclass covers designs that guide steam-generating water sequentially from one tube to the next within the inclined boiler set.
[F22B 17/12] the sectional headers being in vertical or substantially-vertical arrangement -- F22B 17/12 relates to water-tube boilers whose tube bundles are mounted at a slight incline from the horizontal plane and whose sectional headers are arranged in a vertical or substantially-vertical orientation, facilitating steam generation in horizontally-inclined boiler designs.
[F22B 17/14] the sectional headers being in horizontal or substantially-horizontal arrangement -- The F22B 17/14 subclass covers water-tube boilers whose tubes are arranged on a slightly inclined plane and whose sectional headers are positioned in a horizontal or essentially horizontal orientation. It pertains to designs that keep the header layout level while the boiler body is modestly sloped.
[F22B 17/16] Component parts thereof -- F22B 17/16 covers the individual component parts of water-tube boilers whose tube assemblies are inclined slightly from the horizontal. It includes elements such as inclined tube bundles, drums, supports, inlet and outlet connections, and related fittings that form the boiler's structure and facilitate steam generation.
[F22B 17/18] Header boxes
[F22B 19/00] Water-tube boilers of combined horizontally-inclined type and vertical type, i.e. water-tube boilers of horizontally-inclined type having auxiliary water-tube sets in vertical or substantially-vertical arrangement -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Details, constructional features and component parts of water-tube boilers in which water circulates in tubes heated externally and characterised by horizontally inclined water-tube sets being connected to or having also an auxiliary water-tube set with vertical or substantially vertical water tubes.
[F22B 21/00] Water-tube boilers of vertical or steeply-inclined type, i.e. the water-tube sets being arranged vertically or substantially vertically -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Details, constructional features and component parts of water-tube boilers in which water circulates in tubes heated externally and characterised by vertically or substantially vertically water tubes or water tube walls. This place covers straight water tubes and serpentine, helical bent in U-loop or spirally formed water tubes disposed vertically.
[F22B 21/002] involving a single upper drum  F22B21/36  takes precedence -- F22B 21/002 classifies vertical or steeply-inclined water-tube boilers that generate steam using a single upper drum. The code covers the arrangement and operation of such boilers, but more specific provisions are found in the preceding subclass F22B 21/36, which takes precedence over this entry.
[F22B 21/005] involving a central vertical drum, header or downcomer -- A water-tube boiler in which the tubes are arranged vertically or at a steep angle and a central vertical drum, header or downcomer is used to collect steam and distribute water. The configuration facilitates efficient steam generation and uniform circulation within the tube bundle. This subclass covers vertical or steeply-inclined designs featuring a single central component for steam collection.
[F22B 21/007] specially adapted for locomotives -- The entry concerns vertically arranged or steeply-inclined water-tube boilers that are specially adapted for use in locomotives, meaning the boiler design fits within a locomotive's frame and provides steam for railway engines.
[F22B 21/02] built-up from substantially-straight water tubes -- F22B 21/02 covers vertical or steeply-inclined water-tube boilers that are assembled from substantially straight water tubes, describing the construction method for built-up boiler sets where the tubes are arranged in a vertical or near-vertical configuration. This subclass falls under methods of steam generation within mechanical engineering.
[F22B 21/04] involving a single upper drum and a single lower drum, e.g. the drums being arranged transversely -- F22B 21/04 covers water-tube boilers whose tubes are arranged vertically or at a steep angle and that incorporate a single upper drum and a single lower drum, often placed transversely across the boiler. This configuration is used for steam generation in compact vertical boiler designs.
[F22B 21/06] the water tubes being arranged annularly in sets, e.g. in abutting connection with drums of annular shape -- F22B 21/06 covers water-tube boilers in which the water tubes are arranged annularly, typically in direct abutting connection with drums of annular shape. The subclass applies to vertical or steeply-inclined boiler designs where the tube bundles form a ring around the drum.
[F22B 21/065] involving an upper and lower drum of annular shape -- It relates to vertical or steeply-inclined water-tube boilers that use an annular upper drum together with an annular lower drum as part of the steam-generation system.
[F22B 21/08] the water tubes being arranged sectionally in groups or in banks, e.g. bent over at their ends -- This subgroup pertains to vertical or steeply-inclined water-tube boilers in which the tubes are organized in separate sections forming groups or banks, often with the tube ends bent back toward the furnace. The arrangement facilitates access, cleaning, or heat-transfer optimization.
[F22B 21/081] involving a combustion chamber, placed at the side and built-up from water tubes -- F22B 21/081 pertains to vertical or steeply inclined water-tube boilers that incorporate a side-mounted combustion chamber formed directly from water tubes. The classification addresses the arrangement where the tube bundle itself creates the combustion space, improving heat transfer and allowing compact boiler construction.
[F22B 21/083] involving an upper drum and a lower drum and a fire-place between the two drums -- F22B 21/083 classifies water-tube boilers of vertical or steeply-inclined design that incorporate an upper drum, a lower drum and a fire-place positioned between them. The code is used for patents describing this specific arrangement for steam generation in mechanical engineering.
[F22B 21/085] the tubes being placed in layers -- This subclass covers vertical or steeply-inclined water-tube boilers in which the tubes are arranged in separate horizontal layers, a configuration that enhances water circulation and heat-transfer efficiency within the boiler assembly.
[F22B 21/086] Frames built-up from water tubes -- This subclass concerns vertical or steeply inclined water-tube boilers whose supporting frames are formed by assembling the water tubes themselves into a built-up structure, so the tubes serve both as heat-exchange elements and as the boiler's structural framework.
[F22B 21/088] involving an upper drum and a lower drum and two lateral drums -- F22B 21/088 covers vertical or steeply-inclined water-tube boilers that incorporate an upper drum, a lower drum and two lateral drums, forming a multi-drum arrangement for steam generation. This configuration is used to improve water circulation and heat transfer in steam-raising equipment.
[F22B 21/10] the water tubes being arranged in staggered rows -- F22B 21/10 covers vertical or steeply-inclined water-tube boilers in which the water tubes are arranged in staggered rows, improving heat transfer and space efficiency. It falls under mechanical engineering → steam generation → methods of steam generation for water-tube boilers of this orientation.
[F22B 21/12] involving two or more upper drums and two or more lower drums, e.g. with crosswise-arranged water-tube sets in abutting connection with drums -- This CPC subgroup covers vertical or steeply-inclined water-tube boilers that employ two or more upper drums and two or more lower drums, often with crosswise-arranged tube sets abutting each other. It relates to steam-generation methods in mechanical engineering.
[F22B 21/123] involving crossed water tubes -- F22B 21/123 covers vertical or steeply-inclined water-tube boilers that use crossed water tubes, where the tubes intersect or criss-cross to enhance heat transfer and water circulation. This arrangement is applied in steam generation systems to improve efficiency and allow compact boiler designs. It falls under methods of steam generation in mechanical engineering.
[F22B 21/126] involving more than two lower or upper drums -- F22B 21/126 covers vertical or steeply inclined water-tube boilers that incorporate more than two lower or upper drums. The classification applies to designs where the water-tube sets are arranged substantially vertically, addressing the arrangement and number of drums used in steam generation.
[F22B 21/14] involving a single upper drum and two or more lower drums -- F22B 21/14 designates water-tube boilers arranged vertically or at a steep angle that employ a single upper steam drum together with two or more lower drums. This configuration is used in steam-generation systems where the tubes rise to the upper drum for steam separation.
[F22B 21/16] the lower drums being interconnected by further water tubes -- This subclass covers vertical or steeply-inclined water-tube boilers in which the lower steam-collecting drums are linked together by additional water tubes, allowing circulation between drums and improving heat transfer.
[F22B 21/18] involving two or more upper drums and a single lower drum -- The subclass F22B 21/18 pertains to vertical or steeply-inclined water-tube boilers that are designed with two or more upper drums linked to a single lower drum. This arrangement is used to enhance steam generation efficiency and improve water-circulation dynamics within the boiler system.
[F22B 21/185] involving more than two upper drums and a single lower drum -- This CPC subclass covers vertical or steeply-inclined water-tube boilers that have more than two upper drums feeding a single lower drum, a configuration used to improve steam generation efficiency and water circulation in high-pressure boiler systems.
[F22B 21/20] involving sectional or subdivided headers in separate arrangement for each water-tube set -- F22B 21/20 covers vertical or steep-inclined water-tube boilers that use sectional or subdivided headers arranged separately for each tube set, enabling individual control and improved steam generation efficiency. This subclass falls under methods of steam generation within mechanical engineering, focusing on the layout of header assemblies in vertically configured tube boilers.
[F22B 21/22] built-up from water tubes of form other than straight or substantially straight -- F22B 21/22 classifies water-tube boilers whose tubes are formed in shapes other than straight or substantially straight, covering designs where the tubes are bent, curved, or otherwise contoured. It falls under mechanical engineering, steam generation, and methods of steam generation, specifically vertical or steeply-inclined water-tube sets.
[F22B 21/24] bent in serpentine or sinuous form -- F22B 21/24 covers vertical or steeply inclined water-tube boilers whose tubes are formed in a serpentine or sinuous shape. The bent configuration is used to increase heating surface and promote fluid flow within the boiler.
[F22B 21/26] bent helically, i.e. coiled -- The subgroup F22B 21/26 concerns vertical or steeply-inclined water-tube boilers whose tubes are formed in a helically bent, i.e., coiled, configuration. The coiled design enables a compact arrangement and promotes efficient heat transfer within the steam-generation system.
[F22B 21/28] bent spirally
[F22B 21/30] bent in U-loop form -- The subclass F22B 21/30 covers water-tube boilers where the tubes are bent into a U-loop shape and arranged vertically or at a steep inclination. This U-loop configuration improves water circulation and heat transfer, defining a specific method of steam generation.
[F22B 21/32] disposed horizontally in abutting connection with upright headers or rising water mains -- The entry covers design of water-tube boilers where the tubes are arranged vertically or steeply inclined but positioned horizontally and abut directly onto upright headers or rising water mains, facilitating efficient steam generation in mechanical engineering contexts.
[F22B 21/34] built-up from water tubes grouped in panel form surrounding the combustion chamber, i.e. radiation boilers -- The classification covers vertical or steeply-inclined water-tube boilers in which the tubes are assembled into panels that encircle the combustion chamber, forming a radiation boiler. These built-up tube panels provide a heat-transfer surface that directly absorbs radiant energy from combustion. Used in steam generation where compact, high-pressure designs are required.
[F22B 21/341] Vertical radiation boilers with combustion in the lower part -- F22B 21/341 covers water-tube boilers whose tubes are arranged vertically or at a steep angle and where the fire is placed in the lower part of the boiler, allowing heat to rise and radiate upward through the tube bundle. This subcategory falls under methods of steam generation in mechanical engineering.
[F22B 21/343] the vertical radiation combustion chamber being connected at its upper part to a sidewards convection chamber -- This CPC subclass concerns vertical radiation combustion chambers that are connected at their upper part to a sidewards convection chamber, typically employed in water-tube boilers whose tube sets are arranged vertically or steeply inclined. The configuration directs radiant heat upward into the adjacent convection space to enhance overall heat-transfer efficiency.
[F22B 21/345] with a tube bundle between an upper and a lower drum in the convection pass -- This subclass of the CPC pertains to vertical or steeply-inclined water-tube boilers where a tube bundle is positioned between an upper and a lower drum in the convection pass, facilitating heat transfer and steam generation. It covers arrangements that place the tubes in the space between the drums to improve efficiency.
[F22B 21/346] Horizontal radiation boilers -- F22B 21/346 covers horizontal radiation boilers, a type of water-tube boiler where the tubes are arranged horizontally and heat is transferred by radiation from the fire to the water. These boilers are used for generating steam in industrial processes, offering a compact design and efficient heat transfer compared to vertically oriented water-tube sets.
[F22B 21/348] Radiation boilers with a burner at the top -- A radiation boiler having its burner mounted at the top of a vertical or steeply-inclined water-tube set, where steam is produced mainly by radiant heat from the flame onto the vertically arranged tubes. This falls under mechanical engineering, steam-generation methods for vertical water-tube boilers.
[F22B 21/36] involving an upper drum or headers mounted at the top of the combustion chamber -- F22B 21/36 covers water-tube boilers whose tubes are arranged vertically or at a steep angle and that feature an upper drum or header positioned at the top of the combustion chamber. This design allows steam to be collected directly from the highest point of the boiler, improving separation of water and steam.
[F22B 21/363] involving a horizontal drum mounted in an upper corner of the boiler -- This CPC subclass covers vertical or steeply-inclined water-tube boilers that incorporate a horizontal steam drum mounted in an upper corner of the boiler, allowing steam collection while retaining a compact vertical arrangement. It is part of the mechanical-engineering hierarchy for steam-generation methods.
[F22B 21/366] involving a horizontal drum mounted in the middle of the boiler -- This CPC subclass relates to water-tube boilers whose tube bundles are arranged vertically or at a steep incline and that incorporate a horizontal steam drum positioned centrally within the boiler shell. It covers designs where the drum is mounted in the middle of the vertical tube set to collect steam.
[F22B 21/38] Component parts thereof, e.g. prefabricated panels -- The CPC subclass F22B 21/38 covers component parts of vertical or steeply-inclined water-tube boilers, such as prefabricated panels used in their assembly. It is located within methods of steam generation in the mechanical engineering field.
[F22B 21/40] built-up from water tubes arranged in a comparatively long vertical shaft, i.e. tower boilers -- F22B 21/40 covers water-tube boilers in which the tubes are assembled in a relatively long vertical shaft, commonly called tower boilers, used for generating steam. It is a subclass of methods of steam generation within mechanical engineering and specifies a vertical or steeply inclined arrangement of the water-tube set.
[F22B 23/00] Water-tube boilers built-up from sets of spaced double-walled water tubes of return type in unilateral abutting connection with a boiler drum or with a header box, i.e. built-up from Field water tubes comprising an inner tube arranged within an outer unilaterally-closed tube -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Details, constructional features and component parts of water-tube boilers in which water circulates in tubes heated externally and characterised by sets of spaced double-walled water tubes of return type in unilateral abutting connection with a boiler drum or with a header box forming for example an annular flow.
[F22B 23/02] the water-tube, i.e. Field-tube, sets being horizontal or substantially horizontal -- F22B 23/02 covers horizontal or substantially horizontal field-tube water-tube assemblies in steam generators, where each double-walled tube has an inner tube placed within a unilaterally closed outer tube and is joined in a unilateral abutting connection to a boiler drum or header box.
[F22B 23/04] the water-tube, i.e. Field-tube, sets being vertical or substantially vertical -- F22B 23/04 relates to water-tube boilers that use built-up sets of spaced double-walled, return-type tubes connected unilaterally to a drum or header box, known as field tubes. The classification covers configurations where these tube sets are arranged vertical or substantially vertical.
[F22B 23/06] Component parts thereof, e.g. Field water tubes -- F22B 23/06 covers the component parts of water-tube boilers that use spaced double-walled field water tubes, such as inner tubes placed within outer tubes that are closed on one side and connected unilaterally to a boiler drum or header box. It includes the design and arrangement of these field tubes.
[F22B 25/00] Water-tube boilers built-up from sets of water tubes with internally-arranged flue tubes, or fire tubes, extending through the water tubes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Details, constructional features and component parts of water-tube boilers in which water circulates in tubes heated externally and characterised by sets of water tubes with internally arranged flue tubes or fire tubes forming thereby an annular flow of the water to be evaporated.
[F22B 27/00] Instantaneous or flash steam boilers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  All steam generators and steam generation methods with an instant steam generation process including flash steam boilers. This place covers instantaneous steam boilers built up from fire tubes, from water tubes, from rotary heat-exchange elements or from heat-exchange elements arranged within a confined chamber having heat retaining walls and steam boilers with spray nozzles for sprinkling or injecting water particles on to or into hot heat-exchange elements.
[F22B 27/02] built-up from fire tubes -- F22B 27/02 covers built-up fire-tube boilers, a subtype of instantaneous or flash steam generators where a series of fire tubes are assembled within a shell to produce steam quickly, often used in industrial applications for rapid heating.
[F22B 27/04] built-up from water tubes -- The subclass covers the construction of instantaneous or flash steam boilers assembled from separate water tubes, forming a built-up water-tube generator. It addresses the arrangement and joining of individual tubes to create the boiler pressure vessel for rapid steam generation.
[F22B 27/06] bent in serpentine or sinuous form -- F22B 27/06 covers instantaneous or flash steam boilers in which the heating surfaces are arranged in a bent serpentine or sinuous form, providing enhanced heat transfer within compact units. This falls under mechanical engineering, steam generation, methods of steam generation, instantaneous or flash steam boilers.
[F22B 27/08] bent helically, i.e. coiled -- F22B 27/08 covers instantaneous or flash steam boilers whose heating elements are formed as bent helically-shaped, i.e., coiled, structures. The coiled configuration is used to improve heat transfer efficiency and allow a compact boiler design.
[F22B 27/10] bent spirally -- Bent spirally describes steam-generator tubes that are formed into a helical shape with bends, increasing the heating surface and promoting rapid water circulation within instantaneous or flash steam boilers. This design is used to enhance heat transfer efficiency in compact boiler constructions.
[F22B 27/12] built-up from rotary heat-exchange elements, e.g. from tube assemblies -- The subclass F22B 27/12 relates to instantaneous or flash steam boilers that are built up from rotary heat-exchange elements, such as tube assemblies. It covers designs where a rotating or revolving bundle of tubes forms the heating surface, enabling rapid transfer of heat to water to generate steam.
[F22B 27/14] built-up from heat-exchange elements arranged within a confined chamber having heat-retaining walls -- F22B 27/14 covers built-up instantaneous steam boilers formed from heat-exchange elements arranged inside a sealed chamber whose walls retain heat, enabling rapid flash-steam generation.
[F22B 27/16] involving spray nozzles for sprinkling or injecting water particles on to or into hot heat-exchange elements, e.g. into tubes -- The subclass F22B 27/16 covers methods of steam generation that employ spray nozzles to sprinkle or inject water particles onto hot heat-exchange elements such as tubes, enabling rapid vaporisation in instantaneous or flash boilers. It is part of the mechanical-engineering hierarchy under steam generation, methods of steam generation, and instantaneous or flash steam boilers.
[F22B 27/165] with film flow of water on heated surfaces -- This subclass covers instantaneous or flash steam boilers in which water spreads as a thin film across heated surfaces, allowing rapid heat transfer and quick steam generation. It relates to methods of steam generation that employ film flow on heated walls within mechanical engineering.
[F22B 29/00] Steam boilers of forced-flow type -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Steam boilers in which means are provided, such as a pump, in order to ensure the flow of the working medium. This place covers details, component parts and arrangements of steam boilers of forced-circulation type, steam boilers of combination type in which natural flow (convection circulation) is promoted by additional measures and boilers of once-through type in which flow is forced (forced once through boilers).  **Glossary:** - once-through boiler: type of boiler in which water is input at one side, and steam is extracted from the other side of the flow path - forced-flow boiler: type of boiler in which a pump ensures flow - forced-once-through boiler: combination of a once-through and a forced-flow boiler (a pump ensures flow of a boiler in which water is input at one side, and steam is extracted from the other side of the flow path) - forced-circulation boiler: type of boiler in which recirculation is achieved by a circulation pump - natural-circulation boiler: type of boiler in which the circulation is achieved by the difference in density of the heated water in the boiler causing convection currents
[F22B 29/02] of forced-circulation type -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Forced-flow steam boilers of forced-circulation, i.e. recirculation type.
[F22B 29/023] without drums, i.e. without hot water storage in the boiler -- Steam boilers of the forced-flow type that operate without drums omit any hot-water storage vessel, circulating water continuously through the heating circuit. The design eliminates the traditional steam-water separator, leading to compact, rapid-startup systems often used in high-pressure or mobile applications where space and weight are limited.
[F22B 29/026] operating at critical or supercritical pressure -- The class F22B 29/026 pertains to forced-flow steam boilers that run at pressures at or above the critical point of water, i.e., critical-pressure and supercritical-pressure boilers. It covers designs and operating methods where steam is generated without a distinct liquid-vapor phase change, enabling higher thermal efficiency in power-plant applications.
[F22B 29/04] of combined-circulation type, i.e. in which convection circulation due to the difference in specific gravity between cold and hot water is promoted by additional measures, e.g. by injecting pressure-water temporarily -- F22B 29/04 covers steam boilers of forced-flow type that use a combined-circulation principle, where convection circulation caused by density differences between cold and hot water is enhanced by measures such as temporary pressure-water injection. This design improves heat transfer and system efficiency.
[F22B 29/06] of once-through type, i.e. built-up from tubes receiving water at one end and delivering superheated steam at the other end of the tubes -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Combinations of low- and high-pressure boilers of forced-flow type -> F22B33/16
[F22B 29/061] Construction of tube walls -- This subclass deals with features concerning the design and fabrication of the walls of tubes used in forced-flow steam boilers, including material selection, thickness, reinforcement, and joining methods to withstand pressure and thermal stresses. It covers ways to integrate tubes into boiler shells and arrangements that improve durability and heat transfer.
[F22B 29/062] involving vertically-disposed water tubes -- F22B 29/062 pertains to forced-flow steam boilers that employ vertically-disposed water tubes. It classifies designs where water circulates upward through the tubes while steam is generated, a common arrangement in high-pressure boiler systems for power generation.
[F22B 29/064] involving horizontally- or helically-disposed water tubes -- F22B 29/064 covers forced-flow steam boilers in which the water tubes are arranged horizontally or in a helix, defining the orientation and layout of the tube bank that carries water through the boiler furnace and heat-exchange zones. This classification addresses design features that affect water circulation, heat transfer efficiency and the mechanical support of horizontally- or helically-disposed tubes.
[F22B 29/065] involving upper vertically disposed water tubes and lower horizontally- or helically disposed water tubes -- F22B 29/065 covers forced-flow steam boilers that use a combination of water-tube arrangements, with the upper tubes positioned vertically and the lower tubes arranged horizontally or helically. This configuration promotes efficient water circulation and heat transfer, improving boiler performance and steam generation rates. It is used in industrial plants where compact, high-output steam production is required.
[F22B 29/067] operating at critical or supercritical pressure -- F22B 29/067 covers forced-flow steam boilers designed to run at critical or supercritical pressure, where the water-steam mixture passes the critical point and no boiling occurs, allowing higher thermal efficiency and compact design. The code applies to systems that maintain pressures above roughly 22 MPa and include related control and safety features.
[F22B 29/068] operating with superimposed recirculation during normal operation  F22B29/12  takes precedence -- F22B 29/068 is a Cooperative Patent Classification code for steam boilers of the forced-flow type that operate with superimposed recirculation during normal operation. It falls under Mechanical Engineering → Steam Generation → Methods of Steam Generation, and is subordinate to the broader heading F22B 29/12, which takes precedence.
[F22B 29/08] operating with fixed point of final state of complete evaporation -- F22B 29/08 covers forced-flow steam boilers that are designed to operate so that the steam leaving the boiler reaches a predetermined, fixed final state after complete evaporation, typically defined by a specific pressure and temperature. The classification relates to methods of controlling boiler parameters to maintain this fixed endpoint.
[F22B 29/10] operating with sliding point of final state of complete evaporation -- F22B 29/10 classifies forced-flow steam boilers that achieve complete evaporation by allowing the final evaporation point to slide along the boiler as operating conditions vary. It is situated under mechanical engineering, steam generation, methods of steam generation and steam boilers of forced-flow type.
[F22B 29/12] operating with superimposed recirculation during starting and low-load periods, e.g. composite boilers -- Steam boilers of forced-flow type that employ an additional recirculation circuit during start-up and low-load operation, such as composite boilers, are covered. The recirculation improves temperature uniformity and reduces thermal stress while the boiler is not at full capacity. This classification addresses methods and arrangements for managing the auxiliary flow during these transient operating conditions.
[F22B 3/00] Other methods of steam generation -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Steam generation and steam boilers involving working media other than water, steam generation by drop in pressure of high-pressure hot water within pressure reducing chambers, by transformation of mechanical (kinetic) energy into heat energy and steam generation at critical or supercritical pressure values.
[F22B 3/02] involving the use of working media other than water -- F22B 3/02 covers methods for generating steam where the working fluid is not water, such as organic liquids, gases or supercritical fluids used in heat-transfer cycles. The classification includes systems that substitute water with alternative media to improve efficiency, operating temperature range, or safety in steam-based processes.
[F22B 3/04] by drop in pressure of high-pressure hot water within pressure-reducing chambers, e.g. in accumulators
[F22B 3/045] the drop in pressure being achieved by compressors, e.g. with steam jet pumps -- The subclass deals with methods that lower steam pressure by using compressors such as steam-jet pumps, where a high-velocity jet creates a low-pressure region that draws steam from the main flow. This approach achieves the pressure drop without throttling valves or expansion devices.
[F22B 3/06] by transformation of mechanical, e.g. kinetic, energy into heat energy -- F22B 3/06 covers steam-generation methods that produce heat by converting mechanical, such as kinetic, energy into thermal energy. This includes devices and processes that use moving parts, rotating elements or other mechanical work to raise water temperature and generate steam. It falls under the broader categories of mechanical engineering, steam generation, and other methods of steam generation.
[F22B 3/08] at critical or supercritical pressure values -- F22B 3/08 covers steam-generation techniques that operate at or above the critical pressure of water, including supercritical and trans-critical cycles. It is classified under mechanical engineering, steam generation, and other methods of steam generation.
[F22B 31/00] Modifications of boiler construction, or of tube systems, dependent on installation of combustion apparatus -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Modifications of boiler construction or of tube systems dependent on the installation of combustion apparatus, for example:
[F22B 31/0007] with combustion in a fluidized bed -- The code F22B 31/0007 covers steam-generation methods that modify boiler or tube-system designs to incorporate combustion within a fluidized-bed reactor. It pertains to mechanical-engineering approaches where the fuel is fluidized by a gas stream, improving heat transfer and fuel flexibility.
[F22B 31/0015] for boilers of the water tube type -- F22B 31/0015 pertains to water-tube boilers and addresses alterations in the boiler's construction or tube layout that are directly related to the way combustion apparatus is installed. This subclass falls within mechanical engineering, steam generation, and methods of steam generation, focusing on design changes that improve performance or accommodate specific combustion setups.
[F22B 31/0023] with tubes in the bed  F22B31/003  takes precedence -- This subclass covers boiler designs where tubes are installed directly in the furnace or combustion bed, affecting heat transfer and fluid flow. It falls under methods of steam generation, specifically modifications of boiler construction or tube systems dependent on placement of combustion apparatus.
[F22B 31/003] with tubes surrounding the bed or with water tube wall partitions -- The subclass F22B 31/003 concerns boilers in which the combustion bed is encircled by tubes or where water-filled tube walls create partitions within the bed, a design that enhances heat transfer, structural integrity and control of the combustion zone.
[F22B 31/0038] with tubes in the bed -- F22B 31/0038 covers modifications of boiler or tube-system construction that involve placing heat-exchange tubes directly in the furnace bed. This arrangement allows the combustion gases to pass through the tube bundle, improving heat transfer to the water and enhancing steam generation efficiency.
[F22B 31/0046] for boilers of the shell type, e.g. with furnace box -- This classification covers shell-type boilers that incorporate a furnace box, focusing on design modifications of the boiler shell or tube system related to the installation of combustion equipment. It falls under methods of steam generation in mechanical engineering.
[F22B 31/0053] with auxiliary water tubes -- The subclass F22B 31/0053 covers steam-generation methods that incorporate auxiliary water tubes into the boiler or tube system, adding separate water-filled tubes to increase heat transfer, improve efficiency, or provide additional heating capacity alongside the primary combustion apparatus.
[F22B 31/0061] Constructional features of bed cooling -- F22B 31/0061 relates to the constructional features of bed cooling in steam-generation apparatus, describing designs that cool the solid fuel bed in fluidized-bed boilers or similar systems, including the arrangement of cooling tubes, coolant distribution and associated structural modifications.
[F22B 31/0069] Systems therefor -- F22B 31/0069 covers the overall arrangements and auxiliary components used to implement modifications of boiler or tube-system construction that are required by the installation of a combustion apparatus. It includes integrated feedwater, flue-gas, control and safety subsystems designed to improve boiler performance, adapt to new burners or meet specific operational constraints.
[F22B 31/0084] with recirculation of separated solids or with cooling of the bed particles outside the combustion bed -- F22B 31/0084 covers modifications to boiler or tube systems where solids separated from the combustion zone are recirculated back into the bed, or where the bed particles are cooled outside the combustion bed. The approach is used in steam-generation processes to improve efficiency and control emissions.
[F22B 31/0092] with a fluidized heat exchange bed and a fluidized combustion bed separated by a partition, the bed particles circulating around or through that partition -- The classification covers boilers in which the heat-exchange zone and the combustion zone each form a fluidized bed that are divided by a partition, yet the solid particles can move around or pass through the partition. This arrangement enables separate control of combustion and heat transfer while allowing the bed material to circulate, improving efficiency and heat distribution in steam-generation systems.
[F22B 31/02] Installation of water-tube boilers in chimneys, e.g. in converter chimneys -- The CPC entry F22B 31/02 pertains to the installation of water-tube boilers within chimney structures, including converter chimneys, focusing on integrating boiler and combustion apparatus as part of the chimney system.
[F22B 31/04] Heat supply by installation of two or more combustion apparatus, e.g. of separate combustion apparatus for the boiler and the superheater respectively -- The code F22B 31/04 covers methods of supplying heat to a steam-generation system by employing two or more separate combustion units, for example using distinct burners for the boiler and for the superheater. It relates to modifications of boiler or tube constructions that depend on the installation of multiple combustion apparatuses. This falls under mechanical engineering, steam generation, and methods of steam generation.
[F22B 31/045] Steam generators specially adapted for burning refuse -- F22B 31/045 covers steam generators that are specifically engineered to burn refuse as fuel. The classification includes boilers and tube systems modified with special grates, feeding arrangements, combustion chambers or waste-handling features that accommodate solid or mixed waste streams while producing steam. Such designs address the unique heat release, ash handling and emission requirements of refuse-fired plants.
[F22B 31/06] Installation of emergency heat supply -- F22B 31/06 covers the installation of emergency heat supply in steam-generating equipment. It relates to modifications of boiler or tube system construction that provide backup heating when primary combustion apparatus fails. The provision is used in mechanical engineering for safety and continuity of steam generation.
[F22B 31/08] Installation of heat-exchange apparatus or of means in boilers for heating air supplied for combustion -- This subclass concerns boiler designs that incorporate heat-exchange devices or other arrangements to warm the air fed to the combustion system. It includes modifications of boiler or tube construction aimed at improving combustion efficiency by preheating combustion air.
[F22B 33/00] Steam-generation plants -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Steam generation plants in the meaning of devices for the generation of steam with other apparatus in mutual association with the steam boilers. Steam boilers of different types in mutual association having a single combustion apparatus in common, e.g. combinations of:  self-contained boilers comprising as a unit the steam boiler, the combustor and fuel storage accessory machines,  combinations of low- and high-pressure boilers, and  combinations of steam boilers with other apparatus, like a condenser, a chemical reactor or other are also covered.
[F22B 33/02] Combinations of boilers having a single combustion apparatus in common -- This subclass covers arrangements where two or more boilers share a single furnace or burner, allowing combined heat generation while using one combustion source. It relates to steam-generation plants that integrate multiple boiler units with a common combustion apparatus for efficiency and space saving.
[F22B 33/04] of boilers of furnace-tube type with boilers of water-tube type -- The subclass F22B 33/04 pertains to steam-generation plants that involve both furnace-tube (fire-tube) and water-tube boiler designs, covering configurations that combine or compare these two boiler types within a single system. It addresses methods and arrangements for integrating the distinct construction and operating principles of each boiler type to achieve specific performance or space-saving objectives.
[F22B 33/06] of boilers of furnace-tube type with boilers of fire-tube type -- F22B 33/06 covers inventions that convert, adapt or integrate furnace-tube boilers with fire-tube boilers, or that combine features of both types in a single steam-generation plant. It includes methods, arrangements or modifications that allow a furnace-tube system to operate as or be transformed into a fire-tube configuration.
[F22B 33/08] of boilers of water-tube type with boilers of fire-tube type -- The subclass covers boilers that combine water-tube and fire-tube concepts, where a water-tube arrangement forms the primary heating zone and a fire-tube section is incorporated for additional heat exchange or pressure-raising functions, creating a hybrid system that exploits the advantages of both boiler types.
[F22B 33/10] of two or more superposed boilers with separate water volumes and operating with two or more separate water levels -- A subclass of steam-generation plants concerning installations that have two or more boilers stacked one above another, each boiler possessing its own water-filled volume and maintaining independent water levels during operation. This arrangement allows separate control of water-level and pressure for each boiler, improving efficiency or capacity of the overall system.
[F22B 33/12] Self-contained steam boilers, i.e. comprising as a unit the steam boiler, the combustion apparatus, the fuel storage, accessory machines and equipment -- It covers boilers that incorporate all required components in a single unit, including the furnace, fuel tank and auxiliary equipment, enabling stand-alone operation. These self-contained steam-generation plants integrate the boiler, combustion apparatus, fuel storage and accessory machines into one compact package.
[F22B 33/14] Combinations of low- and high-pressure boilers -- F22B 33/14 covers arrangements that integrate low-pressure and high-pressure boilers within a steam-generation plant. The classification addresses combined cycles, feed-water heating, and staged steam production to improve efficiency or meet varying load demands.
[F22B 33/16] of forced-flow type -- The code F22B 33/16 pertains to steam-generation plants of the forced-flow type, in which water is mechanically circulated through the boiler to produce steam rather than relying on natural convection. It classifies technologies and methods for operating and designing such forced-flow steam generators.
[F22B 33/18] Combinations of steam boilers with other apparatus -- F22B 33/18 covers designs where a steam boiler is combined with other equipment such as turbines, heat exchangers, condensers or auxiliary units within a steam-generation plant. The classification addresses integrated arrangements that improve efficiency, compactness or operational control by connecting the boiler directly to the ancillary apparatus. It is used for patents describing combined boiler-system configurations.
[F22B 33/185] in combination with a steam accumulator -- F22B 33/185 covers steam-generation plants that incorporate a steam accumulator, a device that stores steam at a given pressure for later use. The classification applies to designs where the accumulator is integrated with the boiler or turbine system to improve load flexibility, efficiency or to provide instantaneous steam supply during demand peaks.
[F22B 35/00] Control systems for steam boilers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Control system of steam boilers in general, for example, control by flue gas dampers, control by recirculating flue gases, control systems for steam generators of nuclear power plants, control systems for instantaneous steam boilers, control systems for waste heat boilers, control systems for two or more steam generators, control systems for steam boilers with natural convection circulation and control systems for steam boilers of forced-flow type.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Controlling water feed or water level -> F22D5/00 - Automatic water-feed control for a number of steam boilers designed for different ranges of temperature and pressure -> F22D5/36 - Controlling superheat temperature -> F22G5/00 - Controlling superheat temperature by regulating flue gas flow -> F22G5/04 - Controlling superheat temperature by recirculating flue gases -> F22G5/06 - Controlling superheat temperature by attemperating the superheated steam -> F22G5/12 - Controlling superheat temperature by indirectly cooling or heating the superheated steam in auxiliary enclosed heat exchanger -> F22G5/16  **Glossary:** - once-through boiler: type of boiler in which water is input at one side, and steam is extracted from the other side of the flow path - forced-flow boiler: type of boiler in which a pump ensures flow - forced-once-through boiler: combination of a once-through and a forced-flow boiler (a pump ensures flow of a boiler in which water is input at one side, and steam is extracted from the other side of the flow path) - forced-circulation boiler: type of boiler in which recirculation is achieved by a circulation pump - natural-circulation boiler: type of boiler in which the circulation is achieved by the difference in density of the heated water in the boiler causing convection currents
[F22B 35/001] Controlling by flue-gas dampers  for superheaters  F22G5/04 -- F22B 35/001 covers control of steam-boiler superheaters by adjusting flue-gas dampers. It falls under the CPC hierarchy Mechanical Engineering → Steam Generation → Methods of Steam Generation → Control Systems for Steam Boilers, and details the use of dampers to regulate temperature and pressure in the superheating process.
[F22B 35/002] Control by recirculating flue gases  for superheaters  F22G5/06
[F22B 35/004] Control systems for steam generators of nuclear power plants -- This CPC entry concerns control systems for steam generators used in nuclear power plants, covering monitoring, regulation, safety and automation functions that manage boiler operation and steam output. It falls under the methods of steam generation subgroup of mechanical engineering.
[F22B 35/005] Control systems for instantaneous steam boilers -- F22B 35/005 covers control systems used in instantaneous steam boilers, which generate steam on demand without a storage tank. The classification includes devices and methods that regulate fuel, water feed and steam output to maintain pressure and temperature, ensuring safe and efficient boiler operation.
[F22B 35/007] Control systems for waste heat boilers -- F22B 35/007 covers control systems designed for waste-heat boilers that recover heat from exhaust gases or process streams to generate steam. The code includes sensors, actuators and feedback mechanisms that regulate boiler temperature, pressure and fuel feed based on the recovered heat. It is a subcategory of methods of steam generation in mechanical engineering.
[F22B 35/008] Control systems for two or more steam generators  automatic water-feed control for a number of steam boilers designed for different ranges of temperature and pressure  F22D5/36 -- F22B 35/008 covers control systems that manage two or more steam generators, providing automatic water-feed regulation for boilers operating at different temperature and pressure ranges. It addresses coordinated operation and safety of multiple boilers within a plant.
[F22B 35/02] for steam boilers with natural convection circulation -- The subclass F22B 35/02 covers control-system features specifically designed for steam boilers that operate by natural convection circulation, where steam and water move without mechanical pumps. It is part of the mechanical-engineering hierarchy for methods of steam generation.
[F22B 35/04] during starting-up periods, i.e. during the periods between the lighting of the furnaces and the attainment of the normal operating temperature of the steam boilers -- F22B 35/04 relates to control systems for steam boilers used specifically during the start-up phase, covering the interval from furnace ignition until the boiler reaches its normal operating temperature, and addressing methods to regulate heating, pressure and safety during this transient period.
[F22B 35/06] for steam boilers of forced-flow type -- F22B 35/06 designates control systems specifically for steam boilers that use forced flow of water or steam. It covers mechanisms and methods for regulating pressure, temperature, water level and fuel feed in such boilers to ensure stable and efficient operation.
[F22B 35/08] of forced-circulation type -- This subclass covers control systems for steam boilers that use a forced-circulation type, regulating the movement of water or steam to maintain desired pressure, temperature, and flow for efficient and safe steam generation.
[F22B 35/083] without drum, i.e. without hot water storage in the boiler -- The CPC subclass F22B 35/083 covers control systems applied to drum-less steam boilers, where the boiler operates without a hot-water storage drum. It pertains to methods of steam generation that regulate pressure, temperature, and fuel feed in such drum-free designs.
[F22B 35/086] operating at critical or supercritical pressure -- F22B 35/086 covers control and operating methods for steam boilers that work at or above the critical pressure of water, where the fluid no longer distinguishes between liquid and vapor. The classification includes systems that manage pressure, temperature and flow to maintain safe, efficient operation under supercritical conditions, often used in high-efficiency power plants.
[F22B 35/10] of once-through type -- This subclass covers control arrangements for once-through steam boilers, where water passes through the furnace only once and is converted directly into steam. It includes devices and methods that monitor and adjust feedwater flow, fuel input, pressure and temperature to maintain stable operation and efficiency.
[F22B 35/101] operating with superimposed recirculation during starting or low load periods, e.g. composite boilers  F22B35/125  takes precedence
[F22B 35/102] operating with fixed point of final state of complete evaporation, e.g. in a steam-water separator -- F22B 35/102 covers control of steam-boiler operation in which the final water state is completely evaporated at a fixed point, such as in a steam-water separator. It is a method of steam generation under the broader class of control systems for steam boilers in mechanical engineering.
[F22B 35/104] Control systems by injecting water  for superheaters  F22G5/12
[F22B 35/105] operating at sliding pressure -- The CPC subclass F22B 35/105 covers control techniques for steam boilers that adjust the operating pressure in proportion to the load, known as sliding-pressure operation. It includes methods and devices that continuously vary the pressure set-point to optimise efficiency and safety while the boiler runs under changing demand. This falls under mechanical engineering, steam generation, methods of steam generation, and specifically control systems for steam boilers.
[F22B 35/107] Control systems with auxiliary heating surfaces -- F22B 35/107 covers control systems for steam boilers that incorporate auxiliary heating surfaces to improve temperature regulation, fuel efficiency, or response time. These systems manage additional heating elements in the boiler to assist primary combustion, allowing finer control of steam generation.
[F22B 35/108] Control systems for steam generators having multiple flow paths -- The entry covers control apparatus and strategies for steam generators that employ more than one flow channel for water, steam or fuel. It includes sensing, regulation and distribution mechanisms that balance flow rates, pressure and temperature among the parallel paths to optimise performance and safety.
[F22B 35/12] operating at critical or supercritical pressure -- F22B 35/12 covers control systems for steam boilers that operate at critical or supercritical pressure, i.e., pressures above the critical point where water no longer undergoes a distinct liquid-vapor phase change. The classification pertains to methods of steam generation within mechanical engineering.
[F22B 35/125] operating with superimposed recirculation during starting or low load periods, e.g. composite boilers -- F22B 35/125 covers control strategies for steam boilers that introduce a supplemental recirculation flow when the plant is starting up or operating at low load, such as in composite boiler systems. The technique improves heat distribution and reduces thermal stresses by mixing water streams before the main combustion phase. It is applied primarily to steam-generation equipment in the mechanical-engineering field.
[F22B 35/14] during the starting-up periods, i.e. during the periods between the lighting of the furnaces and the attainment of the normal operating temperature of the steam boilers -- F22B 35/14 covers control devices and methods used while a steam boiler is being started up, that is, the interval from furnace ignition until the boiler reaches its normal operating temperature. It falls under mechanical engineering, steam generation and methods of steam generation, focusing on the regulation of boiler parameters during the transient heating phase.
[F22B 35/16] responsive to the percentage of steam in the mixture of steam and water -- F22B 35/16 covers control systems for steam boilers that adjust operation based on the proportion of steam to water in the boiler. It includes devices and methods that sense the steam-water mixture and modulate fuel, air, or water feed to maintain desired steam quality and safety.
[F22B 35/18] Applications of computers to steam-boiler control -- F22B 35/18 relates to the use of computers for controlling steam boilers, covering digital monitoring, regulation and automation of combustion, pressure and temperature. It addresses computer-based methods that improve the efficiency, safety and performance of steam-generation systems.
[F22B 37/00] Component parts or details of steam boilers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Component parts or details of steam boilers categorised in these groups:  This place, which is applicable to more than one kind of steam boiler and which could be applied also to nuclear steam boilers, steam boilers of pre-fabricated elements, instantaneous type boilers and forced-flow type boilers, covers:
[F22B 37/001] Steam generators built-up from pre-fabricated elements -- F22B 37/001 pertains to steam generators that are constructed from prefabricated elements, emphasizing modular assembly of boiler components. The subclass covers design, fabrication and integration of factory-made sections into complete steam-producing units for mechanical engineering applications.
[F22B 37/002] specially adapted for nuclear steam generators, e.g. maintenance, repairing or inspecting equipment not otherwise provided for -- The F22B 37/002 code covers components, tools and devices that are specifically adapted for nuclear steam-generator systems, such as equipment used for maintenance, repair or inspection that is not otherwise provided for in standard boiler designs. It falls within the broader class of steam-generation methods and component parts of steam boilers.
[F22B 37/003] Maintenance, repairing or inspecting equipment positioned in or via the headers -- This CPC subclass covers the maintenance, repair and inspection of equipment that is situated in or accessed through boiler headers, including valves, sensors and supporting components. It relates to component parts or details of steam boilers within methods of steam generation.
[F22B 37/005] Positioning apparatus specially adapted therefor  F22B37/64  takes precedence -- F22B 37/005 covers positioning devices specially adapted for steam-boiler components, such as supports, guides or alignment tools that assist installation or adjustment of boiler parts. These apparatus are tailored to the specific geometry and operating conditions of steam generation equipment.
[F22B 37/006] Walking equipment, e.g. walking platforms suspended at the tube sheet -- The F22B 37/006 subclass covers walking equipment, such as platforms or walkways that are suspended from the tube sheet of a steam boiler, providing safe access for inspection, cleaning or repair of boiler components. It is part of the hierarchy dealing with component parts or details of steam boilers within methods of steam generation.
[F22B 37/007] Installation or removal of nuclear steam generators -- The CPC subclass F22B 37/007 relates to the processes and equipment for installing or taking out nuclear steam generators in power plants, covering the mechanical handling, mounting, disassembly, and associated support structures. It falls under methods of steam generation and component parts of steam boilers within mechanical engineering.
[F22B 37/008] Adaptations for flue-gas purification in steam generators -- F22B 37/008 covers adaptations incorporated in steam generators to clean the flue gases produced during steam production. It includes devices such as scrubbers, filters, catalytic converters or other purification systems that are fitted to boiler components to reduce pollutants before the gases are released to the atmosphere.
[F22B 37/02] applicable to more than one kind or type of steam boiler -- F22B 37/02 classifies inventions that are relevant to more than one kind or type of steam boiler, covering features or components that are not confined to a single boiler design. It resides within the hierarchy of mechanical engineering, steam generation, methods of steam generation and component parts or details of steam boilers.
[F22B 37/025] Devices and methods for diminishing corrosion, e.g. by preventing cooling beneath the dew point -- F22B 37/025 covers devices and methods that reduce corrosion in steam-generation equipment, particularly by preventing the boiler surfaces from cooling below the moisture-dew point where condensate forms. It includes corrosion-inhibiting coatings, heating elements, and control systems that keep the metal temperature above the dew point to protect boiler components.
[F22B 37/04] and characterised by material, e.g. use of special steel alloy -- The subclass F22B 37/04 pertains to component parts or details of steam boilers that are defined by the material employed, for example the use of special steel alloys to achieve higher strength, corrosion resistance or heat-resistance in the boiler construction.
[F22B 37/06] Flue or fire tubes -- F22B 37/06 covers flue or fire tubes, the cylindrical passages within steam boilers that allow hot combustion gases to flow through water to produce steam. These tubes are a key component of boiler heat-exchange systems and may be straight, bent or have internal enhancements to improve heat transfer.
[F22B 37/08] Fittings preventing burning-off of the tube edges -- F22B 37/08 covers fittings that protect the ends of steam-boiler tubes from burning-off caused by high-temperature gases or flame impingement. These devices are installed on tube edges to prevent erosion, extend service life and maintain boiler efficiency, and are classified under component parts of steam boilers in the methods of steam generation sector of mechanical engineering.
[F22B 37/10] Water tubes -- The code F22B 37/10 designates water tubes, which are the tubular components through which water circulates inside a steam boiler. These tubes form part of the boiler's heat-exchange system, transferring heat from combustion gases to produce steam. This entry falls under mechanical engineering, steam generation, and methods of steam generation covering component parts of steam boilers.
[F22B 37/101] Tubes having fins or ribs -- The CPC subclass F22B 37/101 pertains to tubes in steam boilers that are equipped with external fins or ribs to enlarge the heat-transfer surface. These finned or ribbed tubes improve boiler efficiency by facilitating better heat exchange between the surrounding medium and the tube interior.
[F22B 37/102] Walls built-up from finned tubes -- F22B 37/102 covers steam boiler walls that are constructed by arranging finned tubes to form the boiler's fire-box or combustion chamber. The finned tubes provide increased surface area for heat transfer and can be welded or otherwise joined to create a built-up wall structure.
[F22B 37/103] Internally ribbed tubes -- The CPC subclass F22B 37/103 covers steam-boiler components, specifically tubes that have ribs formed on their inner surface. The internal ribbing enhances turbulence of the heating medium, improving heat transfer efficiency and resistance to fouling within the boiler's water-tube or fire-tube assemblies.
[F22B 37/104] Connection of tubes one with the other or with collectors, drums or distributors
[F22B 37/105] Penetrations of tubes through a wall and their sealing -- This classification covers designs and methods for passing tubes through a boiler wall and sealing the penetration to prevent leaks, addressing joints, gaskets, welding, and related components in steam generation equipment.
[F22B 37/106] Studding of tubes -- F22B 37/106 relates to the use of studs for securing tubes within steam-generation equipment, describing how studs connect tubes to the boiler shell or to adjacent tubes, improving structural integrity and facilitating assembly and servicing.
[F22B 37/107] Protection of water tubes -- F22B 37/107 relates to protective measures for water tubes in steam generators, covering designs and methods that prevent corrosion, erosion, fouling or overheating of tubes within boiler systems. It includes tube casings, coatings, water treatment and mechanical supports that enhance tube durability.
[F22B 37/108] Protection of water tube walls -- F22B 37/108 covers protective measures applied to the walls of water tubes in steam boilers, such as coatings, cladding or other treatments to prevent corrosion, erosion or scaling. These methods are used to enhance durability and performance of boiler water-tube components in steam generation systems.
[F22B 37/12] Forms of water tubes, e.g. of varying cross-section -- F22B 37/12 concerns water tubes for steam boilers that have a varying cross-section along their length, such as tapered or stepped forms. These tube shapes are designed to enhance heat transfer, fluid flow or accommodate structural constraints in steam-generation equipment.
[F22B 37/125] Bifurcates -- The classification F22B 37/125 covers bifurcating components in steam boilers, such as devices that divide the flow of steam or water into separate streams. It pertains to structural or functional elements that split fluid paths within the boiler system.
[F22B 37/14] Supply mains, e.g. rising mains, down-comers, in connection with water tubes -- F22B 37/14 covers the design and arrangement of supply mains, such as rising mains and down-comers, that feed water tubes in steam boilers. It addresses piping systems that deliver water to the boiler tubes and manage flow distribution within the boiler assembly.
[F22B 37/141] involving vertically-disposed water tubes, e.g. walls built-up from vertical tubes -- The subclass F22B 37/141 covers steam-generation boiler designs that use vertically-disposed water tubes, such as walls constructed from an array of vertical tubes. It relates to configurations where the tubes form the primary heat-transfer surface, allowing water to rise and steam to be produced within a vertically oriented tube bundle. This approach is used to improve heat transfer efficiency and boiler compactness.
[F22B 37/142] involving horizontally-or helically-disposed water tubes, e.g. walls built-up from horizontal or helical tubes -- F22B 37/142 relates to steam-boiler designs that employ water tubes positioned horizontally or in a helical pattern, for example walls built up from such tubes. It falls under methods of steam generation within the mechanical-engineering classification.
[F22B 37/143] Panel shaped heating surfaces built up from tubes  F22B37/145  takes precedence
[F22B 37/145] Flag-shaped panels built-up from tubes, e.g. from U-shaped tubes -- F22B 37/145 covers flag-shaped panels formed by assembling tubes, typically U-shaped, into a single structural element used in steam-generation equipment such as boiler walls or fire-tubes. The configuration provides a lightweight, high-strength surface that facilitates heat transfer and can be integrated into the boiler's internal or external structure.
[F22B 37/146] Tube arrangements for ash hoppers and grates and for combustion chambers of the cyclone or similar type out of the flues -- F22B 37/146 relates to the arrangement of tubes in ash hoppers, grates and combustion chambers of cyclone-type or similar flues, specifying how the tubes are positioned within these components of steam boiler systems. It is classified under mechanical engineering, steam generation, methods of steam generation, and component parts or details of steam boilers.
[F22B 37/147] Tube arrangements for cooling orifices, doors and burners -- F22B 37/147 covers the design of tube configurations that incorporate cooling orifices, access doors and burner fittings within steam-generation equipment. The subclass details how tube layouts are arranged to provide effective cooling and facilitate the installation or maintenance of burners and door assemblies in boiler components.
[F22B 37/148] Tube arrangements for the roofs -- The CPC entry F22B 37/148 covers the configuration and layout of tubes installed in the roof of a steam boiler. It addresses how these tubes are arranged to support the boiler structure, facilitate water and steam flow, and enhance heat transfer efficiency within the roof section.
[F22B 37/16] Return bends -- The CPC class F22B 37/16 covers return bends, which are curved sections of piping used in steam boiler systems to change the direction of steam flow and enable compact layout. These components are part of the boiler's internal circuit and relate to methods of steam generation in mechanical engineering.
[F22B 37/165] Closures for access openings in return bends -- The code F22B 37/165 covers devices that seal access openings cut into the return bends of steam-generation equipment. These closures allow inspection, cleaning or repair of the internal piping while preserving the boiler's pressure integrity and preventing steam leakage. They are typically removable plugs, covers or shutters designed to fit the curved geometry of the bend.
[F22B 37/18] Inserts, e.g. for receiving deposits from water -- Inserts for steam boilers are removable components installed inside the boiler to capture mineral or other deposits that form from the water supply. By providing a dedicated surface for buildup, they simplify cleaning, prolong boiler life and maintain efficient heat transfer. This element falls under the classification for component parts of steam generation systems.
[F22B 37/20] Supporting arrangements, e.g. for securing water-tube sets -- This subclass of the Cooperative Patent Classification covers supporting arrangements used to secure water-tube sets in steam boilers, such as brackets, stays, or other structural fixtures. It falls under the component-parts category of methods of steam generation within mechanical engineering.
[F22B 37/201] Suspension and securing arrangements for walls built-up from tubes -- F22B 37/201 relates to the suspension and securing arrangements for boiler walls constructed from tubes, covering brackets, ties, clamps and other supporting structures that hold the tube bundle in place and resist pressure and thermal loads.
[F22B 37/202] Suspension and securing arrangements for contact heating surfaces -- F22B 37/202 covers the devices and methods used to suspend and secure contact heating surfaces within steam boilers, such as tubes, plates, or coils that directly transfer heat to the working fluid. It includes brackets, clamps, stays, and other supporting structures that maintain position and allow for thermal expansion while preventing vibration or displacement.
[F22B 37/203] Horizontal tubes supported only away from their ends on vertical support tubes -- The classification covers boiler configurations where horizontal fire-tubes or water-tubes are held only at points away from their ends by vertical support tubes that intersect or brace them, allowing the tubes to span freely between supports. This arrangement is used to improve heat transfer and accommodate thermal expansion in steam-generation equipment.
[F22B 37/204] Supporting arrangements for individual tubes, e.g. for securing tubes to a refractory wall -- F22B 37/204 falls under mechanical engineering methods of steam generation and concerns component parts of steam boilers. It covers supporting arrangements for individual tubes, such as brackets, clamps or other devices that secure tubes to a refractory wall or similar structures.
[F22B 37/205] Supporting and spacing arrangements for tubes of a tube bundle -- F22B 37/205 covers designs that hold and space the tubes within a tube bundle of a steam boiler, ensuring proper alignment and flow while preventing vibration or damage. These arrangements include supports, spacers, and retaining structures that maintain tube spacing and stability during operation.
[F22B 37/206] Anti-vibration supports for the bends of U-tube steam generators -- F22B 37/206 covers anti-vibration supports designed for the curved sections of U-tube steam generators. These devices attach to the bends of the tubes to dampen oscillations and reduce mechanical stress, improving boiler durability and operational stability.
[F22B 37/207] Supporting arrangements for drums and collectors -- The code F22B 37/207 covers structural support arrangements for drums and collectors in steam-generation equipment such as boilers, including brackets, mounts, frames and similar devices that secure and align these components while withstanding pressure, temperature and vibration loads.
[F22B 37/208] Backstay arrangements -- F22B 37/208 covers backstay arrangements, the structural supports or tensioning systems that brace boiler components such as the firebox or pressure vessel against deformation. These arrangements are used in steam-generation equipment to maintain alignment and integrity under pressure, and are classified under component parts or details of steam boilers.
[F22B 37/22] Drums -- The code F22B 37/22 covers boiler drums, the large pressure vessels that store water and separate it from generated steam within a steam boiler. Drums provide a reservoir for water, collect the produced steam for distribution, and can be horizontal or vertical with internal fittings to aid circulation and control.
[F22B 37/221] Covers for drums, collectors, manholes or the like -- The CPC class F22B 37/221 concerns protective coverings for steam-boiler components such as drums, steam collectors, manholes and similar parts. These covers are designed to seal, protect and permit safe access to the equipment during operation, maintenance or inspection. This subclass falls under the hierarchy of mechanical engineering, steam generation, methods of steam generation, and component parts or details of steam boilers.
[F22B 37/222] Nozzle dams introduced through a smaller manway, e.g. foldable -- F22B 37/222 concerns nozzle dams that are inserted into a steam boiler via a reduced-size manway, often with a foldable or hinged arrangement to allow easy installation and removal while controlling flow to boiler nozzles. These devices are used to regulate or protect nozzle passages during operation or maintenance of steam generation equipment.
[F22B 37/223] Boiler plugs, e.g. for handholes -- Boiler plugs are removable fittings that seal handholes or other access openings in steam-generation equipment, allowing inspection, cleaning or servicing while maintaining pressure integrity. They are typically threaded or clamped and designed to withstand the high temperatures and pressures inside the boiler.
[F22B 37/225] Arrangements on drums or collectors for fixing tubes or for connecting collectors to each other -- The F22B 37/225 subclass deals with the design of drums or tube-collector structures in steam-generation equipment where tubes are held in place or separate collectors are joined. It includes various fixing devices such as brackets, clamps, welds or other securing arrangements that link tubes to the drum or interconnect multiple collectors.
[F22B 37/226] Protection of drums against combustion -- F22B 37/226 covers measures for safeguarding boiler drums from internal combustion, including fire-resistant linings, cooling systems, and protective barriers that prevent flame propagation within the drum. The classification addresses design features and materials used to maintain drum integrity and safe operation during steam generation.
[F22B 37/227] Drums and collectors for mixing -- The classification F22B 37/227 covers drums and collector devices installed in steam boilers to mix water, steam or condensate, promoting uniform temperature and preventing stratification. It includes mixing drums, internal baffles, water-collection chambers and similar components that facilitate circulation and blending of boiler fluids.
[F22B 37/228] Headers for distributing feedwater into steam generator vessels; Accessories therefor -- The classification covers components that channel feedwater into the various sections of a steam boiler, including the main header pipes and any attached devices that regulate or support the distribution. It includes designs for the distribution manifolds and auxiliary fittings that ensure even water flow to the boiler's tubes and drums.
[F22B 37/24] Supporting, suspending or setting arrangements, e.g. heat shielding -- F22B 37/24 relates to the supporting, suspending or setting structures used in steam-boiler components, including devices such as heat-shielding arrangements that hold, protect or position parts within the boiler system. It falls under mechanical engineering, steam generation, and methods of steam generation.
[F22B 37/242] for bottom supported water-tube steam generators -- This CPC subclass (F22B 37/242) covers bottom-supported water-tube steam generators, a type of component in steam boilers where the water-filled tubes are anchored at the lower end. It relates to designs and arrangements specific to such generators used in steam production.
[F22B 37/244] for water-tube steam generators suspended from the top -- F22B 37/244 covers water-tube steam generators whose boiler units are suspended from the top of the supporting structure. This arrangement lifts the tubes away from the furnace and floor, facilitating access, reducing vibration and thermal stresses, and allowing easier cleaning and inspection.
[F22B 37/246] for steam generators of the reservoir type, e.g. nuclear steam generators -- F22B 37/246 covers the design and operation of reservoir-type steam generators, such as those used in nuclear power plants, focusing on the arrangement of tubes, heat-exchange surfaces and associated support structures within a closed water reservoir that produces steam.
[F22B 37/248] with a vertical cylindrical wall -- F22B 37/248 relates to component parts or details of steam boilers that feature a vertical cylindrical wall, such as the outer shell or inner fire-tube lining, used in various methods of steam generation. It covers the design, construction, and integration of this cylindrical pressure vessel element within the boiler system.
[F22B 37/26] Steam-separating arrangements -- F22B 37/26 covers the parts and arrangements used to separate water droplets from steam within boilers, such as steam separators, cyclone separators, and internal baffles that improve dryness of the produced steam. The classification applies to designs that promote efficient steam generation by removing moisture, thereby enhancing turbine performance and reducing corrosion in downstream equipment.
[F22B 37/261] specially adapted for boiler drums -- F22B 37/261 refers to components of steam-generation equipment that are specially adapted for boiler drums, such as reinforced connections, supports or fittings designed to accommodate drum stresses, facilitate water-steam separation and integrate the drum within the boiler structure.
