[F24C 15/34] Elements and arrangements for heat storage or insulation -- F24C 15/34 relates to the parts and configurations that enable heat to be stored or insulated in domestic stoves and ranges, covering thermal-mass elements, heat-retaining compartments, insulation layers and associated arrangements that improve energy efficiency and cooking performance.
[F24C 15/36] Protective guards, e.g. for preventing access to heated parts -- F24C 15/36 refers to protective guards used on domestic stoves or ranges to prevent accidental contact with heated components. It includes barriers, shields or enclosures that cover burners, heating elements or oven interiors, typically made of metal or heat-resistant material, and is intended to enhance user safety by restricting access to hot surfaces.
[F24C 3/00] Stoves or ranges for gaseous fuels -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Stoves or ranges comprising a gas burner.  Details of such stoves or ranges, e.g. the arrangement or mounting of the burners, the arrangement or mounting of ignition devices and control or safety devices used on these devices.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Stoves or ranges specially adapted for the use of two or more kinds of fuel or energy supply -> F24C1/02
[F24C 3/002] Stoves -- F24C 3/002 covers stoves that operate on gaseous fuels, encompassing both freestanding and built-in units used for domestic heating and cooking. The classification addresses design features, fuel delivery systems and safety mechanisms specific to gas-powered appliances.
[F24C 3/004] of the closed type -- F24C 3/004 covers closed-type domestic stoves or ranges that burn gaseous fuels. In a closed type the fire chamber is sealed, limiting direct flame exposure and often using burners with a protective enclosure. These appliances are designed for household cooking and heating.
[F24C 3/006] simulating flames -- The code F24C 3/006 covers features that recreate the appearance of a flame on gas-fuel domestic stoves or ranges, such as visual flame-simulation devices, lighting arrangements or control systems that generate a flame-like effect for aesthetic or user-feedback purposes. This subclass is part of the heating category for mechanical engineering and concerns appliances that use gaseous fuels.
[F24C 3/008] Ranges -- F24C 3/008 refers to ranges-cooking appliances that incorporate a stovetop or oven-and specifically covers those designed to operate on gaseous fuels. The subclass falls under mechanical engineering, heating, and domestic stoves or ranges, and it groups inventions related to the construction, control, and safety features of gas-powered kitchen ranges.
[F24C 3/02] with heat produced solely by flame -- The subclass F24C 3/02 covers domestic stoves or ranges that use gaseous fuel and obtain heat solely from the flame, without any auxiliary heating elements or electric assistance. It falls under mechanical engineering → heating → domestic stoves or ranges → stoves or ranges for gaseous fuels.
[F24C 3/022] Stoves -- F24C 3/022 covers domestic cooking appliances that use gaseous fuel, specifically stoves designed for household use. The classification includes burners, control mechanisms and related components for gas-powered ranges.
[F24C 3/025] of the closed type -- F24C 3/025 classifies closed-type domestic stoves or ranges that burn gaseous fuels, where the combustion chamber is sealed from the cooking surface, typically using a pan or enclosure to contain the flame and gases. The entry covers design features, heat distribution and safety aspects specific to these gas-fired appliances.
[F24C 3/027] Ranges -- F24C 3/027 covers domestic cooking appliances that are ranges using gas as the fuel source. It relates to the design, construction and operation of gas-fired stoves and ovens for household heating and food preparation.
[F24C 3/04] with heat produced wholly or partly by a radiant body, e.g. by a perforated plate
[F24C 3/042] Stoves -- F24C 3/042 covers domestic stoves or ranges that use gaseous fuels such as natural gas or propane for cooking or heating. The classification addresses the design of burners, combustion chambers, and safety or control features specific to gas-fuel appliances.
[F24C 3/045] of the closed type -- The F24C 3/045 subclass covers closed-type domestic stoves or ranges that use gaseous fuels, where the combustion chamber is sealed from the surrounding environment. Such appliances contain an enclosed burner or heating element, often with a glass-ceramic top or a door, to contain flames, improve safety and enhance heat control. This design differentiates them from open-fire or open-burner gas cooking units.
[F24C 3/047] Ranges -- This CPC subclass (F24C 3/047) covers domestic kitchen ranges that use gaseous fuels such as natural gas or propane for cooking. It includes appliances that combine a cooking surface and an oven, focusing on the arrangement of burners, flame distribution, and control mechanisms for gas-fired ranges.
[F24C 3/06] without any visible flame -- F24C 3/06 concerns domestic gas stoves or ranges that heat food without a visible flame, using flameless burners such as infrared or catalytic combustion. The classification applies to appliances that generate heat by radiant or convective means while masking the flame from the user.
[F24C 3/062] stoves -- F24C 3/062 covers domestic stoves and ranges that are powered by gaseous fuels such as natural gas or propane. It includes the design, construction, and operation of burners, control systems, and safety features specific to gas-fuel cooking appliances for household use.
[F24C 3/065] of the closed type -- This CPC subclass covers domestic gas stoves or ranges that are of the closed type, meaning the combustion chamber is sealed from the cooking area, typically using a burner and heat exchanger to transfer heat while preventing direct flame contact with food.
[F24C 3/067] Ranges -- The F24C 3/067 entry classifies domestic cooking ranges that operate on gaseous fuels, covering the overall design, burner arrangements, heat-distribution systems and safety features specific to gas-powered stoves and ranges.
[F24C 3/08] Arrangement or mounting of burners -- F24C 3/08 covers the way burners are positioned and secured in domestic gas stoves or ranges, including the mounting brackets, support structures and the spatial relationship of the burners to the oven cavity or cooking surface.
[F24C 3/082] on stoves -- F24C 3/082 addresses technical aspects of domestic stoves or ranges that use gaseous fuels, focusing on ignition systems, flame-sensing or safety shut-off devices, and the control of fuel and air flow to enhance operation and protection.
[F24C 3/085] on ranges -- This CPC subclass covers ranges designed for use with gaseous fuels, including burners, ignition systems, flame control and safety features specific to gas-powered cooking appliances. It is placed under Mechanical Engineering, Heating, Domestic Stoves or Ranges.
[F24C 3/087] in baking ovens -- F24C 3/087 classifies the technical features of baking ovens that are part of stoves or ranges using gaseous fuels, within the heating segment of mechanical engineering. It encompasses designs, components and operating methods specifically intended for oven functions such as temperature control, heat distribution and baking-specific construction.
[F24C 3/10] Arrangement or mounting of ignition devices -- This subclass covers the specific ways ignition devices are positioned, supported, or attached within gas-fuel domestic stoves or ranges. It includes configurations of spark plugs, burners, pilot lights, and related mounting structures to ensure reliable lighting and safe operation.
[F24C 3/103] of electric ignition devices -- This subclass covers electric ignition devices used in domestic stoves or ranges that operate on gaseous fuels. It includes spark-producing or heating-element systems designed to ignite the gas supply in household cooking appliances. The classification groups such electric ignition technologies within the broader mechanical-engineering heating category.
[F24C 3/106] of flash tubes -- F24C 3/106 covers the incorporation of flash tubes, such as spark-igniters, in domestic stoves or ranges that operate on gaseous fuels. It relates to the ignition system that generates a brief light-flash to initiate combustion, improving safety and reliability of the heating appliance.
[F24C 3/12] Arrangement or mounting of control or safety devices -- F24C 3/12 covers the way control or safety devices are arranged or mounted on domestic gas-fuel stoves or ranges, including the positioning of thermostats, flame-sensors, shut-off valves and related safety mechanisms within the appliance. It is a sub-category of mechanical engineering heating for household cooking equipment.
[F24C 3/122] on stoves -- F24C 3/122 relates to technical features of domestic stoves or ranges that operate on gaseous fuels, covering design elements such as burners, ignition systems, flame control, safety devices and arrangement of components that affect cooking performance or user safety. The subclass addresses improvements and specific implementations within gas-fired cooking appliances.
[F24C 3/124] Control panels -- F24C 3/124 designates control panels for domestic stoves or ranges that run on gaseous fuels, covering the layout, switches, knobs, electronic displays and safety interlocks used to regulate cooking functions. The classification applies to both mechanical and electronic interfaces that enable users to adjust temperature, flame level, timer settings and other operational parameters.
[F24C 3/126] on ranges -- F24C 3/126 relates to domestic cooking ranges that operate on gaseous fuels. It covers the design and construction of stovetop burners, ovens and controls specific to gas-powered units, including the arrangement of burners, flame regulation mechanisms and safety features intended for household use. This subclass is part of the broader categories of mechanical engineering, heating, and domestic stoves or ranges.
[F24C 3/128] in baking ovens -- F24C 3/128 covers aspects of baking ovens that are part of domestic stoves or ranges using gaseous fuel. The code pertains to design, heating control, or other technical features specifically intended for baking operations within such appliances.
[F24C 3/14] with special adaptation for travelling, e.g. collapsible -- This subclass covers gas-powered stoves or ranges that are specially adapted for travel, such as models that can be collapsed, folded or otherwise disassembled for easy transport. The devices are intended for domestic heating or cooking using gaseous fuels while offering compact, portable configurations.
[F24C 5/00] Stoves or ranges for liquid fuels -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Stoves and ranges using liquid fuel.  Details of such stoves or ranges, e.g. the arrangement or mounting of the burners, the arrangement or mounting of ignition devices and control or safety devices used on these devices.  Liquid-fuel supply arrangements.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Stoves or ranges specially adapted for the use of two or more kinds of fuel or energy supply -> F24C1/02
[F24C 5/02] with evaporation burners, e.g. dish type -- F24C 5/02 pertains to domestic stoves or ranges that operate on liquid fuels and incorporate evaporation burners, often called dish-type burners, where the fuel is vaporized before combustion to deliver rapid, efficient heating for cooking.
[F24C 5/04] wick type -- F24C 5/04 covers domestic stoves or ranges that use a wick-type burner to vaporise liquid fuels such as kerosene or alcohol for heating and cooking. The classification applies to designs where a wick draws fuel to the flame and the appliance is intended for household use.
[F24C 5/06] adjustable -- F24C 5/06 covers domestic stoves or ranges that use liquid fuels and include adjustable elements such as variable flame regulators, movable burners or changeable cooking zones, allowing the user to modify heat output or position. The classification pertains to mechanical engineering solutions for heating appliances where the adjustment capability is a key functional feature.
[F24C 5/08] with heat produced wholly or partly by a radiant body -- F24C 5/08 covers domestic stoves or ranges that use liquid fuels and generate heat either entirely or in part from a radiant body, such as a glowing burner or heated plate, rather than solely from convection or direct flame.
[F24C 5/10] with atomising burners -- This subclass covers domestic stoves or ranges that use liquid fuels and employ atomising burners, which break the fuel into a fine spray to improve flame stability, combustion efficiency and heat distribution. It falls under mechanical engineering heating, domestic stoves or ranges.
[F24C 5/12] Arrangement or mounting of burners -- F24C 5/12 covers the specific ways burners are arranged or mounted in liquid-fuel stoves or ranges, including positioning, support structures, and connection to the combustion chamber. It addresses design features that affect flame distribution, safety and ease of maintenance.
[F24C 5/14] Arrangement or mounting of ignition devices -- F24C 5/14 deals with how ignition devices are arranged or mounted in domestic stoves or ranges that burn liquid fuels. It addresses the positioning, securing and integration of spark- or flame-producing components within these heating appliances.
[F24C 5/16] Arrangement or mounting of control or safety devices -- F24C 5/16 covers the way control or safety components-such as thermostats, flame-sensing devices, or shut-off valves-are arranged or mounted within liquid-fuel domestic stoves and ranges. It pertains to the mechanical-engineering field of heating equipment, specifically the design of the internal layout that ensures safe and reliable operation.
[F24C 5/18] Liquid-fuel supply arrangements forming parts of stoves or ranges -- F24C 5/18 covers the design and integration of liquid-fuel supply systems that are built into domestic stoves or ranges. It includes fuel lines, regulators, pumps and related control devices that convey liquid fuel to burners, ensuring safe and efficient operation of the appliance.
[F24C 5/20] with special adaptation for travelling, e.g. collapsible -- This subclass covers portable liquid-fuel stoves or ranges designed for travel, featuring collapsible or otherwise compactable structures that can be folded, disassembled, or otherwise reduced in size for transport while retaining functional heating capability.
[F24C 7/00] Stoves or ranges heated by electric energy -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Stoves and ranges using electrical energy.
