The design enables efficient exchange of thermal energy captured from sunlight to a different medium for use in heating or power applications.
[F24S 10/40] in absorbing elements surrounded by transparent enclosures, e.g. evacuated solar collectors -- The code F24S 10/40 designates solar heat collectors that incorporate absorbing elements placed inside transparent enclosures, such as evacuated tubes, to minimise heat loss. The configuration allows a working fluid to receive solar energy within the sealed envelope, improving efficiency.
[F24S 10/45] the enclosure being cylindrical -- This subgroup refers to solar thermal collectors that employ a working fluid and are housed in a cylindrical enclosure. It includes designs where the absorber, reflector or tube assembly is formed as a round tube or pipe, typical of evacuated tube collectors or cylindrical flat-plate modules. The classification helps differentiate cylindrical-enclosed collector designs from flat or rectangular housings.
[F24S 10/50] the working fluids being conveyed between plates -- F24S 10/50 designates solar heat collectors in which the working fluid is conveyed between plates, typical of flat-plate or evacuated-tube devices where the fluid flows in channels formed by adjacent plates to absorb solar energy.
[F24S 10/501] having conduits of plastic material -- This subclass pertains to solar heat collectors that circulate working fluids through conduits made of plastic material. Using plastic pipes lowers weight and corrosion risk compared with metal and can be applied to various flat-plate or evacuated-tube designs.
[F24S 10/502] having conduits formed by paired plates and internal partition means -- The subclass covers solar heat collectors that employ a working fluid and use conduits created by two plates positioned opposite each other, with an internal partition separating the flow channels. This arrangement enables efficient heat transfer while keeping the collector structure simple and compact.
[F24S 10/503] having conduits formed by paired plates, only one of which is plane -- A solar collector in which the fluid passages are formed by two closely spaced plates, one flat and the other shaped, creating a conduit for the working fluid. This arrangement is a specific sub-category of fluid-filled solar heat collectors.
[F24S 10/504] having conduits formed by paired non-plane plates -- The subclass F24S 10/504 covers solar heat collectors that circulate a working fluid through channels whose walls consist of two non-planar plates positioned opposite each other, forming a conduit for fluid flow. This design is used to enhance heat transfer in solar collectors within the broader mechanical-engineering heating category.
[F24S 10/505] having curved plate-like conduits, e.g. semi-spherical -- This subclass of the Cooperative Patent Classification covers solar heat collectors that use a working fluid and incorporate curved plate-like conduits, such as semi-spherical shapes, to guide the fluid flow and enhance heat transfer.
[F24S 10/506] having conduits formed by inflation of portions of a pair of joined sheets -- The classification F24S 10/506 covers solar heat collectors that create fluid-carrying conduits by inflating selected regions of two sheets that have been joined together, forming internal passageways for the working fluid without the need for separate tubes. This method integrates the channels directly into the collector's sheet structure.
[F24S 10/55] with enlarged surfaces, e.g. with protrusions or corrugations -- This subclass covers solar heat collectors that use a working fluid and incorporate enlarged surfaces such as protrusions, ribs or corrugated plates to increase the absorber area and improve heat-transfer efficiency.
[F24S 10/60] the working fluids trickling freely over absorbing elements -- This subclass covers solar heat collector designs where the heat-transfer fluid flows as a free-falling film over the absorber surface. The fluid is allowed to trickle without pumps or forced circulation, enhancing heat absorption in flat-plate or evacuated-tube collectors.
[F24S 10/70] the working fluids being conveyed through tubular absorbing conduits -- The code F24S 10/70 covers solar heat collectors in which the working fluid is carried inside tubular absorbing conduits. It pertains to designs where fluid flows through enclosed tubes that serve both as collectors of solar radiation and as the medium for heat transfer.
[F24S 10/72] the tubular conduits being integrated in a block; the tubular conduits touching each other -- It refers to solar collectors in which the heat-transfer tubes are formed as an integral block, with adjacent tubes in direct contact to enhance conduction and structural strength, and used with a working fluid as the heat-transfer medium.
[F24S 10/73] the tubular conduits being of plastic material -- The code F24S 10/73 pertains to solar heat collectors that employ a working fluid circulated through tubular conduits made of plastic material. It addresses the design and construction of these plastic tubes forming the absorber circuit in solar thermal systems.
[F24S 10/74] the tubular conduits are not fixed to heat absorbing plates and are not touching each other -- This subclass covers solar heat collectors that use fluid-filled tubes which are mounted without being attached to the heat-absorbing plates and are spaced so they do not touch each other. The design allows the tubes to move independently, improving thermal expansion handling and reducing stress on the collector structure.
[F24S 10/742] the conduits being parallel to each other -- The F24S 10/742 subgroup defines solar heat collectors that employ working-fluid conduits arranged parallel to one another, a layout common in flat-plate and evacuated-tube designs to ensure even flow distribution and efficient heat transfer.
[F24S 10/744] the conduits being helically coiled -- Helically coiled conduits are tube sections wound in a spiral shape and installed within fluid-based solar heat collectors. The coil geometry creates turbulent flow and a larger heat-transfer surface, which raises thermal efficiency while helping to keep pressure losses low.
[F24S 10/746] the conduits being spirally coiled -- This subclass covers solar-thermal collectors that use working fluids routed through conduits formed as helically coiled tubes or pipes. The spiral configuration improves heat-transfer efficiency and may reduce pressure losses, defining a specific design feature of fluid channels within solar heat-collector systems.
[F24S 10/748] the conduits being otherwise bent, e.g. zig-zag -- This subgroup covers solar heat collector systems that employ a working fluid circulating through heat-exchange tubes that are intentionally bent or shaped in a zig-zag configuration to enhance heat transfer and fluid flow. It falls under mechanical engineering, heating, solar heat collectors.
[F24S 10/75] with enlarged surfaces, e.g. with protrusions or corrugations -- The subclass F24S 10/75 pertains to solar heat collectors that employ a working fluid and feature enlarged surfaces such as protrusions or corrugations to increase the effective heat-transfer area and improve collector efficiency.
[F24S 10/753] the conduits being parallel to each other -- F24S 10/753 covers solar heat collector systems that use a working fluid and feature a series of parallel conduits for heat transfer. The arrangement ensures uniform fluid flow and efficient absorption of solar energy within the collector. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering, heating, and solar heat collectors using working fluids.
[F24S 10/754] the conduits being spirally coiled -- The classification F24S 10/754 refers to solar heat collectors that employ a working fluid and specifically addresses conduits that are spirally coiled, describing the coil-shaped tubes or pipes through which the heat-transfer fluid circulates within the collector.
[F24S 10/755] the conduits being otherwise bent, e.g. zig-zag -- This CPC subclass covers solar thermal collectors that circulate a working fluid through heat-exchange conduits that are purposely bent, such as in a zig-zag configuration, to promote turbulence and improve heat transfer. It resides in the hierarchy mechanical engineering → heating → solar heat collectors → solar heat collectors using working fluids.
[F24S 10/80] comprising porous material or permeable masses directly contacting the working fluids -- The F24S 10/80 subclass defines solar heat collectors that integrate a porous or permeable material in direct contact with the working fluid, allowing the fluid to flow through the structure and exchange heat efficiently. This design enables enhanced thermal transfer within collectors that rely on fluid circulation to capture solar energy.
[F24S 10/90] using internal thermosiphonic circulation -- The subclass F24S 10/90 covers solar heat collectors that employ a working fluid circulating through the collector by internal thermosiphonic action, i.e., natural convection driven by temperature-induced density differences, without external pumps.
[F24S 10/95] having evaporator sections and condenser sections, e.g. heat pipes -- The subclass F24S 10/95 covers solar heat collectors that use a working fluid contained in a closed circuit with distinct evaporator and condenser sections, as employed in heat-pipe configurations. It falls under mechanical engineering → heating → solar heat collectors → solar heat collectors using working fluids.
[F24S 20/00] Solar heat collectors specially adapted for particular uses or environments -- F24S 20/00 covers solar thermal collectors that are specifically designed or modified for particular applications or environmental conditions, such as building-integrated panels, water-heating systems, industrial process heaters, or units optimized for cold or humid climates. The classification addresses adaptations that improve performance, durability or integration in these targeted settings.
[F24S 20/02] for swimming pools -- Solar heat collectors adapted for swimming pools are systems that capture solar radiation to heat pool water, often using flat-plate or evacuated-tube panels integrated with circulation pumps and control units to maintain comfortable temperatures.
[F24S 20/04] for showers -- F24S 20/04 relates to solar heat collectors that are specially adapted for use in showers, providing water heating directly from solar energy. It includes systems that integrate collectors with shower installations or supply hot water for shower purposes.
[F24S 2010/71] the conduits having a non-circular cross-section -- F24S 2010/71 covers heat-exchange conduits whose cross-section is not circular, such as rectangular, elliptical, triangular or other polygonal shapes. It includes design features, manufacturing methods and applications where the non-circular geometry improves fluid flow, heat transfer or structural integration.
[F24S 2010/751] Special fins -- The CPC subclass F24S 2010/751 covers specially shaped or arranged fins used in heat-exchange devices such as radiators, evaporators and condensers, including novel geometries, mounting methods and manufacturing techniques that enhance heat transfer or fluid flow. It is applied to inventions that modify standard fin designs for improved performance.
[F24S 2010/752] extending obliquely -- F24S 2010/752 covers shafts, axles or similar members that are prolonged at an angle rather than straight, providing an inclined or oblique extension to connect with other components or to alter the direction of motion. The entry addresses the geometry, mounting and functional aspects of such angled extensions.
[F24S 20/20] Solar heat collectors for receiving concentrated solar energy, e.g. receivers for solar power plants -- Solar heat collectors designed to capture highly concentrated solar energy, typically serving as receivers in solar-thermal power plants. They are optimized for intense irradiation, converting it efficiently into heat for electricity generation or industrial processes. This subclass covers devices such as tower, dish-or parabolic-cylinder receivers that operate under concentrated sunlight.
[F24S 2020/10] Solar modules layout; Modular arrangements -- F24S 2020/10 covers the physical design and arrangement of photovoltaic modules, including mounting structures, inter-module connections, and modular configurations that enable scalable or flexible solar arrays. It addresses layouts that optimize space, installation speed, and integration with building or ground-based installations.
[F24S 2020/11] in the form of multiple rows and multiple columns, all solar modules being coplanar -- The code refers to solar-energy installations where the photovoltaic modules are arranged in a grid of several rows and columns, with all panels positioned in the same plane. This layout is designed to create a coplanar array that maximizes uniform exposure and simplifies mounting and wiring.
[F24S 2020/12] Coplanar arrangements with frame overlapping portions -- F24S 2020/12 relates to coplanar arrangements where a frame overlaps parts of a component, typically used in heating, ventilation, or air-conditioning units. The classification covers designs in which the frame and the functional element lie in the same plane and share overlapping sections, facilitating compact integration.
[F24S 2020/13] Overlaying arrangements similar to roof tiles -- F24S 2020/13 covers overlaying arrangements that mimic the interlocking pattern of roof tiles, used to protect, seal or aesthetically finish surfaces such as ducts, channels or other structural elements in heating, ventilation or air-conditioning systems. The classification includes designs where individual tiles or plates are stacked or staggered to create a continuous protective layer.
[F24S 2020/14] Stepped arrangements, e.g. in parallel planes, without module overlapping -- F24S 2020/14 concerns heat-exchanger designs that incorporate stepped configurations where the individual modules or plates are arranged in parallel planes but are offset so that they do not overlap each other. This layout aims to improve fluid distribution, increase surface area, and facilitate cleaning or maintenance while maintaining a compact overall geometry.
[F24S 2020/15] Non-parallel arrangements -- F24S 2020/15 covers air-conditioning, heating or ventilating systems in which the functional units are arranged in a non-parallel configuration, such as series-connected or otherwise staggered indoor and outdoor components. It includes designs where refrigerant, airflow or control circuits do not run side-by-side but follow a sequential or irregular layout. This subgroup distinguishes such arrangements from parallel-type installations within the broader F24S class.
[F24S 2020/16] Preventing shading effects -- This CPC subgroup falls under F24S which covers solar heat collectors. The 2020/16 sub-entry relates to measures that avoid or reduce shading of the collector's surface, such as tracking systems, reflectors or design features that keep obstructions out of the view of the sun.
[F24S 2020/17] Arrangements of solar thermal modules combined with solar PV modules -- This CPC subclass covers inventions where solar-thermal collectors are integrated with photovoltaic panels in a single arrangement, describing configurations that enable simultaneous generation of heat and electricity, including mounting systems, shared structures, and combined energy management.
[F24S 2020/18] having a particular shape, e.g. prismatic, pyramidal -- F24S 2020/18 classifies patents that specify a heating or heat-exchange element by its geometric form, such as prismatic or pyramidal shapes, where the shape itself contributes to the device's thermal performance or structural function. This feature is treated as a distinct technical characteristic for indexing inventions in heating, ventilation or related fields.
[F24S 2020/183] in the form of louvers -- F24S covers devices for regulating the flow of air, gases or liquids, especially in heating, ventilation and air-conditioning systems. The sub-category 2020/183 specifies arrangements that use louvers - angled slats that can be opened or closed to control passage - as the means of flow control.
[F24S 2020/186] allowing change of position for optimization of heat collection -- F24S 2020/186 covers inventions that adjust the position or orientation of a heat-collection apparatus to improve its efficiency. It includes mechanisms such as tracking, pivoting, or re-configurable mounting that enable the collector to follow the sun or respond to environmental conditions, thereby optimizing thermal gain. This subclass also encompasses control methods that dictate when and how such positional changes occur.
[F24S 2020/23] movable or adjustable -- The CPC class F24S covers devices for controlling the distribution of air in heating, ventilation and air-conditioning systems; subclass 2020/23 includes elements that are movable or adjustable such as adjustable louvers, pivotable air outlets, repositionable ducts or other components that can be shifted to modify airflow direction or volume.
[F24S 2023/83] Other shapes -- F24S 2023/83 covers heating devices or electric heaters whose heating elements are made in shapes that are not covered by other CPC subclasses. It includes radiators, panels or other heat-generating components with unconventional, irregular or custom-designed geometries. The code is used for patents describing such non-standard shaped heating elements.
[F24S 2023/831] corrugated -- F24S 2023/831 classifies corrugated elements used in heating, ventilation and air-conditioning installations, such as flexible or rigid corrugated ducts, trays, pipes and panels for conveying air or fluids. The entry covers the design, manufacture and integration of these corrugated components in HVAC systems.
[F24S 2023/832] curved -- F24S 2023/832 is a CPC subgroup within the solar heating and cooling field that deals with curved components such as curved solar collectors, absorber plates or reflective surfaces. It covers designs where curvature is used to improve solar energy capture, concentration or integration into building elements.
[F24S 2023/833] dish-shaped -- The CPC code F24S 2023/833 designates inventions concerning dish-shaped elements in heating, cooking or ventilation apparatus, covering the geometry, manufacture and functional aspects of shallow, concave components that influence heat transfer, fluid flow or mounting within the broader F24S class of heating and climate-control devices.
[F24S 2023/834] trough-shaped -- F24S 2023/834 designates inventions featuring a trough-shaped configuration, typically used in heating, ventilation, air-conditioning or wind-energy equipment to guide or collect airflow or fluids. The classification covers ducts, channels or other components whose cross-section is concave, forming a trough to improve distribution, drainage or aerodynamic performance.
[F24S 2023/835] asymmetric -- F24S 2023/835 covers duct or air-distribution elements that have an asymmetric shape or construction, intended to influence airflow, pressure or noise characteristics compared to symmetric counterparts. The classification is used for inventions such as unevenly sized outlets, offset bends or variable cross-sections designed to optimise HVAC performance.
[F24S 2023/836] spiral -- The CPC entry F24S 2023/836 spiral designates patents concerning spiral-type heat exchangers, including their structure, production and application in heating, cooling or heat-pump systems. It falls within the F24S group for heat exchangers and related devices.
[F24S 2023/837] hyperbolic -- The CPC code F24S 2023/837 covers heat exchangers that employ hyperbolic geometry in their design, such as channels or surfaces shaped like hyperbolas to improve fluid dynamics and thermal efficiency. It is part of the 2023 revision of the Cooperative Patent Classification for heating and cooling equipment.
[F24S 2023/838] involutes -- F24S 2023/838 classifies steam generators or boilers that employ involute-shaped heating surfaces, such as coiled tubes arranged in an involute geometry to enhance heat transfer and fluid flow within the device. It covers designs where the involute form is integral to the generator's operation.
[F24S 2023/84] Reflective elements inside solar collector casings -- The CPC subclass F24S 2023/84 pertains to solar-thermal collectors whose casings incorporate reflective elements such as mirrors, reflectors or shiny surfaces positioned inside the enclosure to direct additional radiation onto the absorber and thereby increase heat collection efficiency.
[F24S 2023/85] Micro-reflectors -- The CPC subclass F24S 2023/85 pertains to micro-reflectors employed in heating, ventilation and air-conditioning systems to redirect or concentrate radiant energy. It includes tiny reflective elements integrated into light fixtures, solar collectors or thermal panels, together with related manufacturing methods, mounting structures and coating technologies for compact reflective devices.
[F24S 2023/86] in the form of reflective coatings -- F24S 2023/86 covers solar-heating technologies that employ reflective coatings, such as thin-film mirrors or metallic layers applied to surfaces to concentrate or redirect solar radiation for heating purposes. This includes the composition, application methods and integration of such coatings in solar collectors or related systems.
[F24S 2023/87] Reflectors layout -- F24S 2023/87 covers the design and arrangement of reflectors within lighting apparatuses, addressing the geometry, positioning and combination of reflector elements to shape and direct emitted light. It includes configurations for lamps, luminaires or related optical systems where the reflector layout influences illumination patterns, efficiency or visual effects.
[F24S 2023/872] Assemblies of spaced reflective elements on common support, e.g. Fresnel reflectors -- The CPC entry F24S 2023/872 covers devices that consist of multiple reflective elements arranged at fixed intervals on a single backing, such as Fresnel-type reflectors, used to redirect or concentrate light or radiation in applications like solar energy, illumination or optical measurement.
[F24S 2023/874] Reflectors formed by assemblies of adjacent similar reflective facets -- F24S covers lighting apparatus; subclass 2023/874 concerns reflectors constructed from an assembly of adjacent, similar reflective facets. The facets are arranged to form a continuous reflective surface, often used in lamps, projectors, or solar concentrators to direct light efficiently.
[F24S 2023/876] Reflectors formed by assemblies of adjacent reflective elements having different orientation or different features -- The CPC subclass F24S 2023/876 covers reflectors that are constructed from groups of adjacent reflective elements where each element has a different orientation or distinct physical features, such as shape, coating or geometry, enabling the overall assembly to direct light in a customized pattern. This classification is used for patents describing modular or composite reflector designs that combine varied element types to achieve specific optical performance.
[F24S 2023/878] Assemblies of spaced reflective elements in the form of grids, e.g. vertical or inclined reflective elements extending over heat absorbing elements -- The code covers arrangements of spaced reflective elements formed as grids, such as vertical or inclined plates or strips, positioned above a heat-absorbing surface. These assemblies redirect solar radiation to lower heat gain or enhance energy efficiency in buildings, solar collectors and related devices.
[F24S 2023/88] Multi reflective traps -- F24S 2023/88 designates multi reflective traps, which are arrangements of several reflective surfaces that confine and redirect radiant energy to increase heat collection or reduce losses in heating, cooling or energy-recovery systems. The concept is used in solar thermal collectors, radiative cooling panels and other heat-exchange devices to enhance efficiency.
[F24S 20/25] using direct solar radiation in combination with concentrated radiation -- F24S 20/25 relates to solar heat collectors that combine direct sunlight with concentrated radiation, such as by using mirrors or lenses to focus the sun's rays onto the collector, enhancing heating efficiency for specific applications.
[F24S 2025/01] Special support components; Methods of use -- This CPC subclass relates to special support components for building structures such as beams, columns, or other load-bearing elements, and includes methods of using these components. It covers devices that provide reinforcement, alignment, temporary or permanent support, and the procedures for installing or employing them in construction.
[F24S 2025/011] Arrangements for mounting elements inside solar collectors; Spacers inside solar collectors -- F24S 2025/011 covers the design of structures that hold or support components inside solar thermal collectors, such as absorber plates, tubes or reflective elements, and includes spacers placed within the collector to maintain separation, enhance flow or reduce thermal losses.
[F24S 2025/012] Foldable support elements -- F24S 2025/012 relates to foldable support elements such as collapsible frames, brackets or other structural members that can be folded for storage or transport and then unfolded to bear loads, commonly used in furniture, equipment and building components. The subclass covers mechanisms that allow repeated folding and unfolding while maintaining sufficient rigidity and stability.
[F24S 2025/013] Stackable support elements -- F24S 2025/013 in the CPC relates to stackable support elements, i.e. components that can be arranged in a telescoping or piling fashion to provide adjustable height or load-bearing structures in building, furniture, or industrial equipment. It includes designs where individual units interlock or nest to form a stable support system.
[F24S 2025/014] Methods for installing support elements -- This CPC subgroup pertains to fire-fighting technology and specifically covers procedures for mounting or securing support elements such as brackets, poles, or frames that hold hoses, extinguishers, sprinklers and related equipment. The classification includes any method, tool or sequence of steps used to install these supports on buildings, vehicles or other structures.
[F24S 2025/015] Supports with play between elements -- F24S 2025/015 covers supports used in heating, ventilation or air-conditioning installations that incorporate intentional play or movement between connected elements, allowing for thermal expansion, vibration damping or misalignment compensation. It includes brackets, mounts, and frames designed to permit relative motion while maintaining overall system stability.
[F24S 2025/016] Filling or spacing means; Elastic means -- The subclass covers devices that use fillers, spacers or elastic elements to maintain clearances, provide cushioning, or create seals within heating, ventilation and air-conditioning apparatus or related fluid-handling components. Typical examples include rubber gaskets, foam inserts or spring-loaded spacers employed to control flow paths, reduce vibration or accommodate thermal expansion.
[F24S 2025/017] Tensioning means -- F24S 2025/017 covers tensioning means used to apply, maintain or adjust tension in structural or mechanical elements such as cables, ropes, tendons or reinforcing members, typically in construction, bridge or lifting applications. The claim includes mechanical, hydraulic, pneumatic or spring devices that exert a pulling force to keep the element under a predetermined stress.
[F24S 2025/018] Means for preventing movements, e.g. stops -- F24S 2025/018 covers devices that restrain or limit motion in heating, ventilation, air-conditioning, fire-protection or sanitary installations. It includes mechanical stops, locking mechanisms, limit switches and similar means that prevent unwanted movement of components to ensure safety and proper operation.
[F24S 2025/019] Means for accommodating irregularities on mounting surface; Tolerance compensation means -- This CPC subclass covers devices that adjust for uneven mounting surfaces, providing tolerance-compensation between components during installation. It includes mechanisms such as adjustable shims, compliant pads, flexible connectors or other means that accommodate surface irregularities.
[F24S 2025/02] Ballasting means -- This subclass groups inventions that provide ballast devices or mechanisms used to stabilize pressure, flow or position of air or fluids in heating, ventilation or air-conditioning systems, such as water or solid weight containers, adjustable chambers or spring-loaded assemblies. They are intended to improve system balance, reduce vibration or maintain desired performance under varying operating conditions.
[F24S 2025/021] Sealing means between support elements and mounting surface -- F24S 2025/021 pertains to sealing solutions that isolate the junction between a support element-such as a bracket, frame or mounting bracket-and the surface to which it is attached. It includes gaskets, O-rings, sealant compounds, and integrated sealing features designed to prevent fluid ingress, dampen vibration or improve thermal insulation in building, construction and industrial installations.
[F24S 2025/022] Sealing means between support elements, e.g. overlapping arrangements; Gap closing arrangements -- F24S 2025/022 covers sealing devices placed between supporting components to prevent fluid or gas passage, including overlapping seals, joint covers and other gap-closing mechanisms. The classification applies to designs that hermetically join parts in construction, machinery or enclosure applications.
[F24S 2025/023] Means for preventing theft; Locking means -- F24S 2025/023 pertains to devices that deter or prevent unauthorized removal or access, encompassing a variety of locking, sealing and anti-theft mechanisms designed for securing objects, containers, equipment or premises.
[F24S 2025/6001] by using hook and loop-type fasteners -- This CPC subgroup concerns methods and devices for mounting, securing, or adjusting heating, ventilation or air-conditioning components in buildings by using hook-and-loop fasteners. The fasteners enable quick installation, removal or repositioning of equipment without permanent fixtures.
[F24S 2025/6002] by using hooks -- F24S 2025/6002 covers washing or drying machines that use hooks to hold, guide or secure garments, components or accessories inside the drum or related compartments while the machine operates.
[F24S 2025/6003] by clamping -- F24S 2025/6003 refers to heating, ventilation or air-conditioning installations whose components are secured to a surface or structure by a clamping arrangement. The subgroup covers devices and methods that attach ducts, panels, radiators or other elements using clamps rather than screws, bolts or adhesives, facilitating installation, adjustment or removal. It is part of the CPC section on climate-control equipment.
[F24S 2025/6004] by clipping, e.g. by using snap connectors -- F24S 2025/6004 covers joining or fixing of parts by means of clipping, for example using snap-type connectors that snap into place to hold components together without screws or adhesives. The classification applies to devices and arrangements where a flexible element is deformed temporarily to create a secure, releasable connection.
[F24S 2025/6005] by screwed connection -- F24S 2025/6005 covers heating devices-fireplaces, stoves or forced-air units-where the components are assembled by screwed connections. It includes any arrangement in which parts are joined using screws rather than welding, brazing or other fastening methods.
[F24S 2025/6006] by using threaded elements, e.g. stud bolts -- F24S 2025/6006 classifies heating or air-conditioning devices whose heat-exchange or mounting components are assembled by means of threaded elements, such as stud bolts, to secure together pipes, fins or other structural parts. It is used for radiators, convectors and similar apparatus where the threaded fasteners form an essential part of the construction.
[F24S 2025/6007] by using form-fitting connection means, e.g. tongue and groove -- This CPC subgroup covers devices or structures assembled by form-fitting connections such as a tongue inserted into a matching groove. The method relies on the geometry of the parts to achieve a secure, often removable, joint without additional fasteners. It is applied in fields like construction, furniture, and mechanical assemblies.
[F24S 2025/6008] by using toothed elements -- This CPC subgroup pertains to heat-exchange apparatus that achieve heat transfer by employing toothed or serrated elements on the fluid-contact surfaces. The toothed geometry creates turbulence and enlarges the effective surface area, improving efficiency in heating or cooling of liquids or gases and applies to devices such as radiators, condensers and refrigerant heat exchangers that incorporate such elements.
[F24S 2025/6009] by deforming the material, e.g. by crimping or clinching -- This subclass covers joining or fastening techniques that secure parts by locally deforming the material, for example through crimping, clinching or similar mechanical deformation processes, eliminating the need for separate fasteners and typically applied to sheet or metal components.
[F24S 2025/601] by bonding, e.g. by using adhesives -- F24S 2025/601 designates heating, ventilation or related outdoor installations in which the structural components are joined by adhesive bonding rather than mechanical fasteners. It covers the use of glues, sealants or other bonding agents to attach panels, membranes, insulation or similar elements in roof and other exterior building applications.
[F24S 2025/6011] by welding or brazing -- F24S 2025/6011 classifies devices or processes that join metal parts by welding or brazing, covering the methods and associated equipment used to create a permanent, high-strength bond between components, often in the manufacture or repair of heating, ventilation or related mechanical assemblies.
[F24S 2025/6012] Joining different materials -- CPC class F24S 2025/6012 covers techniques and apparatus for joining dissimilar materials, such as metals, polymers, ceramics or composites, by mechanical, thermal, chemical or adhesive means. It includes processes that enable secure bonds between different material types, as well as devices specifically designed to facilitate such heterogeneous joining.
[F24S 2025/6013] Joining glass with non-glass elements -- This subclass covers processes and devices for attaching a glass component to a component made of a different material such as metal, ceramic, polymer or composite. It includes adhesive bonding, sealing, soldering, welding, brazing and mechanical fastening techniques used to create a reliable joint between glass and non-glass parts.
[F24S 2025/80] Special profiles -- F24S 2025/80 pertains to air-conditioning or ventilation components featuring special cross-section profiles, such as non-circular ducts, diffusers, or nozzles designed to modify airflow characteristics. The code is applied to inventions that describe the geometry of these profiles to influence pressure loss, noise, or distribution patterns.
[F24S 2025/801] having hollow parts with closed cross-section -- This subgroup concerns ventilation or heat-exchange elements that incorporate hollow components whose cross-section is completely enclosed, such as ducts, tubes or chambers without openings along their perimeter. It includes designs where the interior space is sealed, allowing fluid flow while preventing leaks.
[F24S 2025/802] having circular or oval cross-section -- F24S 2025/802 refers to heating or combustion devices whose main flow channel, burner tube or similar component is defined by a circular or oval cross-section, covering the geometric shape of the element rather than its function or fuel type. It is a subclass within the broader F24S group for burners, heating elements and similar apparatus.
[F24S 2025/803] having a central web, e.g. I-shaped, inverted T- shaped -- F24S 2025/803 covers heating, ventilation or air-conditioning devices whose duct or heat-exchanger element includes a central web, such as I-shaped or inverted-T cross-sections, which provides structural rigidity and influences fluid flow within the component.
[F24S 2025/804] U-, C- or O-shaped; Hat profiles -- F24S 2025/804 covers duct or pipe sections whose cross-section is formed as a U, C or O shape, or that feature a hat-type profile. These profiles are used in heating, ventilation and air-conditioning systems to guide airflow while providing structural strength and ease of installation.
[F24S 2025/805] in the form of corrugated profiles -- The code F24S 2025/805 covers heating-boiler or combustion-chamber elements that are manufactured as corrugated profiles. Corrugation is used to increase rigidity, allow thermal expansion and enhance heat transfer in metal shells, tubes or panels forming the boiler housing or heat-exchange surfaces.
[F24S 2025/806] having curved portions -- F24S 2025/806 covers fire-extinguishing devices whose essential components incorporate curved sections, such as bent handles, nozzles or housings. The curvature is integral to the design, often improving ergonomics, storage or the pattern of agent discharge.
[F24S 2025/807] having undercut grooves -- F24S 2025/807 designates heating, ventilation or air-conditioning devices that include undercut grooves, which are recesses whose sidewalls are non-parallel to the base, used to increase surface area, guide fluid flow or capture debris.
[F24S 20/30] Solar heat collectors for heating objects, e.g. solar cookers or solar furnaces -- The CPC entry F24S 20/30 pertains to solar heat collectors that are specially adapted to heat objects, such as solar cookers or solar furnaces, rather than to heat spaces or fluids. It covers designs optimized for particular uses or environmental conditions.
[F24S 2030/10] Special components -- F24S 2030/10 refers to special components used in heating installations for non-building interiors, including auxiliary heat exchangers, temperature sensors, control units, safety devices and other ancillary parts that support or enhance the primary heating equipment.
[F24S 2030/11] Driving means -- The CPC subclass F24S 2030/11 covers the components that provide power or motion to ventilation or air-moving devices, such as fans, blowers and ventilators. It includes electric motors, internal-combustion engines, gearboxes, belts, chains, hydraulic or pneumatic drives and other mechanisms used to propel the airflow-generating elements.
[F24S 2030/115] Linear actuators, e.g. pneumatic cylinders -- This CPC subclass covers devices that generate straight-line motion from energy sources, focusing on linear actuators such as pneumatic cylinders and similar mechanisms. It includes the design, operation, control and typical applications of these fluid-power actuators.
[F24S 2030/12] Coupling means -- The F24S 2030/12 subclass defines coupling means employed in fans, blowers and aspirating devices for transmitting torque between a drive shaft and the rotating member. It includes rigid, flexible, universal-joint, magnetic and other couplings that accommodate misalignment, vibration or thermal expansion.
[F24S 2030/13] Transmissions -- F24S 2030/13 of the Cooperative Patent Classification relates to transmissions used in air-conditioning and HVAC equipment, covering mechanical or fluid power transmission components such as gearboxes, couplings, shafts, belts, pulleys and other devices that convey energy, motion or heat between system parts. It is applied to inventions that improve the efficiency, reliability or compactness of these transmission elements within climate-control installations.
[F24S 2030/131] in the form of articulated bars -- F24S 2030/131 pertains to air-distribution components that consist of a series of linked or hinged bars which can be articulated to vary the size and direction of openings. These articulated-bar diffusers are used in heating, ventilation or air-conditioning systems to control airflow and provide adjustable ventilation patterns.
[F24S 2030/132] in the form of compasses, scissors or parallelograms -- F24S 2030/132 classifies heating devices whose heating element or radiating surface is shaped like a compass, scissors or a parallelogram. It applies to space-heating or appliance-heating apparatus where the heat-emitting area is constructed in these geometric forms to direct or spread thermal energy.
[F24S 2030/133] in the form of flexible elements, e.g. belts, chains, ropes -- The subclass F24S 2030/133 covers heating, ventilation or related apparatus that are implemented as flexible elements such as belts, chains or ropes, used to transmit power, motion or support within the system.
[F24S 2030/134] in the form of gearings or rack-and-pinion transmissions -- CPC F24S 2030/134 covers heating or solar-energy installations in which the positioning of collectors, shutters, louvers or similar elements is accomplished by gear trains or rack-and-pinion mechanisms. The classification applies to devices that use these mechanical transmission forms to adjust the orientation or exposure of thermal components.
[F24S 2030/135] in the form of threaded elements -- F24S 2030/135 refers to heat-exchanger designs that use threaded components as the heat-transfer elements, such as screwed rods, threaded tubes or coils. The classification covers devices where the threading provides a means to assemble, secure or increase the effective surface area for heat exchange. It is a subcategory of non-electric heating equipment in the CPC system.
[F24S 2030/136] for moving several solar collectors by common transmission elements -- This subclass covers arrangements that move two or more solar collectors using a shared drive or transmission mechanism. It includes systems where a single motor, gear train, belt, chain or other common element adjusts the orientation of multiple panels simultaneously, such as group tracking or tilt devices for arrays.
[F24S 2030/137] for deriving one movement from another one, e.g. for deriving elevation movement from azimuth movement -- This CPC subgroup covers control systems that generate one type of motion based on another, such as converting a change in azimuth into an elevation adjustment in antennas, radars or similar devices. It includes mechanisms, algorithms or devices that derive secondary movement from a primary input.
[F24S 2030/14] Movement guiding means -- Devices that guide or control the movement of fluids within heat-exchange installations, such as guides, rails, baffles, rollers or other elements that direct flow to maintain proper paths and reduce turbulence.
[F24S 2030/145] Tracks -- F24S 2030/145 covers tracks designed to support, guide or transport insulated pipe or duct sections during installation, maintenance or replacement. The claim typically includes rail-like structures, rollers or movable guides that allow pipe segments to be moved along a predetermined path while preserving their insulation integrity.
[F24S 2030/15] Bearings -- F24S 2030/15 relates to the use of bearings within heating, ventilating and air-conditioning systems, including fans, blowers, compressors and other rotating components. The classification covers bearing types, mounting arrangements, lubrication methods and related improvements that enhance the performance, reliability or efficiency of such HVAC equipment.
[F24S 2030/16] Hinged elements; Pin connections -- F24S 2030/16 classifies patents dealing with hinged building components such as doors, windows, shutters and similar closure elements that are joined to their frames by pins or hinge mechanisms. The subclass covers the design, structure and operation of pivot connections that allow the element to swing open or closed, including pins, bolts and associated fastening features.
[F24S 2030/17] Spherical joints -- F24S 2030/17 covers spherical, ball-and-socket type joints used to connect building elements or structural components, allowing angular movement while transmitting loads. The classification includes designs for mechanical articulation in frames, doors, windows and other assemblies where a rotatable connection is required. It is applied to patents describing the geometry, materials and mounting methods of such joints.
[F24S 2030/18] Load balancing means, e.g. use of counter-weights -- The code covers devices that equalise mechanical forces in heating, ventilation or air-conditioning installations, such as counter-weights attached to dampers, louvers or moving panels to balance load and ensure smooth operation. It includes any means that counteract uneven pressures or torques in the system, improving reliability and control.
[F24S 2030/19] Movement dampening means; Braking means -- The CPC group F24S 2030/19 covers devices that reduce or absorb motion, such as dampers, shock-absorbers and other movement-dampening means, together with mechanisms that provide controlled stopping or slowing, i.e., braking means. These elements are used in a wide range of mechanical and structural systems to improve safety, comfort or performance.
[F24S 20/40] Solar heat collectors combined with other heat sources, e.g. using electrical heating or heat from ambient air -- F24S 20/40 covers solar heat collectors that are combined with auxiliary heat sources such as electric heaters or ambient-air exchangers, enabling supplemental heating or improved performance under low-sun conditions. These hybrid systems are tailored for specific applications or environments where solar energy alone is insufficient.
[F24S 20/50] Rollable or foldable solar heat collector modules -- Rollable or foldable solar heat collector modules are compact, flexible devices designed to capture solar energy while being easily deployed, stored, or adapted to limited or irregular spaces. They belong to the mechanical-engineering subclass for heating, specifically solar collectors tailored for particular uses or environments, and are characterized by their collapsible structure that allows efficient installation on rooftops, vehicles, or portable systems.
[F24S 2050/25] Calibration means; Methods for initial positioning of solar concentrators or solar receivers -- F24S 2050/25 pertains to calibration apparatus and procedures used to initially align solar concentrators or receivers with the sun. It includes mechanisms, sensors and control methods that ensure accurate positioning of mirrors, lenses or other concentrating elements during installation or startup.
[F24S 20/55] made of flexible materials -- This subgroup covers solar heat collectors built from flexible materials such as sheets, membranes, tubes or fabric-based absorbers, allowing lightweight or conformable installations suited to special uses or environments. It includes designs that can be rolled, folded or attached to irregular surfaces for targeted heating applications.
[F24S 20/60] Solar heat collectors integrated in fixed constructions, e.g. in buildings -- The subclass F24S 20/60 pertains to solar heat collectors that are built into permanent structures such as building façades, roofs or walls, making the collector an integral part of the construction rather than a separate unit. It covers designs adapted for specific building applications and environmental conditions.
[F24S 20/61] Passive solar heat collectors, e.g. operated without external energy source -- F24S 20/61 concerns passive solar heat collectors that operate without an external energy source, designed for particular applications or environmental conditions. These devices capture sunlight and convert it to heat using only solar energy, often integrated into building envelopes, water heating systems, or other specialized installations.
[F24S 20/62] in the form of fences, balustrades or handrails -- F24S 20/62 covers solar heat collectors that are built into or form part of fences, balustrades or handrails, allowing these architectural elements to capture solar energy for heating or hot-water production. The design integrates the collector surface with safety or decorative structures, adapting the technology for specific environments or functions.
[F24S 20/63] in the form of windows -- The subclass F24S 20/63 covers solar-heat collectors that are incorporated into window structures, using the glazing area to capture and convert sunlight into thermal energy for building heating or hot-water production. It is a specialized type of solar collector designed for integration into building envelopes.
[F24S 20/64] in the form of floor constructions, grounds or roads -- This subclass covers solar heat collectors that are incorporated directly into floor constructions, ground surfaces or roadways. The collector surface forms part of the pavement or flooring, enabling solar energy to be harvested for heating, water warming or electricity generation while still supporting normal traffic or foot traffic.
[F24S 20/66] in the form of facade constructions, e.g. wall constructions -- Solar heat collectors designed to be integrated into building facades or wall structures, providing heating while serving as part of the exterior envelope. These systems are adapted for specific applications or environmental conditions.
[F24S 20/67] in the form of roof constructions -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Solar heat collectors integrated in fixed constructions in the form of shingles or tiles -> F24S20/69
[F24S 20/69] in the form of shingles or tiles -- F24S 20/69 covers solar heat collectors designed as shingles or tiles that can be installed on roofs and walls, serving both as building cladding and energy-generating components. These integrated collectors convert sunlight into heat for domestic hot water or space heating while blending with conventional roofing materials.
[F24S 20/70] Waterborne solar heat collector modules -- Waterborne solar heat collector modules are solar collectors designed to operate while floating or immersed in water, typically used to heat water directly or provide heat to nearby systems. They are tailored for specific environments such as lakes, reservoirs, or swimming pools, often incorporating buoyant structures and corrosion-resistant materials.
[F24S 2070/62] Heat traps -- Heat traps are devices installed in ventilation or exhaust ducts to capture and retain thermal energy from moving air or gases, thereby reducing heat loss and improving energy efficiency. They typically consist of insulated chambers or heat-exchange elements that absorb heat during flow and release it back into the airstream when conditions reverse, and are used in building HVAC systems and industrial processes.
[F24S 20/80] Airborne solar heat collector modules, e.g. inflatable structures -- F24S 20/80 covers solar heat collector modules that can be deployed in the air, such as inflatable or tethered devices, designed to capture sunlight while airborne. These collectors are intended for portable or temporary installations where ground-based panels are impractical.
[F24S 2080/01] Selection of particular materials -- The code F24S 2080/01 relates to inventions that involve choosing specific materials for components of space-heating equipment such as heat exchangers, burners or insulation, with the aim of attaining particular performance, durability or efficiency characteristics. It covers methods and criteria for material selection within heating system designs.
[F24S 2080/011] Ceramics -- F24S 2080/011 covers cooking-appliance heating elements that are made of ceramic materials. It includes ceramic burners, heating plates and other ceramic components used to generate heat in ovens, stovetops and similar devices. The code is used to classify inventions where the ceramic element provides the primary source of heat for cooking.
[F24S 2080/012] Concrete -- The CPC subclass F24S 2080/012 covers concrete as a construction material, including its composition, production methods, additives, and reinforcement techniques, as well as its use in building elements such as foundations, walls, slabs and prefabricated components. It also addresses processes for curing, testing and improving concrete performance.
[F24S 2080/013] Foams -- The CPC subclass F24S 2080/013 covers the use of foams in heating, ventilating or air-conditioning equipment, such as foamed insulation, foam-filled heat exchangers and other foam-based components designed to control temperature or airflow.
[F24S 2080/014] Carbone, e.g. graphite -- The entry classifies heat-exchange or heating-and-cooling components that employ carbon-based materials such as graphite, including plates, fins, absorbers or other elements where the graphite provides high thermal conductivity or corrosion resistance. It is used for inventions that integrate graphite into boilers, radiators, heat exchangers, ventilation or air-conditioning devices.
[F24S 2080/015] Plastics -- F24S 2080/015 refers to the use of plastics as structural or functional elements in heating, ventilation, air-conditioning and cooling equipment. The sub-group covers designs that incorporate polymeric materials for ducts, casings, heat-exchangers, seals or other components, emphasizing how the material properties influence system performance, corrosion resistance, weight or manufacturability.
[F24S 2080/016] Textiles; Fabrics -- F24S 2080/016 covers inventions that incorporate textile or fabric materials into heating, ventilation, air-conditioning or related environmental-control devices. It includes breathable fabrics, filter media, woven or non-woven structures and other textile elements used as functional components of HVAC, climate-control or air-handling equipment.
[F24S 2080/017] Natural materials, e.g. wood -- F24S 2080/017 pertains to heating, ventilating and air-conditioning apparatus that incorporate natural materials, especially wood, as structural elements, heat-transfer surfaces or insulation. It includes devices where wood forms the main body, panels, or heat-exchange surfaces.
[F24S 2080/018] Recycled materials -- This CPC subclass relates to heating, ventilation or air-conditioning devices in which the components such as heat exchangers, ducts or casings are produced wholly or partially from recycled or reclaimed materials, encompassing metals, polymers or composites recovered from waste streams.
[F24S 2080/03] Arrangements for heat transfer optimization -- F24S 2080/03 covers technical designs that improve the efficiency of heat exchange, such as fin configurations, flow channel geometry, or control strategies that enhance heat transfer rates in heating, cooling or ventilation equipment.
[F24S 2080/05] Flow guiding means; Inserts inside conduits -- F24S 2080/05 covers devices that guide the flow of air, gas or liquid within ducts, pipes or other conduits, including guide vanes, baffles, or other inserts placed inside the conduit to control direction, distribution or turbulence of the moving medium.
[F24S 2080/07] Arrangements for one-way heat transfer, e.g. thermal diodes -- The code F24S 2080/07 covers designs that permit heat to transfer preferentially in a single direction, such as thermal diodes and similar one-way heat-transfer arrangements. It includes structures and materials that create asymmetric thermal conductivity for use in heat exchangers, thermal management and energy-conversion devices.
[F24S 2080/09] Arrangements for reinforcement of solar collector elements -- F24S 2080/09 covers technical arrangements that reinforce solar-collector elements such as absorber plates, tubes or panels, using supports, ribs, frames or other structural measures to improve durability and maintain alignment under thermal or mechanical stress. It falls within the CPC class for solar thermal collectors, specifying reinforcement solutions for the collector's functional components.
[F24S 2080/501] Special shape -- F24S 2080/501 covers heating or cooling installations in which a component such as a heat exchanger, pipe or chamber is formed in a special shape rather than conventional cylindrical or rectangular geometry, the shape being intended to optimise fluid dynamics, heat transfer or space constraints.
[F24S 2080/502] in the form of multiple covering elements -- The CPC code F24S 2080/502 designates heating, ventilation, cooling, filtering, humidifying or dehumidifying apparatus that incorporate multiple covering elements, such as devices with several filters, panels or screens arranged in series or parallel to treat or direct a fluid flow.
[F24S 2080/503] in the form of curved covering elements -- The CPC subclass F24S 2080/503 pertains to heating, ventilation or air-conditioning installations that incorporate curved covering elements, such as ducts, casings or shields that follow a non-linear profile to protect or enclose functional parts. It includes designs where the curvature forms the primary shape of the covering rather than a flat or angular form.
[F24S 21/00] Solar heat collectors not provided for in groups -- F24S 21/00 is a residual sub-group for solar heat collectors that do not fall within any of the more specific CPC classes for solar thermal devices. It covers unconventional, hybrid or otherwise unclassified solar collector types and arrangements, providing a catch-all category within the heating and solar-energy sections.
[F24S 2201/00] Prediction -- F24S 2201/00 covers techniques for predicting the performance of solar-heat collectors, including models, simulations and analytical methods that estimate temperature, thermal output or energy yield. It is used to assess and optimise collector designs and system operation in heating applications.
[F24S 23/00] Arrangements for concentrating solar-rays for solar heat collectors -- Arrangements for concentrating solar rays in solar heat collectors describe devices that gather and focus sunlight onto a receiver using mirrors, lenses or reflective surfaces, often combined with tracking mechanisms to maintain optimal alignment with the sun. These systems increase the intensity of solar energy reaching the collector, improving thermal efficiency for applications such as water heating, industrial process heat or power generation.
[F24S 23/10] Prisms -- The subclass F24S 23/10 pertains to the use of prisms in solar-ray concentrating systems for heat collectors. It covers prism designs, materials and arrangements that redirect or focus sunlight onto a solar collector to improve thermal performance. This classification falls under mechanical engineering, heating and solar heat collectors.
[F24S 23/11] Fluorescent material -- F24S 23/11 pertains to fluorescent materials employed in arrangements that concentrate solar rays for solar-heat collectors, a subcategory of mechanical engineering heating technology. It covers substances that emit visible light when excited by concentrated sunlight, enhancing heat-capture efficiency or enabling visual monitoring of the collector's performance.
[F24S 23/12] Light guides -- F24S 23/12 covers light-guiding elements that transport concentrated solar radiation from a collector's concentrating arrangement to the absorber. These include optical fibers, light pipes, mirrors or other wave-guiding devices used to direct sunlight efficiently within solar-heat systems that employ ray-concentrating collectors.
[F24S 23/30] with lenses -- This CPC subclass covers solar-thermal systems that use lenses to focus sunlight onto a collector. It includes designs where lenses concentrate solar rays for heating, fitting within the hierarchy mechanical engineering - heating - solar heat collectors.
[F24S 23/31] having discontinuous faces, e.g. Fresnel lenses -- This subclass deals with solar heat collector systems that use concentrators made of discontinuous faces, such as Fresnel lenses, to focus sunlight onto the absorber. The design employs multiple small flat facets to approximate a curved optical surface, improving concentration efficiency.
[F24S 23/70] with reflectors -- F24S 23/70 addresses solar-heat collector designs that employ reflectors to concentrate incoming solar rays onto the absorber, thereby raising the collector's temperature and improving thermal efficiency.
[F24S 23/71] with parabolic reflective surfaces -- The CPC subclass F24S 23/71 covers solar-thermal collector arrangements that concentrate sunlight with parabolic reflective surfaces to heat a fluid or absorber. It is positioned under mechanical engineering, heating, and solar heat collectors, and specifies the use of parabolic mirrors to focus solar rays onto a collector.
[F24S 23/715] flexible -- The F24S 23/715 subclass covers flexible components or structures used in arrangements that concentrate solar rays onto solar heat collectors. It includes flexible reflectors, films or membranes that can be shaped or adjusted to direct sunlight onto the absorber, enabling lightweight-adaptable concentrating systems.
[F24S 23/72] with hemispherical reflective surfaces -- This subclass covers solar-thermal collector systems that employ a hemispherical reflective surface to concentrate incoming solar rays onto the absorber or heat-exchange element, enhancing the intensity of solar heating for the collector. It is a specific arrangement within solar heat collectors that uses a dome- or bowl-shaped mirror to focus sunlight.
[F24S 23/74] with trough-shaped or cylindro-parabolic reflective surfaces -- F24S 23/74 covers solar-heat collector systems that employ trough-shaped or cylindro-parabolic reflective surfaces to concentrate sunlight onto a receiver. These arrangements are used to focus solar rays for efficient heat generation in heating applications.
[F24S 23/745] flexible -- The CPC entry F24S 23/745 covers flexible arrangements that concentrate solar rays onto solar-heat collectors. It falls under mechanical engineering, heating, and solar heat collectors, specifying devices where the concentrating elements or support structures are adaptable, bendable or otherwise not rigid.
[F24S 23/75] with conical reflective surfaces -- The subclass F24S 23/75 defines arrangements that use conical reflective surfaces to concentrate solar rays onto a heat-collector, improving the efficiency of solar thermal systems for heating applications.
[F24S 23/77] with flat reflective plates -- A subclass of solar-heat collector technology that concentrates sunlight onto the absorber using flat reflective plates or mirrors. It addresses arrangements where the flat elements direct solar rays toward the collector for enhanced heating performance.
[F24S 23/79] with spaced and opposed interacting reflective surfaces -- F24S 23/79 covers solar-heat collector arrangements that use two or more reflective surfaces positioned opposite each other and spaced apart so the reflected rays interact to concentrate sunlight onto a receiver. This configuration is employed in linear or point-focus concentrators to improve thermal efficiency.
[F24S 23/80] having discontinuous faces -- F24S 23/80 covers concentrating solar-thermal collector arrangements whose reflective or refractive surfaces are broken into separate, non-continuous facets or panels. The discontinuous faces may be spaced or angled individually to direct solar rays onto the absorber, enabling modular or adjustable designs.
[F24S 23/81] flexible  F24S23/715 ,  F24S23/745  take precedence
[F24S 23/82] characterised by the material or the construction of the reflector -- This CPC subclass concerns solar-concentrating heat collectors in which the reflector is distinguished by the material it is made of or by its construction features. It covers designs that use particular reflective surfaces, coatings, substrates or structural geometries to achieve the desired concentration of solar rays.
[F24S 25/00] Arrangement of stationary mountings or supports for solar heat collector modules -- Arrangements also intended for use with photovoltaic modules should further be classified in the relevant groups of subclass H02S .
[F24S 25/10] extending in directions away from a supporting surface -- This subclass covers fixed mounting arrangements for solar heat collector modules that extend outward from the surface they are attached to, such as brackets, cantilevers or angled frames that hold the collector away from a roof or wall. It focuses on the geometry and support structures that project the collector in directions away from the supporting surface.
[F24S 25/11] using shaped bodies, e.g. concrete elements, foamed elements or moulded box-like elements -- F24S 25/11 deals with arranging fixed supports for solar heat collector modules using pre-shaped structural elements such as concrete blocks, foamed pieces or moulded box-like parts, focusing on how these bodies are positioned to hold the collectors securely and simplify installation.
[F24S 25/12] using posts in combination with upper profiles -- This subclass deals with the way stationary supports for solar heat collectors are arranged, specifically the use of vertical posts together with upper profile members to hold the collector modules in place. It falls under mechanical engineering heating solar heat collectors.
[F24S 25/13] Profile arrangements, e.g. trusses -- The code F24S 25/13 concerns designs for fixed mounting structures, such as trusses, that support solar heat-collector modules. It falls under mechanical engineering heating, specifically the arrangement of stationary supports for solar heat collectors.
[F24S 25/15] using bent plates -- The F24S 25/15 subclass covers solar heat collector modules that are mounted or supported on stationary structures using bent-plate elements, detailing arrangements where the plates are formed or shaped to create rigid, adjustable supports for the collectors.
[F24S 25/16] Arrangement of interconnected standing structures -- The code F24S 25/16 concerns the design and layout of linked upright frames or support structures that hold solar-heat collector modules in place. It addresses how multiple standing structures are interconnected to provide stable mounting for solar thermal systems.
[F24S 25/20] Peripheral frames for modules -- F24S 25/20 covers peripheral frames that provide the structural support for stationary solar-heat collector modules. It relates to the design and arrangement of mounting frames that hold the collector panels in place, ensuring stability and alignment for efficient solar heating.
[F24S 25/30] using elongate rigid mounting elements extending substantially along the supporting surface, e.g. for covering buildings with solar heat collectors -- This subclass covers arrangements where solar heat collector modules are mounted on a surface using long, stiff mounting members that run parallel to the surface, such as panels that span across a roof. The mounting elements are rigid and extend substantially along the supporting area, enabling continuous coverage of the building with collectors.
[F24S 25/33] forming substantially planar assemblies, e.g. of coplanar or stacked profiles -- The code F24S 25/33 relates to the manufacturing of essentially flat or stacked assemblies used to support stationary solar heat-collector modules. It includes processes for forming coplanar or stacked profile structures that serve as mounts or frames, ensuring proper alignment and stability of the collectors within a heating system.
[F24S 25/35] by means of profiles with a cross-section defining separate supporting portions for adjacent modules -- F24S 25/35 relates to mounting systems for solar thermal collectors that employ profiles whose cross-section creates distinct supporting portions for adjacent modules. This arrangement provides stationary supports that hold each collector separately while allowing independent load distribution.
[F24S 25/37] forming coplanar grids comprising longitudinal and transversal profiles -- This CPC subclass concerns the construction of coplanar grid structures that incorporate both longitudinal and transversal profiles, intended to serve as stationary mounting or support frameworks for solar heat collector modules. The grids provide a planar, load-bearing arrangement that simplifies installation and alignment of the collectors within heating systems.
[F24S 25/40] using plate-like mounting elements, e.g. profiled or corrugated plates -- The entry F24S 25/40 concerns the arrangement of fixed supports for solar-heat collector modules that employ plate-like mounting elements such as profiled or corrugated plates. It falls under the hierarchy Mechanical Engineering → Heating → Solar Heat Collectors → Arrangement of stationary mountings or supports for solar heat collector modules. This classification covers the design and layout of these plate-type supports for stable, non-tracking solar collectors.
[F24S 25/50] comprising elongate non-rigid elements, e.g. straps, wires or ropes -- The subclass F24S 25/50 covers the arrangement of stationary supports for solar heat collector modules that are made from elongate non-rigid elements such as straps, wires or ropes, providing flexible mounting solutions within solar heating systems.
[F24S 25/60] Fixation means, e.g. fasteners, specially adapted for supporting solar heat collector modules -- It covers devices and methods for mounting solar collector panels on a fixed structure, including specially designed fasteners, brackets, clamps and other support components that securely attach the modules in place.
[F24S 25/61] for fixing to the ground or to building structures -- This subclass covers designs that attach solar thermal collectors to foundations, rooftops or other fixed structures, detailing mounting brackets, anchors and support frames that secure the modules in place. It addresses both ground-mounted and building-mounted installations.
[F24S 25/613] in the form of bent strips or assemblies of strips -- The F24S 25/613 entry covers stationary support structures for solar-heat collectors that are built from bent strips or assemblies of strips, providing a simple, lightweight mounting system for fixed collector modules. This arrangement belongs to the mechanical-engineering heating subclass for solar heat collectors.
[F24S 25/615] for fixing to protruding parts of buildings, e.g. to corrugations or to standing seams -- This subclass covers methods of attaching solar heat collector modules to protruding building elements such as corrugations or standing seams. It specifies arrangements of stationary mounts or supports that fix the collector to these parts, enabling secure installation on irregular surfaces.
[F24S 25/617] Elements driven into the ground, e.g. anchor-piles -- This subclass covers ground-anchored components such as anchor-piles that are driven into the soil to secure stationary mounts or supports for solar heat collector modules. It deals with the design and installation of these driven elements as part of the overall solar heating system.
[F24S 25/63] for fixing modules or their peripheral frames to supporting elements -- The classification covers ways of attaching solar heating collector modules or their peripheral frames to fixed support structures. It includes designs of mounts, brackets, clamps and other stationary arrangements that secure the collectors to roofs, ground frames or other supporting elements.
[F24S 25/632] Side connectors -- Side connectors are components that join adjacent modules of a stationary solar heat collector, providing mechanical linkage and electrical or fluid continuity while allowing for thermal expansion and easy assembly.
[F24S 25/634] Clamps -- F24S 25/634 covers clamps used to attach stationary mounting or support structures for solar heat collector modules. It is classified under mechanical engineering, heating, and solar heat collectors, focusing on the arrangement of fixed supports for these devices.
[F24S 25/636] clamping by screw-threaded elements -- F24S 25/636 relates to clamping solar heat collector modules to stationary mounts using screw-threaded elements. It describes how screw-type fasteners secure the collector's support structure, providing a stable attachment for fixed installations.
[F24S 25/65] for coupling adjacent supporting elements, e.g. for connecting profiles together -- The entry F24S 25/65 covers means for joining adjacent support components in the stationary mounting of solar heat-collector modules, such as connectors that link profile sections together to form a continuous support structure. This classification applies to devices and methods that provide mechanical coupling between neighboring elements of a solar collector support frame.
[F24S 25/67] for coupling adjacent modules or their peripheral frames -- This subclass addresses the arrangement of fixed supports that join neighboring solar heat collector modules or their peripheral frames, providing a stable coupling of adjacent panels within a solar heating system.
[F24S 25/70] with means for adjusting the final position or orientation of supporting elements in relation to each other or to a mounting surface -- This subclass pertains to stationary mounting structures for solar heat collector modules that incorporate means to adjust the final position or orientation of the supporting elements, either among themselves or with respect to the mounting surface. The adjustments may be achieved through screws, hinges, or other mechanical devices to optimize collector alignment.
[F24S 30/00] Arrangements for moving or orienting solar heat collector modules -- Arrangements also intended for use with photovoltaic modules should further be classified in the relevant groups of subclass H02S .
[F24S 30/20] for linear movement -- The F24S 30/20 subclass concerns arrangements that impart linear movement to solar-heat collector modules, enabling them to shift position or orientation along a straight path for sun-tracking or seasonal adjustment. It includes mechanisms such as rails, sliders, linear guides and actuators that translate the collector panels in a linear direction while maintaining their functional alignment.
[F24S 30/40] for rotary movement -- F24S 30/40 covers arrangements that give solar heat collector modules rotary movement to change their orientation, such as rotating mounts, turntables or similar mechanisms that turn the collector around an axis for sun tracking.
[F24S 30/42] with only one rotation axis -- This subclass deals with mechanisms that adjust solar heat collector modules by rotating them about a single axis, allowing the collector to tilt or swivel to follow the sun's path. It includes devices where the panel's orientation is changed using only one rotational degree of freedom.
[F24S 30/422] Vertical axis -- F24S 30/422 covers solar collector arrangements that rotate about a vertical axis to follow the sun. The class includes mechanisms, drives, supports and control systems for turning flat-plate or concentrating modules around a vertical shaft, enabling optimal orientation throughout the day.
[F24S 30/425] Horizontal axis -- It covers arrangements that enable a solar heat collector with a horizontal axis to be moved or oriented, such as tracking mechanisms that rotate the collector about that axis to follow the sun and optimise energy capture.
[F24S 30/428] with inclined axis -- The subclass concerns devices that adjust the position of solar-heat collector modules by tilting or rotating them so the collector axis is inclined, enabling optimum orientation toward the sun for varying angles of incident radiation.
[F24S 30/45] with two rotation axes -- The subclass F24S 30/45 covers solar heat collector assemblies that can be rotated about two separate axes to follow the sun's position. This dual-axis orientation improves the collector's incidence angle throughout the day, enhancing thermal efficiency. It falls under mechanical engineering, heating, and solar heat collectors.
[F24S 30/452] Vertical primary axis -- The F24S 30/452 classification covers systems that adjust the position of solar heat-collector modules by rotating them around a vertical primary axis. It includes mechanisms that track the sun's movement to improve thermal capture, such as rotating racks, turntables or pivoting frames that keep the collectors optimally oriented. This falls under mechanical engineering, heating, and solar heat collector technologies.
[F24S 30/455] Horizontal primary axis -- The code F24S 30/455 refers to solar-heat collector systems whose primary axis is horizontal and that include mechanisms for moving or orienting the collector modules to track the sun. It describes designs that adjust tilt or rotation while the main axis remains horizontal, enhancing solar energy capture.
[F24S 30/458] with inclined primary axis -- This CPC subclass deals with arrangements that move or orient solar-heat-collector modules whose primary axis is inclined, covering mechanisms such as tilting, tracking or rotating systems that adjust the collector's angle to optimise solar exposure. It falls under Mechanical Engineering → Heating → Solar Heat Collectors.
[F24S 30/48] with three or more rotation axes or with multiple degrees of freedom -- This subclass covers arrangements that move or orient solar heat collector modules using three or more rotation axes or multiple degrees of freedom, providing complex multi-axis tracking to maximize solar exposure under varying sun positions.
[F24S 40/00] Safety or protection arrangements of solar heat collectors -- F24S 40/00 covers protective measures and safety devices for solar heat collectors, such as overheating controls, pressure-relief valves, fire-preventing structures and other arrangements that secure the collector and its system against damage or hazards during operation. It addresses designs that ensure reliable, safe performance of solar thermal installations.
[F24S 40/10] Protective covers or shrouds -- The F24S 40/10 subclass covers protective covers, shields or shrouds designed to safeguard solar heat collectors from external hazards, weather, or accidental damage. It includes safety or protection arrangements that prevent overheating, mechanical impact, or intrusion while maintaining collector performance. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering, heating, and solar heat collectors.
[F24S 40/20] Cleaning -- Cleaning refers to the measures, devices or procedures used to remove dust, debris, moisture or other contaminants from solar heat collector surfaces, ensuring efficient heat absorption and preventing damage or safety hazards that could arise from fouling.
[F24S 40/40] Preventing corrosion -- F24S 40/40 deals with methods and devices that protect solar heat collectors from corrosion. It includes the use of corrosion-resistant materials, protective coatings, chemical inhibitors, and design features that isolate vulnerable parts from moisture or corrosive agents. The classification addresses both preventive and remedial measures to maintain collector efficiency and lifespan.
[F24S 40/42] Preventing condensation inside solar modules -- The code covers design features and methods that stop moisture from forming inside solar thermal modules, including seals, vents, drainage and heating elements that keep the collector interior dry. Such protection helps maintain efficiency and prevents damage caused by condensation.
