[F25J 1/0035] by gas expansion with extraction of work -- F25J 1/0035 pertains to processes or apparatus that liquefy or solidify gases by expanding the gas while extracting work, such as turbine or engine expansions that produce cooling as a by-product. This sub-group falls under mechanical engineering, refrigeration or cooling, specifically the liquefaction, solidification or separation of gases by pressure and cold treatment.
[F25J 1/0037] of a return stream -- The subgroup deals with systems that include a return stream of gas within a liquefaction or solidification process, such as recirculating part of the cooled or liquefied gas to earlier stages like heat exchangers or compressors to improve efficiency and control.
[F25J 1/004] by flash gas recovery  F25J1/0267  takes precedence -- F25J 1/004 refers to processes or apparatus for liquefying or solidifying gases that incorporate flash-gas recovery, meaning the system captures and re-uses gas released during rapid pressure drops. This technique enhances energy efficiency in refrigeration and gas-liquefaction installations and is situated in the mechanical-engineering hierarchy under refrigeration and cold-treatment of gases. The related subclass F25J1/0267 has precedence.
[F25J 1/0042] by liquid expansion with extraction of work -- This subclass covers processes or apparatus that liquefy or solidify gases or gas mixtures by allowing a liquid to expand, thereby extracting work and producing a cooling effect. The expansion-driven cooling is used to achieve the required pressure-temperature conditions for gas condensation or solidification.
[F25J 1/0045] by vaporising a liquid return stream -- The CPC sub-group F25J 1/0045 covers methods for liquefying or solidifying gases in which a liquid stream that has already been used in the system is vaporised to provide the cooling needed for the refrigerant. It deals with recovering heat from the return liquid and using its phase change to aid gas liquefaction.
[F25J 1/0047] using an "external" refrigerant stream in a closed vapor compression cycle  F25J1/0221 ,  F25J1/0225  take precedence -- F25J 1/0047 covers systems in which a separate refrigerant stream is introduced to a closed vapor-compression refrigeration cycle to enhance heat transfer or cascade operation, typically involving heat exchangers that link the external stream with the cycle's evaporator or condenser. It supersedes the more specific sub-classes F25J1/0221 and F25J1/0225.
[F25J 1/005] by expansion of a gaseous refrigerant stream with extraction of work -- The subclass concerns processes or devices that chill a gas by expanding a high-pressure refrigerant stream while extracting mechanical work from the expansion. Typical examples include turbo-expanders or expander-driven refrigeration cycles that combine cooling with power recovery in gas-liquefaction or solidification operations.
[F25J 1/0052] by vaporising a liquid refrigerant stream -- F25J 1/0052 relates to processes or apparatus that liquefy or solidify gases by vaporising a liquid refrigerant stream, using the refrigerant's phase change to absorb heat and achieve the required low-temperature treatment.
[F25J 1/0055] originating from an incorporated cascade -- A cascade-type arrangement incorporated into a liquefaction or solidification plant, where two or more refrigeration cycles operate in series so that the output of one stage serves as the low-temperature source for the next, enabling efficient cooling of gases or gas mixtures.
[F25J 1/0057] after expansion of the liquid refrigerant stream with extraction of work -- This subclass covers devices or methods in which a liquid refrigerant is expanded and mechanical work is recovered, such as expansion turbines or work-extracting expanders used in refrigeration or gas-liquefaction cycles. The focus is on the stage after the liquid stream leaves the condenser and before it evaporates, where part of the pressure drop is converted to useful work.
[F25J 1/006] characterised by the refrigerant fluid used  refrigerants in vapor compression cycles  F25B9/002 , refrigerant materials  per se   C09K5/00 -- The F25J 1/006 subgroup concerns liquefaction or solidification of gases where the key feature is the refrigerant fluid employed. It includes vapor-compression cycles defined by the specific refrigerants used (F25B9/002) and the refrigerant substances themselves as defined in C09K5/00.
[F25J 1/0062] Light or noble gases, mixtures thereof  F25J1/007  takes precedence -- This CPC group concerns refrigeration or cooling processes and equipment that liquefy or solidify light and noble gases or their mixtures by applying pressure and low temperatures. It includes methods for handling hydrogen, helium, neon, argon and similar gases. The more detailed subgroup F25J 1/007 now takes precedence over this entry.
[F25J 1/0065] Helium -- F25J 1/0065 deals with the use of helium in processes or apparatus for liquefying or solidifying gases or gaseous mixtures, where pressure and low-temperature treatment are applied. It classifies technologies that employ helium as a refrigerant or working fluid in mechanical-engineering cooling and refrigeration systems.
[F25J 1/0067] Hydrogen -- F25J 1/0067 Hydrogen covers methods and apparatus for liquefying or solidifying hydrogen by applying pressure and low-temperature treatment, situated within refrigeration and cooling technologies in mechanical engineering.
[F25J 1/007] Primary atmospheric gases, mixtures thereof -- This subgroup covers processes and apparatus for liquefying or solidifying the main components of air-nitrogen, oxygen, argon and their mixtures-by applying high pressure and low temperature. It is used in refrigeration and gas-separation technologies that handle primary atmospheric gases.
[F25J 1/0072] Nitrogen -- F25J 1/0072 relates to processes and apparatus for liquefying or solidifying nitrogen, or using nitrogen as a refrigerant in cycles that achieve low-temperature, high-pressure treatment of gases. It covers equipment and methods specifically designed to handle nitrogen in its gaseous, liquid or solid states for cooling, separation or storage applications.
[F25J 1/0075] Oxygen -- The code F25J 1/0075 designates technologies for liquefying or solidifying oxygen, encompassing cryogenic processes, equipment, and methods that handle oxygen in refrigeration or cooling cycles, including its separation, storage, and transport in liquid or solid form.
[F25J 1/0077] Argon -- F25J 1/0077 concerns the use of argon in processes or devices for liquefying or solidifying gases by applying pressure and low temperature. It includes methods for producing liquid or solid argon and equipment designed to handle the gas under these conditions.
[F25J 1/008] Hydrocarbons -- This CPC subclass covers technologies for liquefying or solidifying hydrocarbon gases, including methods and equipment that use pressure and low temperature to achieve phase change for storage, transport, or processing. It is situated under mechanical engineering, refrigeration or cooling, and gas separation by pressure and cold treatment.
[F25J 1/0082] Methane -- The entry covers methods, processes and apparatus for liquefying, solidifying or otherwise treating methane, a primary component of natural gas, often for storage, transport or industrial use. It includes equipment and techniques that apply pressure and low-temperature treatment to convert methane into a liquid or solid state.
[F25J 1/0085] Ethane; Ethylene -- The CPC subclass F25J 1/0085 covers technologies for liquefying or solidifying the gases ethane and ethylene, including related processes and apparatus. It falls under mechanical engineering → refrigeration or cooling → liquefaction, solidification or separation of gases by pressure and cold treatment. This entry addresses methods and equipment specifically aimed at handling these hydrocarbons in the gas-to-liquid or solid state.
[F25J 1/0087] Propane; Propylene -- F25J 1/0087 covers technologies for liquefying, solidifying or otherwise cold-treating the gases propane and propylene, including the processes and apparatus such as compressors, heat exchangers and refrigeration cycles specifically designed for these hydrocarbons.
[F25J 1/009] Hydrocarbons with four or more carbon atoms -- F25J 1/009 pertains to processes and apparatus for liquefying or solidifying hydrocarbons that contain four or more carbon atoms, falling under mechanical engineering, refrigeration or cooling, and the treatment of gases or gaseous mixtures by pressure and cold.
[F25J 1/0092] Mixtures of hydrocarbons comprising possibly also minor amounts of nitrogen -- This subclass deals with hydrocarbon mixtures, optionally containing minor nitrogen, employed as working fluids in processes or apparatus for liquefying or solidifying gases and gaseous mixtures.
[F25J 1/0095] Oxides of carbon, e.g. CO 2 -- The CPC subgroup F25J 1/0095 concerns the use of carbon oxides, especially carbon dioxide, in processes or apparatus for liquefying or solidifying gases. It includes methods and equipment that employ CO₂ as a refrigerant, working fluid or coolant under high-pressure and low-temperature conditions in refrigeration, heat-pump or gas-separation systems.
[F25J 1/0097] Others, e.g. F-, Cl-, HF-, HClF-, HCl-hydrocarbons etc. or mixtures thereof -- The code F25J 1/0097 relates to cooling and refrigeration processes used to liquefy or solidify gases such as fluorine, chlorine, hydrogen fluoride, HClF, HCl-hydrocarbons and similar mixtures. It falls under mechanical engineering, refrigeration or cooling, specifically the sub-category dealing with pressure and cold treatment for gas separation. The entry covers any apparatus or method for handling these particular gases.
[F25J 1/02] requiring the use of refrigeration, e.g. of helium or hydrogen -- This subclass covers processes or apparatus for liquefying or solidifying gases that depend on refrigeration, such as using liquid helium or hydrogen to achieve the required low temperatures. It includes systems that integrate external cooling cycles to provide the cold needed for gas liquefaction or solidification.
[F25J 1/0201] using only internal refrigeration means, i.e. without external refrigeration -- F25J 1/0201 concerns processes or devices that liquefy or solidify gases or gaseous mixtures solely with internal refrigeration, meaning the cooling cycle is generated within the system itself and no external refrigerant source is employed. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering, refrigeration or cooling, specifically the liquefaction, solidification or separation of gases by pressure and cold treatment.
[F25J 1/0202] in a quasi-closed internal refrigeration loop  F25J1/0208 ,  F25J1/0219 ,  F25J1/0224  take precedence -- The subclass F25J 1/0202 covers refrigeration or cooling systems that operate in a quasi-closed internal loop, where the refrigerant circulates largely within a sealed circuit and only limited external exchange occurs. It applies to processes or apparatus for liquefying or solidifying gases using such loops, and is subordinate to the more specific groups F25J 1/0208, F25J 1/0219 and F25J 1/0224, which take precedence.
[F25J 1/0203] using a single-component refrigerant [SCR] fluid in a closed vapor compression cycle  F25J1/0211  takes precedence -- The subclass F25J 1/0203 relates to closed vapor-compression refrigeration cycles that employ a single-component refrigerant fluid. It covers the use of a pure substance as the working medium for liquefying or solidifying gases, and takes precedence over the broader subclass F25J 1/0211.
[F25J 1/0204] as a single flow SCR cycle -- The subclass covers refrigeration processes that liquefy or solidify gases by applying pressure and low temperature within a single-flow SCR (self-refrigerating) cycle, meaning the working fluid passes once through the system without recirculation. It includes apparatus designed for this method.
[F25J 1/0205] as a dual level SCR refrigeration cascade -- A dual-level SCR refrigeration cascade is a vapor-compression system that employs two consecutive compression stages, each with its own refrigerant circuit, arranged in cascade to achieve very low temperatures for liquefying or solidifying gases or gas mixtures. The configuration allows the high-stage compressor to handle the heat rejected by the low-stage, improving efficiency and enabling the production of cryogenic conditions.
[F25J 1/0207] as at least a three level SCR refrigeration cascade -- The code F25J 1/0207 covers refrigeration systems that employ a cascade of at least three stages using scroll compressors (SCR) to achieve very low temperatures for the liquefaction or solidification of gases or gas mixtures. It relates to apparatus and processes where successive compression and cooling loops are linked to increase overall efficiency and reach the required pressure-temperature conditions. This falls within the broader class of mechanical engineering methods for gas separation by pressure and cold treatment.
[F25J 1/0208] in combination with an internal quasi-closed refrigeration loop, e.g. with deep flash recycle loop  F25J1/021  takes precedence -- F25J 1/0208 covers processes or apparatus that liquefy or solidify gases using an internal quasi-closed refrigeration loop, for example a deep flash recycle loop, where the refrigerant is partially recovered and reused within the system. This sub-category falls under mechanical engineering, refrigeration or cooling, and deals with pressure-and cold-treatment separation of gaseous mixtures.
[F25J 1/0209] as at least a three level refrigeration cascade -- The subclass F25J 1/0209 pertains to refrigeration systems that employ at least three successive cooling stages, or cascades, to achieve lower temperatures. It includes processes and apparatus where each stage uses a different refrigerant or pressure level to progressively liquefy or solidify gases.
[F25J 1/021] using a deep flash recycle loop -- F25J 1/021 covers processes that employ a deep-flash recycle loop, where a portion of the cooled gas or liquid is flashed to a lower pressure, the resulting cold vapour is recompressed and returned to the main liquefaction or solidification circuit to improve energy efficiency and product yield.
[F25J 1/0211] using a multi-component refrigerant [MCR] fluid in a closed vapor compression cycle -- F25J 1/0211 covers processes and apparatus in which a multi-component refrigerant fluid is employed within a closed vapor-compression cycle to liquefy or solidify gases or gaseous mixtures, typically enhancing thermodynamic efficiency and allowing tailored properties for specific cooling applications.
[F25J 1/0212] as a single flow MCR cycle -- This subclass covers devices or methods that use a single-flow mixed-refrigerant (MCR) cycle to liquefy or solidify gases. The cycle circulates one refrigerant mixture through compression, heat exchange and expansion without separate streams, simplifying the plant and improving efficiency. It is employed in cryogenic applications such as liquefied natural gas production and air-separation processes.
[F25J 1/0214] as a dual level refrigeration cascade with at least one MCR cycle -- The subclass relates to dual-level refrigeration cascade arrangements that incorporate at least one mixed-refrigerant (MCR) cycle, using successive stages of compression and expansion to achieve very low temperatures for liquefying or solidifying gases or gas mixtures.
[F25J 1/0215] with one SCR cycle -- This subclass deals with liquefaction or solidification of gases using a single-compression refrigeration (SCR) cycle, where the gas is compressed, cooled by heat exchange, expanded to a lower temperature and then returned to the compressor in a single loop. It covers apparatus specifically arranged for that cycle.
[F25J 1/0216] using a C3 pre-cooling cycle -- The subclass F25J 1/0216 covers processes or equipment that employ a C3 pre-cooling cycle as part of the liquefaction or solidification of gases or gas mixtures, typically within refrigeration or cooling systems.
[F25J 1/0217] as at least a three level refrigeration cascade with at least one MCR cycle
[F25J 1/0218] with one or more SCR cycles, e.g. with a C3 pre-cooling cycle -- The sub-group F25J 1/0218 covers processes or equipment for liquefying or solidifying gases that employ one or more SCR (successive compression-refrigeration) cycles, for example a C3 pre-cooling stage, to enhance the overall cooling efficiency. It falls under mechanical-engineering refrigeration methods that use pressure and cold treatment to separate gaseous mixtures.
[F25J 1/0219] in combination with an internal quasi-closed refrigeration loop, e.g. using a deep flash recycle loop -- This subclass pertains to methods or equipment that liquefy or solidify gases by employing an internal quasi-closed refrigeration loop, such as a deep flash recycle loop, where the refrigerant is repeatedly flashed and recompressed within the same system to enhance efficiency and reduce external energy input.
[F25J 1/0221] using the cold stored in an external cryogenic component in an open refrigeration loop -- This subclass covers methods or apparatus that draw the cooling effect stored in an external cryogenic component and apply it to an open-loop refrigeration system, where the refrigerant is not returned to the source but is expelled after use. It includes using a pre-cooled fluid from a separate tank to absorb heat in the evaporator and then discarding or venting the warmed fluid.
[F25J 1/0222] in combination with an intermediate heat exchange fluid between the cryogenic component and the fluid to be liquefied  F25J1/0224  takes precedence -- The subclass covers methods or apparatus for liquefying gases in which an intermediate heat-exchange fluid is placed between the cryogenic component and the gas to be liquefied, allowing indirect cooling and better thermal management; it is superseded by the related subclass F25J 1/0224.
[F25J 1/0223] in combination with the subsequent re-vaporisation of the originally liquefied gas at a second location to produce the external cryogenic component -- The subclass concerns systems that first liquefy a gas or gaseous mixture, transport it, and then re-vaporise it at a separate location to supply an external cryogenic component. It includes the combined steps of liquefaction, conveyance and on-site re-evaporation for cooling or other cryogenic purposes.
[F25J 1/0224] in combination with an internal quasi-closed refrigeration loop  F25J1/0208 ,  F25J1/0219  take precedence
[F25J 1/0225] using other external refrigeration means not provided before, e.g. heat driven absorption chillers -- This CPC subclass covers processes or apparatus that liquefy or solidify gases using external refrigeration sources that were not previously integrated, such as heat-driven absorption chillers. It falls under mechanical engineering, refrigeration or cooling, specifically the subcategory for pressure and cold treatment of gases.
[F25J 1/0227] within a refrigeration cascade -- The F25J 1/0227 subclass covers refrigeration cascade systems, where two or more refrigeration cycles are linked in series so that the condenser of the higher-temperature cycle serves as the evaporator of the lower-temperature cycle. It includes apparatus and methods that use intermediate refrigerants to achieve very low temperatures by successive pressure and cold treatment of gases or mixtures.
[F25J 1/0228] Coupling of the liquefaction unit to other units or processes, so-called integrated processes  combined plants, e.g. engine plant combined with an industrial process  F01K23/064 ; gas turbine plants in combination with other processes  F02C6/00 -- The code F25J 1/0228 addresses the integration of a gas-liquefaction unit with other process units, describing combined-plant arrangements where the liquefaction system is coupled to power-generation equipment such as engines or gas turbines, or to additional industrial processes, enabling coordinated operation and energy recovery.
[F25J 1/0229] Integration with a unit for using hydrocarbons, e.g. consuming hydrocarbons as feed stock -- This CPC subclass covers systems where a plant for liquefying or solidifying gases is combined with a downstream unit that consumes hydrocarbons, such as using the hydrocarbons as feedstock for chemical or energy processes. The integration may involve heat exchange, shared compressors or combined cycles to improve overall efficiency. It applies to designs that simultaneously produce liquid gases and utilize hydrocarbons.
[F25J 1/023] for the combustion as fuels, i.e. integration with the fuel gas system -- F25J 1/023 pertains to processes and apparatus that integrate the liquefaction or solidification of gases with their use as combustion fuels, linking the liquefied gas directly to a fuel-gas system. It covers combined cycles, heat-exchange arrangements and control strategies that enable the treated gas to be fed to burners or engines.
[F25J 1/0231] for the working-up of the hydrocarbon feed, e.g. reinjection of heavier hydrocarbons into the liquefied gas -- The subclass covers processes or apparatus that treat the hydrocarbon feed after liquefaction, for example by reinjecting heavier hydrocarbons into the liquefied gas stream. It belongs to mechanical engineering, refrigeration or cooling, specifically to the liquefaction, solidification or separation of gases or gaseous mixtures by pressure and cold treatment.
[F25J 1/0232] integration within a pressure letdown station of a high pressure pipeline system -- F25J 1/0232 covers the integration of equipment in a pressure-letdown station of a high-pressure pipeline system, focusing on how the station is combined with liquefaction or solidification units for gases. It deals with the arrangement and interfacing of valves, compressors, heat exchangers and control devices that reduce pipeline pressure while preparing the gas for subsequent cooling or phase change.
[F25J 1/0234] Integration with a cryogenic air separation unit  cryogenic separation of air  F25J3/04 -- This subclass covers processes and apparatus that combine the liquefaction or solidification of gases with a cryogenic air-separation unit, describing how air is separated at low temperatures and how the liquefaction system is integrated with the separation plant. It includes design features, control strategies and equipment arrangements that enable efficient cryogenic separation of air components within a single integrated system.
[F25J 1/0235] Heat exchange integration -- F25J 1/0235 covers the integration of heat-exchange elements within systems that liquefy or solidify gases or gas mixtures by pressure and cold treatment, using heat from one part of the process to heat another to improve overall energy efficiency.
[F25J 1/0236] providing refrigeration for different processes treating not the same feed stream -- This subclass covers systems that supply refrigeration to multiple separate processes within the same installation where the processes handle different feed streams. It includes arrangements where each stream receives independent cooling, often using shared compressors or separate refrigerant loops, enabling simultaneous liquefaction or solidification of distinct gases.
[F25J 1/0237] integrating refrigeration provided for liquefaction and purification/treatment of the gas to be liquefied, e.g. heavy hydrocarbon removal from natural gas  details related to rectification  F25J3/02 ; details related to partial condensation  F25J3/06 ; working-up natural gas  C10L3/10 -- F25J 1/0237 covers systems that combine refrigeration for liquefying gases with simultaneous purification or treatment of the feed gas, such as removing heavy hydrocarbons from natural gas. It includes aspects of rectification, partial condensation and other working-up steps needed to produce a clean liquefied product.
[F25J 1/0238] Purification or treatment step is integrated within one refrigeration cycle only, i.e. the same or single refrigeration cycle provides feed gas cooling (if present) and overhead gas cooling -- The classification covers refrigeration cycles that incorporate a single-step purification or treatment function, where the same cycle cools both the incoming feed gas, if present, and the overhead gas. It applies to processes and apparatus that achieve gas liquefaction, solidification, or separation while integrating the treatment within one refrigeration loop.
[F25J 1/0239] Purification or treatment step being integrated between two refrigeration cycles of a refrigeration cascade, i.e. first cycle providing feed gas cooling and second cycle providing overhead gas cooling -- The entry covers a process or apparatus that inserts a purification or treatment stage between the two cycles of a refrigeration cascade, with the first cycle cooling the feed gas and the second cycle cooling the overhead gas, enabling continuous conditioning of gases during liquefaction or solidification.
[F25J 1/0241] wherein the overhead cooling comprises providing reflux for a fractionation step -- The classification covers a liquefaction or solidification process in which the overhead cooling stage incorporates a reflux loop that returns a portion of the condensed liquid to a fractionation step, enhancing separation efficiency and product purity.
[F25J 1/0242] Waste heat recovery, e.g. from heat of compression -- The CPC subclass F25J 1/0242 covers methods or devices that reclaim waste heat, such as the heat generated during gas compression, within processes for liquefying or solidifying gases or gas mixtures. It focuses on using recovered thermal energy to improve overall efficiency of refrigeration and gas-liquefaction cycles.
[F25J 1/0243] Start-up or control of the process; Details of the apparatus used; Details of the refrigerant compression system used -- The F25J 1/0243 subclass covers methods and devices for initiating and regulating processes that liquefy or solidify gases, specifying how the equipment is configured and operated. It includes detailed features of the refrigerant compression system and related control mechanisms used in such pressure-and-cold treatment apparatus.
[F25J 1/0244] Operation; Control and regulation; Instrumentation  F25J1/0279  takes precedence -- F25J 1/0244 covers the operation, control and regulation, and instrumentation of equipment used to liquefy or solidify gases or gaseous mixtures by pressure and cold treatment. This includes devices that monitor temperature, pressure, flow or other process variables to maintain desired conditions. It is subordinated to the broader F25J 1/0279 category, which takes precedence in the classification hierarchy.
[F25J 1/0245] Different modes, i.e. 'runs', of operation; Process control -- F25J 1/0245 covers the control of processes that liquefy or solidify gases, including methods for selecting and managing different operating runs or modes. It includes systems that adjust parameters to switch between cycles, monitor performance and ensure stable operation of refrigeration or cooling apparatus.
[F25J 1/0247] start-up of the process -- F25J 1/0247 addresses the start-up stage of processes and apparatus used to liquefy or solidify gases or gaseous mixtures, detailing the methods for initiating operation. It includes procedures for preparing equipment, establishing the required temperature and pressure conditions, and managing flow as the system begins its refrigeration or cryogenic cycle.
[F25J 1/0248] Stopping of the process, e.g. defrosting or deriming, maintenance; Back-up mode or systems -- F25J 1/0248 covers methods and devices for stopping a gas-liquefaction or solidification process, including defrosting, deriming and routine maintenance, as well as backup modes or auxiliary systems that take over operation when the main process is interrupted. It is situated in the mechanical-engineering field of refrigeration and cooling, specifically under the subgroup for liquefaction, solidification or separation of gases by pressure and cold treatment.
[F25J 1/0249] Controlling refrigerant inventory, i.e. composition or quantity  charging or discharging refrigerants in cooling systems  F25B45/00
[F25J 1/025] Details related to the refrigerant production or treatment, e.g. make-up supply from feed gas itself -- The subclass concerns methods and equipment for producing or treating refrigerants, especially the generation of make-up refrigerant directly from the feed gas, including related processes for supplying or conditioning the refrigerant within liquefaction or solidification systems.
[F25J 1/0251] Intermittent or alternating process, so-called batch process, e.g. "peak-shaving" -- The subclass F25J 1/0251 covers intermittent or alternating, batch-type processes used in the liquefaction or solidification of gases or gaseous mixtures, such as peak-shaving operations that start and stop the treatment in cycles to balance production or energy demand. It is a specialized subcategory within mechanical engineering for refrigeration and cooling processes that manage gases by pressure and cold treatment.
[F25J 1/0252] Control strategy, e.g. advanced process control or dynamic modeling -- F25J 1/0252 relates to control strategies such as advanced process control and dynamic modeling for equipment and processes that liquefy or solidify gases or gas mixtures. The class falls under mechanical engineering, refrigeration and cooling, focusing on pressure-and cold-treatment methods for gas separation.
[F25J 1/0254] controlling particular process parameter, e.g. pressure, temperature -- F25J 1/0254 relates to processes or apparatus for liquefying or solidifying gases in which a particular parameter-typically pressure, temperature or a similar variable-is actively monitored and regulated to achieve the desired phase change.
[F25J 1/0255] controlling the composition of the feed or liquefied gas, e.g. to achieve a particular heating value of natural gas -- The subgroup deals with methods or apparatus for adjusting the composition of the feed gas or the liquefied product, such as by mixing, separating or adding components, to obtain a specified heating value or other desired properties of the final gas. It is applied in refrigeration or cooling processes that liquefy or solidify gases.
[F25J 1/0256] Safety aspects of operation  F25J1/0298  takes precedence -- The entry F25J 1/0256 concerns safety aspects of operating equipment that liquefies or solidifies gases or gaseous mixtures, including measures such as monitoring, protective devices, emergency shutdown and hazard prevention. It falls under refrigeration and cooling processes, with the more specific subclass F25J1/0298 taking precedence.
[F25J 1/0257] Construction and layout of liquefaction equipments, e.g. valves, machines  F25J1/0279  takes precedence -- The code F25J 1/0257 covers the design and arrangement of equipment used to liquefy or solidify gases, including components such as valves, compressors, heat exchangers and other machinery. It falls under mechanical engineering, refrigeration, and the process of gas liquefaction by pressure and cold treatment.
[F25J 1/0258] vertical layout of the equipments within in the cold box -- F25J 1/0258 covers the vertical arrangement of components inside a cold box used in processes that liquefy or solidify gases. It relates to positioning equipment such as heat exchangers, compressors and separators one above another to improve space efficiency and temperature gradients. The classification falls under mechanical engineering, refrigeration and gas liquefaction technologies.
[F25J 1/0259] Modularity and arrangement of parts of the liquefaction unit and in particular of the cold box, e.g. pre-fabrication, assembling and erection, dimensions, horizontal layout "plot" -- The subclass F25J 1/0259 covers design and spatial organization of liquefaction units, especially the cold box, including prefabricated modules, assembly methods, erection procedures, overall dimensions and horizontal layout plans. It addresses how the parts are arranged to optimise construction, installation and space utilisation in gas-liquefying plants.
[F25J 1/0261] Details of cold box insulation, housing and internal structure  buildings forming parts of cooling plants  E04H5/10 -- F25J 1/0261 covers the design and construction details of cold-box insulation, housings and internal structures that form the buildings of cooling plants, such as those used for liquefying or solidifying gases. It addresses material choices, thermal-break features and structural layouts that ensure efficient cold-box performance, linking to the building-related aspects classified under E04H5/10.
[F25J 1/0262] Details of the cold heat exchange system  constructional details  F25J5/00 , construction of cold-exchangers in general  F28 -- The subclass F25J 1/0262 concerns the constructional details of cold heat-exchange systems used in processes that liquefy or solidify gases or gaseous mixtures. It falls under mechanical engineering, refrigeration and cooling, specifically the design of apparatus for pressure- and cold-treatment of gases, and relates to general cold-exchanger construction (F25J5/00, F28).
[F25J 1/0263] using different types of heat exchangers -- This subclass covers processes or apparatus where multiple heat exchangers of different types are employed to liquefy or solidify gases or gas mixtures, typically in refrigeration or cooling systems. It focuses on the use of varied heat exchange configurations to achieve the required temperature differentials.
[F25J 1/0264] Arrangement of heat exchanger cores in parallel with different functions, e.g. different cooling streams  F25J1/0272  takes precedence -- F25J 1/0264 classifies arrangements where multiple heat-exchanger cores operate in parallel but perform distinct functions, such as handling separate cooling streams in liquefaction or solidification processes. It falls under mechanical engineering - refrigeration or cooling - liquefaction, solidification or separation of gases by pressure and cold treatment. The more specific subclass F25J 1/0272 supersedes it.
[F25J 1/0265] comprising cores associated exclusively with the cooling of a refrigerant stream, e.g. for auto-refrigeration or economizer -- This subclass covers apparatus in which cooling cores are dedicated solely to the refrigerant stream, such as those used in automotive refrigeration or economizer circuits. The cores provide heat exchange for the refrigerant without handling other fluids, facilitating the liquefaction or solidification of gases under pressure and cold treatment.
[F25J 1/0267] using flash gas as heat sink -- F25J 1/0267 refers to methods or equipment for liquefying or solidifying gases in which flash gas-gas that expands rapidly after pressure release-is employed as a heat-sink to absorb the cooling load. The technique is used in refrigeration or cooling systems that separate or solidify gaseous mixtures by pressure and cold treatment.
[F25J 1/0268] using a dedicated refrigeration means  F25J1/0296  takes precedence -- The CPC subclass F25J 1/0268 covers processes or apparatus that liquefy or solidify gases by employing a dedicated refrigeration system, meaning a separate cooling unit is used solely for that purpose. It lies within mechanical engineering, refrigeration or cooling, specifically the liquefaction, solidification or separation of gases by pressure and cold treatment.
[F25J 1/0269] Arrangement of liquefaction units or equipments fulfilling the same process step, e.g. multiple "trains" concept  F25J1/0294  takes precedence -- The code F25J 1/0269 covers configurations where several liquefaction units or equipment that perform the same processing step are arranged in parallel or series, such as multiple "trains" of compressors or exchangers, to increase capacity or improve efficiency. This includes the layout and interconnection of identical units within a refrigeration or gas-liquefaction plant.
[F25J 1/027] Inter-connecting multiple hot equipments upstream of the cold box -- The code relates to systems that link several hot process units such as compressors, heaters or pre-coolers before the main cold-box in a gas-liquefaction or solidification plant. By inter-connecting these upstream equipments, the arrangement improves heat recovery, flow distribution and overall efficiency of the low-temperature treatment.
[F25J 1/0271] Inter-connecting multiple cold equipments within or downstream of the cold box -- F25J 1/0271 covers arrangements that interconnect two or more cold equipment units either within the cold box or downstream of it. The interconnection may share refrigerant circuits, heat exchangers or control lines to enhance overall performance, balance loads, or simplify system integration in liquefaction or solidification processes.
[F25J 1/0272] Multiple identical heat exchangers in parallel -- F25J 1/0272 covers configurations in which two or more identical heat exchangers are connected in parallel to the same fluid circuit. The parallel arrangement increases overall heat-transfer capacity or provides redundancy while each exchanger remains the same size and type.
[F25J 1/0274] Retrofitting or revamping of an existing liquefaction unit -- The code F25J 1/0274 refers to processes or apparatus that modify, upgrade or re-engineer an already installed liquefaction plant, adapting it to new requirements, improving performance or extending its service life. It falls under the mechanical-engineering branch of refrigeration, specifically the subgroup dealing with liquefaction, solidification or separation of gases by pressure and cold treatment.
[F25J 1/0275] adapted for special use of the liquefaction unit, e.g. portable or transportable devices -- The subclass F25J 1/0275 pertains to liquefaction units that have been adapted for special uses such as portable or transportable devices, enabling the liquefying or solidifying of gases in mobile applications. It falls under mechanical engineering, refrigeration and the processing of gases by pressure and cold treatment.
[F25J 1/0276] Laboratory or other miniature devices -- F25J 1/0276 covers small-scale or laboratory equipment designed to liquefy or solidify gases or gas mixtures using pressure and low temperature, such as bench-top cryogenic units, miniature compressors, or compact heat exchangers. These devices are intended for experimental, research or educational use rather than industrial-scale production.
[F25J 1/0277] Offshore use, e.g. during shipping -- F25J 1/0277 covers equipment and methods for liquefying or solidifying gases that are designed for offshore applications such as ships, rigs or other maritime settings. The entry pertains to refrigeration or cooling systems used in the handling of cryogenic or pressurised gas streams during transport or offshore operation.
[F25J 1/0278] Unit being stationary, e.g. on floating barge or fixed platform -- The code F25J 1/0278 covers stationary units used for liquefying or solidifying gases, where the whole plant is fixed in place, for example installed on a floating barge or a permanent platform. It pertains to the design, installation and operation of such immobile gas-handling installations.
[F25J 1/0279] Compression of refrigerant or internal recycle fluid, e.g. kind of compressor, accumulator, suction drum etc. -- This subclass covers devices and methods for compressing refrigerants or internal recycle fluids within refrigeration cycles, including various compressor types, accumulators and suction drums used to raise the pressure of the working fluid prior to condensation. It pertains to processes or apparatus for liquefying or solidifying gases by pressure and cooling.
[F25J 1/0281] characterised by the type of prime driver, e.g. hot gas expander -- F25J 1/0281 groups refrigeration and cooling processes that liquefy or solidify gases by pressure and cold treatment and are distinguished by the kind of prime driver employed, such as a hot-gas expander, which provides the main mechanical power for the cycle.
[F25J 1/0282] Steam turbine as the prime mechanical driver -- F25J 1/0282 covers systems in which a steam turbine provides the primary mechanical power for processes that liquefy or solidify gases or gas mixtures. The classification applies to apparatus where the turbine drives compressors, expanders or other equipment used in cryogenic or pressure-based gas treatment.
[F25J 1/0283] Gas turbine as the prime mechanical driver -- The subclass covers systems for liquefying or solidifying gases in which the main source of mechanical power is a gas turbine, rather than a piston or electric motor. It includes arrangements where the turbine drives compressors, pumps or other equipment used in pressure-based cold-treatment processes.
[F25J 1/0284] Electrical motor as the prime mechanical driver -- The subclass F25J 1/0284 covers refrigeration or cooling systems that use an electric motor as the principal mechanical drive for liquefying or solidifying gases or gas mixtures. It includes apparatus where the motor directly powers compressors, expanders or other components that generate the required pressure and low temperature.
[F25J 1/0285] Combination of different types of drivers mechanically coupled to the same refrigerant compressor, possibly split on multiple compressor casings -- The subclass covers systems where a refrigerant compressor is driven by two or more different types of power sources that are mechanically linked, such as electric and combustion engines, and may be arranged in separate compressor housings that together operate the same compressor unit.
[F25J 1/0287] including an electrical motor -- F25J 1/0287 covers systems or methods for liquefying or solidifying gases that incorporate an electrical motor as part of the drive or compression mechanism, falling under mechanical engineering, refrigeration or cooling, and the sub-field of gas liquefaction and solidification by pressure and cold treatment.
[F25J 1/0288] using work extraction by mechanical coupling of compression and expansion of the refrigerant, so-called companders -- F25J 1/0288 concerns methods and devices that extract work by mechanically coupling the compression and expansion stages of a refrigerant cycle, a technique known as a compander, to improve efficiency in liquefying or solidifying gases or gas mixtures.
[F25J 1/0289] Use of different types of prime drivers of at least two refrigerant compressors in a cascade refrigeration system -- This classification covers cascade refrigeration arrangements where at least two compressors are driven by different kinds of prime movers, such as electric, engine, or other power sources, to improve efficiency or performance. It includes methods and apparatus that coordinate multiple drive types within the cascade.
[F25J 1/029] Mechanically coupling of different refrigerant compressors in a cascade refrigeration system to a common driver -- F25J 1/029 covers the mechanical coupling of different refrigerant compressors in a cascade refrigeration system to a common driver, enabling them to operate together on a single power source. It falls under the broader class for processes and apparatus that liquefy or solidify gases by pressure and cold treatment.
[F25J 1/0291] Refrigerant compression by combined gas compression and liquid pumping -- The F25J 1/0291 subclass covers refrigeration cycles that compress the refrigerant using a combination of gas-phase compression and liquid-phase pumping, often employing hybrid compressors that first raise the pressure of the gas and then move the condensed liquid to achieve the desired pressure increase. This approach is used to improve efficiency in systems that liquefy or solidify gases by pressure and cold treatment.
[F25J 1/0292] Refrigerant compression by cold or cryogenic suction of the refrigerant gas -- The subclass F25J 1/0292 covers methods and devices that compress a refrigerant by drawing the gas into the compressor under cold or cryogenic suction conditions, using the low temperature of the gas to assist compression. It applies to systems where the refrigerant is chilled before or during the suction stroke to improve efficiency or enable low-temperature operation.
[F25J 1/0294] Multiple compressor casings/strings in parallel, e.g. split arrangement -- The code covers refrigerating or cooling apparatus that employs two or more compressor casings or strings operating in parallel, often in a split-arrangement, to share load, improve efficiency, or provide redundancy. Such configurations are used in systems that liquefy or solidify gases by applying pressure and cold treatment.
[F25J 1/0295] Shifting of the compression load between different cooling stages within a refrigerant cycle or within a cascade refrigeration system -- It covers methods or apparatus that transfer the compression work between different cooling stages in a multi-stage refrigerant cycle or cascade-refrigeration system, allowing the load to be shifted to optimise efficiency, capacity or temperature control.
[F25J 1/0296] Removal of the heat of compression, e.g. within an inter- or afterstage-cooler against an ambient heat sink -- F25J 1/0296 pertains to processes or apparatus that extract the heat generated by compressing gases, typically by passing the compressed gas through an interstage or after-stage cooler that transfers the thermal energy to the surrounding air or another ambient sink. This heat-removal step is essential in liquefaction, solidification or separation of gases.
[F25J 1/0297] using an externally chilled fluid, e.g. chilled water -- F25J 1/0297 covers methods and devices that employ an externally chilled fluid, for example chilled water, to provide the low temperatures needed for liquefying or solidifying gases or gas mixtures in refrigeration or cooling systems. The external cooling fluid removes heat from the refrigerant circuit, allowing the desired phase change of the target gas.
[F25J 1/0298] Safety aspects and control of the refrigerant compression system, e.g. anti-surge control -- The F25J 1/0298 entry covers safety measures and control strategies for refrigerant compression systems, including devices and methods that prevent surge, over-pressure, and other hazardous operating conditions during compression. It encompasses anti-surge control mechanisms, monitoring, and protective functions designed to ensure reliable and safe operation of refrigeration or cooling plants.
[F25J 2200/00] Processes or apparatus using separation by rectification -- The F25J 2200/00 class covers processes and equipment that separate gases or gaseous mixtures by rectification, that is, fractional distillation carried out under pressure and low-temperature conditions. It includes methods where liquefaction, solidification or other cold-treatment steps are used to achieve the separation.
[F25J 2200/02] in a single pressure main column system -- The CPC subclass F25J 2200/02 pertains to refrigeration or cooling methods that liquefy, solidify or separate gases by applying pressure and low temperature, employing a single-pressure main column for rectification. It covers apparatus and processes that achieve gas mixture separation in a single-pressure column system.
[F25J 2200/04] in a dual pressure main column system -- F25J 2200/04 classifies refrigeration or cooling processes that separate gases by rectification using a dual-pressure main column, where the column operates at two distinct pressures to improve liquefaction, solidification, or separation of gaseous mixtures.
[F25J 2200/06] in a classical double column flow-sheet, i.e. with thermal coupling by a main reboiler-condenser in the bottom of low pressure respectively top of high pressure column -- This subclass covers processes where two rectification columns of differing pressure are thermally linked through a single reboiler-condenser that acts as the reboiler for the low-pressure column and the condenser for the high-pressure column, enabling energy integration in a classic double-column flow-sheet. It is used for efficient liquefaction and separation of gas mixtures by pressure and cold treatment.
[F25J 2200/08] in a triple pressure main column system -- The code F25J 2200/08 covers refrigeration or cooling processes that separate gases by rectification using a triple-pressure main column system, where the column operates at three distinct pressure levels to achieve liquefaction, solidification, or separation of gas mixtures through combined pressure and cold treatment.
[F25J 2200/10] in a quadruple, or more, column or pressure system -- F25J 2200/10 pertains to refrigeration or cooling systems that separate gas mixtures by rectification using four or more columns or pressure stages, typically in liquefaction, solidification or gas-separation processes. It covers apparatus and methods where multiple stages enhance the efficiency of pressure-driven, low-temperature gas separation.
[F25J 2200/20] in an elevated pressure multiple column system wherein the lowest pressure column is at a pressure well above the minimum pressure needed to overcome pressure drop to reject the products to atmosphere -- This CPC subclass covers refrigeration or cooling processes that separate gas mixtures by rectification in a multi-column system operating at elevated pressures. The lowest-pressure column is maintained at a pressure considerably higher than the minimum required to overcome the pressure drop for venting the product stream to the atmosphere.
[F25J 2200/30] using a side column in a single pressure column system -- F25J 2200/30 covers apparatus or processes that employ a side column attached to a single-pressure rectification column for separating gases or gaseous mixtures by pressure and cold treatment, typically used in refrigeration or cooling systems to achieve liquefaction, solidification, or gas separation.
[F25J 2200/32] using a side column fed by a stream from the high pressure column -- This subclass relates to refrigeration or cooling processes that separate gases by pressure and cold treatment, specifically using a side column that is supplied with a stream from the high-pressure column in a rectification system. It covers apparatus or methods where the side column assists in liquefaction, solidification, or gas mixture separation through this side-stream feed arrangement.
[F25J 2200/34] using a side column fed by a stream from the low pressure column -- A process or apparatus that separates gas mixtures by rectification using a side column that receives a stream from a low-pressure column, allowing further separation or purification under pressure and cold treatment conditions. It is classified under mechanical engineering, refrigeration or cooling, and liquefaction, solidification or separation of gases.
[F25J 2200/38] using pre-separation or distributed distillation before a main column system, e.g. in a at least a double column system -- This subclass covers methods where a preliminary separation step such as pre-separation or distributed distillation is performed before the main distillation column, often in double-column arrangements, to improve efficiency of gas or gas-mixture liquefaction or separation.
[F25J 2200/40] Features relating to the provision of boil-up in the bottom of a column -- This subclass covers design elements that create or control boil-up at the base of a rectification column used in refrigeration-related processes that separate gases or gas mixtures by pressure and cooling. It includes apparatus or methods for generating vapor to initiate upward flow and improve separation efficiency.
[F25J 2200/50] using multiple (re-)boiler-condensers at different heights of the column -- F25J 2200/50 pertains to refrigeration or cooling systems that separate gas mixtures by rectification, employing several reboiler-condensers positioned at different heights along the column to enhance heat exchange and separation efficiency.
[F25J 2200/52] in the high pressure column of a double pressure main column system -- The entry covers the high-pressure column within a double-pressure main column system used for rectifying and separating gases or gas mixtures by pressure and cold treatment, a key component of liquefaction, solidification or refrigeration processes. It pertains to apparatus design and operation of the high-pressure segment of the dual-column arrangement.
[F25J 2200/54] in the low pressure column of a double pressure main column system -- The code F25J 2200/54 pertains to apparatus that separates gases or gaseous mixtures by rectification using a double-pressure main column, specifically describing the low-pressure column within such a system. It falls under mechanical engineering, refrigeration, and processes for liquefaction, solidification, or gas separation by pressure and cold treatment.
[F25J 2200/70] Refluxing the column with a condensed part of the feed stream, i.e. fractionator top is stripped or self-rectified -- This CPC subclass covers processes or apparatus where a fractionating column is refluxed with a condensed portion of the feed stream, enabling the column's top to be stripped or self-rectified, a method used in the liquefaction, solidification or pressure-cold separation of gases and gaseous mixtures.
[F25J 2200/72] Refluxing the column with at least a part of the totally condensed overhead gas -- The entry covers a rectification process in which the overhead gas that has been fully condensed is partially returned to the column as reflux, enhancing separation efficiency during liquefaction or solidification of gaseous mixtures. This technique is applied in refrigeration and cooling systems for gas separation.
[F25J 2200/74] Refluxing the column with at least a part of the partially condensed overhead gas -- The entry covers a technique in gas-liquefaction or cryogenic rectification where a portion of the overhead vapor that has been partially condensed is redirected back into the separation column as reflux. This recycled condensate helps control column temperature and improve the efficiency of the gas separation process.
[F25J 2200/76] Refluxing the column with condensed overhead gas being cycled in a quasi-closed loop refrigeration cycle -- The code F25J 2200/76 covers refrigeration or cooling processes that separate gases by rectification using a quasi-closed loop where the condensed overhead gas from a distillation column is refluxed back into the system, providing continuous cooling and improving separation efficiency.
[F25J 2200/78] Refluxing the column with a liquid stream originating from an upstream or downstream fractionator column -- This CPC subclass covers methods where a reflux liquid is taken from a fractionating column located either above or below the main column and returned to the column to improve separation efficiency in gas liquefaction or cooling processes.
[F25J 2200/80] using integrated mass and heat exchange, i.e. non-adiabatic rectification in a reflux exchanger or dephlegmator -- F25J 2200/80 covers processes or equipment that perform rectification by integrating mass and heat exchange, such as non-adiabatic rectification performed in a reflux exchanger or dephlegmator. It is applied in refrigeration or cooling systems for liquefying, solidifying or separating gases and gaseous mixtures through pressure and cold treatment.
[F25J 2200/90] Details relating to column internals, e.g. structured packing, gas or liquid distribution -- F25J 2200/90 covers the design of internal components of columns used for gas-liquid separation by rectification, such as structured packing, trays and devices that distribute gas or liquid flow. The code applies to details that influence mass-transfer efficiency in refrigeration and liquefaction processes.
[F25J 2200/92] Details relating to the feed point -- This subclass covers aspects of the feed introduction in rectification-based gas-separation equipment, including design, positioning and control of the feed point in liquefaction or solidification processes.
[F25J 2200/94] Details relating to the withdrawal point -- The code F25J 2200/94 covers information about the withdrawal point in equipment or processes that separate gases or gas mixtures by rectification, a method used in liquefaction, solidification or other cold-treatment separation techniques. It pertains to the design, location or operation of the point where the purified stream is taken from the system.
[F25J 2200/96] Dividing wall column -- The entry covers dividing-wall columns, a type of rectification apparatus in which an internal wall creates two separate contact zones within a single column, allowing simultaneous counter-current separation of a feed into different product streams. This design improves energy efficiency and reduces equipment size in processes that liquefy, solidify, or separate gases by pressure and cold treatment.
[F25J 2205/00] <NO TITLE>
[F25J 2205/02] using simple phase separation in a vessel or drum -- F25J 2205/02 covers methods that separate gas phases by letting the mixture settle in a vessel or drum where pressure and low temperature cause one component to condense or solidify, allowing the remaining gas to be drawn off. The process relies on simple phase-separation without additional mechanical or chemical treatment. It is used in refrigeration and cooling systems for gas liquefaction or solidification.
[F25J 2205/04] in the feed line, i.e. upstream of the fractionation step -- F25J 2205/04 covers elements of a refrigeration system that treat the gas mixture in the feed line before it reaches the fractionation stage, including pipelines, valves, pre-cooling heat exchangers and related control devices used in pressure-driven, low-temperature gas liquefaction or separation processes.
[F25J 2205/10] using combined expansion and separation, e.g. in a vortex tube, "Ranque tube" or a "cyclonic fluid separator", i.e. combination of an isentropic nozzle and a cyclonic separator -- The code F25J 2205/10 covers devices that separate gases or gas mixtures by first expanding the flow through an isentropic nozzle and then directing it into a cyclonic separator, such as vortex or Ranque tubes. This combined expansion-separation principle is used for cooling, liquefaction or purification of gases.
[F25J 2205/20] using solidification of components -- F25J 2205/20 covers processes that solidify one or more components of a gas mixture during liquefaction, cooling or separation by pressure and low temperature, enabling selective removal or recovery of those components, often as part of gas purification or fractionation operations.
[F25J 2205/24] using regenerators, cold accumulators or reversible heat exchangers -- F25J 2205/24 classifies refrigeration or cooling systems that achieve liquefaction, solidification or gas separation by pressure and low temperature using devices such as regenerators, cold accumulators, or reversible heat exchangers. It covers methods where thermal energy is stored and recovered to improve the efficiency of the cooling cycle.
[F25J 2205/30] using a washing, e.g. "scrubbing" or bubble column for purification purposes -- This subclass covers methods that use a washing step-such as scrubbing or a bubble-column contactor-to purify gas streams during liquefaction, solidification, or other pressure-and-cold-treatment separation processes, falling under mechanical engineering, refrigeration or cooling.
[F25J 2205/32] as direct contact cooling tower to produce a cooled gas stream, e.g. direct contact after cooler [DCAC] -- The code F25J 2205/32 covers direct-contact cooling towers that cool a gaseous stream by allowing it to mix directly with a cooling medium, producing a cooled gas output often used immediately after a conventional cooler. This method combines gas-liquefaction or separation with pressure-based cooling in a single, compact unit.
[F25J 2205/34] as evaporative cooling tower to produce chilled water, e.g. evaporative water chiller [EWC] -- The code F25J 2205/34 falls under mechanical engineering, refrigeration, specifically liquefaction or solidification by pressure and cold treatment, and covers evaporative cooling towers used to generate chilled water, such as evaporative water chillers.
[F25J 2205/40] using hybrid system, i.e. combining cryogenic and non-cryogenic separation techniques -- The CPC entry F25J 2205/40 covers hybrid processes that merge cryogenic and non-cryogenic techniques for separating gases or gas mixtures, using pressure and low-temperature treatment. It falls under mechanical engineering, specifically refrigeration and cooling methods that involve liquefaction, solidification, or gas separation by cold treatment. This classification is used for systems that combine both extreme-cold and moderate-temperature separation stages.
[F25J 2205/50] using absorption, i.e. with selective solvents or lean oil, heavier CnHm and including generally a regeneration step for the solvent or lean oil -- In the CPC hierarchy under mechanical engineering, refrigeration and cooling, and liquefaction, solidification or separation of gases by pressure and cold treatment, the code F25J 2205/50 refers to processes that use absorption with selective solvents or lean oil to handle heavier hydrocarbons and normally include a regeneration step for the solvent or lean oil.
[F25J 2205/60] using adsorption on solid adsorbents, e.g. by temperature-swing adsorption [TSA] at the hot or cold end -- This subclass pertains to refrigeration or cooling processes that separate, liquefy, or solidify gases using solid adsorbents, specifically by employing temperature-swing adsorption where the adsorbent is regenerated at either the hot or the cold end of the system.
[F25J 2205/62] Purifying more than one feed stream in multiple adsorption vessels, e.g. for two feed streams at different pressures -- This class relates to the purification of two or more feed streams in separate adsorption vessels, where each stream may be at a different pressure, used in liquefaction, solidification or gas-separation processes. It applies to mechanical-engineering systems for refrigeration or cooling that rely on pressure and cold treatment.
[F25J 2205/64] by pressure-swing adsorption [PSA] at the hot end -- This classification covers processes that separate or liquefy gases by pressure-swing adsorption performed at the heated, or "hot," end of the system. It applies to refrigeration or cooling equipment that uses PSA at the hot end to recover or purify gas mixtures.
[F25J 2205/66] Regenerating the adsorption vessel, e.g. kind of reactivation gas -- It covers methods for restoring the adsorption capacity of vessels used in gas-separation processes, such as applying reactivation gases to desorb previously captured gases. The techniques are applied in refrigeration or cooling systems that liquefy, solidify or separate gases by pressure and cold treatment.
[F25J 2205/68] Cooling the adsorption vessel -- The entry F25J 2205/68 relates to methods or devices for lowering the temperature of an adsorption vessel, a key step in adsorption-based refrigeration, gas liquefaction or separation processes. It covers techniques that combine pressure and cold treatment to cool the adsorbent material, enhancing its capacity to capture gases or vapours during operation.
[F25J 2205/70] Heating the adsorption vessel -- F25J 2205/70 relates to methods and apparatus for heating the adsorption vessel in refrigeration or cooling systems that separate gases by pressure and low-temperature treatment. It covers techniques for applying heat to the vessel to desorb gases, regenerate adsorbents, or control temperature during liquefaction or solidification processes.
[F25J 2205/72] Pressurising or depressurising the adsorption vessel -- F25J 2205/72 refers to techniques for pressurising or depressurising an adsorption vessel used in refrigeration or cooling systems that solidify or separate gases by pressure and low temperature. It covers methods that control the vessel's internal pressure to facilitate gas adsorption, desorption, or phase change during liquefaction or separation processes.
[F25J 2205/80] using membrane, i.e. including a permeation step -- F25J 2205/80 covers refrigeration or cooling methods that employ a membrane to separate or liquefy gases, incorporating a permeation step as part of the pressure-and-cold treatment process. It relates to systems that use selective gas permeation through a barrier to achieve gas mixture separation or solidification.
[F25J 2205/82] using a reactor with combustion or catalytic reaction -- F25J 2205/82 covers refrigeration or cooling systems that use a reactor in which heat is generated by combustion or a catalytic reaction to drive the liquefaction, solidification, or separation of gases. The classification applies to designs where the reactor is integral to the cycle, supplying thermal energy for compressing or expanding the refrigerant.
[F25J 2205/84] using filter -- F25J 2205/84 covers refrigeration or cooling systems that perform liquefaction, solidification or gas separation by pressure and low-temperature treatment and that incorporate a filter to remove contaminants or moisture from the gas stream.
[F25J 2205/86] using electrical phenomena, e.g. Corona discharge, electrolysis or magnetic field -- This subclass covers apparatus or processes that employ electrical effects such as corona discharge, electrolysis or magnetic fields to assist the liquefaction, solidification or separation of gases or gas mixtures through pressure and low-temperature treatment.
[F25J 2205/90] Mixing of components -- F25J 2205/90 covers techniques for mixing two or more substances during the cold-treatment processes used to liquefy, solidify or separate gases. It includes apparatus and methods that introduce, blend or homogenize components within refrigeration or cryogenic equipment, such as mixers, injectors or stirring devices operating under high pressure and low temperature.
[F25J 2210/00] Processes characterised by the type or other details of the feed stream -- F25J 2210/00 relates to refrigeration or cooling processes in which the type, composition, temperature, pressure or phase of the feed stream is specifically defined, influencing how gases or gaseous mixtures are liquefied, solidified or separated by pressure and cold treatment.
[F25J 2210/02] Multiple feed streams, e.g. originating from different sources -- This subclass covers refrigeration or cooling processes that handle more than one feed stream, such as streams coming from different sources, within systems for liquefying, solidifying or separating gases by pressure and cold treatment. It includes arrangements where multiple inputs are mixed, staged or otherwise managed to achieve the desired cooling or separation effect.
[F25J 2210/04] Mixing or blending of fluids with the feed stream -- F25J 2210/04 deals with methods for mixing or blending additional fluids directly with the feed stream in refrigeration or cooling processes that liquefy, solidify or separate gases by applying pressure and low temperature. The classification focuses on how the nature of the feed influences the mixing operation.
[F25J 2210/06] Splitting of the feed stream, e.g. for treating or cooling in different ways -- The code F25J 2210/06 designates processes in refrigeration or cooling where the incoming feed is divided into separate streams for distinct treatment or cooling paths. It falls under mechanical engineering, specifically the sub-area of liquefaction, solidification or separation of gases by pressure and cold treatment, and addresses methods that split the feed to apply different conditions to each portion.
[F25J 2210/12] Refinery or petrochemical off-gas -- The F25J 2210/12 class covers processes that treat refinery or petrochemical off-gas, focusing on the type or specific characteristics of the feed stream, and employing pressure and low-temperature methods to liquefy, solidify, or separate the gases. It falls under mechanical engineering, refrigeration or cooling, and gas-treatment technologies.
[F25J 2210/14] Coke-ovens gas -- F25J 2210/14 refers to processes for liquefying, solidifying or separating gases by pressure and cold treatment where the feed is coke-oven gas. It falls under mechanical engineering, refrigeration or cooling, specifically handling coke-oven gas streams. The code addresses methods and equipment for treating this gas in industrial refrigeration cycles.

[F25J 2215/00] Processes characterised by the type or other details of the product stream -- A CPC subgroup for refrigeration and cooling technologies that deals with liquefaction, solidification or separation of gases by pressure and cold treatment, where the process is defined by the nature of the product stream-its composition, phase, purity or other specific characteristics-rather than by the equipment used. This category groups methods that specify the type or details of the gas stream produced as the distinguishing feature of the process.
[F25J 2215/02] Mixing or blending of fluids to yield a certain product -- The subclass F25J 2215/02 covers processes that mix or blend fluids-typically gases or gas mixtures-under pressure and cold treatment to produce a specified product, emphasizing the type and characteristics of the resulting product stream. It falls within mechanical engineering, refrigeration or cooling, and the broader category of liquefaction, solidification, or separation of gases by pressure and low temperature.

[F25J 2230/08] Cold compressor, i.e. suction of the gas at cryogenic temperature and generally without afterstage-cooler -- F25J 2230/08 covers cold compressors that draw gas directly at cryogenic temperatures and compress it, typically without an afterstage-cooler. The technology is used in processes that raise the pressure of gaseous streams for liquefaction, solidification or gas separation. Such integrated devices are used in liquefaction, solidification or gas-separation processes that rely on pressure increase and cold treatment.
[F25J 2230/22] Compressor driver arrangement, e.g. power supply by motor, gas or steam turbine -- A compressor driver arrangement refers to the configuration that supplies power to a compressor, typically using an electric motor, a gas turbine, or a steam turbine. [F25J 2230/24] Multiple compressors or compressor stages in parallel -- This subclass covers systems that employ two or more compressors or compressor stages operating side-by-by-parallel to raise the pressure of a gaseous stream. Parallel arrangement is used to increase capacity, improve efficiency, or provide redundancy in refrigeration or gas-liquefaction processes. The classification applies to both mechanical and process designs where the compressors share the same inlet or discharge flow.

[F25J 2250/00] Details related to the use of reboiler-condensers -- This CPC subclass deals with the design and operation of reboiler-condensers used in refrigeration or cooling systems that liquefy, solidify or separate gases by applying pressure and low temperature. [F25J 2250/02] Bath type boiler-condenser using thermo-siphon effect, e.g. with natural or forced circulation or pool boiling, i.e. core-in-kettle heat exchanger -- This code covers bath-type boiler-condenser units that rely on the thermo-siphon effect to circulate fluid, either naturally or with forced flow, and employ pool boiling within a core-in-kettle heat exchanger. The arrangement combines heating and condensation in a single vessel, allowing gases or vapors to be liquefied, solidified or separated by pressure and cold treatment.

[F25J 2250/20] Boiler-condenser with multiple exchanger cores in parallel or with multiple re-boiling or condensing streams -- The code F25J 2250/20 covers boiler-condensers that contain several heat-exchange cores arranged in parallel, or that handle multiple separate re-boiling or condensing streams within the same unit, allowing simultaneous heating and cooling processes in a single apparatus.
