[F25J 2250/58] One fluid being argon or crude argon -- This subclass covers processes that use a single working fluid-argon or crude argon-in reboiler-condenser arrangements to liquefy, solidify or separate gases by applying pressure and low temperature, focusing on the equipment and methods for such cooling cycles.
[F25J 2260/00] Coupling of processes or apparatus to other units -- F25J 2260/00 covers the integration of refrigeration or cooling equipment with other systems, describing methods and devices for linking processes or apparatus such as compressors, heat exchangers or liquefaction units to external units for combined operation or energy recovery.
[F25J 2260/02] Integration in an installation for exchanging heat, e.g. for waste heat recovery -- The code F25J 2260/02 covers the integration of heat-exchanging equipment into an overall plant, for example linking a waste-heat recovery unit with other process or refrigeration units. It addresses how such heat-exchange modules are coupled to surrounding systems to improve energy utilisation within the broader mechanical-engineering context.
[F25J 2260/10] Integration in a gas transmission system at a pressure reduction, e.g. "let down" station -- A system or method that links gas-handling equipment, such as liquefaction or cooling units, to a gas transmission network at a pressure-reduction point, typically a "let-down" station, allowing the integrated operation of the processes or apparatus with the transmission line.
[F25J 2260/20] Integration in an installation for liquefying or solidifying a fluid stream -- The code F25J 2260/20 covers arrangements where a plant that liquefies or solidifies a fluid stream is combined with other equipment in the same installation. It includes coupling of the liquefaction or solidification unit to upstream, downstream or auxiliary units for heat exchange, feed handling or product recovery, enabling integrated operation of the overall system.
[F25J 2260/30] Integration in an installation using renewable energy -- This subclass covers methods and apparatuses that combine refrigeration or cooling processes with renewable-energy sources such as solar, wind or geothermal within larger installations. It includes coupling of liquefaction, solidification or gas-separation units to renewable-energy supply systems, emphasizing energy efficiency and sustainable operation.
[F25J 2260/42] Integration in an installation using nitrogen, e.g. as utility gas, for inerting or purging purposes in IGCC, POX, GTL, PSA, float glass forming, incineration processes, for heat recovery or for enhanced oil recovery -- This classification covers the integration of nitrogen supply systems into industrial plants such as integrated gasification combined cycles, partial oxidation, gas-to-liquids, pressure-swing adsorption, float-glass furnaces or incinerators, where nitrogen is used for inerting, purging, heat recovery or enhanced oil recovery.
[F25J 2260/44] using nitrogen for cooling purposes -- F25J 2260/44 pertains to the use of nitrogen as a cooling agent in systems that combine refrigeration or gas-treatment processes with other plant units. It addresses apparatus or methods where nitrogen-based cooling is integrated with downstream or upstream equipment for liquefaction, solidification or gas separation.
[F25J 2260/50] Integration in an installation using oxygen, e.g. in the burner of a glass facility, waste incineration or oxygen based process [OBP] in general
[F25J 2260/58] Integration in an installation using argon -- The entry covers the integration of argon gas within refrigeration or cooling installations that involve liquefying, solidifying, or separating gases by pressure and low-temperature treatment. It addresses how argon-based processes or equipment are coupled to other units in the system, ensuring effective flow control, heat exchange, or protective atmospheres within the overall plant.
[F25J 2260/60] Integration in an installation using hydrocarbons, e.g. for fuel purposes -- The code F25J 2260/60 covers the integration of hydrocarbon-based units, such as those using liquid or gaseous hydrocarbons for fuel, into a larger refrigeration or cooling system. It addresses the coupling of these processes or equipment with other plant modules to achieve combined operation or heat-energy recovery.
[F25J 2260/80] Integration in an installation using carbon dioxide, e.g. for EOR, sequestration, refrigeration etc.
[F25J 2270/00] Refrigeration techniques used -- F25J 2270/00 covers the specific methods employed in refrigeration systems to achieve cooling, including vapor-compression cycles, absorption, thermoelectric, magnetic and other techniques that use pressure and low temperature to remove heat. The subclass groups inventions that describe how the refrigeration process is carried out rather than the apparatus itself.
[F25J 2270/02] Internal refrigeration with liquid vaporising loop -- The code F25J 2270/02 covers internal refrigeration systems that employ a liquid-vaporising loop to provide cooling, where the refrigerant circulates in a sealed circuit and vaporises within the equipment to absorb heat. This arrangement is used in applications such as cryogenic storage, refrigerated transport or machine cooling where the refrigerant's phase change occurs inside the device itself.
[F25J 2270/04] Internal refrigeration with work-producing gas expansion loop -- The F25J 2270/04 subclass pertains to internal refrigeration systems that incorporate a gas-expansion loop which simultaneously generates mechanical work while providing cooling, usually within a closed cycle where the expanding gas absorbs heat from the refrigerated space. It falls under mechanical-engineering refrigeration techniques for liquefying or separating gases by pressure and cold treatment.
[F25J 2270/06] with multiple gas expansion loops -- The CPC subclass F25J 2270/06 pertains to refrigeration methods that incorporate multiple gas expansion loops, used to enhance temperature reduction and efficiency in the liquefaction, solidification or separation of gases by pressure and cold treatment. It covers arrangements where two or more expansion stages are connected in series or parallel within a cooling system.
[F25J 2270/08] Internal refrigeration by flash gas recovery loop -- The entry classifies internal refrigeration systems that employ a flash-gas recovery loop, in which gas liberated during expansion is captured and fed back to the compressor to boost cycle efficiency and reduce energy consumption. This technique is used in appliances and industrial chillers to improve performance while minimizing waste.
[F25J 2270/12] External refrigeration with liquid vaporising loop -- This subclass covers refrigeration installations that use a closed loop in which a liquid refrigerant is vaporised outside the primary heat-absorbing space to provide cooling or to liquefy gases. The process relies on pressure-induced boiling of the liquid in an external evaporator and is used in industrial and cryogenic applications.
[F25J 2270/14] External refrigeration with work-producing gas expansion loop -- F25J 2270/14 covers external refrigeration systems that incorporate a gas-expansion loop which both provides cooling and generates mechanical work. The method relies on pressure-induced liquefaction or solidification of gases, using the expansion process to recover energy while maintaining the refrigeration cycle.
[F25J 2270/16] with mutliple gas expansion loops of the same refrigerant -- This sub-class covers refrigeration or cooling arrangements that incorporate two or more gas expansion loops operating with the same refrigerant, such as cascaded or parallel expansion stages to enhance thermodynamic performance or capacity in liquefaction or gas separation processes.
[F25J 2270/18] External refrigeration with incorporated cascade loop -- The code F25J 2270/18 designates external refrigeration systems that incorporate a cascade loop, where a secondary refrigerant circuit is integrated within the primary external unit to achieve lower temperatures or improved efficiency. This sub-category falls under mechanical engineering, specifically refrigeration methods that use pressure and cold treatment for gas liquefaction or separation.
[F25J 2270/20] Quasi-closed internal or closed external hydrogen refrigeration cycle -- F25J 2270/20 covers refrigeration cycles that use hydrogen in a quasi-closed internal system or a closed external system to provide cooling, typically for processes such as liquefying, solidifying or separating gases by pressure and low temperature.
[F25J 2270/24] Quasi-closed internal or closed external carbon monoxide refrigeration cycle -- The F25J 2270/24 entry covers refrigeration methods that employ a quasi-closed internal or a closed external cycle using carbon monoxide as the working fluid. It falls under mechanical engineering, refrigeration or cooling, and the sub-category of liquefaction, solidification or separation of gases by pressure and cold treatment.
[F25J 2270/30] Quasi-closed internal or closed external helium refrigeration cycle -- A quasi-closed internal or closed external helium refrigeration cycle is a low-temperature cooling method in which helium circulates primarily within a sealed or nearly sealed loop, minimizing fluid loss while providing efficient heat extraction for liquefaction, solidification or gas-separation processes. This technique is applied where precise temperature control and high thermodynamic efficiency are required, such as in cryogenic plants and scientific equipment.
[F25J 2270/40] Quasi-closed internal or closed external air refrigeration cycle -- The code F25J 2270/40 designates refrigeration cycles that operate with air as the working fluid in a quasi-closed internal or fully closed external configuration, where the refrigerant is largely contained within the system and limited exchange with the environment occurs. This falls under mechanical engineering, refrigeration and gas-separation technologies.
[F25J 2270/42] Quasi-closed internal or closed external nitrogen refrigeration cycle -- This classification covers refrigeration systems that employ nitrogen in a quasi-closed internal or closed external cycle, where the refrigerant is largely recirculated with minimal loss to the environment. Such cycles are used for low-temperature applications, including gas liquefaction and cryogenic cooling.
[F25J 2270/50] Quasi-closed internal or closed external oxygen refrigeration cycle -- A quasi-closed internal or closed external oxygen refrigeration cycle is a low-temperature cooling method in which the oxygen refrigerant circulates in a system that is either sealed internally with limited mass exchange or sealed externally, minimizing loss of the gas. It is used primarily in air-separation plants to liquefy or solidify oxygen while maintaining high efficiency and low contamination.
[F25J 2270/58] Quasi-closed internal or closed external argon refrigeration cycle -- A quasi-closed internal or closed external argon refrigeration cycle uses argon as the working fluid in a system that is largely sealed from the atmosphere, allowing pressure and temperature to be controlled for efficient cooling. This approach is applied in refrigeration processes that require precise low-temperature operation and minimal refrigerant loss.
[F25J 2270/60] Closed external refrigeration cycle with single component refrigerant [SCR], e.g. C1-, C2- or C3-hydrocarbons -- The code F25J 2270/60 covers closed external refrigeration cycles that use a single-component refrigerant such as methane, ethane or propane. It relates to systems where the refrigerant circulates in a sealed loop and the cooling effect is achieved by compressing, condensing, expanding and evaporating the same pure compound, rather than mixing multiple refrigerants. This classification is used for designs of simple, single-fluid refrigeration plants.
[F25J 2270/66] Closed external refrigeration cycle with multi component refrigerant [MCR], e.g. mixture of hydrocarbons -- F25J 2270/66 covers closed external refrigeration cycles that employ multi-component refrigerants, such as blends of hydrocarbons, in a sealed loop where cooling is obtained through pressure and temperature variation. It pertains to systems that use mixed refrigerants to achieve liquefaction, solidification or separation of gases by cold treatment. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering, refrigeration or cooling, and gas-treatment by pressure and cold.
[F25J 2270/80] Quasi-closed internal or closed external carbon dioxide refrigeration cycle -- The code F25J 2270/80 covers refrigeration systems that employ a quasi-closed internal loop or a closed external loop using carbon dioxide as the working fluid. These cycles are designed to achieve cooling or liquefaction by circulating CO₂ in a largely sealed circuit, often to improve efficiency and reduce refrigerant losses.
[F25J 2270/88] Quasi-closed internal refrigeration or heat pump cycle, if not otherwise provided -- This classification covers quasi-closed internal refrigeration or heat-pump cycles that are not otherwise provided. Such cycles operate with minimal mass exchange with the environment, using pressure and temperature differences to achieve cooling or heating in a closed or near-closed system.
[F25J 2270/90] External refrigeration, e.g. conventional closed-loop mechanical refrigeration unit using Freon or NH -- This subclass covers external refrigeration systems that employ a conventional closed-loop mechanical cycle using refrigerants such as Freon or ammonia. It relates to devices where the cooling unit is located outside the conditioned space and provides cooling by pressure and cold treatment of a gaseous mixture.
[F25J 2270/902] Details about the refrigeration cycle used, e.g. composition of refrigerant, arrangement of compressors or cascade, make up sources, use of reflux exchangers etc. -- F25J 2270/902 covers information on the specific refrigeration cycle employed in a system, including the type and composition of the refrigerant, the configuration of compressors or cascade arrangements, sources of makeup fluid, and the use of reflux exchangers or similar cycle components. The subclass is part of the CPC group for mechanical engineering, refrigeration or cooling, and addresses the technical details that define how the cycle operates.
[F25J 2270/904] by liquid or gaseous cryogen in an open loop -- F25J 2270/904 covers refrigeration systems that use a liquid or gaseous cryogen in an open-loop circuit, where the cryogen absorbs heat from the load and is then discharged rather than recirculated. Such techniques are employed for cooling, liquefaction, solidification or separation of gases by pressure and low-temperature treatment.
[F25J 2270/906] by heat driven absorption chillers -- This subclass covers refrigeration methods that use heat-driven absorption chillers, where thermal energy-such as waste heat or solar heat-powers an absorber-refrigerant cycle to produce cooling for liquefying, solidifying or separating gases by pressure and cold treatment.
[F25J 2270/908] by regenerative chillers, i.e. oscillating or dynamic systems, e.g. Stirling refrigerator, thermoelectric ("Peltier") or magnetic refrigeration -- F25J 2270/908 covers refrigeration methods that employ regenerative chillers, such as oscillating or dynamic systems like Stirling refrigerators, as well as thermoelectric (Peltier) and magnetic refrigeration. These techniques achieve cooling by cyclic compression and expansion of a working gas or by direct solid-state heat pumping.
[F25J 2270/91] using pulse tube refrigeration -- F25J 2270/91 covers refrigeration systems that employ a pulse tube as the cooling element, using oscillating gas flow to achieve low temperatures without moving parts such as pistons or valves. The technology is used for cryogenic or low-temperature applications where reliability and low vibration are important.
[F25J 2270/912] Liquefaction cycle of a low-boiling (feed) gas in a cryocooler, i.e. in a closed-loop refrigerator -- The CPC subclass F25J 2270/912 covers the liquefaction cycle of a low-boiling feed gas inside a cryocooler, i.e., a closed-loop refrigeration system that cools and condenses the gas by pressure and cold treatment. It is relevant to refrigeration and cooling technologies that separate gases through liquefaction.
[F25J 2280/00] Control of the process or apparatus -- The F25J 2280/00 class covers techniques and devices for controlling the operation of apparatus used in liquefying, solidifying or separating gases by pressure and low-temperature treatment. It includes automation, feedback, regulation and monitoring systems that adjust parameters such as pressure, temperature, flow and compressor speed to maintain desired process conditions. This ensures efficient and safe performance of refrigeration and gas-handling equipment.
[F25J 2280/02] Control in general, load changes, different modes ("runs"), measurements -- This subclass covers general control of equipment that liquefies or separates gases by pressure and cold treatment, addressing load changes, switching between different operating modes or runs, and performing related measurements. It includes control systems, sensors and algorithms that regulate temperature, pressure and flow during various process conditions.
[F25J 2280/10] Control for or during start-up and cooling down of the installation -- This CPC subclass deals with control methods applied during the start-up and cooling-down phases of installations used for liquefaction, solidification or separation of gases by pressure and cold treatment, within refrigeration mechanical engineering. It covers control of the process or apparatus.
[F25J 2280/20] Control for stopping, deriming or defrosting after an emergency shut-down of the installation or for back up system -- The F25J 2280/20 subclass covers control schemes that initiate stopping, deriming or defrosting operations after an emergency shutdown of a refrigeration or gas-liquefaction plant, or that provide a backup system to resume normal operation. It addresses automatic or semi-automatic procedures ensuring safe restart and removal of ice or sediments following an abrupt interruption.
[F25J 2280/30] Control of a discontinuous or intermittent ("batch") process -- F25J 2280/30 covers control strategies for batch or intermittent refrigeration processes where gas or liquid is liquefied, solidified, or separated by pressure and low temperature. It includes monitoring, timing, and regulation of valves, compressors, and temperature to ensure proper operation of the discontinuous cycle.
[F25J 2280/40] Control of freezing of components -- Relates to methods and devices for regulating the freezing of individual components during gas liquefaction, solidification or separation by pressure and cold treatment. The code covers control strategies, sensors and feedback systems that maintain desired temperature or phase conditions in refrigeration or cooling equipment.
[F25J 2280/50] Advanced process control, e.g. adaptive or multivariable control -- F25J 2280/50 covers the use of advanced process control strategies, such as adaptive or multivariable control, for refrigeration or cooling systems that liquefy, solidify or separate gases by pressure and cold treatment. The subclass relates to implementing intelligent control algorithms to regulate temperature, pressure and flow in such equipment.
[F25J 2290/00] Other details not covered by groups -- F25J 2290/00 pertains to additional features or aspects of refrigeration or cooling systems that involve liquefying, solidifying or separating gases through pressure and low temperature, which are not addressed by more specific groups in the classification. It captures miscellaneous technical details such as auxiliary components, control methods, or unconventional arrangements related to these processes.
[F25J 2290/02] Comparison of processes or apparatuses -- F25J 2290/02 covers patent disclosures that compare alternative processes or apparatuses used for liquefying, solidifying or separating gases or gas mixtures through pressure and cold treatment, where the comparison is not otherwise covered by existing subclasses. It includes studies of performance, efficiency, cost or other criteria between competing technologies within the broader field of refrigeration and cooling engineering. This subclass is used when the comparative analysis does not fit more specific categories.
[F25J 2290/10] Mathematical formulae, modeling, plot or curves -- F25J 2290/10 covers the use of mathematical equations, models, plots or curves to describe and predict the behavior of processes that liquefy, solidify or separate gases or gas mixtures by applying pressure and low temperature, within the field of mechanical engineering refrigeration and cooling.
[F25J 2290/12] Particular process parameters like pressure, temperature, ratios -- The CPC subclass F25J 2290/12 covers the specification of individual process parameters such as pressure, temperature and component ratios for methods that liquefy, solidify or separate gases or gaseous mixtures by pressure and cold treatment. It falls within the mechanical engineering domain under refrigeration or cooling technologies.
[F25J 2290/20] Particular dimensions -- This subclass covers inventions that specify or control the size, shape, or geometric relationships of components used in refrigeration, cooling, liquefaction, solidification or gas-separation systems operating under pressure and cold treatment, such as heat exchangers, valves, pipelines and related equipment. It focuses on particular dimensions that affect the performance or integration of these devices.
[F25J 2290/30] Details about heat insulation or cold insulation -- The entry covers technical information on thermal insulation for equipment used in refrigeration or cooling processes that involve liquefying, solidifying, or separating gases by pressure and cold treatment. It includes details of insulating materials, design features and methods that reduce heat gain or loss in such systems.
[F25J 2290/32] Details on header or distribution passages of heat exchangers, e.g. of reboiler-condenser or plate heat exchangers -- F25J 2290/32 classifies inventions that describe the design or features of header and distribution passages in heat exchangers, including reboiler-condenser units and plate-type exchangers, within the broader context of mechanical engineering, refrigeration or cooling, and gas liquefaction or separation processes.

It covers technical aspects related to subcooling of liquids that are not addressed by other groups.
[F25J 2290/40] Vertical layout or arrangement of cold equipments within in the cold box, e.g. columns, condensers, heat exchangers etc. -- F25J 2290/40 covers the vertical layout or arrangement of cold-equipment within a cold box, such as columns, condensers, heat exchangers and similar components, focusing on their positioning rather than on specific device designs.
[F25J 2290/42] Modularity, pre-fabrication of modules, assembling and erection, horizontal layout, i.e. plot plan, and vertical arrangement of parts of the cryogenic unit, e.g. of the cold box -- This subclass covers the modular design and prefabrication of cryogenic units such as cold boxes, including the horizontal plot-plan layout, vertical arrangement of components, and methods of assembling and erecting the modules for gas liquefaction or separation processes.

This sub-class falls under mechanical engineering, refrigeration or cooling, and the broader topic of gas liquefaction and solidification.
[F25J 2290/50] Arrangement of multiple equipments fulfilling the same process step in parallel -- The code F25J 2290/50 covers the parallel arrangement of two or more pieces of equipment that perform the same process step within refrigeration or cooling systems that liquefy, solidify or separate gases by pressure and cold-treatment. It applies when the configuration is not specified by any more detailed subgroup.
[F25J 2290/60] Details about pipelines, i.e. network, for feed or product distribution -- F25J 2290/60 covers inventions that describe the design, arrangement or management of pipelines and distribution networks used to transport feed streams or final products in systems that liquefy, solidify or separate gases by pressure and low-temperature treatment. It pertains to mechanical engineering solutions for refrigerating and cooling plant infrastructure.
[F25J 2290/62] Details of storing a fluid in a tank -- The subclass covers technical aspects of storing fluids, especially gases or cryogenic liquids, in tanks, including tank construction, insulation, pressure control, safety devices and related equipment. It addresses methods for maintaining temperature and pressure during storage and for handling and retrieving the stored fluid.
[F25J 2290/70] Processing device is mobile or transportable, e.g. by hand, car, ship, rocket engine etc. -- The subclass F25J 2290/70 covers processing devices for liquefying, solidifying or separating gases that are designed to be mobile or transportable, such as units carried by hand, mounted on vehicles, ships or rockets. It applies to refrigeration and cooling systems where the equipment can be moved to different locations.
[F25J 2290/72] Processing device is used off-shore, e.g. on a platform or floating on a ship or barge -- The CPC subclass F25J 2290/72 covers processing equipment for liquefying, solidifying or separating gases by pressure and cold treatment that is not otherwise classified, and that is used in offshore environments such as platforms, ships or barges. It falls under mechanical engineering, refrigeration or cooling, and the specific sub-field of gas handling by pressure and cold treatment.
[F25J 2290/80] Retrofitting, revamping or debottlenecking of existing plant -- F25J 2290/80 covers activities that modify, upgrade or improve an existing refrigeration or cooling installation, such as retrofitting, revamping or removing bottlenecks to enhance performance, reliability or efficiency. It applies to any plant where the primary purpose is liquefaction, solidification or separation of gases by pressure and cold treatment, without being limited to a specific technical group.
[F25J 2290/90] Details about safety operation of the installation -- F25J 2290/90 relates to safety-related information for installations that liquefy, solidify or separate gases by pressure and low-temperature treatment. It covers operational procedures, protective measures and any other safety details that are not captured by more specific CPC groups within the refrigeration and cooling field.
[F25J 3/00] Processes or apparatus for separating the constituents of gaseous mixtures involving the use of liquefaction or solidification -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Processes or apparatus for separating the constituents of a feed stream at cryogenic temperatures involving the use of liquefaction or solidification by rectification or partial condensation. The feed stream can be a gas, a gaseous mixture, a cryogenic liquid or a cryogenic liquid mixture.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Distillation or related processes other than cryogenic processing -> B01D3/00 - Recovering volatile solvents by condensation -> B01D5/00 - Production of hydrogen containing gas not using cryogenic processing -> C01B3/00 - Preparation of oxygen not using cryogenic processing -> C01B13/02 - Production or purification of nitrogen not using cryogenic processing -> C01B21/04 - Purification or separation of noble gases not using cryogenic processing -> C01B23/00 - Production of carbon monoxide containing gas not using cryogenic processing -> C01B32/40 , C10J , C10K - Production of carbon dioxide -> C01B32/50 - Purification of hydrocarbons -> C07C7/00 - Distillation of hydrocarbon oils -> C10G7/00 - Working-up liquefied petroleum gas -> C10L3/12 - Treating radioactively contaminated gases -> G21F9/00  **Glossary:** - Cold box: Housing comprising all the cold equipments of the separation/liquefaction process, i.e. working normally under cryogenic temperatures conditions - Natural gas liquids: C2+ hydrocarbons - Liquefied petroleum gas: C3+ hydrocarbons
[F25J 3/02] by rectification, i.e. by continuous interchange of heat and material between a vapour stream and a liquid stream -- The F25J 3/02 entry covers rectification methods that separate components of a gaseous mixture by continuously exchanging heat and material between a vapor stream and a liquid stream, typically after the gases have been liquefied or solidified. It falls under mechanical-engineering refrigeration technologies that use pressure and cold treatment to achieve gas-phase separation.

It includes start-up procedures, control strategies, and the design features of internal components that affect the separation efficiency.
[F25J 3/04] for air -- The F25J 3/04 code covers processes or apparatus that separate the components of gaseous mixtures, such as air, by first liquefying or solidifying the mixture and then extracting the desired constituents through pressure-driven separation.
[F25J 3/04006] Providing pressurised feed air or process streams within or from the air fractionation unit -- This subclass addresses systems that deliver pressurised feed air or other process streams into, or withdraw them from, an air-fractionation unit used in the liquefaction or solidification separation of gas mixtures. It is applied in refrigeration or cooling technologies for separating gaseous mixtures by pressure and cold treatment.
[F25J 3/04012] by compression of warm gaseous streams; details of intake or interstage cooling  F25J3/04048  takes precedence; operation of compressors  F25J3/04781 ; particular layout of compressors used in air fractionation units  F25J3/04866 -- F25J 3/04012 concerns processes and equipment for separating gaseous mixtures by first liquefying or solidifying them, specifically using compression of warm gas streams together with intake or interstage cooling, the operation of the compressors and the arrangement of compressors within air fractionation plants.
[F25J 3/04018] of main feed air -- This subclass covers processes or apparatus that separate the components of a gaseous mixture by first cooling and liquefying the main feed air, as used in cryogenic air-separation plants. It includes stages where the primary air stream is subjected to high pressure and low temperature to produce purified gases such as nitrogen or oxygen.
[F25J 3/04024] of purified feed air, so-called boosted air -- The subclass F25J 3/04024 deals with processes and equipment that use purified or so-called boosted air as the feed gas in cryogenic or pressure-induced separation of gaseous mixtures by liquefaction or solidification. It specifically covers the preparation of feed air at high purity or elevated pressure to improve the efficiency of air-separation plants.
[F25J 3/0403] of nitrogen
[F25J 3/04036] of oxygen
[F25J 3/04042] of argon or argon enriched stream -- This subclass covers processes or apparatus that separate the components of gaseous mixtures by first liquefying or solidifying them, specifically targeting streams of argon or argon-enriched gas. It falls under mechanical engineering for refrigeration or cooling, dealing with gas liquefaction, solidification, and pressure-based separation techniques.
[F25J 3/04048] by compression of cold gaseous streams, e.g. intermediate or oxygen enriched (waste) streams
[F25J 3/04054] of air -- F25J 3/04054 covers processes and apparatus that separate the components of air by cooling and pressurizing the mixture until it liquefies or solidifies, allowing the individual gases such as nitrogen, oxygen and argon to be drawn off. The classification pertains to the use of cryogenic techniques within refrigeration and cooling technology to achieve gas separation.
[F25J 3/0406] of nitrogen
[F25J 3/04066] of oxygen

[F25J 3/04078] providing pressurized products by liquid compression and vaporisation with cold recovery, i.e. so-called internal compression  operation of pumps  F25J3/04781 ; particular layout of pumps used in air fractionation units  F25J3/04866 -- The entry covers methods and pump designs that create pressurised gases by compressing a liquid and then vaporising it while reclaiming the cold energy, a process known as internal compression. It includes specific pump configurations employed in air-fractionation plants to separate gaseous mixtures through liquefaction and solidification.
[F25J 3/04084] of nitrogen
[F25J 3/0409] of oxygen
[F25J 3/04096] of argon or argon enriched stream -- This subgroup concerns processes or equipment that separate argon, or produce streams enriched in argon, from gaseous mixtures by applying high-pressure cooling, liquefaction or solidification methods usually employed in refrigeration or cooling systems.
[F25J 3/04103] using solely hydrostatic liquid head -- This CPC subclass covers methods and equipment that separate the components of a gas mixture by exploiting the pressure generated solely by a column of liquid, without additional mechanical compression. The hydrostatic head is used to achieve liquefaction or solidification of the target gases for separation.
[F25J 3/04109] Arrangements of compressors and /or their drivers  using work extraction by mechanical coupling of compression and cold expansion  F25J3/04381 -- A system that couples a compressor (or its drive) to a cold-expansion device so that work generated during compression is mechanically recovered during the expansion, improving energy efficiency in refrigeration or gas-separation cycles that rely on liquefaction or solidification.
[F25J 3/04115] characterised by the type of prime driver, e.g. hot gas expander -- The classification F25J 3/04115 covers refrigeration-based processes or equipment that separate gaseous mixtures by liquefaction or solidification, where the primary energy source is identified by the type of prime driver such as a hot-gas expander. It pertains to systems that use that driver to produce the necessary temperature and pressure differentials for component separation.
[F25J 3/04121] Steam turbine as the prime mechanical driver -- The subclass covers systems that employ a steam turbine as the primary mechanical driver to liquefy or solidify gases for separating the constituents of a gaseous mixture. It is situated within the CPC segment on refrigeration and cooling processes that use pressure and cold treatment for gas separation.
[F25J 3/04127] Gas turbine as the prime mechanical driver -- This subclass covers the use of a gas turbine as the main power source for equipment that separates components of gas mixtures by liquefaction or solidification. It includes processes and apparatus where the turbine directly drives compressors, expanders or other units involved in pressure-and-cold treatment.
[F25J 3/04133] Electrical motor as the prime mechanical driver -- F25J 3/04133 covers systems in which an electric motor provides the primary mechanical power for processes that separate the components of gas mixtures by liquefaction or solidification, such as cryogenic distillation apparatus. The classification falls under mechanical engineering, refrigeration or cooling, and gas-mixture separation by pressure and cold treatment.
[F25J 3/04139] Combination of different types of drivers mechanically coupled to the same compressor, possibly split on multiple compressor casings -- The subclass covers compressors used in refrigeration or cooling that are powered by two or more different driver types, such as electric, hydraulic or pneumatic, which are mechanically linked to the same compression unit and may be distributed across multiple casings.
[F25J 3/04145] Mechanically coupling of different compressors of the air fractionation process to the same driver(s) -- This CPC subclass covers systems where multiple compressors used in an air fractionation (cryogenic distillation) process are mechanically linked to a common drive, allowing them to be powered by the same motor or engine. The arrangement improves efficiency and synchronization in processes that separate gaseous mixtures by liquefaction or solidification.
[F25J 3/04151] Purification and (pre-)cooling of the feed air; recuperative heat-exchange with product streams -- This subclass covers methods and apparatus that purify and pre-cool the feed air for gas-separation processes, typically by removing contaminants and moisture before liquefaction or solidification, and incorporate recuperative heat-exchange that recovers thermal energy from the product streams to improve overall efficiency.
[F25J 3/04157] Afterstage cooling and so-called "pre-cooling" of the feed air upstream the air purification unit and main heat exchange line  F25J3/04618  takes precedence -- F25J 3/04157 covers methods and devices that cool the feed air after initial stages and pre-cool it before it reaches an air-purification unit or the main heat-exchange line, a step used in processes that separate gas mixtures by liquefaction or solidification. This subclass refines the broader category of refrigeration systems for gas-mixture separation, focusing on the upstream cooling of the intake stream.
[F25J 3/04163] Hot end purification of the feed air  arrangements of cold regenerators  F25J5/00 -- The code refers to methods and equipment that clean the incoming air stream at the hot side of a refrigeration system by using cold regeneration units to remove contaminants before liquefaction or solidification. It falls under the mechanical engineering class for separating gas mixtures by pressure and low temperature.
[F25J 3/04169] by adsorption of the impurities  adsorption in general  B01D53/02 -- The subclass F25J 3/04169 covers processes or apparatus that separate components of a gaseous mixture by first liquefying or solidifying the mixture and then removing impurities through adsorption, applying general adsorption techniques as defined in B01D53/02. It is situated within mechanical engineering, refrigeration or cooling, specifically the liquefaction, solidification or pressure-cold treatment of gases.
[F25J 3/04175] at a pressure of substantially more than the highest pressure column -- The subclass covers gas-mixture separation methods that operate at pressures substantially higher than the highest pressure column in the system, employing high-pressure liquefaction or solidification to isolate individual components.
[F25J 3/04181] Regenerating the adsorbents -- The code covers techniques for restoring adsorbent materials that are employed in gas-separation systems using liquefaction or solidification under pressure and low temperature. It includes processes such as heat-based desorption, pressure swing, and other regeneration cycles that prepare the adsorbents for reuse in refrigeration-related separation apparatus.
[F25J 3/04187] Cooling of the purified feed air by recuperative heat-exchange; Heat-exchange with product streams  arrangements of cold exchangers  F25J5/002 -- The code F25J 3/04187 covers processes that cool purified feed air by means of recuperative heat exchange, where heat is transferred between the incoming air and product streams using cold exchangers. It falls under mechanical engineering, refrigeration or cooling, specifically the liquefaction and solidification of gases by pressure and cold treatment.
[F25J 3/04193] Division of the main heat exchange line in consecutive sections having different functions -- The CPC entry F25J 3/04193 covers equipment that splits a primary heat-exchange line into consecutive sections, each engineered for a different function such as heating, cooling, subcooling or heat recovery within refrigeration, liquefaction or gas-separation processes.
[F25J 3/042] having an intermediate feed connection -- F25J 3/042 covers apparatus and processes for separating gas mixtures by liquefaction or solidification that include an intermediate feed connection, allowing additional feed to be introduced between stages of the main refrigeration circuit. The feature is used to improve efficiency or accommodate varying feed compositions.
[F25J 3/04206] including a so-called "auxiliary vaporiser" for vaporising and producing a gaseous product -- This subclass covers processes or equipment that separate components of a gas mixture by first liquefying or solidifying them and then using an auxiliary vaporiser to re-vaporise the desired portion, producing a gaseous product.
[F25J 3/04212] and simultaneously condensing vapor from a column serving as reflux within the or another column -- This subclass covers methods and equipment that, during a gas-separation process based on liquefaction or solidification, condense the vapor emerging from a reflux column at the same time as it is fed into the same or a different column. It is used in refrigeration or cooling systems that separate gas mixtures by pressure and cold treatment.
[F25J 3/04218] Parallel arrangement of the main heat exchange line in cores having different functions, e.g. in low pressure and high pressure cores  F25J3/04503  takes precedence -- The subclass F25J 3/04218 concerns refrigeration or cooling systems that employ a parallel configuration of the main heat-exchange line across multiple cores having distinct functions, such as low-pressure and high-pressure sections, to enable separate heat-transfer paths within the same apparatus. This arrangement is used in processes that liquefy, solidify or separate gases by pressure and cold treatment.
[F25J 3/04224] Cores associated with a liquefaction or refrigeration cycle -- F25J 3/04224 covers cores that are incorporated into a liquefaction or refrigeration cycle and serve to aid the separation of gaseous mixtures, typically by providing a surface for heat exchange, phase change or condensation within the system. Such cores are used in cryogenic heat exchangers, cold boxes or other apparatus where the gas is liquefied or solidified to enable separation.
[F25J 3/0423] Subcooling of liquid process streams -- F25J 3/0423 pertains to subcooling of liquid process streams in systems that separate gaseous mixtures by liquefaction or solidification. It covers methods and apparatus that further cool a condensed liquid below its saturation temperature to increase separation efficiency, reduce vapor formation, or improve storage stability.
[F25J 3/04236] Integration of different exchangers in a single core, so-called integrated cores  F25J3/04624  takes precedence -- The code F25J 3/04236 covers technologies that combine multiple heat-exchange elements into one unified core, creating an integrated core where different exchangers operate together. This approach is used in processes that separate gas mixtures by liquefying or solidifying them, improving efficiency and compactness.
[F25J 3/04242] Cold end purification of the feed air -- This CPC subclass covers technologies for cleaning the cold-end portion of an air feed in cryogenic separation plants, where contaminants such as moisture, carbon dioxide and hydrocarbons are removed before liquefaction. It includes apparatus and processes that purify feed air at low temperature using adsorption, condensation or similar methods.
[F25J 3/04248] Generation of cold for compensating heat leaks or liquid production, e.g. by Joule-Thompson expansion -- This subclass covers processes and apparatus that generate cold to offset heat leaks or to produce liquid, typically by Joule-Thompson expansion or similar throttling techniques, within systems for liquefying or solidifying gaseous mixtures.
[F25J 3/04254] using the cold stored in external cryogenic fluids  closed loop  F25J3/04278
[F25J 3/0426] The cryogenic component does not participate in the fractionation -- This subclass covers cryogenic separation processes where the cold-producing unit is isolated from the fractionation stage, providing cooling but not directly handling the gas streams during component separation. The apparatus may use a separate liquefier or refrigerator that supplies low temperature to the fractionation column without itself being involved in the separation.
[F25J 3/04266] and being liquefied hydrocarbons -- The subclass concerns methods and apparatus that separate the components of a gaseous mixture when the mixture contains hydrocarbons that are already in a liquefied state, using pressure and cold treatment to effect the separation. It covers processes such as distillation, flash evaporation or other techniques applied to liquefied natural gas, LPG or similar hydrocarbons to isolate individual fractions.
[F25J 3/04272] and comprising means for reducing the risk of pollution of hydrocarbons into the air fractionation -- The F25J 3/04272 subclass covers processes or equipment that separate the components of gaseous mixtures by liquefying or solidifying them and include features designed to limit the release of hydrocarbons into the air stream, thereby reducing pollution risk during the fractionation step.
[F25J 3/04278] using external refrigeration units, e.g. closed mechanical or regenerative refrigeration units -- This subclass deals with processes or apparatus that separate gaseous mixtures by liquefying or solidifying components using external refrigeration units, such as closed mechanical cycles or regenerative refrigeration plants, to achieve the necessary low temperatures and pressures for the separation.
[F25J 3/04284] using internal refrigeration by open-loop gas work expansion, e.g. of intermediate or oxygen enriched (waste-)streams  F25J3/04333  takes precedence -- Processes or apparatus that separate gas mixture constituents by employing internal refrigeration generated through open-loop expansion of a gas work stream, such as intermediate-temperature or oxygen-enriched waste streams, to liquefy or solidify components. This subclass is subordinate to F25J3/04333, which has priority over it.
[F25J 3/0429] of feed air, e.g. used as waste or product air or expanded into an auxiliary column -- This subcategory concerns the treatment of the feed air in gas-separation cycles that use liquefaction or solidification, covering the use of waste or product air and the expansion of such air in an auxiliary column as part of the separation process.
[F25J 3/04296] Claude expansion, i.e. expanded into the main or high pressure column -- The CPC subclass F25J 3/04296 concerns Claude expansion, a technique where a refrigerant or gas stream is expanded into the main or high-pressure column of a liquefaction or gas-separation system. The pressure drop creates cooling that aids the separation of gaseous mixtures in refrigeration or cryogenic processes.
[F25J 3/04303] Lachmann expansion, i.e. expanded into oxygen producing or low pressure column -- The F25J 3/04303 entry covers the Lachmann expansion technique, where a gas stream is expanded into a low-pressure column to produce oxygen by exploiting pressure-driven cooling and partial liquefaction. It relates to apparatus and processes that separate gas mixtures via liquefaction or solidification in refrigeration or cooling systems.

[F25J 3/044] using a single pressure main column system only  F25J3/0446 ,  F25J3/04624 ,  F25J3/04636  take precedence
[F25J 3/04406] using a dual pressure main column system  F25J3/0446 ,  F25J3/04624 ,  F25J3/04636  and  F25J3/04715  take precedence -- This CPC entry F25J 3/04406 covers processes or apparatus for separating the components of gaseous mixtures by using liquefaction or solidification together with a dual-pressure main column system. It belongs to mechanical engineering, refrigeration, and the sub-class for pressure-and-cold-treatment separation, and it takes precedence over the related codes F25J 3/0446, 3/04624, 3/04636 and 3/04715.
[F25J 3/04412] in a classical double column flowsheet, i.e. with thermal coupling by a main reboiler-condenser in the bottom of low pressure respectively top of high pressure column -- F25J 3/04412 covers a classical double-column flowsheet in which a single reboiler-condenser is thermally coupled to both columns, positioned at the bottom of the low-pressure column and the top of the high-pressure column, to facilitate separation of gaseous mixtures by liquefaction or solidification.
[F25J 3/04418] with thermally overlapping high and low pressure columns -- This subclass covers gas-separation processes or equipment that employ both a high-pressure and a low-pressure column whose temperature zones overlap so that heat is exchanged between them during liquefaction or solidification of the mixture. The thermal coupling improves efficiency by using the cold from one column to assist condensation or solidification in the other.
[F25J 3/04424] without thermally coupled high and low pressure columns, i.e. a so-called split columns -- The code F25J 3/04424 covers processes or apparatus for separating gaseous mixtures by liquefaction or solidification that do not use thermally coupled high- and low-pressure columns, known as split columns. In such split column systems the high-pressure and low-pressure stages operate independently, improving energy efficiency.
[F25J 3/0443] A main column system not otherwise provided, e.g. a modified double column flowsheet -- F25J 3/0443 relates to the design of a main separation column in a liquefaction-based gas-separation plant when the column is not defined by a standard arrangement, for example a modified double-column flowsheet. It addresses the structural and functional aspects of integrating such a column into the overall process.
[F25J 3/04436] using at least a triple pressure main column system  F25J3/0446 ,  F25J3/04624 ,  F25J3/04636  and  F25J3/04715  take precedence -- This CPC subclass F25J 3/04436 falls under mechanical engineering, refrigeration and cooling, specifically processes or apparatus for separating gaseous mixtures by liquefaction or solidification. It covers systems that employ at least a triple-pressure main column arrangement and supersedes the more specific groups F25J3/0446, F25J3/04624, F25J3/04636 and F25J3/04715.
[F25J 3/04442] in a double column flowsheet with a high pressure pre-rectifier -- F25J 3/04442 pertains to the design of a double-column flowsheet that includes a high-pressure pre-rectifier for separating gaseous mixtures by first liquefying or solidifying them. The subclass falls under mechanical engineering, refrigeration or cooling, and the broader group dealing with separation of gases using pressure and cold treatment.
[F25J 3/04448] in a double column flowsheet with an intermediate pressure column -- This CPC subclass covers gas-mixture separation methods that use liquefaction or solidification in a double-column arrangement where an intermediate-pressure column sits between a high-pressure and a low-pressure column, typically applied to cryogenic processes such as air or natural-gas separation.
[F25J 3/04454] a main column system not otherwise provided, e.g. serially coupling of columns or more than three pressure levels -- The entry covers configurations in which a primary separation column is extended by serially coupling additional columns or by adding more than three pressure stages, creating a main column system for the liquefaction, solidification or cold-treatment separation of gaseous mixtures. It pertains to apparatus that uses multiple pressure levels beyond the standard three to improve separation efficiency.
[F25J 3/0446] using the heat generated by mixing two different phases -- This subgroup covers processes or devices that separate gases by exploiting the heat released when two phases, such as liquid-gas or two liquids, are mixed, using that energy to drive liquefaction or solidification steps within the separation system.
[F25J 3/04466] for producing oxygen as a mixing column overhead gas by mixing gaseous air feed and liquid oxygen -- The code covers apparatus or processes that produce oxygen as the overhead gas in a mixing column by introducing a gaseous air feed together with liquid oxygen, using cryogenic liquefaction or solidification techniques to separate gas mixtures. It is used in cryogenic separation systems where the combined streams generate an oxygen-rich vapor at the top of the column.
[F25J 3/04472] using the cold from cryogenic liquids produced within the air fractionation unit and stored in internal or intermediate storages -- The subclass concerns processes that recover the cold energy released by cryogenic liquids generated in an air-fractionation unit and stored in internal or intermediate tanks, using that stored chill to aid the liquefaction, solidification or separation of gaseous mixtures.
[F25J 3/04478] for controlling purposes, e.g. start-up or back-up procedures  F25J3/04496  takes precedence -- The CPC subclass F25J 3/04478 covers control measures applied to liquefaction, solidification or pressure-cold treatment processes for separating gas mixtures, specifically start-up, backup or other operating-procedure controls. It is part of the refrigeration-engineering group, with subclass F25J 3/04496 regarded as the higher-ranking entry.
[F25J 3/04484] for purity control during steady state operation -- This subclass concerns methods and devices that monitor and maintain the purity of a gas stream while a liquefaction or solidification plant is operating under steady-state conditions. It includes sensors, feedback loops, and control strategies that detect contaminants and adjust operating parameters to keep product quality consistent.
[F25J 3/0449] for rapid load change of the air fractionation unit
[F25J 3/04496] for compensating variable air feed or variable product demand by alternating between periods of liquid storage and liquid assist
[F25J 3/04503] by exchanging "cold" between at least two different cryogenic liquids, e.g. independently from the main heat exchange line of the air fractionation and/or by using external alternating storage systems -- F25J 3/04503 covers methods for separating components of gaseous mixtures by transferring cold between two or more cryogenic liquids, either separate from the primary heat-exchange circuit in air fractionation or via external alternating storage units. The approach utilizes the cold energy of one liquid to condense or solidify constituents in another, facilitating efficient cryogenic separation.
[F25J 3/04509] within the cold part of the air fractionation, i.e. exchanging "cold" within the fractionation and/or main heat exchange line -- The code designates processes or apparatus that perform internal cold exchange within the cold section of an air-fractionation system, such as transferring "cold" between the fractionating column and the main heat-exchange line, using liquefaction or solidification methods to separate gaseous mixtures.
[F25J 3/04515] Simultaneously changing air feed and products output -- This subclass concerns processes or apparatus for separating gaseous mixtures by liquefaction or solidification in which the air feed and the product streams are varied simultaneously, such as adjusting the inlet flow while concurrently controlling the withdrawal of separated gases.
[F25J 3/04521] Coupling of the air fractionation unit to an air gas-consuming unit, so-called integrated processes  combined plants, e.g. engine plant combined with an industrial process  F01K23/064 ; gas-turbine plants supplying working fluid to a chemical process  F02C6/10 -- F25J 3/04521 covers processes or apparatus that integrate an air-fractionation unit with an air-consuming unit, creating combined plants where the separated gases feed directly into operations such as engines, gas-turbine power units, or chemical processes. This coupling enables more efficient use of the produced air fractions within a single system.
[F25J 3/04527] Integration with an oxygen consuming unit, e.g. glass facility, waste incineration or oxygen based processes in general -- The code F25J 3/04527 covers systems that combine a liquefaction or solidification gas-separation process with an oxygen-consuming unit such as a glass-making furnace, waste incinerator or other oxygen-based industrial process, allowing the cooling or separation apparatus to be integrated directly with the oxygen-using equipment for improved energy efficiency or heat recovery.
[F25J 3/04533] for the direct combustion of fuels in a power plant, so-called "oxyfuel combustion" -- The CPC subclass F25J 3/04533 covers processes or equipment that separate the components of gaseous mixtures by liquefaction or solidification when the fuel is burned directly in a power-plant using oxyfuel combustion. In this method oxygen rather than air is supplied to the furnace, producing a CO2-rich stream that can be readily condensed or otherwise separated.
[F25J 3/04539] for the H 2 /CO synthesis by partial oxidation or oxygen consuming reforming processes of fuels -- A process for producing a synthesis gas mixture of hydrogen and carbon monoxide by partially oxidising a fuel or by an oxygen-consuming reforming reaction, in which the gaseous feed is cooled, liquefied or solidified to aid separation of its components. The method falls under mechanical engineering, refrigeration, and the liquefaction-based separation of gas mixtures.
[F25J 3/04545] for the gasification of solid or heavy liquid fuels, e.g. integrated gasification combined cycle [IGCC] -- F25J 3/04545 covers processes that gasify solid or heavy liquid fuels, such as those used in integrated gasification combined cycle plants, by employing liquefaction or solidification steps to separate the resulting gaseous mixture into its constituent components. It falls under the broader field of refrigeration-based gas separation.
[F25J 3/04551] for the metal production -- The F25J 3/04551 subclass covers processes or apparatus that separate the components of gaseous mixtures by using liquefaction or solidification, where the resulting purified gases are employed in metal production. It falls under the broader categories of mechanical engineering, refrigeration or cooling, and the liquefaction, solidification or separation of gases by pressure and cold treatment.
[F25J 3/04557] for pig iron or steel making, e.g. blast furnace, Corex -- This subgroup concerns processes or equipment that employ liquefaction or solidification of gases to separate constituents within pig-iron or steel production, including gas-handling in blast furnaces and Corex systems. It falls under mechanical engineering, refrigeration or cooling, and the treatment of gaseous mixtures by pressure and cold.
[F25J 3/04563] Integration with a nitrogen consuming unit, e.g. for purging, inerting, cooling or heating -- This subclass covers methods and devices that combine a nitrogen-consuming unit - such as a system for purging, inerting, cooling, or heating - with a liquefaction or solidification process used to separate gas mixtures. It focuses on how the nitrogen stream is used concurrently with the main separation apparatus to improve efficiency or safety.
[F25J 3/04569] for enhanced or tertiary oil recovery -- The subclass F25J 3/04569 covers processes or equipment that separate gas mixture components by liquefaction or solidification when used to improve or conduct enhanced and tertiary oil recovery, such as injecting cooled gases or liquids to mobilise residual hydrocarbons.
[F25J 3/04575] for a gas expansion plant, e.g. dilution of the combustion gas in a gas turbine -- The subclass covers gas-expansion installations that separate components of a gaseous mixture by employing liquefaction or solidification techniques, specifically designs where combustion gas is diluted before being fed to a gas turbine. It pertains to processes and apparatus for such gas-mix treatment in refrigeration or cooling systems.
[F25J 3/04581] Hot gas expansion of indirect heated nitrogen -- The CPC subclass F25J 3/04581 covers processes in which nitrogen that has been heated indirectly is expanded as a hot gas to achieve cooling, a step used in separating the components of gaseous mixtures by liquefaction or solidification. It falls under mechanical engineering, refrigeration and cooling, and deals with gas-mixture separation through pressure and cold treatment.
[F25J 3/04587] for the NH 3  synthesis, e.g. for adjusting the H 2 /N 2  ratio
[F25J 3/04593] The air gas consuming unit is also fed by an air stream -- This subclass covers systems in which the air-gas-consuming unit of a liquefaction or solidification separation process is simultaneously supplied with an external air stream. It relates to refrigeration or cooling technology that separates gas mixtures by pressure-induced condensation or freezing, typically in industrial air-separation plants.
[F25J 3/046] Completely integrated air feed compression, i.e. common MAC -- F25J 3/046 covers processes and apparatus in which the air feed compressor is fully integrated with the liquefaction or solidification unit, forming a common mechanical arrangement (common MAC). This integration is used for separating gaseous mixtures by pressure and cold treatment in refrigeration or cooling systems.
[F25J 3/04606] Partially integrated air feed compression, i.e. independent MAC for the air fractionation unit plus additional air feed from the air gas consuming unit -- This classification covers processes where a separate motor-driven air compressor supplies part of the air to a fractionation unit while additional air is drawn from a downstream consumer unit, allowing partial integration of the air feed compression step. It relates to systems that combine independent compression with recycling of consumed air in liquefaction or solidification gas-separation operations.
[F25J 3/04612] Heat exchange integration with process streams, e.g. from the air gas consuming unit -- The subclass covers apparatus or processes that integrate heat exchangers with streams that consume air-derived gases, recovering heat from those streams to assist liquefaction, solidification or other cold-treatment steps used to separate gas mixtures.
[F25J 3/04618] for cooling an air stream fed to the air fractionation unit -- This subclass deals with methods or devices that lower the temperature of an incoming air stream before it enters an air fractionation system, typically using liquefaction or solidification techniques to enable efficient separation of the gas components.
[F25J 3/04624] using integrated mass and heat exchange, so-called non-adiabatic rectification, e.g. dephlegmator, reflux exchanger -- This subclass covers equipment or methods that combine mass and heat transfer in a single unit to achieve gas separation by non-adiabatic rectification, such as dephlegmators or reflux exchangers, where cooling and condensing occur together within the apparatus.
[F25J 3/0463] Simultaneously between rectifying and stripping sections, i.e. double dephlegmator -- A double dephlegmator positioned between the rectifying and stripping sections of a gas-separation plant provides simultaneous condensation and reflux, enhancing the liquefaction or solidification of the mixture and improving energy efficiency in the separation process.
[F25J 3/04636] using a hybrid air separation unit, e.g. combined process by cryogenic separation and non-cryogenic separation techniques  F25J3/04733  and  F25J3/04757  take precedence -- The subclass covers processes or equipment that separate gaseous mixtures by employing a hybrid air-separation unit, which integrates cryogenic separation with one or more non-cryogenic methods such as pressure-swing or membrane techniques. It falls under mechanical engineering, refrigeration, and liquefaction of gases.

[F25J 3/04648] argon -- F25J 3/04648 covers processes or apparatus that separate argon from gaseous mixtures by first liquefying or solidifying the mixture and then isolating the argon component, typically using cryogenic distillation or fractional crystallization under pressure and low temperature.

[F25J 3/0466] as a parallel working rectification column or auxiliary column system in a single pressure main column system -- The code covers a configuration where a secondary or parallel rectification column is operated alongside the main column at the same pressure, serving as an auxiliary system to improve separation of gaseous mixtures by liquefaction or solidification within a single-pressure main column arrangement.
[F25J 3/04666] as a parallel working rectification column of the low pressure column in a dual pressure main column system -- The entry F25J 3/04666 pertains to a configuration in which a parallel-working rectification column is installed alongside the low-pressure column of a dual-pressure main column system, used to improve the separation of gaseous mixtures through liquefaction or solidification under combined pressure and cooling. This apparatus falls under mechanical engineering, refrigeration or cooling, and specifically addresses processes for separating gas constituents by pressure-driven cold treatment.
[F25J 3/04672] having a top condenser -- This subclass covers refrigeration or gas-separation apparatus where the condenser is positioned at the top of the equipment, allowing vapour to condense and flow downward for further liquefaction or separation.

This arrangement supports the primary separation process in refrigeration or cooling applications that rely on pressure and cold treatment.
[F25J 3/04715] The auxiliary column system simultaneously produces oxygen -- This subclass covers auxiliary column arrangements in cryogenic air-separation plants that generate oxygen concurrently with the primary column. The system integrates an additional column to improve oxygen yield while the main column handles other gases. It falls under mechanical engineering, refrigeration, and gas-liquefaction technologies.
[F25J 3/04721] Producing pure argon, e.g. recovered from a crude argon column -- The subclass covers processes and apparatus that obtain high-purity argon by separating it from other gases using liquefaction or solidification methods, such as recovery from a crude argon column. It is situated in the CPC hierarchy under mechanical engineering, refrigeration or cooling, and liquefaction, solidification or separation of gases by pressure and cold treatment.

It relates to methods that manage temperature, pressure and flow to initiate and regulate the separation.

It falls under mechanical engineering, specifically refrigeration or cooling, and addresses the management of temperature, pressure and flow to achieve efficient gas separation.
[F25J 3/04775] Air purification and pre-cooling -- F25J 3/04775 covers processes or apparatus that separate constituents of gaseous mixtures by first liquefying or solidifying them, with a focus on cleaning and pre-cooling air before further treatment. The invention typically involves cooling air to condense moisture or contaminants, thereby improving subsequent purification steps.
[F25J 3/04781] Pressure changing devices, e.g. for compression, expansion, liquid pumping -- The subclass deals with apparatus that change the pressure of fluids in processes that separate gas mixtures by liquefaction or solidification, such as compressors, expanders and liquid pumps. These pressure-changing devices are used in refrigeration or cooling systems to enable the condensation, solidification or subsequent separation of gaseous constituents.

[F25J 3/04824] Stopping of the process, e.g. defrosting or deriming; Back-up procedures -- F25J 3/04824 relates to methods and apparatus for stopping a gas-separation process that uses liquefaction or solidification, including operations such as defrosting, deriming, and other shutdown or backup procedures.
[F25J 3/0483] Rapid load change of the air fractionation unit -- This code covers methods and equipment for handling sudden changes in demand or operating conditions of air-fractionation units that separate air into its components by liquefaction or solidification. It addresses control strategies, design features, and operational procedures that enable the plant to respond quickly to load variations while maintaining product quality and efficiency.
[F25J 3/04836] Variable air feed, i.e. "load" or product demand during specified periods, e.g. during periods with high respectively low power costs  F25J3/0483  takes precedence -- F25J 3/04836 covers refrigeration or gas-separation processes that adjust the air feed rate according to changing load or product demand, such as varying the intake during periods of high or low electricity cost.
[F25J 3/04842] Intermittent process, so-called batch process -- The code refers to batch, or intermittent, processes that separate the components of a gaseous mixture by first liquefying or solidifying the mixture and then extracting the desired gases in separate steps. Such methods are used in refrigeration and cooling apparatus where the treatment occurs in distinct cycles rather than continuously. This classification covers the equipment and procedures that implement the on-off operation for gas separation.
[F25J 3/04848] Control strategy, e.g. advanced process control or dynamic modeling -- This classification covers control strategies for equipment that separate gas mixtures by liquefaction or solidification, such as advanced process control, dynamic modeling, and optimization of operating parameters like temperature, pressure and flow. It includes algorithms, feedback loops and model-based control used in refrigeration-based gas separation plants.
[F25J 3/04854] Safety aspects of operation -- This CPC subclass deals with safety considerations in operating equipment that separates gas mixtures by liquefaction or solidification, such as cryogenic distillation or gas liquefiers. It covers measures, devices, and procedures to prevent hazards like overpressure, leaks, or cryogenic burns during the process.

[F25J 3/04903] Plates or trays -- This subclass covers plates or trays used in cryogenic separation equipment that rely on liquefaction or solidification to divide gaseous mixtures. The plates provide structured surfaces within columns or chambers where condensed or frozen components contact the gas phase, enabling efficient component separation.
[F25J 3/04909] Structured packings -- Structured packings are internally arranged, often corrugated or lattice-like elements placed in separation columns to provide a large surface area for mass-transfer, improving the efficiency of gas liquefaction or solidification separation processes. They are employed in refrigeration and cooling systems to enhance contact between the gas mixture and cold surfaces, facilitating condensation or freezing of specific components.

[F25J 3/0695] Start-up or control of the process; Details of the apparatus used -- The class covers the methods and equipment used to start up, regulate or monitor processes that separate gaseous mixtures by liquefaction or solidification, including the specific features of the apparatus that control temperature, pressure and flow during these operations.
