[F25J 5/00] Arrangements of cold exchangers or cold accumulators in separation or liquefaction plants -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Particular configuration and constructional design of cold exchangers and cold accumulators for use in cryogenic separation or liquefaction plants (in contrast to the description of the general functioning of said exchangers and accumulators as found in the flowsheet of the cryogenic process which are to be classified in the corresponding main groups F25J1/00 and F25J3/00 )  **Glossary:** - Reversing heat exchanger: Recuperative ("counter-current") heat exchanger with continuous heat exchange during the purification phase followed by a regeneration phase; there is no cold accumulation; can be arranged in a single, compact heat exchanger - Cold accumulator: Heat exchange means containing a cold regenerative material for storing and exchanging the cold in a discontinuous manner, i.e. alternating purification and regenerating phase; always at least two heat exchange vessels are necessary - Bath type reboiler: Thermo-siphon reboiler, with natural or forced liquid circulation, or core-in-kettle reboiler with pool boiling - Down-flowing type reboiler: Thin falling liquid film flowing down the heat exchange surface, plate or tube, and is subsequently vaporised
[F25J 5/002] for continuously recuperating cold, i.e. in a so-called recuperative heat exchanger -- It covers arrangements in refrigeration or gas separation plants where a recuperative heat exchanger continuously recovers cold from one stream to pre-cool another, enabling energy saving. Used for continuous cold recuperation in liquefaction or separation processes.
[F25J 5/005] in a reboiler-condenser, e.g. within a column -- The code F25J 5/005 concerns the placement of cold exchangers or cold accumulators within the reboiler-condenser section of a column used for gas liquefaction, cooling or separation, detailing how these heat-exchange elements are arranged inside the column to improve thermal efficiency.
[F25J 5/007] combined with mass exchange, i.e. in a so-called dephlegmator -- This subclass covers plant layouts where heat-exchange devices or cold storage units are integrated with a mass-exchange section, known as a dephlegmator, to improve separation or liquefaction of gases by simultaneous heat and mass transfer.
[F26] DRYING -- F26 in the CPC covers technologies for removing moisture from materials or products, including equipment, methods and process conditions used in drying operations. It includes thermal, convection, vacuum, freeze and microwave drying as applied in mechanical engineering and related industries.
[F26B] DRYING SOLID MATERIALS OR OBJECTS BY REMOVING LIQUID THEREFROM -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Processes and specific machines and arrangements for drying objects, solid materials or fluent materials containing solids by removing water or other liquids therefrom.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Drying gases and vapours -> B01D
[F26B 1/00] Processes for drying -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Preliminary treatment, i.e. not the actual drying treatment, of solid materials or objects.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Applying a combustible liquid, which is burnt off later, onto the solid materials or objects -> F26B3/005 - Dipping the solid materials or objects into, or mixing with, chemical liquids for subsequent drying -> F26B5/005
[F26B 1/005] by means of disintegrating, e.g. crushing, shredding, milling the materials to be dried  F26B17/102 ,  F26B17/103  take precedence
[F26B 11/00] Machines or apparatus for drying solid materials or objects with movement which is non-progressive -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Machines or apparatus for drying solid materials or objects with primarily non-progressive, i.e. stirring, movement.  The movement of solid materials in e.g. horizontal rotating drums such as kilns is considered not primarily progressive even though opposed inlets and outlets are typically present.
[F26B 11/02] in moving drums or other mainly-closed receptacles -- F26B 11/02 covers drying machines that use rotating drums or other largely closed vessels to remove liquid from solid materials. The classification applies to equipment where the material moves within the drum while the drying action occurs, rather than progressing through a linear system.
[F26B 11/022] Arrangements of drives, bearings, supports -- F26B 11/022 designates machines that dry solid materials or objects while the items remain essentially stationary, covering the layout of drive mechanisms, bearing assemblies and supporting structures that power and hold the dryer in place.
[F26B 11/024] Arrangements for gas-sealing the drum -- The F26B 11/024 subclass covers designs that provide a gas-tight seal for the drum of drying machines used on solid materials. Such arrangements prevent unwanted air ingress or egress, improving drying efficiency and allowing precise control of the internal atmosphere during non-progressive drum movement.
[F26B 11/026] Arrangements for charging or discharging the materials to be dried, e.g. discharging by reversing drum rotation, using spiral-type inserts -- The classification refers to mechanisms that load or unload solid material in non-progressive drying machines, such as reversing the drum's rotation to discharge material or using spiral-type inserts to move material out of the dryer.
[F26B 11/028] Arrangements for the supply or exhaust of gaseous drying medium for direct heat transfer, e.g. perforated tubes, annular passages, burner arrangements, dust separation, combined direct and indirect heating -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Arrangement for the supply or exhaust of gaseous drying medium for direct heat transfer, e.g. perforated tubes, annular passages, burner arrangements, dust separation, combined direct and indirect heat transfer
[F26B 11/04] rotating about a horizontal or slightly-inclined axis -- F26B 11/04 covers machines that dry solid items by rotating them around a horizontal or slightly inclined axis, providing a non-progressive motion that tosses the material to enhance moisture removal. Typical examples are rotary drum dryers and tumblers where the drum spins in a horizontal plane or slight incline, allowing heated air to pass through the material as it is lifted and dropped.
[F26B 11/0404] with internal subdivision of the drum, e.g. for subdividing or recycling the material to be dried  spiral-type or other inserts for discharging purposes  F26B11/026 -- F26B 11/0404 covers drying machines that use a rotating drum equipped with internal subdivisions such as spiral or other inserts. These features subdivide the material inside the drum to improve drying efficiency or enable recycling, and may also aid in discharging the dried product.
[F26B 11/0409] the subdivision consisting of a plurality of substantially radially oriented internal walls, e.g. forming multiple sector-shaped chambers -- F26B 11/0409 covers drying machines that incorporate several substantially radially oriented internal walls, forming multiple sector-shaped chambers within the dryer. This design creates separate compartments that can be heated or ventilated independently, improving uniformity and efficiency when drying solid materials in a non-progressive motion.
[F26B 11/0413] the subdivision consisting of concentric walls, e.g. multi-pass or recirculation systems; the subdivision consisting of spiral-shaped walls  tubular or annular passages for supply or exhaust of drying gas  F26B11/028 -- F26B 11/0413 covers drying machines that employ concentric walls, such as multi-pass or recirculation systems, and spiral-shaped walls, including tubular or annular passages used to supply or exhaust the drying gas.
[F26B 11/0418] the subdivision consisting of a plurality of parallel tubes, e.g. through which the material to be dried is conveyed in single or multi-pass fashion  sector-shaped tubes  F26B11/0409 -- F26B 11/0418 covers drying machines in which solid material moves through a series of parallel tubes, such as sector-shaped conduits, allowing the material to pass once or multiple times for heat transfer. The design is used for non-progressive movement of the material during drying.
[F26B 11/0422] the tubes having internal members -- F26B 11/0422 classifies drying machines that employ tubes containing internal members such as baffles, rods or coils to enhance heat transfer or airflow. It falls under non-progressive movement drying apparatus for solid materials, describing the structural features of the tubes used in the drying process.
[F26B 11/0427] Constructional details, e.g. arrangements of drives, supports, bearings, gas-sealing, heating medium supply or exhaust -- F26B 11/0427 covers the structural features of drying machines for solid materials that move without progressive travel, including the layout of drives, supports and bearings, methods of gas sealing, and configurations for supplying or exhausting the heating medium.
[F26B 11/0431] Arrangements for feeding or discharging materials to be dried -- The code F26B 11/0431 covers the feeding and discharge arrangements used in machines that dry solid materials while the material remains stationary relative to the dryer. It includes designs for delivering material into the dryer and removing it after drying, such as hoppers, conveyors, or rotary feeds that do not advance the material through the drying zone. This falls under mechanical engineering for drying solid objects.
[F26B 11/0436] comprising multiple stages, e.g. multiple rotating drums subsequently receiving the material to be dried; Provisions for heat recuperation -- F26B 11/0436 pertains to drying machines that employ a series of rotating drums arranged in successive stages, allowing material to move from one drum to the next, and incorporate means for heat recuperation to enhance energy efficiency.
[F26B 11/044] the drum or receptacle having a variable outer or inner diameter in axial direction, e.g. trunconical; the drum or receptacle having a polygonal or non-cylindrical shape  F26B11/0436 ,  F26B11/049  take precedence -- F26B 11/044 covers drying machines in which the drum or receptacle has a changing cross-section along its axis, such as a tapered or polygonal shape, allowing the volume to vary during operation. This design improves heat transfer and material handling in non-progressive drying of solid objects.
[F26B 11/0445] having conductive heating arrangements, e.g. heated drum wall -- This subclass covers machines that dry solid materials by non-progressive movement, such as rotating or oscillating action, where the heat is generated through a conductive element like a heated drum wall. It falls under mechanical engineering, drying of solid materials, and specifically apparatus with conductive heating arrangements.
[F26B 11/045] using heated internal elements, e.g. which move through or convey the materials to be dried  loose bodies, e.g. balls  F26B11/0472 -- F26B 11/045 relates to machines that dry solid materials by means of heated internal elements that move through or convey the material, for example devices that transport loose bodies such as balls while the elements are heated.
[F26B 11/0454] the elements being discs -- This subclass covers drying machines in which the active drying surfaces consist of disc-shaped elements, such as rotating or stationary discs that transfer heat to solid materials while the workpiece remains stationary or moves only locally. It falls under the F26B group for drying solid materials with non-progressive motion.
[F26B 11/0459] the elements being chains -- F26B 11/0459 pertains to the chain components of drying machines that handle solid materials without progressive motion. It covers the design, arrangement and drive of chains used to transport or agitate items within dryers such as belt or rotary dryers, where the chain forms the moving element that facilitates drying.
[F26B 11/0463] having internal elements, e.g. which are being moved or rotated by means other than the rotating drum wall  F26B11/0404 ,  F26B11/0445  take precedence -- The code F26B 11/0463 designates drying machines for solid objects that include internal components which are moved or rotated by mechanisms other than the rotating drum wall, such as internal rollers or agitators. It falls under the hierarchy of mechanical engineering, drying, and drying solid materials with non-progressive movement, and is superseded by the more specific sub-entries F26B11/0404 and F26B11/0445.
[F26B 11/0468] for disintegrating, crushing, or for being mixed with the materials to be dried -- This CPC entry describes machines or apparatus for drying solid materials that include means for disintegrating, crushing, or mixing the material while it is being dried. It belongs to the mechanical-engineering drying subclass for non-progressive motion, code F26B 11/0468.
[F26B 11/0472] the elements being loose bodies or materials, e.g. balls, which may have a sorbent effect  chains fixed to the drum  F26B11/0459 -- F26B 11/0472 covers drying machines that employ non-progressive movement of the workpiece and include loose bodies such as balls or other sorbent materials inside the drum. These elements can be chained to the drum to enhance moisture removal by providing additional surface area and mechanical agitation.
[F26B 11/0477] for mixing, stirring or conveying the materials to be dried, e.g. mounted to the wall, rotating with the drum -- The subclass F26B 11/0477 describes drying machines for solid materials that include mechanisms for mixing, stirring or conveying the load during operation, such as wall-mounted agitators or drums that rotate with the material. These features are intended to improve heat transfer and achieve more uniform drying.
[F26B 11/0481] the elements having a screw- or auger-like shape, or form screw- or auger-like channels -- This subclass covers machines or apparatus for drying solid materials where the drying elements have a screw or auger shape, or contain screw-like channels, creating a non-progressive movement of the material through the dryer.
[F26B 11/0486] the elements being held stationary, e.g. internal scraper blades -- F26B 11/0486 covers drying machines in which certain components, such as internal scraper blades, remain fixed while the material to be dried moves past them. The stationary elements convey, scrape or shape the material during the drying process, enabling continuous non-progressive movement.
[F26B 11/049] with provisions for working under increased or reduced pressure, with or without heating -- F26B 11/049 covers machines or apparatus for drying solid materials where the drying chamber operates under increased or reduced pressure, optionally with heating, and the material is moved by non-progressive motions such as shaking, tumbling or vibrating rather than linear transport.
[F26B 11/0495] with provisions for drying by electro-magnetic means, e.g. radiation, microwaves  burner arrangements  F26B11/028 -- It covers machines for drying solid items where the drying action uses electromagnetic energy such as infrared or microwaves, integrated within non-progressive movement drying apparatus. The classification applies to equipment that combines such electromagnetic heating with traditional dryer mechanisms.
[F26B 11/08] rotating about a vertical or steeply-inclined axis -- Machines or apparatus that dry solid materials or objects while the material remains essentially stationary and the equipment rotates around a vertical or sharply tilted axis, using heat or other drying means to remove liquid. The classification covers non-progressive motion drying devices in the mechanical engineering field.
[F26B 11/10] with stirring devices which are held stationary -- F26B 11/10 covers drying machines for solid materials where the workpiece does not travel through the equipment, and which incorporate stationary stirring devices to improve heat transfer and uniform drying. The classification falls under mechanical engineering, drying solid materials by removing liquid, and specifies apparatus with non-progressive motion and built-in stirrers.
[F26B 11/12] in stationary drums or other mainly-closed receptacles with moving stirring devices -- F26B 11/12 pertains to machines that dry solid materials in stationary drums or largely closed receptacles by using moving stirring devices. The stirring keeps the material in non-progressive motion while heat removes the liquid, providing efficient drying in a confined container.
[F26B 11/14] the stirring device moving in a horizontal or slightly-inclined plane -- F26B 11/14 refers to drying machines that employ a stirring device moving in a horizontal or slightly inclined plane to agitate solid materials during the drying process, providing non-progressive motion for uniform moisture removal.
[F26B 11/16] the stirring device moving in a vertical or steeply-inclined plane -- F26B 11/16 covers machines or apparatus for drying solid materials where the drying action involves a stirring device that moves in a vertical or steeply inclined plane, providing non-progressive movement of the material during the drying process. The device is used to enhance heat transfer and moisture removal by continuously turning the material while it remains largely in place.
[F26B 11/18] on or in moving dishes, trays, pans, or other mainly-open receptacles -- F26B 11/18 covers machines or apparatus that dry solid items placed in moving dishes, trays, pans, or other mainly-open receptacles, where the receptacle's motion is non-progressive such as oscillating or rotating. The classification relates to drying processes that remove liquid from solid materials using such equipment.
[F26B 11/181] the receptacle being a foraminous, perforated or open-structured drum or drum-like container, e.g. rotating around a substantially horizontal or vertical axis; the receptacle being multiple perforated drums, e.g. in superimposed arrangement -- F26B 11/181 covers drying machines whose receptacle is a foraminous, perforated or open-structured drum or drum-like container that rotates about a horizontal or vertical axis, including configurations with several perforated drums arranged in superimposed or stacked positions. This subclass pertains to apparatus that moves solid material through such drums to remove liquid.
[F26B 11/182] Arrangements for the supply or exhaust of gaseous drying medium, e.g. perforated tubes  F26B11/185  takes precedence -- This CPC subclass covers machines or apparatus for drying solid materials where the drying gas is supplied or exhausted through arrangements such as perforated tubes. It falls under mechanical engineering, drying solid materials by removing liquid, and is coded F26B 11/182, with the more specific subclass F26B 11/185 taking precedence.
[F26B 11/184] provided with internal means for mixing, stirring or conveying the materials to be dried  F26B11/182 ,  F26B11/185  take precedence -- This subclass covers machines or apparatus for drying solid materials or objects by removing liquid, where the material is moved in a non-progressive manner and the device incorporates internal means for mixing, stirring or conveying the material during drying. It falls under the broader categories of mechanical engineering, drying and drying solid materials, and is subordinate to subclasses 11/182 and 11/185, which take precedence.
[F26B 11/185] the drum provided with internal subdivisions or multiple walls -- Machines for drying solid materials that employ a drum equipped with internal subdivisions or multiple walls, creating separate chambers or increased surface area to improve heat transfer and moisture removal during non-progressive motion. This design is used in rotating drum dryers to enhance efficiency and control of the drying process.
[F26B 11/187] the subdivisions consisting of sector-shaped perforated chambers -- F26B 11/187 refers to drying machines that split the drying space into sector-shaped chambers perforated to permit airflow, allowing solid materials or objects to be dried uniformly while remaining stationary; the design is used where the drying medium moves non-progressively.
[F26B 11/188] the subdivisions consisting of concentric perforated walls, or spiral-shaped walls -- F26B 11/188 classifies drying machines for solid materials that use non-progressive motion and incorporate internal subdivisions formed by concentric perforated walls or spiral-shaped walls to enhance heat transfer and moisture removal.
[F26B 11/20] with stirring devices which are held stationary -- This subclass pertains to drying machines that handle solid materials or objects with a non-progressive movement, i.e., the workpiece does not travel through the equipment. It specifically covers apparatus equipped with stirring devices that remain stationary while agitating the material during the drying process, such as drum or tunnel dryers fitted with fixed paddles or mixers.
[F26B 11/22] on or in stationary dishes, trays, pans, or other mainly-open receptacles, with moving stirring devices -- This subgroup covers machines that dry solid items placed in stationary open containers such as dishes, trays, or pans while a stirring mechanism moves within the container to enhance drying. The devices combine non-progressive motion of the material with agitation to improve heat transfer and moisture removal.
[F26B 13/00] Machines and apparatus for drying fabrics, fibres, yarns, or other materials in long lengths, with progressive movement -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Machines and apparatus for drying materials in long length. The materials move progressively through the apparatus either in the form of a coil or uncoiled, e.g. in the form of a web.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Drying yarn hanks -> F26B15/124
[F26B 13/001] Drying and oxidising yarns, ribbons or the like -- The subclass F26B 13/001 covers machines and apparatus that dry and simultaneously oxidise yarns, ribbons or similar textile products, usually by passing them through heated air or other oxidising media while they move continuously. It belongs to the mechanical-engineering group for drying solid materials such as fabrics and fibres.
[F26B 13/002] Drying coated, e.g. enamelled, varnished, wires -- This subclass of F26B deals with equipment for drying coated wires such as enamelled or varnished types, where the wire moves continuously through a dryer to remove liquid from the coating.
[F26B 13/003] in the shape of spools, coils, bobbins or the like  F26B21/007  takes precedence; drying yarn hanks  F26B15/124 -- F26B 13/003 designates machines and apparatus that dry fabrics, fibres, yarns or similar solid materials when they are arranged as spools, coils, bobbins or comparable shapes and are moved progressively through the dryer. The group is subordinate to F26B 21/007 and related to drying yarn hanks covered by F26B 15/124.
[F26B 13/004] Drying ribbons -- This CPC subclass covers machines and apparatus that dry ribbons - continuous strips of material - by removing moisture while the ribbon moves through the dryer. It includes equipment designed for fabrics, fibers or yarns in long lengths with progressive movement.
[F26B 13/005] Seals, locks, e.g. gas barriers for web drying enclosures -- The CPC subclass F26B 13/005 covers seals, locks and gas-barrier devices used in web-drying enclosures for continuous fabric, fibre or yarn processing. These components prevent uncontrolled airflow and moisture exchange, maintaining the required drying atmosphere around the moving web.
[F26B 13/006] with movement in a spiral path -- This subclass defines machines and apparatus that dry fabrics, fibres, yarns or other long-length materials by moving them along a spiral path, removing liquid as they advance. It is situated under mechanical engineering, drying solid materials by liquid removal.
[F26B 13/007] Treating a particular portion of the web or plate, e.g. the edge -- The CPC subclass F26B 13/007 covers machines or apparatus that dry fabrics, fibres, yarns or other continuous materials while treating a specific portion of the web or plate, such as its edge, during the drying process. It falls under mechanical engineering, drying solid materials by removing liquid, and pertains to equipment with progressive movement that applies localized treatment while the material advances.
[F26B 13/008] Controlling the moisture profile across the width of the material -- F26B 13/008 covers devices and methods that adjust the moisture distribution across the width of a continuously moving web such as fabric, yarn or fiber during drying. It includes control systems, sensors and adjustable drying elements that create a uniform or intentionally varied moisture profile to improve product quality, reduce defects or meet specific performance requirements.
[F26B 13/06] with movement in a sinuous or zig-zag path -- This subclass covers drying machines that handle fabrics, fibers, yarns or similar continuous materials and convey them along a winding or zig-zag trajectory while the material is dried. The sinuous path enables uniform heat exposure and a compact equipment layout.
[F26B 13/08] using rollers -- F26B 13/08 covers machines that dry fabrics, fibers, yarns or similar materials presented in continuous lengths by passing them over rollers that remove moisture. The rollers are part of a drying system that advances the material while heat or other means evaporate liquid. It falls under mechanical engineering, drying solid materials.
[F26B 13/10] Arrangements for feeding, heating or supporting materials -- Machines and apparatus that move long lengths of fabric, fiber, yarn or similar material through a dryer while providing means to feed, heat, or support the material, such as rollers, conveyors, guides or heated elements, to ensure uniform drying and continuous processing.
[F26B 13/101] Supporting materials without tension, e.g. on or between foraminous belts -- The classification covers drying equipment that uses a support layer placed on or between perforated transport belts to hold fabrics, fibers, yarns or other continuous materials without applying tension. The support material keeps the workpiece flat and stable while the belts move through the drying zone, improving contact with hot air or other drying media.
[F26B 13/102] the materials, e.g. web, being supported in loops by rods or poles, which may be moving transversely, e.g. festoon dryers -- F26B 13/102 covers drying apparatus where the material, such as a web, is held in loops on rods or poles that may move sideways, typical of festoon dryers used for continuous fabrics, fibres or yarns. The looped support allows the material to be exposed to hot air while being conveyed transversely.
[F26B 13/103] with mechanical supporting means, e.g. belts, rollers, and fluid impingement arrangement having a displacing effect on the materials -- F26B 13/103 covers machines that dry continuous lengths of fabrics, fibres, yarns or similar materials by moving them over mechanical supports such as belts or rollers while applying a fluid jet that pushes the material, enhancing moisture removal.
[F26B 13/104] supported by fluid jets only; Fluid blowing arrangements for flotation dryers, e.g. coanda nozzles -- This subclass covers drying machines that support the material only with fluid jets, such as flotation dryers that use Coanda-effect nozzles to levitate and transport the workpiece while blowing air or gas to remove moisture.
[F26B 13/105] Drying webs by contact with heated surfaces other than rollers or drums -- The CPC subclass F26B 13/105 covers machines that dry continuous webs such as fabrics, fibres or yarns by bringing them into contact with heated surfaces that are not rollers or drums. It pertains to drying solid materials in a progressive-movement process using flat or plate-type heating elements.
[F26B 13/106] by moving them through a fluidised bed of heated particles -- This subclass covers drying equipment that transports fabrics, fibers, yarns or other long-length materials through a fluidised bed of heated particles, using the particle stream to remove moisture. The process relies on convective heat transfer and particle movement to achieve efficient drying of solid materials.
[F26B 13/107] Arrangements for guiding the feed end or trailing end of the materials, e.g. threading of webs -- The F26B 13/107 subclass covers devices that control the entry or exit of material in continuous-drying equipment, such as mechanisms that thread, guide, or align the leading and trailing ends of fabrics, fibers, yarns or other webs as they move through the dryer. These arrangements ensure proper positioning, tension control and smooth progression of the material during the drying process.
[F26B 13/108] using one or more blowing devices, e.g. nozzle bar, the effective area of which is adjustable to the width of the material -- The entry F26B 13/108 covers machines for drying fabrics, fibers, yarns or other continuous materials that employ one or more blowing devices such as a nozzle bar, where the effective blowing area can be adjusted to match the width of the material being processed.
[F26B 13/12] Controlling movement, tension or position of material
[F26B 13/14] Rollers, ; -- F26B 13/14 relates to rollers employed in machines that continuously dry fabrics, fibres, yarns or other long-length materials. The subclass covers heated or unheated rollers that transfer heat and tension to remove liquid as the material moves through the dryer.
[F26B 13/145] on the non-perforated outside surface of which the material is being dried by convection or radiation -- F26B 13/145 covers machines and apparatus for drying fabrics, fibres, yarns or other elongated materials in which the material's outer surface is solid and non-perforated, and drying is achieved by convection or radiation of heat. This subclass falls under drying solid materials by removing liquid, specifically for continuous-movement drying equipment.
[F26B 13/16] perforated -- This subclass covers drying machines for continuous lengths of textile or similar material that incorporate perforated elements such as belts, drums or plates to facilitate airflow and moisture removal. It relates to apparatus where the material moves progressively while being dried by contact with perforated surfaces.
[F26B 13/18] heated cooled,
[F26B 13/183] Arrangements for heating, cooling, condensate removal -- F26B 13/183 covers the design of heating, cooling and condensate-removal systems integrated into continuous drying machines for fabrics, fibres, yarns or similar long-length materials. It pertains to equipment that moves the material through the dryer while managing temperature and moisture extraction.
[F26B 13/186] using combustion -- F26B 13/186 covers machines and apparatus that dry fabrics, fibres, yarns or other continuous solid materials by removing liquid through the use of combustion. The classification applies to equipment that employs a moving process to achieve drying of solid items.
[F26B 13/22] Arrangements of gas flames -- F26B 13/22 concerns the specific layout and configuration of gas flame burners in continuous drying equipment for fabrics, fibres, yarns and similar solid materials. It includes the positioning, shaping and control of flames to achieve uniform heat transfer and efficient moisture removal as the material moves through the dryer.
[F26B 13/24] Arrangements of devices using drying processes not involving heating -- F26B 13/24 relates to drying machines that remove moisture from fabrics, fibres, yarns or similar long-length materials without employing heat, using methods such as airflow, vacuum, or chemical absorbents to achieve drying in a continuously moving process.
[F26B 13/26] using sorbent surfaces, e.g. bands or coverings on rollers -- The code F26B 13/26 covers machines that dry continuous sheets or yarns by contacting them with sorbent surfaces such as bands or roller coverings that absorb moisture. It pertains to mechanical drying apparatus where the material moves progressively past the sorbent elements. This classification falls under mechanical engineering, drying solid materials, specifically dryers for fabrics and similar long-length materials.
[F26B 13/28] for applying pressure -- F26B 13/28 classifies continuous-movement drying machines that use pressure to assist liquid removal from fabrics, fibres, yarns or similar solid materials. It covers apparatus such as rollers, belts or presses that apply a compressive force while the material advances through the dryer, enhancing moisture evacuation.
[F26B 13/30] for applying suction -- This subclass covers drying machines that use suction to remove moisture from continuous lengths of fabrics, fibers, yarns or similar materials, typically moving the material through the dryer by rollers or belts. It belongs to the CPC group for mechanical engineering drying solid materials by removing liquid.
[F26B 15/00] Machines or apparatus for drying objects with progressive movement -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Machines or apparatus for drying objects or object-like batches, e.g. material in a container.  The objects or batches move progressively through the machine or apparatus.
[F26B 15/02] with movement in the whole or part of a circle -- F26B 15/02 covers machines or apparatus that dry solid objects by removing liquid while the objects are conveyed along a circular or partially-circular path. It belongs to the CPC subclass for drying solid materials with progressive movement, under mechanical engineering.
[F26B 15/04] in a horizontal plane -- The entry F26B 15/04 covers machines or apparatus that dry solid objects while they move in a horizontal plane, such as conveyor-type dryers, horizontally rotating drums or belts that transport material across a drying zone.
[F26B 15/06] involving several planes, one above the other -- F26B 15/06 covers drying machines that handle solid objects moving through a series of horizontal or inclined planes arranged one above the other. The arrangement allows progressive removal of moisture as the material passes from one plane to the next, typically using heated air, infrared or other drying media.
[F26B 15/08] in a vertical plane -- F26B 15/08 covers machines or apparatus that dry solid objects by moving them progressively in a vertical plane, such as conveyor-type dryers where the material travels upward or downward while heat is applied.
[F26B 15/085] with endless clamp or tray conveyor, e.g. wicket conveyor -- The code refers to drying machines that move objects through the process using an endless clamp or tray system, such as a wicket conveyor. The conveyor continuously carries the items past the drying zone, allowing efficient removal of moisture from solid materials while maintaining a steady flow.
[F26B 15/10] with movement in a path composed of one or more straight lines, e.g. compound -- F26B 15/10 covers drying machines that move objects along one or more straight-line paths, such as conveyors or rollers, to remove liquid from solid materials.
[F26B 15/105] the articles, e.g. can lids, discs, being conveyed by means of rotating screw spindles
[F26B 15/12] the lines being all horizontal or slightly inclined -- This classification covers machines or apparatus that dry solid objects by moving them progressively, where the drying elements are arranged as horizontal or slightly inclined lines. It belongs to the mechanical-engineering drying of solid materials hierarchy, code F26B 15/12.
[F26B 15/122] the objects or batches of material being carried by transversely moving rollers or rods which may rotate -- This subclass covers drying machines that convey solid objects or batches on rollers or rods moving transversely across the drying zone, with the rollers or rods capable of rotating to improve heat transfer. It is used for apparatus where progressive movement of the material is combined with rotating conveyance elements.
[F26B 15/124] the objects being yarn hanks
[F26B 15/126] the material being "pasta" products, e.g. spaghetti -- This subclass covers machines that dry pasta products such as spaghetti by moving the items continuously through a dryer. [F26B 15/128] the rods being attached at one end to an endless conveying means, the other end being free to receive hollow articles, e.g. cans -- In this CPC subclass, machines for drying solid items using a moving conveyor feature rods that are fixed at one end to an endless belt while the opposite ends are free to hold hollow objects such as cans during the drying process.
[F26B 15/14] the objects or batches of materials being carried by trays or racks -- F26B 15/14 covers drying machines in which solid objects or batches are transported on trays, racks or similar carriers while passing through the drying zone. The code applies to apparatus that move the material progressively during drying, distinguishing it from static dryers.
[F26B 15/143] the receptacles being wholly or partly foraminous, e.g. containing a batch of loose material  F26B15/146 ,  F26B15/205 ,  F26B15/26  take precedence -- F26B 15/143 designates drying machines that handle solid objects moving through the process and use wholly or partly perforated receptacles, such as containers holding loose material, to facilitate moisture removal. It falls under the hierarchy Mechanical Engineering > Drying > Drying solid materials or objects by removing liquid therefrom and is superseded by the narrower groups F26B 15/146, F26B 15/205 and F26B 15/26.
[F26B 15/146] applying multiple superimposed tray conveyors, the materials to be dried being dropped onto subsequent conveyor stretches, e.g. by allowing the trays to tilt at one point -- F26B 15/146 covers drying machines that employ several stacked tray conveyors, where material to be dried is released onto lower conveyor sections, often by tilting a tray at a single point. The design enables continuous, progressive movement of the objects through successive drying zones, improving efficiency for solid-material processing.
[F26B 15/16] the objects or batches of materials being carried by wheeled trucks -- F26B 15/16 concerns the objects or batches of material that are carried on wheeled trucks through drying machines. It addresses how the loads are arranged, secured and moved on the truck to ensure uniform exposure to the drying process.
[F26B 15/18] the objects or batches of materials being carried by endless belts -- The subclass concerns drying machines that transport solid objects on continuously moving belts, allowing the material to pass progressively through a drying zone. It includes apparatus where batches are carried on endless belts for efficient removal of moisture.
[F26B 15/20] the lines being all vertical or steeply inclined -- F26B 15/20 refers to dryers for solid objects in which the material advances past lines that are vertical or sharply inclined, such as vertically arranged belts, plates or racks. The configuration uses gravity and airflow to enhance liquid removal during drying.
[F26B 15/205] the objects or batches of materials being carried by a vertical stack of foraminous trays or shelves either moving through a shaft or forming, with their edges, a moving shaft, the shaft being supplied with drying air -- F26B 15/205 covers drying machines in which batches of solid material are placed on a vertical stack of perforated trays or shelves that move through a shaft, or whose edges form a moving shaft, while drying air is passed through the trays to remove moisture. This arrangement enables continuous drying of bulk solids.
[F26B 15/22] the objects or batches of materials being carried by endless belts -- F26B 15/22 covers machines that dry solid objects or material batches while they are conveyed on continuous, endless belts, enabling progressive movement through the drying zone. The code is part of the mechanical-engineering subclass for drying solid materials, focusing on belt-carried handling of the items during the process.
[F26B 15/24] in a zig-zag path -- This subclass concerns drying machines that move solid objects along a zig-zag path, using a reciprocating or oscillating conveyor to expose the material repeatedly to heated air while maintaining continuous feed. It falls under mechanical engineering for drying solid materials by removing liquid with progressive movement.
[F26B 15/26] with movement in a helical path -- F26B 15/26 covers drying machines that convey objects along a helical trajectory, typically using a screw or spiraled conveyor to provide continuous movement while removing moisture. The helical motion allows uniform exposure to heat or airflow, making it suitable for bulk solids such as grains, powders, or textiles.
[F26B 17/00] Machines or apparatus for drying materials in loose, plastic, or fluidised form, e.g. granules, staple fibres, with progressive movement -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Machines or apparatus for drying materials in loose form, plastic or fluidized form.  The materials move progressively through the machine or apparatus.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Machines with primarily non-progressive movement of materials, e.g. mixing of materials -> F26B11/00 - Fibres forming a material of long length, e.g. yarn, web -> F26B13/00
[F26B 17/001] the material moving down superimposed floors  superimposed belts  F26B17/08 -- This subclass covers machines that dry solid bulk materials by conveying them down through overlapping floors or belts, allowing progressive movement while exposing each layer to drying action. The arrangement improves throughput and uniform drying for items such as granules, fibers or other loose or plastic substances.
[F26B 17/002] with floors which may rotate and turn over as a whole or in part, e.g. around a horizontal axis  superimposed tray conveyors  F26B15/146
[F26B 17/003] with fixed floors provided with scrapers  F26B17/006  and  F26B17/007  take precedence
[F26B 17/005] with rotating floors, e.g. around a vertical axis, which may have scrapers  F26B17/002 ,  F26B17/006 ,  F26B17/007  take precedence -- The subclass F26B 17/005 pertains to drying machines that use rotating floors, usually arranged around a vertical axis, and may be fitted with scrapers to convey the material. It applies to equipment that dries loose, plastic or fluidised solids such as granules or staple fibres by progressively moving them across the heated floor.
[F26B 17/006] the movement being imparted by oscillation or vibration -- F26B 17/006 relates to drying machines that move solid, loose or fluidised materials by means of oscillation or vibration, such as vibrating trays, belts or conveyors, to enhance moisture removal from granules, fibers or similar substances.
[F26B 17/007] having a sieve, e.g. classifying arrangement -- F26B 17/007 covers drying machines for loose or fluidised solids that incorporate a sieve or similar classification arrangement to separate material during the drying process. The sieve helps continuously sort particles while they move through the dryer.
[F26B 17/008] the material being a slurry or paste applied onto moving elements, e.g. chains, plates, for drying thereon, and subsequently removed therefrom  the element being an endless web or belt-like conveyor  F26B17/023 ; the element being a disc  F26B17/282 ; the element being loose inert particles  F26B3/205 -- The class covers machines that dry solid materials presented as a slurry or paste on moving surfaces. The material is applied to elements such as endless belt-like conveyors, chains, discs, or to a bed of inert particles, dried while the element moves, and then removed from the element.
[F26B 17/02] with movement performed by belts carrying the materials -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Machines or apparatus for drying materials in loose, plastic or fluidised form, e.g. granules or staple fibres, with progressive movement down superimposed fixed floors provided with scrapers -> F26B17/003 - Machines or apparatus for drying materials in loose, plastic or fluidised form, e.g. granules or staple fibres, with movement performed by reciprocating or oscillating conveyors that make a rotary working movement while being transversely moved in one direction (with the reverse or return movement being inoperative, e.g. lifted or at rest) -> F26B17/263
[F26B 17/023] the material being a slurry or paste, which adheres to a moving belt-like endless conveyor for drying thereon, from which it may be removed in dried state, e.g. by scrapers, brushes or vibration -- F26B 17/023 relates to drying machines where a slurry or paste adheres to a continuously moving belt-like endless conveyor, is dried while on the belt, and the dried material is then removed using scrapers, brushes, vibration or comparable devices.
[F26B 17/026] the material being moved in-between belts which may be perforated -- F26B 17/026 relates to drying machines that convey solid or plastic bulk material through the space between moving belts, which can be perforated to permit airflow and enhance moisture removal. The configuration is used for granules, fibers or similar substances that are transported in a loose or fluidised state while being dried.
[F26B 17/04] the belts being all horizontal or slightly inclined -- This subdivision refers to drying machines that employ belt conveyors arranged essentially horizontally or with a slight incline to transport loose, plastic or fluidised solid materials such as granules or staple fibres while they are dried. The belts provide progressive movement of the material through the drying zone without significant vertical lifting.
[F26B 17/045] the material on the belt being agitated, dispersed or turned over by mechanical means, e.g. by vibrating the belt, by fixed, rotating or oscillating elements -- F26B 17/045 designates machines that dry loose, plastic or fluidised solid materials on a moving belt where the material is mechanically agitated, dispersed or turned over, for example by vibrating the belt or by fixed, rotating or oscillating elements that act on the belt.
[F26B 17/06] the belts being all vertical or steeply inclined -- The subclass concerns drying machines that convey solid or plastic bulk, such as granules or staple fibres, on belts arranged vertically or at a steep inclination. The vertical or steeply inclined belt facilitates continuous movement of the material while moisture is removed.
[F26B 17/08] the belts being arranged in a sinuous or zig-zag path -- F26B 17/08 covers drying machines that use conveyor belts arranged in a sinuous or zig-zag path, allowing the material to follow a folded route for longer exposure to heat while remaining on a moving belt. This design enhances drying efficiency for loose, plastic or fluidised solids such as granules or staple fibres.
[F26B 17/10] with movement performed by fluid currents, e.g. issuing from a nozzle, -- The subclass covers drying equipment for bulk, plastic, or fluidised solid materials in which the material is conveyed by fluid streams such as air or gas jets issued from nozzles. It applies to machines that combine drying with the progressive movement of the material driven by these fluid currents, commonly used for granules, fibres, or similar loose substances.
[F26B 17/101] the drying enclosure having the shape of one or a plurality of shafts or ducts, e.g. with substantially straight and vertical axis  F26B17/107  takes precedence -- F26B 17/101 refers to drying enclosures shaped as one or several shafts or ducts, typically with a substantially straight, vertical axis, used in machines that dry loose, plastic or fluidised solids such as granules or fibres. This subclass follows the more specific F26B 17/107, which has precedence.
[F26B 17/102] with material recirculation, classifying or disintegrating means  F26B17/103  takes precedence -- The entry F26B 17/102 covers drying machines that recycle the material through the dryer and incorporate devices for classifying or breaking up the material during the process. It applies to equipment handling loose, plastic or fluidised solids such as granules or fibers, where material is continuously moved and reintroduced to improve drying efficiency. It is subordinate to F26B 17/103.
[F26B 17/103] with specific material feeding arrangements, e.g. combined with disintegrating means -- F26B 17/103 covers machines or apparatus for drying solid materials in loose, plastic or fluidised form that incorporate specific material-feeding arrangements, such as feeders coupled with disintegrating means, to ensure continuous movement of the material through the dryer. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering, drying solid materials or objects by removing liquid, and relates to equipment handling granules, staple fibres and similar bulk items.
[F26B 17/104] with fixed or moving internal bodies for defining or changing the course of the entrained material -- F26B 17/104 covers machines for drying solid, loose or fluidised materials that incorporate fixed or moving internal components to define or alter the material's flow path during drying, such as rods, baffles or rollers that guide granules, fibers or similar objects through the drying zone.
[F26B 17/105] the shaft or duct, e.g. its axis, being other than straight, i.e. curved, zig-zag, closed-loop, spiral -- F26B 17/105 covers machines or apparatus for drying solid materials where the drying shaft or duct is not straight, such as curved, zig-zag, closed-loop or spiral configurations, enabling progressive movement of the material through the dryer.
[F26B 17/106] the drying enclosure, e.g. its axis, being substantially straight and horizontal, e.g. pneumatic drum dryers; the drying enclosure consisting of multiple substantially straight and horizontal stretches  F26B17/107  takes precedence -- F26B 17/106 relates to machines that dry solid materials in loose, plastic or fluidised form using a drying enclosure whose axis is substantially straight and horizontal, such as pneumatic drum dryers; the enclosure may consist of several straight horizontal stretches. This falls under the broader class for drying solid materials or objects by removing liquid, within mechanical engineering.
[F26B 17/107] pneumatically inducing within the drying enclosure a curved flow path, e.g. circular, spiral, helical; Cyclone or Vortex dryers  swirl or curved flow path induced mechanically, i.e. by rotating element  F26B3/0923 -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Drying processes for loose materials involving a fluidised bed -> F26B3/08
[F26B 17/108] using impinging streams of entrained material -- F26B 17/108 covers machines or apparatus that dry granular, fibrous or other loose solid materials by directing streams of air or gas that carry entrained particles onto the material, creating impingement that removes moisture. The method employs progressive movement of the material through the drying zone, allowing efficient drying of plastics, powders, or staple fibres.
[F26B 17/12] with movement performed solely by gravity
[F26B 17/122] the material moving through a cross-flow of drying gas; the drying enclosure, e.g. shaft, consisting of substantially vertical, perforated walls -- F26B 17/122 covers machines or apparatus that dry loose, plastic or fluidised solid materials by moving them through a cross-flow of drying gas within an enclosure whose walls are substantially vertical and perforated, such as a shaft-type dryer. The design permits progressive material movement while gas passes horizontally across the material stream, enhancing heat transfer and moisture removal. This arrangement is typical for drying granules, staple fibres or similar bulk solids.
[F26B 17/124] the vertical walls having the shape of at least two concentric cylinders with the material to be dried moving in-between -- This subclass covers drying machines that convey solid or plastic material through the gap between two or more vertical walls formed as concentric cylinders. The walls create a cylindrical drying chamber while the material moves longitudinally, allowing heat or airflow to remove moisture as the product travels between the cylinders.
[F26B 17/126] the vertical walls consisting of baffles, e.g. in louvre-arrangement
[F26B 17/128] with provisions for working under reduced or increased pressure, with or without heating -- This subclass relates to machines or apparatus that dry solid, loose, plastic or fluidised materials such as granules or staple fibres while they move progressively, and that incorporate means to operate under reduced or increased pressure, optionally combined with heating.
[F26B 17/14] the materials moving through a counter-current of gas -- F26B 17/14 covers machines that dry loose, plastic or fluidised solid materials-such as granules or staple fibres-by moving the material through a gas stream flowing in the opposite direction. The counter-current flow enhances heat transfer and moisture removal, allowing continuous drying of bulk solid products. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering drying of solid materials.
[F26B 17/1408] the gas being supplied and optionally extracted through ducts extending into the moving stack of material  in combination with fluid-heated closed tubes or other heating elements in contact with the stack of material  F26B17/16 -- This subclass relates to drying machines that move a loose, plastic or fluidised stream of solid material while supplying heated gas through ducts extending into the material stack, optionally extracting gas, and using fluid-heated closed tubes or other heating elements in direct contact with the material.
[F26B 17/1416] the ducts being half open or perforated and arranged horizontally -- This subgroup pertains to drying machines for loose, plastic or fluidised solids such as granules or staple fibres, in which the air-flow ducts are partially open or perforated and positioned horizontally to enable efficient convective drying.
[F26B 17/1425] the ducts being perforated and arranged vertically -- F26B 17/1425 covers drying machines for solid, loose or fluidised materials in which the drying air is distributed through ducts that are perforated and positioned vertically, allowing the airflow to rise through the material bed and enhance heat transfer while the material progresses through the apparatus.
[F26B 17/1433] the drying enclosure, e.g. shaft, having internal members or bodies for guiding, mixing or agitating the material, e.g. imposing a zig-zag movement onto the material  F26B17/1408 ,  F26B17/16  take precedence -- F26B 17/1433 covers drying enclosures, such as shafts, that contain internal members or bodies used to guide, mix or agitate the material being dried, often creating a zig-zag movement within the chamber. The arrangement enhances heat transfer and promotes uniform moisture removal from bulk, loose or fluidised solids.
[F26B 17/1441] the members or bodies being stationary, e.g. fixed panels, baffles, grids, the position of which may be adjustable -- This subclass covers drying machines where the internal members or bodies-such as fixed panels, baffles or grids-remain stationary while the material moves past them, and where the position of these components can be adjusted to influence airflow or heat transfer. It applies to equipment used for drying loose, plastic or fluidised solids like granules or staple fibres.
[F26B 17/145] consisting of non-perforated panels or baffles -- This subclass relates to equipment for drying bulk solids, such as granules or staple fibers, that uses non-perforated panels or baffles to generate airflow and convey the material through the dryer.
[F26B 17/1458] consisting of perforated panels or baffles; consisting of grids -- F26B 17/1458 relates to drying machinery that employs perforated panels, baffles or grid structures to remove liquid from loose, plastic or fluidised solid materials such as granules or staple fibres. The design allows progressive movement of the material through the apertured elements, enhancing heat transfer and moisture removal.
[F26B 17/1466] the members or bodies being in movement -- F26B 17/1466 covers machines or apparatus that dry solid or granular materials while the drying members or bodies themselves move, such as rotating drums, conveyors, or fluidised-bed systems that transport the material through a heated zone. The classification applies to equipment designed for continuous drying of loose, plastic, or fluidised substances.
[F26B 17/1475] the movement being a vibration or oscillation  F26B17/26  takes precedence -- This subclass covers drying machines for loose, plastic or fluidised solids where the material is conveyed by a vibration or oscillation, the vibrating motion providing the progressive movement needed for uniform moisture removal. It falls under mechanical-engineering drying of solid materials.
[F26B 17/1483] the movement being a rotation around a vertical axis -- The code F26B 17/1483 relates to drying machines that handle loose, plastic or fluidised solids such as granules or staple fibres, where the material is conveyed by rotating around a vertical axis. It falls under mechanical engineering, specifically drying of solid materials by liquid removal, and describes apparatus that provides progressive movement through vertical-axis rotation.
[F26B 17/1491] the movement being a rotation around a horizontal axis -- F26B 17/1491 refers to drying machines in which the material is conveyed by a rotation around a horizontal axis. The apparatus typically uses a rotating drum or similar cylinder to dry loose, plastic or fluidised solids such as granules or staple fibres while they move continuously. It belongs to the mechanical-engineering subclass for drying solid materials by removing liquid.
[F26B 17/16] the materials passing down a heated surface -- The subclass concerns drying apparatus in which the material is conveyed over a heated surface, allowing moisture to evaporate as the product moves downward by gravity or mechanical drive. Typical examples are belt, drum or conveyor dryers that handle loose, plastic or fluidised solids.
[F26B 17/18] with movement performed by rotating helical blades or other rotary conveyors moving materials in stationary chambers -- This subclass covers drying machines that move solid or semi-solid bulk material through a stationary chamber using rotating helical blades or other rotary conveyors, enabling progressive transport while the material is exposed to drying conditions.
[F26B 17/20] the axis of rotation being horizontal or slightly inclined -- The CPC subclass F26B 17/20 covers machines that dry solid materials such as granules, staple fibres or other loose, plastic or fluidised items, using a device that moves the material progressively while the axis of rotation is horizontal or only slightly inclined.
[F26B 17/205] with multiple chambers, e.g. troughs, in superimposed arrangement -- Machines for drying loose, plastic or fluidised solid materials that employ several drying chambers stacked in a superimposed, often trough-like, arrangement, allowing the material to pass progressively from one chamber to the next for sequential heating and moisture removal.
[F26B 17/22] the axis of rotation being vertical or steeply inclined -- F26B 17/22 covers machines or apparatus for drying loose, plastic or fluidised solid materials-such as granules or staple fibres-where the drying drum or cylinder rotates about a vertical or steeply inclined axis, enabling progressive movement of the material through the dryer.
[F26B 17/24] with movement performed by shooting or throwing the materials -- This subcategory of mechanical engineering drying devices concerns machines that transport loose, plastic or fluidised materials through the dryer by propelling them-shooting, throwing or otherwise ejecting the items-rather than using conventional conveyors. It applies to equipment handling granules, staple fibres or similar solids in a progressive flow while removing moisture.
[F26B 17/26] with movement performed by reciprocating or oscillating conveyors propelling materials over stationary surfaces -- F26B 17/26 covers drying machines that move loose or fluidised solid materials by means of reciprocating or oscillating conveyors that push the material over fixed drying surfaces, enabling continuous removal of moisture during the drying process.
[F26B 17/263] the conveying element making a rotary working movement while being transversely moved in one direction, the reverse or return movement being effected in an inoperative state, e.g. lifted, in rest -- The code refers to a dryer conveyer where the moving element performs a rotary working movement while being shifted sideways in one direction, and during the return stroke it is lifted or otherwise made inoperative. This arrangement lets solid or fluidised material be advanced for drying while the element's reverse motion does not interfere with the process. It is used in dryers handling granules, fibers or similar loose forms.
[F26B 17/266] the materials to be dried being moved in a helical, spiral or circular path, e.g. vibrated helix -- F26B 17/266 covers drying apparatus in which the material to be dried is conveyed along a helical, spiral or circular trajectory, for example by means of a vibrating helix. The design combines continuous movement with heat transfer to remove moisture from granules, fibers or other loose solids. It is used in processes where efficient drying is achieved through rotary or spiral paths.
[F26B 17/28] with movement performed by rollers or discs with material passing over or between them, e.g. suction drum, sieve -- This subclass covers machines for drying solid materials where the material is moved by rollers or discs, passing over or between them, such as suction drums or sieves. It is located in the Cooperative Patent Classification under mechanical engineering, drying solid materials or objects by removing liquid, and specifically addresses apparatus with progressive movement.
[F26B 17/282] the materials adhering to, and being dried on, the surface of rotating discs with or without scraping devices -- F26B 17/282 covers machines that dry solid materials by bringing them into contact with the surface of rotating discs, where the material adheres to the disc and can be removed with scraping devices, suitable for granules, fibers or fluidised particles. The apparatus may include mechanisms to continuously scrape and discharge the dried material from the disc.
[F26B 17/284] the materials being dried on the non-perforated surface of heated rollers or drums  arrangements for heating or cooling drums, for removal of condensate  F26B13/183 -- The subclass F26B 17/284 covers drying machines in which solid or plastic materials are placed on a non-perforated heated roller or drum, and includes the design of heating or cooling arrangements for the drum and systems for removing condensate.
[F26B 17/286] Arrangements for application of materials to be dried onto the drums or rollers; Arrangements for removing dried materials from the drums or rollers, e.g. doctor blades -- The F26B 17/286 subclass covers equipment that applies material to drying drums or rollers and devices that remove the dried material, such as doctor blades. It pertains to machines that handle loose, plastic or fluidised solids during drying processes.
[F26B 17/288] the materials being dried on perforated drums or rollers, e.g. sieve or suction drums -- F26B 17/288 refers to drying machines that convey solid or fluidised materials-such as granules, staple fibres or loose powders-over perforated drums or rollers, including sieve or suction drums, where the moving contact allows liquid to be removed through the openings. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering, drying solid materials by progressive movement of the workpiece.
[F26B 17/30] with movement performed by rotary or oscillating containers -- F26B 17/30 pertains to machines that dry bulk solid materials such as granules or staple fibres by moving them in rotary or oscillating containers. The container motion creates progressive transport, continually exposing the material to drying air or heat to remove moisture efficiently.
[F26B 17/32] the movement being in a horizontal or slightly inclined plane -- F26B 17/32 refers to drying machines that convey loose, plastic or fluidised solid material along a horizontal or slightly inclined plane while removing liquid. It includes apparatus such as belt or conveyor dryers where the material moves progressively in a near-level direction to achieve drying.
[F26B 17/34] the movement being in a vertical or steeply inclined plane -- F26B 17/34 covers drying machines where the material moves along a vertical or steeply inclined path. It applies to equipment that transports loose, plastic or fluidised solids such as granules or fibers while removing moisture. The classification is used for systems that rely on gravity or inclined conveyance to enhance drying efficiency.
[F26B 19/00] Machines or apparatus for drying solid materials or objects not covered by groups -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Miscellaneous machines and machines of any type for drying solid materials or objects featuring a self contained mobile arrangement, the drying taking place with the mobile machine at rest or with moving machine, e.g. moving along or around the large object to be dried.
[F26B 19/005] Self-contained mobile devices, e.g. for agricultural produce  movable devices with radiation means  F26B3/28 ; small self-contained devices for drying objects at rest  F26B9/003 -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Small self-contained devices for drying objects at rest -> F26B9/003
[F26B 20/00] Combinations of machines or apparatus covered by two or more of groups -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Combinations of machines or apparatus, either creating a synergetic effect or being a simple agglomeration, covered by two or more of groups F26B9/00 - F26B19/00 .
[F26B 2015/003] the load carrying elements having provisions for defining drying gas ducts, e.g. panels -- This CPC group concerns load-bearing parts of fire-extinguishing equipment, such as panels or frames, that are formed with built-in passages or ducts for directing drying gas, enabling moisture removal from the system during operation.
[F26B 2015/006] the drying tunnel not being partitioned by load carrying elements -- This subgroup covers tunnel dryers where the drying chamber is a single continuous space without internal partitions formed by conveyors, trays, or other load-carrying structures, allowing the material to pass through unsegmented. It is used for processes such as wood, food, or textile drying where uniform exposure is required.
[F26B 21/00] Details of general application -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Arrangements for supplying air, gases or steam to the drying apparatus, or directly to or into the solid materials or objects.  Arrangements for routing and circulating the air, gases or steam for drying.  Arrangements for controlling the properties of air, gases or steam for drying.  Condensing vapour other than steam onto the surface of the materials or objects to be dried.
[F26B 21/001] Air generating units, e.g. movable or independent of drying enclosure -- F26B 21/001 covers air-generating units employed in the drying of solid materials, such as fans, blowers, compressors, or other devices that supply airstreams independent of or movable relative to the drying enclosure. This sub-class falls under mechanical engineering for drying solid objects by removing liquid.
[F26B 21/002] with means for indirect air heating, i.e. using heat exchangers -- F26B 21/002 covers drying of solid materials in which the air used for heating is warmed indirectly, usually via a heat exchanger that transfers heat from another source without the air contacting the material directly. This method helps prevent contamination or oxidation of the product.
[F26B 21/003] Air or gas filters -- F26B 21/003 covers devices that filter air or gases in systems used for drying solid materials, removing liquid by passing the gas through a filter element to capture contaminants or particulates. Such filters are typically integral to drying apparatuses, improving product quality and protecting downstream equipment.
[F26B 21/006] with the air or gases passing through hollow spaces or cores within the materials or objects to be dried, e.g. tubes, pipes or bottles -- The subclass F26B 21/006 covers drying of solid materials or objects by passing air or gases through internal hollow spaces or cores, such as tubes, pipes or bottles, to remove moisture from within. It is a subcategory of mechanical-engineering drying methods that use internal gas flow.
[F26B 21/007] the objects being bobbin- or spool-like bodies -- F26B 21/007 covers drying operations where the article to be dried is a bobbin- or spool-like body. It includes apparatus or methods that use the cylindrical, rotating or stationary shape of the bobbin or spool to facilitate removal of liquid, often by heat, airflow or vacuum.
[F26B 21/008] the objects being flexible, e.g. inflatable tubes or sausage casings -- F26B 21/008 covers drying processes applied to flexible objects such as inflatable tubes, hoses or sausage casings, where the material can deform during liquid removal. The classification is used for inventions that remove moisture from these pliable solid forms, typically by applying heat, vacuum or gas flow while the object remains flexible.
[F26B 21/20] Circulating air or gases in closed cycles, e.g. wholly within the drying enclosure -- This subclass deals with drying apparatuses that recirculate air or other gases in a closed loop entirely inside the drying chamber, using the moving medium to improve moisture removal from solid objects or materials. It covers devices where the drying gas is confined to the enclosure and continuously circulated throughout the drying cycle.
[F26B 21/202] with means for changing the flow pattern, e.g. by reversing gas flow or by moving the materials or objects through subsequent compartments, at least two of which have a different flow direction -- This subclass concerns drying devices that modify the gas flow pattern, for example by reversing the airflow or moving the material through successive chambers where at least two sections have opposite flow directions. The arrangement is intended to enhance heat and mass transfer during drying.
[F26B 21/204] by using movable fan units -- F26B 21/204 pertains to drying solid materials by removing liquid through the use of movable fan units that generate airflow across the object being dried. It includes apparatuses where fans are positioned or driven to direct air streams for enhanced evaporation, typically in industrial or mechanical drying systems.
[F26B 21/206] by reversing fan rotation -- The CPC subclass F26B 21/206 pertains to drying solid materials by removing liquid through a method that reverses the fan's rotation to change airflow direction, thereby improving moisture removal efficiency. It is nested under mechanical engineering, drying, and specifically drying solid materials or objects by removing liquid from them.
[F26B 21/208] by air valves, movable baffles or nozzle arrangements -- F26B 21/208 covers drying processes for solid materials where air is directed through the product by means such as air valves, movable baffles or specially arranged nozzles. The classification applies to equipment that controls the flow of drying air to improve removal of liquid from the material. It is used for technologies that modulate airflow paths rather than relying solely on static vents.
[F26B 21/25] partly outside the drying enclosure -- F26B 21/25 covers drying arrangements where at least part of the material or the drying mechanism is positioned outside the main drying enclosure, allowing heat or air to be applied from outside or moisture to be removed through openings.
[F26B 21/30] Controlling, e.g. regulating, parameters of gas supply -- This CPC subclass covers devices or methods that adjust gas-flow characteristics-such as pressure, temperature, composition or flow rate-to control the drying of solid materials or objects. It includes regulators, valves and control systems that modulate the supply of drying gas.
[F26B 21/33] Humidity -- The F26B 21/33 entry deals with humidity in the drying of solid materials or objects, covering how moisture content is measured, monitored or controlled during the removal of liquids, and its effect on drying efficiency and product quality.
[F26B 21/331] by using sorbent or hygroscopic materials, e.g. chemical substances or molecular sieves -- This CPC subclass covers drying of solid materials or objects by removing liquid through the use of sorbent or hygroscopic substances, such as chemical drying agents or molecular sieves, applied to eliminate moisture from the workpiece.
[F26B 21/333] by condensing the moisture in the drying medium, which may be recycled, e.g. using a heat pump cycle -- F26B 21/333 covers methods for drying solid materials or objects by condensing moisture from the drying medium, allowing the medium to be reused, typically employing a heat-pump cycle to recycle heat. The approach improves energy efficiency by recovering latent heat during condensation.
[F26B 21/35] Temperature -- F26B 21/35 pertains to the temperature aspects of drying solid materials, encompassing heating or cooling methods, temperature regulation and measurement, and related control techniques used to optimise moisture removal during the drying process.
[F26B 21/37] Velocity of flow -- F26B 21/37 addresses the velocity of flow in drying operations for solid materials, covering how gas or air speed is measured, controlled, and its impact on moisture removal efficiency. The entry relates to mechanical-engineering techniques that optimise drying by adjusting flow velocity.
[F26B 21/40] using gases other than air -- This subclass concerns drying of solid objects by means of a non-air gas. It includes processes where inert gases, nitrogen, carbon-dioxide or other gases replace atmospheric air to remove moisture, often to prevent oxidation or improve efficiency.
[F26B 21/45] using steam -- Group F26B21/45 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group F26B21/00 . Groups F26B21/00 and F26B21/45 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[F26B 21/452] characterised by the steam generating means -- F26B 21/452 covers drying apparatus for solid materials in which the method of producing steam is a defining feature of the device. It includes dryers that generate steam on-site as the heat source for removing liquid from the product, such as steam-heat or steam-flushed systems used in industrial processing.
[F26B 21/471] condensing vapours onto the surface of the materials to be dried -- A method for drying solid materials or objects in which vapour generated during the process is condensed and deposited onto the material's surface, thereby removing moisture through heat transfer and phase change. This technique falls under mechanical-engineering drying technologies that remove liquid by surface condensation of vapours.
[F26B 21/50] Ducting arrangements from the source of air or other gases to the materials or objects being dried -- Group F26B21/50 is impacted by reclassification into group F26B21/55 . Groups F26B21/50 and F26B21/55 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[F26B 21/55] Outlets for directing or distributing the air or other gases -- Group F26B21/55 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group F26B21/50 . Groups F26B21/50 and F26B21/55 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[F26B 2200/00] Indexing schemes for drying specific solid materials or objects -- F26B 2200/00 concerns methods and systems for organizing or indexing drying processes applied to particular solid materials or objects, detailing classification schemes that specify parameters, sequences and control strategies for liquid removal during drying. It falls under mechanical engineering, drying solid materials by removing liquid.
[F26B 2200/02] Biomass, e.g. waste vegetative matter, straw -- The CPC subclass F26B 2200/02 relates to mechanical-engineering drying processes that remove liquid from solid biomass, such as waste vegetative matter or straw. [F26B 2200/04] Garbage -- F26B 2200/04 pertains to the drying of garbage, encompassing methods and apparatus for removing liquid from solid waste to reduce its moisture content.

It covers equipment, heat-transfer methods, airflow and control strategies used to remove moisture from these bulk materials in industrial drying operations.

[F26B 2200/18] Sludges, e.g. sewage, waste, industrial processes, cooling towers -- The CPC entry F26B 2200/18 covers technologies for drying sludges, such as those generated in sewage treatment, waste handling, industrial processes and cooling-tower operations, focusing on methods and apparatus that remove liquid from these solid residues. It is situated within the mechanical-engineering category for drying solid materials.

[F26B 2200/24] Wood particles, e.g. shavings, cuttings, saw dust -- F26B 2200/24 concerns methods for drying wood particles such as shavings, cuttings or sawdust, focusing on the removal of liquid moisture from these solid wood materials. It is a subclass within the mechanical-engineering hierarchy for drying solid objects.
