[F27B 2007/163] using only a ring of lifting elements to lift the charge -- The subclass concerns systems that raise an explosive charge using a single annular arrangement of lifting elements, where a ring of lugs or similar components provides the upward force. It focuses on the mechanical configuration that replaces multiple separate lift points with a continuous circular lift mechanism.
[F27B 2007/165] forming a helical lifting projection -- The CPC subclass F27B 2007/165 covers processes for producing a helical lifting projection on a workpiece, such as threads or screw-like features that provide axial movement or load-bearing capability. It includes methods such as rolling, forming, or machining to create the helical element on shafts, fasteners, pump components and similar parts.
[F27B 2007/168] Annular partition -- An annular partition is a circular internal barrier within a pump housing that separates fluid chambers or directs flow between sections. It is used in centrifugal and other types of pumps to improve hydraulic efficiency, control pressure zones, and prevent mixing of fluids.
[F27B 2007/2008] Devices for reintroducing dust in the drum
[F27B 2007/2091] Means for eliminating compounds from gases by condensation, e.g. alkali metals -- F27B 2007/2091 covers devices that remove unwanted compounds from gas streams by condensing them, particularly targeting alkali-metal vapors. The classification applies to systems that cool or otherwise induce condensation to capture and eliminate these substances from exhaust or process gases, improving gas purity and reducing emissions.
[F27B 2007/2213] mounted floatingly on the drum -- The F27B 2007/2213 subclass covers devices in which an element such as a bearing, weight or ancillary part is attached to a rotating drum with a floating or loosely constrained mount, allowing limited movement to absorb vibration, mis-alignment or thermal expansion, commonly applied in piston-type engines, compressors or similar rotary machinery.
[F27B 2007/222] the mounting comprising radially resilient elements, e.g. springs -- This subclass covers mounts for rotating parts that incorporate radially acting resilient elements such as coil or leaf springs. The springs absorb vibration and permit slight radial movement while keeping the component aligned, and are used in applications like wind-turbine gearboxes, fans and other rotating machinery.
[F27B 2007/2226] the mounting comprising elements to maintain the ring between series of abutments -- This classification covers a pump mounting in which a ring is held in place by a series of supporting abutments, ensuring proper alignment and stability during operation. The design includes specific elements that engage the ring between the abutments to prevent axial or radial movement.
[F27B 2007/2233] the ring being fixed to the drum, e.g. welded -- F27B 2007/2233 defines a technical feature of centrifugal equipment where a ring is permanently secured to a drum, most commonly by welding, to create a sealed or reinforced assembly. The classification applies to designs that integrate the ring as an integral part of the drum structure for improved strength or containment.
[F27B 2007/2246] Support rollers -- Support rollers are cylindrical or spherical components that guide and bear loads on rotating shafts, spindles or conveyors, reducing friction and ensuring accurate alignment in machines such as pumps, compressors, printing presses and material handling equipment. They may be fixed, adjustable or spring-loaded.
[F27B 2007/2253] mounted movable, e.g. resiliently on the ground -- The code F27B 2007/2253 classifies soil-working equipment or attachments that are mounted on a movable support and connected to the ground in a resilient way, such as vibratory plates or ground-mounted compaction tools that can flex or absorb shocks while operating. This subdivision falls under the broader F27B group for earth-moving or soil-working machines.
[F27B 2007/226] constituted of series of two rollers mounted on tiltable support along the drum -- The subclass deals with drums or cylinders that are equipped with a pair of rollers mounted on a support that can be tilted relative to the drum's axis, allowing the rollers to be positioned along the drum for processing or handling material.
[F27B 2007/2266] the mounting allowing a movement of the rollers support in a horizontal plane -- This subclass relates to mounts that permit the support of rollers to be shifted in a horizontal plane, enabling adjustment or repositioning of roller assemblies on conveyors or similar machines. Typical solutions include sliding rails, hinged brackets or lockable carriage systems that provide lateral movement while maintaining the rollers' functional orientation.
[F27B 2007/2273] with arrangements, e.g. rollers, to maintain the drum against longitudinal movement -- This subclass pertains to drums that are restrained against longitudinal movement by means such as rollers, bearing blocks or similar guiding arrangements, providing axial stability during operation in devices like looms, paper machines or other rotating-drum equipment.
[F27B 2007/228] comprising rollable bodies -- F27B 2007/228 refers to fan or blower assemblies that incorporate rollable bodies such as flexible ducts, sleeves or shutters, which can be wound or unrolled to adjust airflow or allow compact storage.
[F27B 2007/2286] supporting the drum directly, without the use of a bearing ring -- The CPC subclass F27B 2007/2286 relates to drum-support arrangements where the rotating drum is mounted directly on a supporting structure, such as a shaft, cradle or plates, and bears the load without employing a conventional bearing ring. This design simplifies construction and can improve rigidity in machines that handle paper, textile or similar webs.
[F27B 2007/2293] the furnace being suspended -- This CPC subclass covers furnaces that are mounted in a suspended manner rather than fixed to a floor or base, such as hanging or hanging-type heating units used in metallurgical or industrial processes. It includes designs where the furnace is supported from above by chains, brackets or a crane, allowing placement in confined spaces or on moving platforms.
[F27B 2007/261] working with a ring embracing the drum -- The code applies to drum-driven mechanisms in which a surrounding ring embraces the drum and transfers motion or torque to it, typically through friction, clamping or gearing. It includes designs where the ring functions as the driving or driven element in mixers, printing cylinders, textile rollers and similar equipment.
[F27B 2007/262] A gear ring combined with a dented wheel drive -- This subclass covers gear rings that are mechanically integrated with a dented (toothed) wheel to create a combined drive unit. It includes designs where the gear ring and wheel share a common axis or are directly coupled to transmit torque in a compact assembly. Typical applications are gearboxes, conveyor drives and other machinery requiring an integrated gear-and-wheel configuration.
[F27B 2007/263] A gear ring combined with a ram drive -- The F27B 2007/263 subclass covers gear rings that incorporate a ram-type drive mechanism, in which a piston or ram applies axial force directly to the gear teeth to transmit torque. It includes arrangements where the ram engages, adjusts or locks the gear ring, typically for compact or variable-speed power transmission applications.
[F27B 2007/265] the ring being mounted floatingly -- This CPC group pertains to fire-prevention or fire-extinguishing devices that incorporate a ring mounted in a floating manner, allowing it to move or expand while maintaining a seal. It typically applies to sprinkler heads, fire-extinguisher components or similar apparatus where the floating ring accommodates thermal changes or pressure variations.
[F27B 2007/266] the ring being a bearing ring -- The CPC subclass F27B 2007/266 covers bearing rings incorporated in lifting or hoisting devices, such as winches, hoists, and similar apparatus. It pertains to the ring that functions as a bearing element, supporting rotatable shafts or other moving parts to reduce friction and ensure smooth operation within the equipment.
[F27B 2007/267] A gear ring combined with a chain drive -- F27B 2007/267 of the CPC covers gear rings that incorporate a chain drive, where the chain is mounted on or integral to the gear ring to transmit rotary motion. The classification applies to mechanisms where the chain and gear work together as a single unit for power transmission in machines.
[F27B 2007/268] Details of the motor or the pinions -- F27B 2007/268 covers information on the motor and pinion arrangements of pump assemblies, including motor type, power-transmission, gear characteristics, mounting and coupling details that affect pump performance.
[F27B 2007/3211] at the open end of the drum -- The CPC subclass F27B 2007/3211 covers features or devices that are situated at the open end of a drum in paper-making or similar processing equipment, such as feeding, discharge, sealing or cleaning arrangements positioned where the drum is accessible. It is used to classify inventions that exploit the drum's open end for material handling or auxiliary functions.
[F27B 2007/3217] axially, optionally at some distance in the kiln -- The F27B 2007/3217 classification covers kiln processes where the material or heating action is positioned along the longitudinal axis of the kiln, optionally at a specified distance from the inlet or other reference point, to achieve a controlled temperature profile or material flow within the furnace. This pertains to the axial placement of burners, feed zones or workpieces in rotary or stationary kilns for uniform treatment.
[F27B 2007/3223] the charging device being movable axially, e.g. reciprocable -- F27B 2007/3223 relates to pumps whose charging device - such as a priming or inlet-assembly component - can be shifted along the pump's axis, for example by reciprocating it to adjust clearance or aid priming. This axial mobility is intended to improve starting, sealing or flow control.
[F27B 2007/3229] via a centrifugal device -- F27B 2007/3229 classifies inventions that use a centrifugal device to move, separate or treat fluids or particles, employing rotation-generated radial forces for handling material. It covers centrifuges, separators and related mechanisms that direct substances toward or away from the axis for processing.
[F27B 2007/3235] the introducing device comprising a spray or a lance -- F27B 2007/3235 covers devices that introduce a fluid into a moving stream by means of a spray or a lance, typically used in fans, blowers or suction apparatus to mix, atomise or disperse liquids, gases or powders within the airflow. This subclass groups mechanisms that direct the introduced medium through a nozzle-like lance or spray pattern.
[F27B 2007/3241] in the flame of the burner -- This CPC subgroup covers elements and methods that operate within the actual flame of a burner, including flame-stabilising devices, flame-monitoring sensors and techniques for directly controlling combustion inside the flame zone.
[F27B 2007/3247] through a lateral opening in the drum -- F27B 2007/3247 covers paper or web processing machines that use a lateral opening in the rotating drum, allowing material to be introduced, withdrawn or treated through the side of the drum during operation. It applies to devices such as rollers, dryers or cutting units that employ side access.
[F27B 2007/3252] with lifting scoops attached to the drum -- This subclass covers rotary drums or mixers that incorporate lifting scoops mounted on the drum periphery to raise and discharge material during operation. It pertains to devices where the scoops are integral to the drum and used for handling bulk solids.
[F27B 2007/3258] at the open end of the drum -- The subclass concerns components, devices or operations located at the open end of a rotary-drum, such as sealing arrangements, feed or discharge mechanisms, support structures, and associated control features that interact with the drum opening.
[F27B 2007/3264] using special discharge means located around the discharge end, e.g. lifting scoops or a transversal annular partition -- This subgroup covers devices that employ special discharge elements placed around the outlet of a container to control material flow, such as lifting scoops or a transverse annular partition, enabling selective or staged emptying of bulk substances from hoppers, silos or similar vessels.
[F27B 2007/327] centrifugally through lateral openings in the drum -- A system in which material in a rotating drum is driven outward by centrifugal force and discharged through openings cut in the drum wall. The lateral outlets allow continuous removal of solids or liquids during processes such as drying, grinding or separation.
[F27B 2007/3276] with a collector means extending longitudinally into the drum -- F27B 2007/3276 covers centrifugal pumps that incorporate a collector element which extends longitudinally into the pump drum, directing the fluid flow or removing entrained gases within the casing. This feature improves fluid handling efficiency by guiding liquids or gases along a dedicated passage inside the drum.
[F27B 2007/3282] Details
[F27B 2007/3288] Sieves or grading means -- F27B 2007/3288 classifies sieves, screens and other grading devices incorporated in pump assemblies to separate or size solid particles within a fluid stream. It covers any mechanism used to filter, screen or grade material as part of a liquid-pumping system. This entry is part of the CPC section for mechanical engineering, specifically pumps.
[F27B 2007/3294] Means to fluidise the charge in the air -- F27B 2007/3294 covers devices that create an airstream to fluidise electrically charged particles in a gas, keeping the charge suspended or dispersed for handling, transport or treatment. Typical examples include air-blast or suction systems that generate turbulent flow to break up agglomerates of dust, powder or other particulate matter that carries an electric charge. The classification is used for equipment that combines pneumatic action with electrostatic effects to achieve fluidisation.
[F27B 2007/365] longitudinally -- The CPC entry F27B 2007/365 pertains to steam-turbine designs in which components are arranged or extend in the longitudinal direction, for example longitudinally positioned blades, casings, flow passages or other features that run along the turbine's axis. This sub-group classifies inventions that exploit longitudinal orientation to influence turbine performance or construction.
[F27B 2007/367] transversally through the wall of the drum -- F27B 2007/367 covers features that extend transversely through the wall of a rotating drum, such as shafts, nozzles or sealing arrangements. It relates to the design and integration of these components to preserve the drum's structural integrity and fluid tightness.
[F27B 2009/026] Two or more conveyors, e.g. mounted successively -- The CPC subclass F27B 2009/026 covers arrangements that employ two or more conveyors, for example conveyors mounted in succession, to transport material or products between stages, enabling continuous handling, speed matching, or a change of direction within a processing line.
[F27B 2009/027] working in parallel -- F27B 2009/027 covers mixers, agitators or stirrers that are arranged to work in parallel, meaning two or more mixing elements operate simultaneously within the same apparatus or process line to boost throughput or achieve more uniform blending.
[F27B 2009/122] Preheating -- F27B 2009/122 covers methods and devices for preheating air or gases before they are delivered by fans, blowers or ventilators in heating, ventilation and air-conditioning systems. The classification includes heat exchangers, electric or combustion heaters, and control arrangements that raise the temperature of the incoming stream to improve efficiency, comfort or moisture control.
[F27B 2009/124] Cooling -- F27B 2009/124 relates to cooling mechanisms for vacuum pumps, compressors, fans or blowers, including heat exchangers, water or air cooling systems, and methods for removing heat from moving or stationary components to maintain performance and prevent overheating.
[F27B 2009/126] involving the circulation of cooling gases, e.g. air -- The CPC subclass F27B covers fans, blowers and the like used for moving gases, and the code 2009/126 specifies arrangements that circulate cooling gases such as air to provide ventilation or remove heat from equipment, spaces or processes.
[F27B 2009/128] the gases being further utilised as oxidants in the burners -- This subclass covers burners that use gases other than air as the oxidising agent, including waste, exhaust, oxygen-enriched or hydrogen-rich streams, to support combustion and often to recover energy or improve efficiency.
[F27B 2009/2438] with means to transfer the heat from the outcoming band to the incoming band -- F27B 2009/2438 is a sub-group of the CPC class for heat-exchange devices that incorporate a mechanism to convey heat from an outgoing fluid stream to an incoming one, such as recuperators, regenerative exchangers, or similar structures used in heating, cooling or refrigeration cycles. This classification covers designs where the transferred heat improves overall energy efficiency by pre-heating or pre-cooling the receiving flow.
[F27B 2009/2446] with means to control the tension of the band -- This CPC subgroup designates band-driven mechanisms that incorporate a tension-control device. It covers belts, chains or similar loops equipped with springs, rollers, hydraulic or electronic adjusters that keep the band at a predetermined tension during operation, and is applied to conveyors, drives and other equipment where band tension is actively regulated.
[F27B 2009/2484] the conveyor being a helical device -- The CPC subclass F27B pertains to conveyors that employ a helical screw or auger to move bulk material along a continuous, spiral path. Such helical devices are used for loading, unloading, mixing or transporting solids in industries ranging from agriculture to chemicals.
[F27B 2009/2492] the conveyor being constituted by series of little rams or ratchets, moving the charge along -- F27B 2009/2492 covers conveyors that move material by a chain of small ramming or ratchet elements that advance the charge in a stepwise fashion. The design uses repeated mechanical pushes to transport bulk or discrete items along a defined path.
[F27B 2009/264] the truck carrying a partition -- F27B 2009/264 covers trucks equipped with a fixed or removable partition that separates the driver's cabin from the cargo or passenger area, used for safety, hygiene or regulatory purposes. It includes the design, mounting and structural features of such partitions.
[F27B 2009/266] the truck having conducts for guiding the oven atmosphere -- The entry concerns trucks or other mobile units equipped with ducts or conductors that guide the oven atmosphere, directing hot gases or heated air through the onboard oven to achieve uniform temperature control for industrial baking or heating processes on wheels.
[F27B 2009/268] through the structure of the car and through the charge -- F27B 2009/268 covers furnace or burner arrangements in which heat is transferred through the vehicle's (car's) structure and through the material being charged, such as fuel or the load, to effect combustion or heating. It describes designs that use the car's frame or components as part of the heat-transfer path and methods where the charge itself participates in distributing the thermal energy.
[F27B 2009/3016] with arrangements to circulate gases through the charge -- This CPC entry relates to electric-arc furnace designs that incorporate a system for moving gases through the metal charge, allowing the gas flow to improve heat transfer, control the furnace atmosphere, or aid in impurity removal during melting and refining.
[F27B 2009/3022] with arrangements to maintain oxidising reducing or neutral zones -- F27B 2009/3022 classifies pumps that are designed with internal compartments or barriers to keep oxidising, reducing or neutral zones separate during operation, preventing unwanted chemical reactions between different media. The arrangement may involve distinct chambers, seals or flow paths that maintain each zone's chemical environment, commonly used in processes handling reactive chemicals. This subclass falls under the 2009 version of the CPC system for pump technology.
[F27B 2009/3027] Use of registers, partitions -- F27B 2009/3027 covers pumps that incorporate internal registers or partitions to divide flow paths, create separate chambers, or direct fluid streams within the pump housing. The classification applies to designs where such elements are used to control the distribution, mixing, or separation of liquids during operation.
[F27B 2009/3033] Fumes circulating in the same direction as the charge -- F27B 2009/3033 covers arrangements where exhaust-fume flow is directed parallel to the incoming charge flow, typically in combustion chambers or turbines, to promote efficient mixing or reduce pressure losses. The configuration can improve thermal efficiency and simplify scavenging.
[F27B 2009/3038] Fumes or gases alternatively changing their longitudinal direction -- This CPC subgroup covers devices that cause a gas or vapor stream to periodically reverse or oscillate its longitudinal flow direction, such as swirl generators, pulsating nozzles, or apparatuses producing alternating-direction jets to improve mixing or dispersion.
[F27B 2009/305] Particular conformation of the furnace -- F27B 2009/305 pertains to the specific geometric shape or structural arrangement of a furnace, including the design of heating chambers, walls and refractory components that affect heat distribution and efficiency. It covers patents that claim particular conformations such as cylindrical, rectangular or irregular forms designed to optimise thermal performance or material handling.
[F27B 2009/3055] Non-uniform section through the length of the furnace -- This subclass covers furnaces whose interior cross-section varies along the length of the chamber, such as tapered, stepped or contoured designs that change shape from one end to the other to control temperature gradients, material flow or loading geometry. It includes any furnace where the section is intentionally non-uniform to improve process performance.
[F27B 2009/3061] Furnaces with longitudinal grooves -- F27B 2009/3061 covers furnaces that incorporate longitudinal grooves in their interiors or walls, typically to enhance heat flow, facilitate material handling, or accommodate accessories such as rollers or conveyors. The grooves run parallel to the furnace's length and may be used in heating, annealing, sintering or similar thermal processes. This subclass is part of the broader CPC group for industrial furnaces and ovens.
[F27B 2009/3066] Cooling the under-structure of the kiln, e.g. under the cars -- F27B 2009/3066 relates to cooling systems applied to the under-structure of kilns, for example beneath the moving carriages that carry workpieces. It includes devices or techniques that remove heat from these lower sections to protect components and improve temperature control.
[F27B 2009/3072] Balancing the pressure between the upper part and the lower part of the kiln, above and under the track
[F27B 2009/3083] Arrangements to handle skid marks -- The code refers to features on earth-moving machines that detect, remove or otherwise manage skid marks left by tracks or wheels, such as sensors that identify slipping, brushes or scrapers that clean the ground, and protective surfaces that maintain traction and minimise soil disturbance.
[F27B 2009/3088] Drying arrangements -- F27B 2009/3088 relates to drying arrangements used in industrial and agricultural processing equipment. It covers devices and methods for removing moisture from solid or liquid materials, including heated-air chambers, conveyor dryers, rotary drums, and associated control systems that facilitate efficient drying in manufacturing, food, or waste treatment processes.
[F27B 2009/3094] Means to store a part of the charge in the furnace -- The subclass relates to devices or arrangements that retain a portion of the material being processed inside the furnace during operation, such as temporary storage chambers, refractory pockets or internal trays, enabling staged feeding, temperature control or recycling of the charge.
[F27B 2009/3607] Heaters located above the track of the charge -- F27B 2009/3607 covers heating devices that are positioned above the travel path of a moving charge such as a web, sheet or strip. The heaters radiate or conduct heat onto the material as it passes, enabling drying, curing, pre-heating or temperature control without direct contact. This arrangement is common in paper, textile and printing processes where uniform heating from above is required.
[F27B 2009/3615] Burner in the ceiling directed vertically downwards -- F27B 2009/3615 classifies burners that are mounted in a ceiling and direct the flame or hot gases vertically downwards, typically used in overhead heating appliances such as furnaces, boilers or radiant panels to provide downward-directed heat for industrial or commercial spaces.
[F27B 2009/3623] Heaters located under the track -- This CPC entry covers heating devices installed beneath railway tracks to prevent ice and snow accumulation, maintain track temperature, and improve adhesion. It includes systems using electric heating elements, heated air, water or steam circulation, and control mechanisms for de-icing or temperature regulation of the track structure.
[F27B 2009/363] Burners in the hearth directed towards the ceiling -- The entry pertains to heating appliances whose combustion chamber contains a burner positioned in the hearth that projects flames or hot gases upward toward the ceiling, improving convection and room heating. It includes designs for fireplaces, stoves and similar devices that use an upward-directed burner to enhance heat distribution.
[F27B 2009/3638] Heaters located above and under the track -- The entry covers heating arrangements for railways where heating elements are installed either on the upper surface of the rail or beneath the track structure. Such systems are intended to melt snow, ice or frost on the track, improve traction and maintain safe operating conditions in cold environments.
[F27B 2009/3646] Heating the ceiling or the walls for a reverberatory effect -- This class covers processes that heat the ceiling or walls of a furnace or chamber so that reflected heat creates a reverberatory environment, enhancing temperature uniformity for melting, annealing, drying or similar treatments. It includes designs where radiant energy is intentionally directed at interior surfaces to improve heat distribution and energy efficiency.
[F27B 2009/3653] Preheated fuel -- The CPC entry F27B 2009/3653 covers inventions that preheat fuel before it is supplied to a combustion chamber. It includes systems, components and control methods designed to raise fuel temperature to improve combustion efficiency, reduce emissions, or prevent freezing. The classification is used for patents related to fuel heating in engines, boilers and other combustion equipment.
[F27B 2009/3661] preheated with the exhaust gases -- F27B 2009/3661 covers devices or methods in which the intake air or fuel for a combustion system is preheated by recirculating waste exhaust gases, improving thermal efficiency and reducing emissions. The classification is used for patents concerning heat exchangers, recuperators or similar arrangements integrated with fans, blowers, or compressors.
[F27B 2009/3669] preheated with the gases of the cooling zone -- The CPC subgroup F27B 2009/3669 covers methods or apparatuses where a material such as a gas or solid feed is preheated by using the hot gases that have already passed through a cooling zone of the same process, typically in furnaces, reactors or heat exchangers, thereby improving energy efficiency through internal heat recycling.
[F27B 2009/3676] preheated with the gases of the preheating zone -- The CPC entry F27B 2009/3676 covers processes in industrial furnaces where the charge or workpiece is preheated by exposing it to hot gases flowing in a designated pre-heating zone before entering the main heating chamber. This improves energy efficiency and temperature uniformity.
[F27B 2009/3684] Combustion within a combustion chamber with outlets in the kiln chamber -- F27B 2009/3684 covers systems where the combustion takes place in a separate chamber and the generated gases or flames are directed through outlets into the kiln chamber for heating. It includes arrangements of burners, ducts and nozzles that supply the combustion products to the interior of a kiln used in processes such as ceramic firing or material calcination.
[F27B 2009/3692] The charge containing combustible materials -- F27B 2009/3692 covers the composition and arrangement of a charge that includes combustible materials for use in furnaces, kilns, incinerators or similar heating devices. It addresses how the fuel mixture is formed, loaded and managed to ensure efficient and controlled combustion.
[F27B 2009/382] Charging -- The entry covers methods and apparatus for supplying electrical energy to storage devices such as rechargeable batteries or capacitors, typically integrated with electric machines, power converters or motor drives. It includes charging circuits, control strategies, monitoring and protection features that ensure safe, efficient and regulated replenishment of stored energy.
[F27B 2009/384] Discharging -- The CPC subclass F27B 2009/384 covers devices and methods for emptying or releasing bulk solid or particulate material from storage containers, hoppers, bins, or transport lines. It includes discharge gates, chutes, auger and screw feeders, and other mechanisms that control the flow of material out of a vessel or carrier.
[F27B 2009/386] Lateral intake or outtake -- The CPC subclass F27B 2009/386 covers devices that take in or discharge fluids, gases, or particulate matter through openings positioned on the side of a machine rather than at the front or rear. It includes lateral ports, ducts, or nozzles designed for side-flow intake or outtake in handling, processing or transport equipment.
[F27B 2009/388] Centrally in the lateral wall -- The CPC subclass F27B 2009/388 refers to powder-metallurgy articles that have a feature placed centrally in the lateral wall of the component, such as a reinforcement, cavity or insertion positioned midway along the side surface. It refines the broader F27B group for metal-powder shaping by specifying this particular wall location.
[F27B 2014/002] Smelting process, e.g. sequences to melt a specific material -- F27B 2014/002 relates to smelting processes, covering the specific sequences and steps used to melt a particular material. It includes methods for heating, controlling temperature, and managing melt flow to achieve desired metallurgical properties, and is applicable to a range of metals and alloys.
[F27B 2014/004] Process involving a smelting step, e.g. vaporisation -- The subclass covers metallurgical or chemical processes that include a smelting step followed by vaporisation of the molten material, such as in metal recycling, waste treatment or alloy production. It encompasses methods where solid feedstock is melted to a liquid phase and then converted to vapour for further handling, deposition or recovery.
[F27B 2014/006] involving a salt bath or help metal bath -- F27B 2014/006 covers the use of a molten salt or molten metal bath as the heating medium in heat-treating operations, such as case hardening, annealing or tempering. It includes equipment and methods where the workpiece is immersed in the bath to achieve the desired thermal and surface-chemical effects.
[F27B 2014/008] Continuous casting -- Continuous casting refers to processes and apparatus that solidify molten metal directly into a semi-finished shape such as a billet, slab, bloom or rod by feeding the liquid metal into a moving mold or strand and controlling solidification with chillers, secondary cooling zones and related equipment. It includes methods for controlling temperature, strand speed, and mould design to achieve desired metallurgical properties.
[F27B 2014/045] Vacuum -- The CPC code F27B 2014/045 designates vacuum applications in the field of compressors and fans, covering devices, methods and systems that create or use vacuum for fluid handling, suction or pressure reduction in industrial processes.
[F27B 2014/066] Construction of the induction furnace -- The CPC subclass F27B 2014/066 covers inventions related to the structural design, assembly and material selection for induction furnaces used in metal melting. It includes details on the furnace body, insulating linings, cooling systems and mounting arrangements that optimise heating efficiency and durability.
[F27B 2014/068] with the use of an electrode producing a current in the melt -- This CPC subgroup relates to processes in which an electrode is placed in a molten metal and a current is passed through the melt to achieve heating, stirring, refining or alloying, often in electroslag or electrolytic furnaces. It covers the use of the electrode-generated current as the primary energy source for melt treatment.
[F27B 2014/0812] Continuously charging -- The CPC entry F27B 2014/0812 relates to steam generators in which the charge-such as fuel, water, or another working fluid-is supplied continuously during operation, enabling uninterrupted steam production. It covers designs for feeding mechanisms, control systems and associated components that maintain a steady charge flow.
[F27B 2014/0818] Discharging -- F27B 2014/0818 relates to the discharge side of pumps, covering the design, arrangement and operation of outlets, nozzles, diffusers and other components that control how fluid leaves the pump and enters the downstream system.
[F27B 2014/0825] Crucible or pot support -- The F27B 2014/0825 entry of the Cooperative Patent Classification identifies technical features that support a crucible or pot within a furnace or heating apparatus. It covers structures such as brackets, frames, or holders designed to secure the vessel in place, maintain proper orientation, and enable safe handling during high-temperature processing. This classification is used for inventions relating to the mechanical support of melting or heat-treatment containers.
[F27B 2014/0831] Support or means for the transport of crucibles -- The code designates devices that hold, secure, or facilitate moving crucibles during heating or processing operations. It includes frames, carriers, trays, or any supporting structures that enable safe transport of crucibles within furnaces, laboratories, or manufacturing equipment.
[F27B 2014/0837] Cooling arrangements -- The CPC subclass F27B 2014/0837 pertains to cooling arrangements for heating, steam-generating or hot-water supply equipment. It includes devices such as heat exchangers, radiators, fans, and other structures that remove or disperse heat generated by the primary system, ensuring temperature control and safe operation.
[F27B 2014/0843] Lining or casing -- F27B 2014/0843 classifies the lining or casing of liquid-pumping equipment, covering protective inner layers, sleeves or housings that shield pump components from wear, corrosion or fluid-induced damage, and the methods or materials used to install them. This includes designs where the lining forms a removable or integral barrier within the pump's pressure-tight chamber.
[F27B 2014/085] Preheating of the charge -- F27B 2014/085 covers methods and apparatuses for preheating the charge, i.e., the material to be processed in a furnace or kiln, before it enters the main heating zone. It includes devices that raise the temperature of the charge using external burners, waste-heat recovery, or conductive heating to improve energy efficiency and material handling.
[F27B 2014/0856] Preheating of the crucible -- This CPC subgroup covers devices and processes for preheating the crucible used in heat-treating or melting operations. It includes heating elements, burners or circulation systems that raise the crucible temperature before material insertion to reduce thermal shock and improve energy efficiency.
[F27B 2014/0862] Flux guides -- In CPC class F27B, a flux guide is a component installed in steam-generating or combustion equipment that shapes and directs the flow of gases, steam or heat-carrying fluid. By steering the flux, it improves mixing, heat transfer and prevents localized overheating, thereby enhancing the efficiency and stability of the plant.
[F27B 2014/0868] Magnetic shields -- F27B 2014/0868 - Magnetic shields covers devices or arrangements that reduce, redirect or contain magnetic fields, typically using high-permeability materials, superconductors or composite structures, and are employed in electrical machines, transformers, measurement instruments or other equipment to protect components or personnel from stray magnetic flux.
[F27B 2014/0875] Two zones or chambers, e.g. one used for charging -- F27B 2014/0875 covers pumps that incorporate two distinct zones or chambers, typically one chamber dedicated to charging or filling the pump with fluid and a second chamber used for the pumping action. The classification applies to designs where the separate chambers improve fluid handling, pressure control or efficiency, such as dual-chamber hydraulic or pneumatic pumps.
[F27B 2014/0881] Two or more crucibles -- F27B 2014/0881 covers inventions that employ two or more crucibles in a single apparatus or process. The classification applies to arrangements where multiple crucibles are used together for simultaneous or sequential heating, melting, or material treatment, often to increase throughput, enable staged temperature zones, or facilitate continuous operation in metallurgical, ceramic, or glass production.
[F27B 2014/0887] Movement of the melt -- F27B 2014/0887 classifies devices or methods that control the motion of molten material, typically in casting or metallurgical processes, to ensure uniform flow, reduce turbulence, and improve solidification quality. It covers mechanisms such as pumps, agitators, and guiding channels that influence melt movement during processing. This subclass is used for inventions aimed at managing melt dynamics for better product consistency.
[F27B 2014/0893] Heat-conductive material disposed on the surface of the melt -- This subclass covers methods and devices where a heat-conductive layer is placed on the surface of a molten metal or other melt to improve heat removal, control solidification, or protect the melt during casting or processing. It includes embodiments in which the conductive material may be a metal sheet, ceramic coating, or fluid film applied during continuous or batch processes.
[F27B 2014/102] Form of the crucibles -- The code F27B 2014/102 covers patents that describe the shape or structural design of crucibles used for melting or heat-treating metals, alloys, or similar materials. It includes variations in geometry, wall thickness, openings, and supporting features that affect thermal efficiency, durability, or handling during high-temperature processes.
[F27B 2014/104] Crucible linings -- The subclass covers protective linings and refractory coatings applied to crucibles used in high-temperature processes such as melting, heating or chemical treatment. It includes materials, structures and methods for installing or bonding these linings to improve thermal insulation, chemical resistance and mechanical durability of the crucible.
[F27B 2014/106] Ladles -- F27B 2014/106 pertains to ladles used in metalworking, specifically containers for transporting, pouring or holding molten metal during casting processes, including designs, materials and related accessories. This subclass covers both stationary and portable ladles, as well as features that facilitate safe handling of high-temperature liquids.
[F27B 2014/108] Cold crucibles (transparent to electromagnetic radiations) -- F27B 2014/108 covers cold crucible technology in which the crucible material is transparent to electromagnetic radiation, allowing heating by induction without direct contact. It is used in melting and refining processes where the crucible does not absorb the radiation, reducing contamination and enabling precise temperature control.
[F27B 2014/146] Recuperation of lost heat, e.g. regenerators -- F27B 2014/146 relates to technologies that capture waste heat from industrial or mechanical processes and reuse it, typically through devices such as heat-exchangers, regenerators or recuperators, to improve overall energy efficiency.
[F27B 2017/0091] Series of chambers, e.g. associated in their use -- F27B 2017/0091 relates to devices comprising a series of chambers that work together, such as successive reactors, heat exchangers, separators or storage units arranged in a line or network. The classification covers arrangements where the chambers are linked by conduits or other means so that material or energy flows from one chamber to the next to achieve a combined function.
[F27B 21/00] Open or uncovered sintering apparatus -- The entry covers sintering equipment that operates without a closed chamber, allowing the material to be heated in an open or uncovered environment. It includes open-air sintering furnaces, pits, retorts and similar apparatus used for metal or ceramic production where direct atmospheric exposure is required.
[F27B 21/02] Sintering grates or tables -- F27B 21/02 pertains to sintering grates or tables employed in open or uncovered sintering apparatus, defining the support structures that hold material while it is heated for sintering. It falls under mechanical engineering, furnaces, and general ovens, specifying the grate or table component of the process.
[F27B 21/04] Sintering pots or sintering pans -- F27B 21/04 covers open or uncovered sintering apparatus such as pots and pans that hold material during the sintering process in furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts. The classification is used for devices that support powder or particulate consolidation by heat without enclosing the workpiece.
[F27B 21/06] Endless-strand sintering machines -- Endless-strand sintering machines are continuous furnaces that feed a moving strand of material through a heated zone to achieve sintering, commonly used for producing porous metal parts and ceramics. The apparatus operates with an open or uncovered sintering chamber where the strand advances without interruption, providing uniform temperature exposure and high productivity.
[F27B 3/00] Hearth-type furnaces, e.g. of reverberatory type -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Furnaces through which the charge is moved mechanically, e.g. of tunnel type; Similar furnaces in which the charge moves by gravity -> F27B9/00 - Bell-type furnaces -> F27B11/00 - Furnaces with both stationary charge and progression of heating -> F27B13/00 - Crucible or pot furnaces -> F27B14/00 - Fluidised-bed furnaces; Other furnaces using or treating finely-divided materials in dispersion -> F27B15/00 - Open or uncovered sintering apparatus; Other heat-treatment apparatus of like construction -> F27B21/00
[F27B 3/002] Siemens-Martin type furnaces -- Siemens-Martin type furnaces are open-hearth, hearth-type steelmaking furnaces in which the charge is heated by radiant and convection burners and the flame passes over a refractory hearth, allowing controlled melting, refining and alloying of steel in a large, flat working surface.
[F27B 3/005] Port construction -- F27B 3/005 covers the design and manufacturing of ports - openings, inlet or outlet apertures, and associated sealing or mounting structures - in hearth-type furnaces such as reverberatory furnaces. It specifies features that control the flow of gases, protect the furnace interior, and facilitate maintenance or material handling within the heating chamber.
[F27B 3/007] Removable burner head -- A removable burner head is a detachable component of hearth-type furnaces such as reverberatory ovens, allowing the burner assembly to be easily accessed, serviced, or replaced without dismantling the whole furnace structure. It is used to improve maintenance efficiency and operational flexibility.
[F27B 3/02] of single-chamber fixed-hearth type -- F27B 3/02 covers single-chamber furnaces with a fixed hearth, where the material rests on a stationary floor and heat is reflected from the roof or walls in a reverberatory style. These devices are used for melting, heat-treatment or sintering in metalworking and related processes.
[F27B 3/04] of multiple-hearth type -- F27B 3/04 covers hearth-type furnaces, such as reverberatory furnaces, that contain more than one hearth. The multiple-hearth arrangement allows successive heating zones or stages within a single vessel, improving temperature control and processing efficiency for metal melting or heat-treatment operations. This subclass falls under the broader categories of mechanical engineering, furnaces, and general ovens or retorts.
[F27B 3/045] Multiple chambers, e.g. one of which is used for charging -- This subclass covers hearth-type furnaces that incorporate two or more chambers, with one chamber specifically provided for loading or charging material while the other chamber(s) carry out heating or processing. It is applied to reverberatory and similar furnace designs where separate charging and working zones are used.
[F27B 3/06] with movable working chambers or hearths, e.g. tiltable -- This subclass relates to hearth-type furnaces, such as reverberatory kilns, that incorporate a movable or tiltable working chamber or hearth to aid loading, unloading, or process control. The hearth may be pivoted, tilted or otherwise shifted to improve access to the material. It is applied chiefly in metallurgical and heat-treatment operations.
[F27B 3/065] tiltable -- The code refers to hearth-type furnaces such as reverberatory furnaces that can be tilted to facilitate charging, discharging, or maintenance. Tilting mechanisms allow the furnace shell or hearth to pivot about a horizontal axis while remaining sealed.
[F27B 3/08] heated electrically, with or without any other source of heat -- F27B 3/08 covers hearth-type furnaces such as reverberatory furnaces that are heated primarily by electricity, either alone or together with additional heat sources. The classification applies to designs where heat is applied to the furnace floor or hearth, enabling processes like melting, annealing or heat treatment.
[F27B 3/085] Arc furnaces -- Arc furnaces are electric melting units that generate heat by one or more electric arcs struck between electrodes and the material placed on a hearth or in a shallow bath. The hearth-type design, such as reverberatory furnaces, reflects radiant energy toward the charge, enabling efficient melting of ferrous and non-ferrous metals. They are employed in steelmaking, foundries and other metallurgical processes.
[F27B 3/10] Details, accessories or equipment, e.g. dust-collectors, specially adapted for hearth-type furnaces -- F27B 3/10 covers details, accessories or equipment specially adapted for hearth-type furnaces such as reverberatory furnaces, including devices like dust collectors, burners, or protective linings. These components are designed to improve the operation, safety or efficiency of the furnace.
[F27B 3/105] Slag chamber -- F27B 3/105 relates to the slag chamber, a component of hearth-type furnaces such as reverberatory ovens that collects and removes molten slag produced during metalworking or smelting. The chamber is situated below the furnace hearth and may include liners, tap holes or draining features to facilitate slag handling and protect the furnace structure.
[F27B 3/12] Working chambers or casings -- The subclass relates to the internal working chambers or casings of hearth-type furnaces, such as reverberatory ovens, describing their shape, lining, support and construction that contain the fire and material being processed.
[F27B 3/14] Arrangements of linings -- F27B 3/14 pertains to hearth-type furnaces such as reverberatory furnaces and defines the various configurations and methods for arranging the refractory linings within the furnace chamber. It includes the design of lining layers, their positioning, support structures and any modular or interchangeable lining systems that affect heat distribution, durability and maintenance.
[F27B 3/16] Walls -- F27B 3/16 covers the design and construction of walls in hearth-type furnaces such as reverberatory furnaces, kilns and ovens. The classification addresses features like material selection, insulation, cooling and structural support that affect heat distribution and overall furnace efficiency.
[F27B 3/18] Arrangements of devices for charging -- The classification F27B 3/18 covers the design and layout of charging mechanisms for hearth-type furnaces such as reverberatory furnaces, including bins, hoppers, feeders and distribution systems that introduce material into the furnace. It falls under mechanical engineering, specifically furnaces and ovens, focusing on how raw material is supplied to the hearth.
[F27B 3/183] Charging of arc furnaces vertically through the roof, e.g. in three points -- This CPC subclass covers the vertical introduction of charge material into arc furnaces through openings in the roof, typically using three separate points to feed the melt. It applies to hearth-type furnaces such as reverberatory or similar designs where the charge is delivered from above rather than from the side.
[F27B 3/186] Charging in a vertical chamber adjacent to the melting chamber -- The classification covers methods and apparatus for feeding solid or molten material into a hearth-type furnace through a vertical auxiliary chamber positioned next to the main melting zone. The side chamber allows charge to be introduced while maintaining furnace atmosphere and temperature control.
[F27B 3/19] Arrangements of devices for discharging -- This subclass concerns the layout and integration of mechanisms that remove material, gases or ashes from hearth-type furnaces such as reverberatory furnaces, including the positioning of discharge ports, hoppers, conveyors and related control devices.
[F27B 3/20] Arrangements of heating devices -- F27B 3/20 covers the layout and integration of heating elements within hearth-type furnaces such as reverberatory furnaces, including the positioning, mounting and interconnection of burners, coils, gas nozzles or other heat sources to achieve uniform temperature distribution and efficient operation.
[F27B 3/205] Burners -- The CPC subclass F27B 3/205 covers burners specifically designed for hearth-type furnaces such as reverberatory furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts. It addresses the arrangement, operation and fuel supply of devices that produce the flame or heat source within these open-air hearth configurations. This subclass falls under the broader categories of mechanical engineering, furnaces and general hearth-type heating equipment.
[F27B 3/22] Arrangements of air or gas supply devices -- This CPC subclass deals with hearth-type furnaces such as reverberatory furnaces and defines the design and layout of devices that introduce air or fuel gas into the furnace chamber. It includes the positioning, ducts, nozzles and distribution systems that control the supply of combustion media for uniform heating and efficient operation.
[F27B 3/225] Oxygen blowing -- The F27B 3/225 entry pertains to the use of oxygen blowing in hearth-type furnaces, such as reverberatory furnaces, to intensify combustion and raise process temperatures. It includes methods for introducing oxygen via burners, lances or other delivery systems directly into the furnace chamber. This technique is applied to improve efficiency and control of heat in metal-working or heat-treatment processes.
[F27B 3/24] Cooling arrangements -- This CPC subclass covers the means and methods for removing heat from hearth-type furnaces such as reverberatory furnaces. It includes cooling systems, heat exchangers, water or air circulation, and related control devices designed to protect the furnace structure and improve efficiency.
[F27B 3/26] Arrangements of heat-exchange apparatus -- F27B 3/26 covers the design and layout of heat-exchange equipment within hearth-type furnaces such as reverberatory ovens. It includes the positioning, mounting and integration of tubes, plates or other exchangers that recover or transfer heat from the furnace interior to external processes or heating media. The focus is on improving thermal efficiency and control in these general-purpose furnace systems.
[F27B 3/263] Regenerators -- F27B 3/263 covers regenerators used in hearth-type furnaces such as reverberatory furnaces, describing devices that store and release heat to improve thermal efficiency, typically consisting of heat-absorbing bricks or panels that cyclically alternate exposure to hot and cold gas streams.
[F27B 3/266] Exhaust gases reversing flow devices -- F27B 3/266 refers to devices installed in hearth-type furnaces, such as reverberatory furnaces, that reverse the direction of exhaust gas flow to enhance heat recovery, improve temperature uniformity and reduce emissions within the furnace chamber.
[F27B 3/28] Arrangement of controlling, monitoring, alarm or the like devices -- F27B 3/28 covers the layout and integration of control, monitoring or alarm devices in hearth-type furnaces such as reverberatory kilns and ovens. It addresses how sensors, switches, displays or safety systems are positioned and interconnected to manage temperature, combustion or operation within the furnace structure.
[F27B 5/00] Muffle furnaces -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Furnaces through which the charge is moved mechanically or in which the charge moves by gravity -> F27B9/00
[F27B 5/02] of multiple-chamber type -- The F27B 5/02 class covers muffle furnaces of the multiple-chamber type, where a protective inner chamber isolates the material being heated from the combustion gases in an outer chamber. These designs are used in laboratory and industrial settings to achieve uniform high temperatures while preventing contamination of the workpiece.
[F27B 5/04] adapted for treating the charge in vacuum or special atmosphere -- A muffle furnace designed to process its charge under vacuum or a controlled atmosphere, enabling specialized heating conditions such as reduced oxidation or inert gas environments for sensitive materials. This adaptation falls within the mechanical-engineering category of furnaces, kilns, ovens and retorts.
[F27B 5/06] Details, accessories or equipment specially adapted for furnaces of these types -- F27B 5/06 pertains to the specialized parts, accessories and auxiliary equipment engineered for muffle furnaces, a subset of furnaces, kilns, ovens and retorts used in mechanical engineering. It includes items such as custom lids, doors, shelves, heating elements, temperature-control devices and protective linings that are adapted to the sealed chamber design of muffle furnaces for precise heating or processing operations.
[F27B 5/08] Arrangements of linings -- F27B 5/08 pertains to the specific design and configuration of interior linings within muffle furnaces, covering how refractory or protective materials are arranged to protect the furnace walls and control heat distribution. It includes the layout of multiple lining sections, interlocking patterns, and the positioning of insulating layers to enhance thermal efficiency and durability.
[F27B 5/10] Muffles -- Muffles are sealed chambers incorporated in a furnace that isolate the workpiece from the combustion gases, allowing precise temperature control and preventing contamination; they are used for processes such as heat treatment, sintering, and laboratory heating where a clean atmosphere is required.
[F27B 5/12] Arrangement of devices for charging -- F27B 5/12 refers to the layout and organization of charging mechanisms in muffle furnaces, including the positioning of loading doors, feeders and related handling equipment used to introduce material into the heated chamber. It addresses how these components are arranged to ensure efficient material flow while maintaining temperature control.
[F27B 5/13] Arrangement of devices for discharging -- F27B 5/13 refers to the specific design of how discharge devices are arranged in a muffle furnace, covering the placement of ports, hatches, ducts or other mechanisms that remove processed material or gases from the furnace chamber. It addresses the spatial layout and integration of these discharge components within the overall furnace structure.
[F27B 5/14] Arrangements of heating devices -- The subclass F27B 5/14 pertains to the design and spatial organization of heating elements inside muffle furnaces, covering how burners, resistive heaters and associated ducts or conduits are arranged to provide uniform temperature control and efficient heat distribution.
[F27B 5/16] Arrangements of air or gas supply devices -- The subclass F27B 5/16 covers the design and layout of devices that supply air or other gases to muffle furnaces, including ducts, nozzles, burners and other distribution arrangements that control the flow of combustion or process gases within the furnace chamber. It addresses how these supply systems are positioned, connected and regulated to achieve uniform heating or specific atmospheric conditions during operation.
[F27B 5/18] Arrangement of controlling, monitoring, alarm or like devices -- F27B 5/18 covers the layout and integration of control, monitoring and alarm equipment in muffle furnaces, including the positioning of sensors, regulators, safety interlocks and display units that manage temperature, atmosphere and operational status. It addresses how these devices are arranged to ensure reliable performance and operator safety within the furnace structure.
[F27B 7/00] Rotary-drum furnaces, i.e. horizontal or slightly inclined -- Rotary-drum furnaces are continuous heating units in which a cylindrical drum rotates about its axis, typically positioned horizontally or with a slight incline, to move material through a controlled temperature zone for processes such as drying, calcination, sintering or heat treatment. The rotation ensures uniform exposure and enables steady feed and discharge, making them suitable for bulk solids handling in various industrial applications.
[F27B 7/02] of multiple-chamber or multiple-drum type -- F27B 7/02 covers rotary-drum furnaces that incorporate multiple drums or separate chambers within the rotating vessel, enabling staged heating or material handling in a horizontal or slightly inclined configuration.
[F27B 7/04] with longitudinal divisions -- Rotary-drum furnaces that incorporate longitudinal partitions running along the drum's length to create separate zones or compartments within the rotating vessel, allowing distinct processing conditions or material segregation during heating. This subclass covers the design and use of such longitudinal divisions in horizontal or slightly inclined rotary furnaces.
[F27B 7/06] adapted for treating the charge in vacuum or special atmosphere -- Rotary-drum furnaces that are specifically designed to treat the charge in a vacuum or a controlled special atmosphere, providing precise temperature and environment control for specialized heat-treatment processes.
[F27B 7/08] externally heated -- The F27B 7/08 class covers rotary-drum furnaces, kilns or ovens that are heated from the outside of the rotating drum rather than by internal burners. It includes designs where the heat source-such as gas flames, electric elements or hot gases-acts on the drum's outer surface to raise the temperature of the material inside. This external heating arrangement is typical for processes like drying, calcining or sintering in a horizontal or slightly inclined drum.
[F27B 7/10] internally heated, e.g. by means of passages in the wall -- The CPC subclass F27B 7/10 covers rotary-drum furnaces that are heated from within, typically by incorporating heating passages or channels inside the furnace wall. This internal heating method supplies energy directly to the material as the drum rotates.
[F27B 7/12] tiltable -- F27B 7/12 refers to rotary-drum furnaces that are equipped with a tilting feature, allowing the drum to be inclined relative to its axis for easier charging, discharging or cleaning. The tilting mechanism improves material handling in furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts that operate on a horizontal or slightly inclined principle.
[F27B 7/14] with means for agitating or moving the charge -- F27B 7/14 covers rotary-drum furnaces, also called horizontal or slightly inclined drum kilns, that incorporate devices to agitate or transport the material being processed. The classification applies to mechanisms such as paddles, screws, lifters or other means that move the charge within the rotating drum to improve mixing or heat transfer.
[F27B 7/16] the means being fixed relatively to the drum, -- The code classifies the stationary components of a rotary-drum furnace, such as supporting structures, guides, brackets or heating elements that are anchored to the drum rather than rotating with it. These parts remain fixed relative to the drum and define the handling, sealing or feeding of material.
[F27B 7/161] the means comprising projections jutting out from the wall -- F27B 7/161 refers to rotary-drum furnaces that incorporate projections extending from the drum wall, such as ribs, fins or bumps, which serve to support, agitate or enhance heat transfer to the material inside the drum. These wall-mounted elements are integral to the furnace design and affect material flow and thermal efficiency.
[F27B 7/162] the projections consisting of separate lifting elements, e.g. lifting shovels -- F27B 7/162 relates to rotary-drum furnaces in which the internal projections are composed of separate lifting elements, such as individual shovels, used to raise and discharge material within the drum.
[F27B 7/166] the means comprising chains -- F27B 7/166 covers chain-driven mechanisms used in rotary-drum furnaces, such as horizontal or slightly inclined drums, where chains transmit power to rotate the drum or convey material within it. It includes the arrangement of sprockets, tensioners and chain loops that form the drive system for the furnace.
[F27B 7/167] the means comprising partitions -- F27B 7/167 covers the use of internal partitions in rotary-drum furnaces, whether horizontal or slightly inclined. The partitions divide the drum into separate zones to control temperature gradients, retain different atmospheres, or improve heat transfer efficiency. This subdivision applies specifically to general-purpose rotary furnaces, kilns and ovens.
[F27B 7/18] the means being movable within the drum -- F27B 7/18 covers the internal moving components of rotary-drum furnaces, such as lifters, rollers, conveyors or any mechanism that travels within the drum to transport, tumble or position the material being processed. These elements are designed to operate while the drum rotates or inclines.
[F27B 7/20] Details, accessories or equipment specially adapted for rotary-drum furnaces -- The CPC subclass F27B 7/20 covers specialised details, accessories and auxiliary equipment designed specifically for rotary-drum furnaces, which are horizontal or slightly inclined drums used for heating, calcining or sintering material. It falls under mechanical engineering, furnaces, and more generally rotary-drum furnace technology.
[F27B 7/2016] Arrangements of preheating devices for the charge -- The CPC subclass F27B 7/2016 concerns the configuration of devices that preheat the material to be processed (the charge) inside rotary-drum furnaces, which are horizontal or slightly inclined. It deals with how such preheating equipment is arranged relative to the drum and the flow of the charge.
[F27B 7/2025] consisting of a single string of cyclones -- The subclass F27B 7/2025 covers rotary-drum furnaces in which the exhaust gases are passed through one continuous string of cyclones for dust and particle removal, rather than multiple separate cyclone stages. This arrangement is used to improve cleaning efficiency and simplify the furnace gas-cleaning system while maintaining a single, uninterrupted cyclone train.
[F27B 7/2033] with means for precalcining the raw material -- The code F27B 7/2033 covers rotary-drum furnaces-horizontal or slightly inclined drums-fitted with equipment that performs a precalcining step on the raw material before it enters the main heating zone. This arrangement is used to improve material homogeneity and energy efficiency in furnace operations.
[F27B 7/2041] consisting of at least two strings of cyclones with two different admissions of raw material -- This subclass covers rotary drum furnaces that incorporate a cyclone system arranged in at least two parallel strings, each receiving raw material through separate inlets, enabling staged or dual-feed processing of feedstock. The design improves material distribution and heat transfer within the drum.
[F27B 7/205] with precalcining means on the string supplied with exhaust gases from the cooler -- This subclass deals with rotary-drum furnaces, horizontal or slightly inclined, that incorporate a precalcining stage on the material string and use exhaust gases from a downstream cooler to supply heat. The precalcining unit is positioned on the same conveyance line that receives the cooler's exhaust, improving energy efficiency and temperature control before the main heating zone.
[F27B 7/2058] with precalcining means on each string -- F27B 7/2058 relates to rotary-drum furnaces that are equipped with a precalcining unit on each string of the drum, enabling material to undergo partial calcination before proceeding to the main heating zone.
[F27B 7/2066] comprising a band transporter -- F27B 7/2066 classifies rotary-drum furnaces, either horizontal or slightly inclined, that are equipped with a band transporter for conveying material through the drum during heating. The band transporter provides continuous movement of workpieces within the furnace interior.
[F27B 7/2075] Removing incrustations -- This classification covers methods and apparatus for removing deposits, scale or other incrustations that accumulate on the interior surfaces or rotating drums of horizontal or slightly inclined rotary furnaces, including mechanical scraping, gas or liquid cleaning, ultrasonic techniques, and thermal-shock treatments applied during operation or maintenance.
[F27B 7/2083] Arrangements for the melting of metals or the treatment of molten metals -- The CPC subclass F27B 7/2083 concerns the design and layout of rotary-drum furnaces employed for melting metals or treating molten metal streams. It includes the drum's orientation, charging, heating, and discharge arrangements that enable continuous or batch processing of molten metal.
[F27B 7/22] Rotary drums -- Rotary-drum furnaces are horizontal or slightly inclined cylindrical vessels that rotate around their axis while being heated, allowing continuous processing of bulk solids for heating, drying, calcining, sintering or roasting. The classification covers their construction, drive mechanisms, internal lifters or flights, and variations in insulation, burners and material feed systems. It also includes related control and monitoring features specific to these rotating heating units.
[F27B 7/2206] Bearing rings -- The F27B 7/2206 subclass covers bearing rings employed in rotary-drum furnaces, specifying their design, material selection, lubrication methods and mounting arrangements that support the rotating drum and enable reliable operation under high-temperature conditions.
[F27B 7/224] Discharge ends -- F27B 7/224 concerns the discharge ends of rotary-drum furnaces, covering the design and arrangement of outlet openings, chutes, seals and other mechanisms that allow material to exit the rotating drum.
[F27B 7/24] Seals between rotary and stationary parts -- F27B 7/24 classifies patents dealing with sealing arrangements that connect rotating and fixed elements of rotary-drum furnaces, kilns or ovens. The focus is on preventing leakage of gases, vapours or solids at the shaft, drum or feed interfaces, and on materials and designs that withstand high temperatures and mechanical stresses.
[F27B 7/26] Drives -- The "Drives" sub-class covers the devices and mechanisms that power and transmit motion to rotary-drum furnaces, such as motors, gearboxes, belts, chains, shafts and related drive-train components that rotate the drum in horizontal or slightly inclined positions. It includes both direct and indirect drive systems used to start, control and maintain the furnace's turning action.
[F27B 7/28] Arrangements of linings -- F27B 7/28 pertains to the specific design and configuration of internal linings in rotary-drum furnaces, which are horizontal or slightly inclined. It covers the selection, arrangement and mounting of refractory or protective materials that line the drum interior to shield the structure, control heat flow and facilitate material movement.
[F27B 7/30] Arrangements of partitions -- F27B 7/30 relates to rotary-drum furnaces that are horizontal or slightly inclined and deals with the design and arrangement of internal partitions. It covers the positioning, number, shape and sealing of partitions used to divide the drum into zones for temperature control, product flow or combustion management.
[F27B 7/32] Arrangement of devices for charging -- The F27B 7/32 group relates to how charging equipment is organised for rotary-drum furnaces, including horizontal or slightly inclined drums used in kilns, ovens or retorts. It covers the layout and integration of feeders, distributors, and other devices that introduce material into the rotating chamber.
[F27B 7/3205] Charging -- This subclass covers methods and apparatus for introducing raw material, fuel, or other charge into a rotary-drum furnace, including loading mechanisms, feed systems, and associated control arrangements.
[F27B 7/33] Arrangement of devices for discharging -- The code F27B 7/33 pertains to the layout and integration of equipment that removes material from rotary-drum furnaces, including horizontal or slightly inclined drums. It covers discharge openings, chutes, conveyors, valves or other mechanisms designed to extract solids, liquids or gases from the furnace interior in an efficient and controlled manner.
[F27B 7/34] Arrangements of heating devices -- F27B 7/34 covers the design and layout of heating elements or devices within rotary-drum furnaces, which are horizontal or slightly inclined chambers used for processes such as drying, calcining or sintering. The classification addresses how burners, electric heaters, or other heat sources are positioned, mounted or configured to achieve uniform temperature distribution and efficient energy use inside the rotating drum. It is a subcategory of mechanical engineering focused on furnace and kiln technologies.
[F27B 7/36] Arrangements of air or gas supply devices -- F27B 7/36 concerns the design and positioning of devices that deliver air or other gases to the interior of rotary-drum furnaces, such as blowers, ducts, nozzles or distribution manifolds, and their integration with the drum to control combustion, heating or atmosphere.
[F27B 7/362] Introducing gas into the drum axially or through the wall -- F27B 7/362 refers to rotary-drum furnaces, such as horizontal or slightly inclined kilns, where the process gas is introduced into the drum either along its axis or through the drum wall, providing direct heating and improved material flow within the rotating chamber.
[F27B 7/38] Arrangements of cooling devices -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Arrangements of cooling devices
[F27B 7/383] Cooling devices for the charge -- A cooling device for the charge in a rotary-drum furnace, where the furnace is a horizontal or slightly inclined rotating cylinder used for heat treatment. The device removes heat from the material loaded in the drum, improving process control and product quality.
[F27B 7/386] Rotary-drum cooler -- F27B 7/386 relates to rotary-drum coolers, which are rotating cylindrical vessels used to cool materials after heating in furnace processes. These coolers operate horizontally or with a slight incline and are employed in metal, glass and other thermal treatment industries.
[F27B 7/40] Planetary coolers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Planetary coolers
[F27B 7/42] Arrangement of controlling, monitoring, alarm or like devices -- The CPC subclass F27B 7/42 relates to rotary-drum furnaces, including horizontal or slightly inclined types, and covers the arrangement of devices used to control, monitor, signal alarms or perform similar functions. It specifies how instrumentation, sensors, control panels and safety alarms are integrated within the furnace system.
[F27B 9/00] Furnaces through which the charge is moved mechanically, e.g. of tunnel type -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Rotary-drum furnaces with means for agitating or moving the charge -> F27B7/14
[F27B 9/02] of multiple-track type -- A multiple-track furnace is a type of tunnel or continuous furnace in which the material to be heated moves along several parallel tracks, each providing a separate heating zone. This arrangement allows simultaneous processing of multiple streams, improving throughput and temperature uniformity.
[F27B 9/021] having two or more parallel tracks -- F27B 9/021 pertains to tunnel-type furnaces where the charge is moved mechanically and the furnace incorporates two or more parallel tracks, allowing simultaneous processing of material or increased throughput in the heating zone.
[F27B 9/022] With two tracks moving in opposite directions -- This subclass covers tunnel-type furnaces that use two mechanically driven tracks moving in opposite directions to convey the charge through the heating zone, enabling continuous processing and improved heat distribution.
[F27B 9/023] with a U turn at one end -- This subclass covers mechanically fed tunnel furnaces in which the conveying system bends back in a U-shaped turn at one end, allowing the charge to be redirected within the heating chamber. It is used for continuous processing where material enters, travels through a heated zone, makes the U turn, and exits.
[F27B 9/024] with superimposed tracks -- A tunnel-type furnace in which the charge is conveyed mechanically along the longitudinal direction and the transport system includes an additional set of tracks superimposed on the primary ones, allowing simultaneous or sequential movement of material for improved loading, heating or discharge. This arrangement is used to enhance control of the charge flow and to accommodate multiple processing zones within the same furnace.
[F27B 9/025] having two or more superimposed tracks  F27B9/024  takes precedence -- F27B 9/025 covers furnaces in which the material is transported on two or more stacked tracks, allowing simultaneous movement of separate charge layers within the same tunnel-type furnace. This subclass refines the more general arrangement of superimposed tracks defined in F27B 9/024.
[F27B 9/028] Multi-chamber type furnaces,  F27B9/029  takes precedence -- A multi-chamber furnace in which the material being processed is continuously moved mechanically from one chamber to the next, typically in a tunnel-type arrangement, allowing successive heating zones and improved temperature control. This design falls under the broader category of mechanically assisted furnaces.
[F27B 9/029] Multicellular type furnaces constructed with add-on modules -- A multicellular furnace built from add-on modules, allowing the charge to be moved mechanically through a tunnel-type configuration. The modular design enables scalable capacity and easier maintenance while maintaining continuous processing of material. This construction falls under the mechanical engineering subclass for furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts in general.
[F27B 9/04] adapted for treating the charge in vacuum or special atmosphere -- F27B 9/04 covers tunnel-type furnaces in which the charge is moved mechanically and the unit is adapted to treat the material in vacuum or a special controlled atmosphere, such as an inert gas, to enable specific heat-treatment processes or prevent oxidation.
[F27B 9/042] Vacuum furnaces -- F27B 9/042 concerns vacuum furnaces in which the workpiece is conveyed mechanically, typically in a tunnel-type configuration, and processed under reduced pressure to prevent oxidation and achieve uniform heat treatment.
[F27B 9/045] Furnaces with controlled atmosphere -- F27B 9/045 covers furnaces whose internal atmosphere is actively regulated, typically by introducing inert, oxidizing or reducing gases and controlling pressure, flow and sealing to achieve specific chemical conditions during heating. The classification includes designs for gas supply, exhaust and monitoring systems that maintain the desired atmosphere throughout the process.
[F27B 9/047] the atmosphere consisting of protective gases -- The subclass F27B 9/047 covers furnaces, especially tunnel-type units where the charge moves mechanically, that operate with a protective gas atmosphere. It addresses the selection, supply and control of inert or otherwise protective gases to prevent oxidation, contamination or unwanted reactions during heating. This includes systems that maintain a specific gas composition throughout the furnace interior.
[F27B 9/06] heated without contact between combustion gases and charge -- F27B 9/06 covers furnaces, such as tunnel-type kilns, in which the material to be processed is moved mechanically while being heated by indirect contact; the combustion gases do not touch the charge, and heat is transferred through the furnace walls or a separate medium.
[F27B 9/061] with at least two longitudinal chambers carrying combustion gases, i.e. of the Dressler type
[F27B 9/062] electrically heated -- F27B 9/062 covers tunnel-type or mechanically moving-charge furnaces in which the heat is supplied primarily by electricity, such as resistive, electrode or induction heating. The subclass is used for patents describing designs where the material is conveyed through a heated chamber powered by an electrical source.
[F27B 9/063] Resistor heating, e.g. with resistors also emitting IR rays -- F27B 9/063 covers mechanically driven furnaces-such as tunnel-type units-in which the material to be processed moves through the heating zone, and the heat is generated by electrical resistors that may also emit infrared radiation. This subclass falls under Mechanical Engineering → Furnaces → Furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts in general.
[F27B 9/065] the resistance being transported by the conveyor -- F27B 9/065 addresses furnace designs in which the heating resistance, such as electric heating elements, is mounted on or carried by a conveyor that moves the charge through the furnace, typical of tunnel-type kilns. The class covers the mechanical arrangement and drive of the conveyor that transports the resistance along the heating zone.
