[F27B 9/066] heated by lamps -- F27B 9/066 covers mechanically driven furnaces, such as tunnel-type systems, in which the material is moved through the heating zone and the heat source consists of lamps. The classification applies to any furnace that uses radiant lamp heating rather than conventional burners or electric resistance elements.
[F27B 9/067] heated by induction -- The code F27B 9/067 refers to mechanically-driven furnaces, such as tunnel-type kilns, in which the material charge is conveyed through the heating zone and the heat is generated by induction. This subclass focuses on the combination of continuous charge movement and induction heating within the broader category of mechanical engineering furnace technologies.
[F27B 9/068] heated by radiant tubes, the tube being heated by a hot medium, e.g. hot gases -- F27B 9/068 covers mechanical furnaces, such as tunnel-type units, in which the charge moves through the furnace and heat is supplied by radiant tubes that are warmed by a hot medium like combustion gases.
[F27B 9/08] heated through chamber walls -- F27B 9/08 refers to mechanically fed furnaces, such as tunnel-type units, where the heat is supplied primarily by the walls of the furnace chamber rather than by direct flame or separate heating elements. This subclass covers designs that rely on heated chamber surfaces to raise the temperature of the moving charge.
[F27B 9/082] Muffle furnaces -- Muffle furnaces are tunnel-type heating devices in which the material to be processed moves mechanically along the furnace while being isolated from the combustion gases by a protective chamber or "muffle." This construction allows precise temperature control and clean processing of the charge, making it suitable for heat-treatment, sintering, or analytical procedures in mechanical engineering applications.
[F27B 9/084] the muffle being fixed and in a single piece -- F27B 9/084 covers tunnel-type or other mechanically-driven furnaces in which the protective inner chamber, or muffle, is constructed as a single, fixed piece rather than being segmented or removable. The fixed muffle provides a continuous barrier between the heating zone and the moving material, improving temperature uniformity and reducing leakage.
[F27B 9/086] with two or more fixed muffles -- This subclass covers tunnel-type or mechanically conveyed furnaces where the charge moves through the heating zone and the interior contains two or more stationary muffles that shape or direct the gas flow and temperature distribution. It is used for processes requiring precise heat control such as metal heat treatment.
[F27B 9/088] Series of separate muffles conveyed through the furnace -- F27B 9/088 covers furnaces in which the charge is moved mechanically, such as tunnel-type units, featuring a series of individual muffles that are conveyed through the furnace to shape the heat zone and protect the lining.
[F27B 9/10] heated by hot air or gas -- F27B 9/10 classifies mechanically-driven furnaces, such as tunnel-type units, in which the material charge is conveyed by a moving system and the heating is supplied by hot air or gas. It falls under mechanical engineering, specifically the broader group of furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts.
[F27B 9/12] with special arrangements for preheating or cooling the charge -- F27B 9/12 refers to mechanically-driven furnaces, such as tunnel-type ovens, that incorporate special provisions for preheating or cooling the material being processed. The subclass addresses design features that control the temperature of the charge before, during, or after its passage through the furnace.
[F27B 9/14] characterised by the path of the charge during treatment -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Furnaces for treating continuous lengths of work -> F27B9/28
[F27B 9/142] the charge moving along a vertical axis -- A furnace in which the material to be processed is transported mechanically along a vertical path, typical of tunnel-type designs, is covered by this subclass. It includes arrangements for moving the charge upward or downward within the heating zone, such as lift screws, belts or other vertical conveyance devices.
[F27B 9/145] the charge moving along a serpentine path -- F27B 9/145 pertains to furnaces in which the charge is conveyed along a serpentine path, often implemented in tunnel-type designs. This mechanical movement of the material through the heating zone provides continuous processing and enhanced temperature uniformity.
[F27B 9/147] the charge moving on an inclined floor -- This subclass covers furnace designs where the solid charge is conveyed along an inclined floor, using gravity or mechanical assistance, to move the material through the heating zone, typical of tunnel-type kilns and continuous-process ovens.
[F27B 9/16] the charge moving in a circular or arcuate path -- F27B 9/16 covers furnace designs where the material being heated is conveyed along a circular or curved trajectory, typically in rotary or arc-shaped hearths. This arrangement allows continuous processing and uniform heating in tunnel-type or rotary kilns.
[F27B 9/18] under the action of scrapers or pushers -- The subclass F27B 9/18 pertains to furnaces in which the charge is conveyed mechanically by means such as scrapers or pushers, typical of tunnel-type furnaces that move material continuously through the heating zone. It falls under the broader group for furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts where the charge is mechanically transferred.
[F27B 9/185] multiple hearth type furnaces -- Multiple-hearth furnaces are mechanically fed furnaces in which the charge passes sequentially over a series of hearths, each providing a distinct heating zone. The arrangement allows continuous processing as material moves by gravity or conveyors through the multiple levels, enabling controlled temperature profiles and efficient heat utilization. This type is classified under mechanically moved charge furnaces within the broader category of furnaces, kilns, ovens and retorts.
[F27B 9/20] the charge moving in a substantially straight path -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Furnaces through which the charge is moved mechanically, e.g. tunnel furnaces.
[F27B 9/201] walking beam furnace -- A walking beam furnace is a tunnel-type furnace in which the material charge is advanced mechanically by a reciprocating beam that lifts and shifts the load through successive heating zones. This arrangement provides continuous processing, uniform temperature exposure, and efficient handling of bulk solids or ore during heat treatment.
[F27B 9/202] Conveyor mechanisms therefor -- F27B 9/202 covers conveyor mechanisms used in furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts where the material (charge) is moved mechanically through the heating zone, such as in tunnel-type furnaces. It includes devices that transport, feed, or withdraw the charge during processing, encompassing rollers, belts, chains, screws, or other moving elements integrated with the furnace structure.
[F27B 9/203] having ramps  F27B9/206  takes precedence
[F27B 9/205] having excentrics or lever arms  F27B9/206  takes precedence -- This CPC subclass F27B 9/205 covers furnaces in which the charge is moved mechanically by excentric or lever arm mechanisms. It falls under mechanical engineering, furnaces, and general furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts, specifically tunnel-type furnaces. The later subclass F27B 9/206 supersedes it.
[F27B 9/206] consisting of a single central beam -- F27B 9/206 defines tunnel-type furnaces in which the charge is moved mechanically and the entire structure is supported by a single central beam that serves as the main spine for the furnace envelope and transport mechanisms, allowing uniform heat distribution and efficient material handling.
[F27B 9/207] consisting of two or more conveyors -- F27B 9/207 classifies furnaces in which the material charge is moved mechanically by using two or more conveyors, typical of tunnel-type heating units that transport the workpiece continuously through the furnace.
[F27B 9/208] the workpieces being rotated during their advance -- F27B 9/208 classifies furnace designs in which the workpieces are rotated while being conveyed forward through the heating zone, a feature used in tunnel-type continuous furnaces to achieve uniform heating and improved surface treatment.
[F27B 9/22] under the action of scrapers or pushers -- F27B 9/22 refers to furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts in which the charge is moved mechanically, typically of tunnel type, and where scrapers or pushers are employed to advance the material through the heating zone.
[F27B 9/222] the path comprising a section specially adapted for effecting equalisation of the temperature of the charge -- F27B 9/222 covers a segment of a mechanically driven furnace, such as a tunnel furnace, that incorporates a specially designed zone to equalise the temperature of the moving charge, ensuring uniform heating throughout the process.
[F27B 9/225] the charge being subjected to an additional manipulation along the path -- F27B 9/225 covers mechanical furnaces, such as tunnel-type units, in which the material being processed is not only conveyed but also subjected to further manipulation-e.g., mixing, turning, or selective heating-while moving through the furnace.
[F27B 9/227] with rotation of the charge  F27B9/147  takes precedence
[F27B 9/24] being carried by a conveyor -- F27B 9/24 covers furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts where the material being processed is transported mechanically through the heating zone, typically in tunnel-type designs. The entry specifically addresses systems that use conveyors or similar moving devices to convey the charge continuously during heating.
[F27B 9/2407] the conveyor being constituted by rollers (roller hearth furnace) -- F27B 9/2407 designates furnaces in which the material to be processed is advanced mechanically by a conveyor formed of rollers, commonly referred to as roller-hearth furnaces. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering, specifically furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts, and relates to tunnel-type heating units where the charge is moved continuously along a rolled hearth.
[F27B 9/2415] the charge rotating about an axis transversal to the axis of advancement of the charge -- This CPC subgroup covers furnaces, such as tunnel-type units, where the material being processed is rotated around an axis perpendicular to its forward movement. The design enables mechanical conveyance of the charge while it spins, improving heat transfer and mixing.
[F27B 9/2423] the charge rotating about an axis parallel to the axis of advancement of the charge -- A tunnel-type furnace in which the material (charge) is conveyed while rotating around an axis that runs parallel to the direction of its forward movement, allowing continuous processing of the charge as it advances through the furnace.
[F27B 9/243] Endless-strand conveyor -- F27B 9/243 designates endless-strand conveyors used in tunnel-type furnaces where the charge is moved mechanically through the heating zone. The subclass covers belt, roller or chain loops that form a continuous loop to feed, heat and discharge material in a furnace, kiln, oven or retort.
[F27B 9/2453] Vibrating conveyor (shaker hearth furnace) -- F27B 9/2453 covers vibrating conveyor mechanisms used in shaker hearth furnaces where the material charge is moved mechanically through a tunnel-type furnace. The technology combines a vibrating or oscillating conveyor with heating zones to transport and process workpieces continuously.
[F27B 9/2461] the charge being suspended from the conveyor -- The CPC subclass F27B 9/2461 relates to furnace designs in which the charge is suspended from a moving conveyor, typical of tunnel-type kilns or ovens. It covers mechanisms that keep the material airborne during transport and heating, allowing continuous processing and uniform temperature exposure.
[F27B 9/2469] the conveyor being constituted by rollable bodies -- F27B 9/2469 covers conveyors in mechanically fed furnaces, such as tunnel-type kilns, where the charge is transported by a series of rollable bodies that move along the furnace interior. This entry specifies the design and operation of roll-based conveyor systems that continuously advance material through the heating zone.
[F27B 9/2476] the conveyor being constituted by air cushion -- This entry covers furnaces in which the material is moved on a conveyor that uses an air-cushion to levitate or support the charge, reducing friction and enabling smooth transport through the heating zone, typical of tunnel-type furnaces where the charge is mechanically conveyed.
[F27B 9/26] on or in trucks, sleds, or containers -- F27B 9/26 concerns tunnel-type furnaces in which the charge is moved mechanically through the heating zone by means such as trucks, sleds or containers, addressing the loading, transport and retrieval of material inside the furnace.
[F27B 9/262] on or in trucks -- F27B 9/262 refers to mobile tunnel-type furnaces whose charge is conveyed mechanically, installed on or in trucks for on-site heating, sintering, or material treatment. The classification covers the complete furnace arrangement, drive mechanisms and control systems adapted to vehicle mounting.
[F27B 9/28] for treating continuous lengths of work -- F27B 9/28 covers furnace designs where the charge is moved mechanically through the heating zone, exemplified by tunnel-type furnaces used to treat continuous lengths of work such as strips, sheets or other elongated pieces.
[F27B 9/30] Details, accessories or equipment specially adapted for furnaces of these types -- This subclass covers specialized components, devices and auxiliary apparatus designed specifically for furnaces where the material is moved mechanically, such as tunnel furnaces, including feeding mechanisms, sealing devices and temperature control accessories.
[F27B 9/3005] arrangements for circulating gases -- F27B 9/3005 covers designs that direct and recirculate combustion or process gases within mechanically-driven furnaces, such as tunnel-type kilns. It includes ducts, blowers, baffles and related devices that create controlled gas flow to improve heating uniformity and overall efficiency.
[F27B 9/3011] arrangements for circulating gases transversally -- F27B 9/3011 covers specific designs for moving gases across the width of a furnace whose charge is mechanically conveyed, such as tunnel-type kilns or ovens. It details transverse gas-circulation arrangements that improve heat distribution and process efficiency in these continuously fed heating units.
[F27B 9/3044] Furnace regenerators -- F27B 9/3044 covers furnace regenerators that use a mechanically driven charge movement, such as tunnel-type furnaces where material is continuously conveyed through the heating zone, enabling heat recovery and improved thermal efficiency.
[F27B 9/3077] Arrangements for treating electronic components, e.g. semiconductors -- F27B 9/3077 relates to furnace arrangements designed to treat electronic components such as semiconductors, where the material is conveyed through the heating zone by a mechanical transport system, often of tunnel type. The subclass addresses the integration of handling, heating, and processing equipment to ensure precise thermal treatment of delicate devices.
[F27B 9/32] Casings -- F27B 9/32 Casings refers to the outer enclosure or shell of mechanically fed furnaces such as tunnel-type ovens, which contains the heating zone and may provide insulation, cooling or removable sections for access and maintenance. The classification covers designs, materials and construction features of these protective structures used to contain and support the moving charge.
[F27B 9/34] Arrangements of linings -- F27B 9/34 covers the design and configuration of internal linings in mechanically fed furnaces such as tunnel-type kilns and continuous ovens. It includes the placement, shaping and attachment of refractory or protective layers that line the furnace chamber to withstand heat, chemical attack and mechanical wear while facilitating charge movement.
[F27B 9/36] Arrangements of heating devices -- The subclass covers the layout and integration of heating elements in furnaces where the material is conveyed mechanically, such as tunnel, conveyor-belt or rotary kilns. It includes the positioning, arrangement and interconnection of burners, electrodes or other heat sources to provide uniform temperature control during continuous processing.
[F27B 9/38] Arrangements of devices for charging -- F27B 9/38 covers the design and layout of mechanisms that introduce material into a furnace, kiln, oven or retort where the charge is moved by mechanical means, such as conveyor or tunnel systems. It includes the arrangement of feeders, hoppers, rollers, belts or other devices that position and transport the charge into the heating chamber.
[F27B 9/39] Arrangements of devices for discharging -- F27B 9/39 pertains to the layout of mechanisms that remove material from furnaces whose charge is moved mechanically, such as tunnel-type ovens. It includes the design of conveyors, augers, or other discharge devices that transport the processed product out of the heating chamber.
[F27B 9/40] Arrangements of controlling or monitoring devices -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Arrangement of controlling or monitoring devices
[F27D] DETAILS OR ACCESSORIES OF FURNACES, KILNS, OVENS OR RETORTS, IN SO FAR AS THEY ARE OF KINDS OCCURRING IN MORE THAN ONE KIND OF FURNACE -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Details, accessories of furnaces, kilns or ovens in general, not for specific furnaces, kilns or ovens.
[F27D 1/00] Casings -- Casings are structural components that enclose the heating chamber of furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts, providing physical support, insulation and protection against external influences. Designed for use in more than one furnace type, they form the outer shell that retains heat, safeguards the process environment and contributes to overall safety.
[F27D 1/0003] Linings or walls -- F27D 1/0003 covers the linings or walls that form the interior surface of furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts, where the same type of lining is used in more than one kind of furnace. It pertains to the design, material selection and construction of these protective interior structures.
[F27D 1/0006] Linings or walls formed from bricks or layers with a particular composition or specific characteristics -- F27D 1/0006 covers the design and construction of furnace or kiln linings and walls that are built from bricks or layered materials having a defined composition or special properties, such as refractory performance, thermal conductivity or chemical resistance, and that are used in more than one type of furnace.
[F27D 1/0009] Comprising ceramic fibre elements -- The code F27D 1/0009 designates furnace casings that incorporate ceramic fibre elements. Such casings serve as insulating or protective accessories for a variety of furnaces, kilns, ovens and retorts, and the classification covers any enclosure component where ceramic fibre forms part of the structure.
[F27D 1/0013] the fibre elements being in the form of a folded blanket or a juxtaposition of folded blankets -- F27D 1/0013 pertains to furnace casings in which the insulating fibre material is provided as a folded blanket or as several folded blankets positioned side by side, serving as thermal protection for furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts.
[F27D 1/0016] Interleaved multiple folded blankets -- The entry covers furnace casings that use an interleaved arrangement of multiple folded insulation blankets, a detail common to several furnace, kiln, oven or retort designs. It pertains to thermal-protective liners built from layered, folded blanket material to enhance heat retention and protect the furnace shell.
[F27D 1/002] the fibre elements being composed of adjacent separate strips -- This subgroup concerns furnace casings whose fibre elements are formed from adjacent separate strips rather than a single continuous material. It applies to furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts that incorporate such strip-based fibre construction.
[F27D 1/0023] Linings or walls comprising expansion joints or means to restrain expansion due to thermic flows -- F27D 1/0023 refers to furnace, kiln, oven or retort linings or walls that incorporate expansion joints or other devices to control thermal expansion, preventing damage from temperature-induced movements in the structure.
[F27D 1/0026] the expansion joint being a resilient element, e.g. a metallic plate between two bricks -- This CPC entry (F27D 1/0026) covers resilient expansion joints used in furnace casings, such as metallic plates positioned between bricks to accommodate thermal movement. It pertains to details or accessories of furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts that are common to more than one type of furnace.
[F27D 1/003] Linings or walls comprising porous bricks -- This subgroup deals with furnace, kiln or oven linings where the wall or protective layer is formed from porous refractory bricks, allowing gas flow and thermal-shock resistance while protecting the structure. It covers designs where such bricks constitute the lining material in various furnace types.
[F27D 1/0033] Linings or walls comprising heat shields, e.g. heat shields -- F27D 1/0033 covers linings or walls that incorporate heat-shield elements in furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts. It applies to protective casings that use separate heat-shield components to guard the furnace structure from high temperatures. The code falls under mechanical engineering, furnaces, and accessories common to multiple furnace types.
[F27D 1/0036] Linings or walls comprising means for supporting electric resistances in the furnace -- This CPC entry (F27D 1/0036) relates to furnace linings or walls that include built-in means for supporting electric resistances used for heating. It addresses accessories common to more than one type of furnace, kiln, oven or retort. The classification falls under mechanical engineering, furnaces, and details or accessories of furnaces.
[F27D 1/004] Linings or walls comprising means for securing bricks -- F27D 1/004 pertains to furnace casings where the lining or wall incorporates devices that secure the bricks in place, such as anchors, clamps or interlocking systems. It applies to linings used in any type of furnace, kiln, oven or retort where brick construction is employed.
[F27D 1/0043] Floors, hearths -- Floors and hearths are the horizontal or slightly inclined surfaces that form the base inside a furnace, kiln, oven or retort, supporting the load and providing a heat-conducting platform for processing material. They are typically made of refractory bricks, castables or metal plates and may incorporate insulation, drainage or anchoring features to improve temperature uniformity, protect the furnace shell and facilitate removal of molten or solid residues.
[F27D 1/02] Crowns -- The CPC subclass F27D 1/02 refers to crowns, the upper casings that close the opening of a furnace, kiln, oven or retort and provide structural support and heat sealing. These components are used in various furnace types and are considered accessories common to more than one kind of furnace.
[F27D 1/021] Suspended roofs -- Suspended roofs are overhead structures that support the covering of a furnace, kiln, oven or retort while being held from the furnace walls or frame rather than directly on the base. They provide a removable protective barrier and facilitate heat distribution, access for maintenance, and insulation across various furnace types.
[F27D 1/022] Parts thereof, e.g. noses, arches -- F27D 1/022 covers components of furnace casings, including elements like noses and arches, that are used across multiple furnace, kiln, oven or retort designs. The classification falls under mechanical engineering, furnaces, and details or accessories common to more than one kind of furnace.
[F27D 1/024] having an anchored layer of lining -- The code F27D 1/024 designates furnace casings that incorporate an anchored layer of lining to secure the refractory material to the furnace shell. This feature is used as a detail or accessory applicable to many furnace, kiln, oven or retort designs, improving durability and thermal performance.
[F27D 1/025] Roofs supported around their periphery, e.g. arched roofs -- F27D 1/025 classifies furnace casings whose roof is supported around the entire perimeter, such as arched or curved roofs, serving as a structural element for furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts that share this roof type.
[F27D 1/027] Skew backs -- Skew backs are angled rear sections of furnace casings that direct the flow of hot gases, simplify loading of material and help reduce thermal stresses; they are employed in a variety of furnace, kiln, oven and retort designs.
[F27D 1/028] Means to prevent deformation of the arch -- F27D 1/028 covers devices incorporated in furnace casings that keep the arch section from sagging or losing its shape during operation. The means may include reinforcement, stays, or support structures that maintain dimensional stability of the arch in kilns, ovens, retorts or similar furnaces.
[F27D 1/04] characterised by the form of the bricks or blocks used -- F27D 1/04 classifies furnace casings that are distinguished by the shape of the bricks or blocks that form them, covering designs where the form of the masonry components is the defining feature of the enclosure. This falls under details or accessories of furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts that are used in more than one type of furnace.
[F27D 1/042] Bricks shaped for use in regenerators -- The CPC subclass F27D 1/042 covers specially shaped bricks designed for incorporation into regenerators, which are heat-exchange components of furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts. These bricks are engineered to provide high surface area and durability, enhancing thermal recovery and efficiency across various furnace types.
[F27D 1/045] Bricks for lining cylindrical bodies, e.g. skids, tubes -- This CPC subgroup covers refractory bricks employed as linings for cylindrical components such as skids, tubes or other round bodies in furnaces, kilns, ovens and retorts. The bricks protect interior surfaces from high temperatures, slag or chemical wear and are used where the same type of brick is applicable to multiple furnace designs.
[F27D 1/06] Composite bricks or blocks -- Composite bricks or blocks are multicomponent casings employed in furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts, serving as structural or insulating elements and used across several furnace designs. They typically combine refractory materials with binders or other constituents to achieve desired thermal and mechanical performance.
[F27D 1/063] Individual composite bricks or blocks -- F27D 1/063 covers individual composite bricks or blocks employed as casings for furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts, where the components combine different materials to form a single structural element. These elements serve as interchangeable or permanent lining pieces that improve thermal resistance, durability or insulation across multiple furnace types.
[F27D 1/066] Individual composite bricks or blocks made from hollow bricks filled up with another material -- Individual composite bricks or blocks are furnace casings formed by filling hollow bricks with a secondary material, such as insulating granules or refractory mixes, to enhance thermal resistance and mechanical strength. They are employed as lining components in a variety of furnace, kiln, oven or retort designs where a single, solid brick would be less effective.
[F27D 1/08] Bricks or blocks with internal reinforcement or metal backing -- It covers furnace casings made from bricks or blocks that incorporate internal reinforcement such as metal mesh or backing, providing added strength and structural integrity. The definition applies to any furnace, kiln, oven or retort where such reinforced masonry is used as a protective enclosure.
[F27D 1/10] Monolithic linings -- supports are classified in F27D1/14
[F27D 11/00] Arrangement of elements for electric heating in or on furnaces -- F27D 11/00 covers the layout and placement of electric heating elements that are incorporated into or mounted on furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts, focusing on designs applicable to multiple furnace types. It addresses how the heating elements are arranged to provide uniform heat distribution and efficient operation across various furnace configurations.
[F27D 11/02] Ohmic resistance heating -- F27D 11/02 covers the arrangement of electric heating elements that use ohmic resistance in furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts, describing how the elements are positioned within or on the heating chamber to provide uniform heat.
[F27D 11/04] with direct passage of current through the material being heated -- F27D 11/04 covers furnace designs where electric heating is achieved by passing the electric current directly through the material being heated, rather than using external heating elements. It relates to the arrangement of electrodes or conductive passages that allow the workpiece or charge to serve as the heating resistor within various types of furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts.
[F27D 11/06] Induction heating, i.e. in which the material being heated, or its container or elements embodied therein, form the secondary of a transformer -- This classification covers the arrangement of electric heating components in or on furnaces that use induction heating, where the material being heated, its container, or embedded elements act as the secondary of a transformer. It includes designs of coils, magnetic circuits and placement strategies that generate heat directly in the workpiece or vessel.
[F27D 11/08] Heating by electric discharge, e.g. arc discharge -- F27D 11/08 relates to heating furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts by means of an electric discharge such as an arc, and covers the arrangement of the heating elements that are placed in or on the furnace. This subgroup falls under details or accessories of furnaces that occur in more than one type of furnace.
[F27D 11/10] Disposition of electrodes -- F27D 11/10 concerns the layout and positioning of electrodes and related electric-heating elements within or on furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts. It addresses how these components are arranged to provide effective heating across different furnace types.
[F27D 11/12] with electromagnetic fields acting directly on the material being heated -- The subclass deals with furnace designs in which electric heating elements are arranged in or on the furnace so that the electromagnetic fields they produce act directly on the material being heated, rather than through a separate heat-transfer medium. It covers the layout of these direct-action heating systems.
[F27D 1/12] incorporating cooling arrangements -- This subclass covers furnace casings that incorporate integrated cooling systems to protect the structure and control temperature, and it applies to a range of furnace, kiln, oven or retort types. It includes arrangements such as built-in cooling channels, jackets or external water or air circuits that are part of the furnace envelope.
[F27D 1/14] Supports for linings -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Supports for monolithic linings -> F27D1/10
[F27D 1/141] Anchors therefor -- F27D 1/141 defines anchoring devices that secure the casings of furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts and are suitable for use with more than one type of furnace. These components belong to the mechanical-engineering group for furnace accessories and ensure the structural stability of the furnace shell.
[F27D 1/142] Anchors made from ceramic material -- Ceramic anchors are components used to secure or position parts within furnace, kiln, oven or retort casings, acting as fasteners that hold refractory elements in place. Made from ceramic material, they tolerate the high temperatures and harsh chemical environments encountered in a wide range of furnace types.
[F27D 1/144] Supports for ceramic fibre materials -- F27D 1/144 covers devices that hold or secure ceramic fibre insulation within furnace casings. These supports ensure the refractory material remains in place under high-temperature conditions, improving structural stability and heat-retention performance. The range applies to any furnace type where such fibre liners are used.
[F27D 1/145] Assembling elements -- F27D 1/145 covers the assembly components used to join or fasten furnace casings and similar housing parts. It relates to the details or accessories of furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts that are common to more than one type of furnace, focusing on how the casing elements are connected to form a complete enclosure.
[F27D 1/147] for bricks -- F27D 1/147 designates brick casings that form the protective or insulating lining of furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts. It covers bricks used as inner or outer shells, or structural panels, employed across multiple furnace types to contain heat, protect the vessel and improve thermal efficiency.
[F27D 1/148] Means to suspend bricks -- The CPC subclass F27D 1/148 covers devices or structures used to hang or support bricks inside furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts, enabling brick suspension for better heat distribution and maintenance. It falls under details or accessories of furnaces that apply to multiple furnace types.
[F27D 1/16] Making or repairing linings -- F27D 1/16 relates to the making or repairing of furnace linings, specifically the casings that protect the interior of furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts. It covers methods for installing, renewing or restoring refractory linings to preserve heat resistance and structural integrity.
[F27D 1/1621] Making linings by using shaped elements, e.g. bricks -- This group deals with the construction of furnace or oven linings by installing pre-shaped refractory elements such as bricks, blocks or panels. It includes methods of arranging, securing and joining these shaped parts to form a continuous protective lining in casings that are used across different furnace types.
[F27D 1/1626] Making linings by compacting a refractory mass in the space defined by a backing mould or pattern and the furnace wall -- This subcategory deals with methods of forming furnace casings by compacting a refractory mass between a backing mould or pattern and the furnace wall, creating a solid lining in situ. It applies to furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts where the lining is produced by this compaction technique.
[F27D 1/1636] Repairing linings by projecting or spraying refractory materials on the lining -- The classification covers methods for restoring furnace or oven interiors where the refractory lining is rebuilt by projecting or spraying material onto the damaged area, allowing in-situ repair without removal of the whole lining. It applies to all furnace types where such spray techniques are used.
[F27D 1/1642] using a gunning apparatus -- This CPC subclass covers the use of a gunning apparatus to apply refractory or protective material to the casings or internal linings of furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts. It includes methods of installing, repairing or refurbishing such furnace accessories by gun-spraying or similar techniques.
[F27D 1/1647] the projected materials being partly melted, e.g. by exothermic reactions of metals (Al, Si) with oxygen -- The subclass relates to furnace casings in which the material fed into the furnace is partly melted by exothermic reactions of metals such as aluminum or silicon with oxygen, a technique applicable to multiple furnace types.
[F27D 1/1652] Flame guniting; Use of a fuel -- F27D 1/1652 deals with flame guniting, the method of feeding a molten refractory or similar material onto furnace walls, kilns or retorts by means of a fuel-driven flame that melts and sprays the material to form a protective lining. The code covers the selection and use of the fuel that powers the guniting torch, as well as related equipment and procedures.
[F27D 1/1668] Plasma guniting -- F27D 1/1668 covers the use of plasma guniting to form or repair the casings of furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts. It pertains to the application of a plasma-produced spray-on coating that builds up protective layers on the inner or outer furnace shell, providing thermal resistance and corrosion protection across different furnace types.
[F27D 1/1673] applied centrifugally -- F27D 1/1673 pertains to furnace, kiln, oven or retort casings that are produced by applying centrifugal force during manufacturing, such as spin-formed shells used as accessories across multiple furnace types.
[F27D 1/1678] Increasing the durability of linings; Means for protecting -- F27D 1/1678 covers technologies that improve the lifespan of furnace, kiln or oven linings, including protective coatings, refractory additions, liners, or other means that shield the interior surface from wear, corrosion or thermal damage, thereby extending service life and maintaining performance.
[F27D 1/1684] by a special coating applied to the lining -- This subclass covers furnaces whose lining is treated with a special coating applied to the casing. The coating provides protective or functional benefits such as corrosion resistance or thermal insulation and can be used across a range of furnace, kiln, oven or retort types.
[F27D 1/1694] Breaking away the lining or removing parts thereof -- F27D 1/1694 relates to devices or procedures for breaking away or removing the lining or portions of a furnace, kiln, oven or retort, covering any type of furnace where such dismantling or repair work is required.
[F27D 1/18] Door frames -- F27D 1/18 pertains to door frames that form the structural support and mounting interface for doors on furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts, providing a sealing boundary and being usable across multiple furnace types.
[F27D 1/1808] Removable covers -- This subclass covers removable covers used on furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts, designed to be detached for access, maintenance or loading and applicable to any furnace type where such covers are employed. It includes hinged, sliding, bolted or otherwise detachable protective enclosures.
[F27D 1/1816] Removable covers specially adapted for arc furnaces -- This CPC subclass covers removable protective covers that are specially designed for arc furnaces, allowing quick removal for maintenance or operation while shielding against heat and arc radiation. The covers function as a type of furnace casing accessory and are applicable to several furnace configurations.
[F27D 1/1858] Doors -- F27D 1/1858 covers doors that serve as casings for furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts, focusing on door designs used in more than one type of furnace. These doors are accessories that provide access, sealing and operational control across various thermal processing equipment.
[F27D 1/1866] Door-frames -- F27D 1/1866 covers door-frames used as part of the casing of furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts. The classification includes the structural members that support and guide the doors, provide alignment and sealing, and are applicable to any type of furnace where such frames are employed.
[F27D 13/00] Apparatus for preheating charges -- F27D 13/00 refers to apparatus that preheats material before it enters a furnace, kiln, oven or retort. Such devices include heating chambers, conveyors, burners or other means that raise the temperature of charges to improve efficiency and quality of the subsequent heating process.
[F27D 13/002] Preheating scrap -- F27D 13/002 covers apparatus that preheat scrap metal before it is fed into a furnace, including burners, conveyors or heating chambers designed for use with multiple furnace types.
[F27D 13/005] Drying of green clay prior to baking -- F27D 13/005 covers equipment used to preheat or dry green clay before it is baked, such as conveyors, rollers, or drying chambers that heat the clay charge prior to entering a furnace or kiln. The classification lies under mechanical engineering, furnaces, and accessories that are applicable to more than one type of furnace, focusing on the preparation stage of ceramic production.
[F27D 15/00] Handling or treating discharged material -- F27D 15/00 covers devices and methods for handling or treating material that has been discharged from furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts. It includes accessories used to move, cool, store or further process the material after it leaves the heating chamber.
[F27D 15/02] Cooling -- The code covers devices and methods for cooling materials that have been discharged from furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts, including heat exchangers, water sprays, air cooling systems and related accessories that operate across multiple furnace types.
[F27D 15/0206] with means to convey the charge -- F27D 15/0206 denotes furnace, kiln, oven or retort accessories that include devices for moving the material being processed (the charge) through the equipment. It covers conveyance means such as belts, screws or rollers, applicable to any type of furnace where the charge must be transferred or positioned.
[F27D 15/0213] comprising a cooling grate -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Cooling of discharged material comprising a cooling grate.
[F27D 15/022] grate plates -- F27D 15/022 covers grate plates used in furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts for handling or treating material that has been discharged from the heating chamber. These plates form part of the furnace grate system that supports and distributes the load of combustion material across the firebox.
[F27D 15/0266] on an endless belt -- F27D 15/0266 refers to devices that handle or treat material discharged from furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts by moving it on a continuously running belt. It covers belt-based conveyance or processing systems used as accessories for various types of heat-treatment equipment.
[F27D 15/0273] on a rotary hearth -- F27D 15/0273 designates equipment for handling or treating material discharged from a furnace that is placed on a rotary hearth, an accessory usable with various types of furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts. It falls under the mechanical-engineering group for furnace details and accessories and addresses the specific method of moving or processing spent material on a rotating platform.
[F27D 15/028] comprising a rotary drum -- This subclass covers furnace accessories that handle or treat material discharged from the furnace, kiln, oven or retort, where the device includes a rotary drum. The rotary drum is used to convey, blend, cool or otherwise process the material after it leaves the heating chamber. It applies to any furnace type where such a rotary drum is employed.
[F27D 15/0286] in a vertical, e.g. annular, shaft -- The code covers arrangements in which material discharged from a furnace is handled or treated within a vertical, often annular, shaft that serves as a conduit or processing zone. Such vertical shafts are used as accessories common to several furnace types for moving, cooling, or further processing the out-flowing material.
[F27D 17/00] Arrangements for using waste heat -- Group F27D17/00 is impacted by reclassification into groups F27D17/20 , F27D17/22 , F27D17/25 and F27D17/28 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[F27D 17/10] Arrangements for using waste heat -- Group F27D17/10 is impacted by reclassification into groups F27D17/12 , F27D17/15 , F27D17/17 and F27D17/18 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[F27D 17/102] including pyrolising the waste gases -- The subclass F27D 17/102 covers accessories and arrangements that recover waste heat from furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts, specifically those usable across several furnace types, and includes systems that pyrolise waste gases to generate useful energy.
[F27D 17/12] using heat storage -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates a waste heat recovery apparatus where exhaust gas from cupola (10) passes through heat exchanger (32) to heat fluid stored in tank (36). The heat is subsequently utilised at heat exchanger (38).
[F27D 17/13] using regenerative heat exchangers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates an exhaust processing and heat recovery system with a regenerative heat exchanger (250), of the rotor type, which stores heat from flue gas (FG1) and then provides that heat to incoming air (A1). Exhaust air streams (A3, B2) use waste heat from furnace (26) to provide heat for other purposes, e.g. coal mill drying and grinding operations, site heating and others.
[F27D 17/15] using boilers -- Group F27D17/15 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group F27D17/10 . Groups F27D17/10 and F27D17/15 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[F27D 17/17] for preheating fluids, e.g. air or gases -- Group F27D17/17 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group F27D17/10 . Groups F27D17/10 and F27D17/17 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[F27D 17/18] for preheating solid materials -- Group F27D17/18 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group F27D17/10 . Groups F27D17/10 and F27D17/18 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[F27D 17/20] Arrangements for treatment or cleaning of waste gases -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative examples of subject matter classified in this place:  1.  Figure 1 illustrates a plurality of nozzles (30) in the flue of furnace (12) which inject ammonia into the exhaust gas to treat NO x emissions.  2.  Figure 2 illustrates a plasma torch (118) at chamber (116) for pyrolysing volatile components in the exhaust gas.
[F27D 17/22] for removing solid constituents -- Groups F27D17/22 and F27D17/25 are incomplete pending reclassification of documents from groups F27D17/00 and F27D17/20 . All groups listed in this warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[F27D 17/25] using cyclones -- It covers furnace accessories that recover waste heat by means of cyclones, using the cyclone's ability to separate hot gases for recirculation or for heating other processes, and is applicable to a range of furnace, kiln or oven types.
[F27D 17/28] for cooling waste gases -- Group F27D17/28 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from groups F27D17/00 and F27D17/20 . Groups F27D17/00 , F27D17/20 and F27D17/28 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[F27D 17/30] Arrangements for extraction or collection of waste gases -- F27D 17/30 covers systems and methods for extracting or collecting waste gases generated by furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts, regardless of furnace type. It includes ducts, fans, filters, condensers and other arrangements that capture, direct or treat exhaust gases to reduce emissions or recover heat. The focus is on the overall configuration that enables removal of gaseous by-products from the heating chamber.
[F27D 17/302] Constructional details of ancillary components, e.g. waste gas conduits or seals -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates a pipe curtain (20) for confining pollutants emitted from the furnace trough (12).
[F27D 17/304] specially adapted for electric arc furnaces -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates an electric arc furnace with an exhaust nozzle (19) integrated into the lid also supporting electrodes (17, 18).
[F27D 19/00] Arrangements of controlling devices -- This subclass covers the overall layout and interconnection of control mechanisms-such as thermostats, pressure switches, timers, safety devices and actuators-used to regulate temperature, pressure or atmosphere in furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts, irrespective of the specific furnace type.
[F27D 2001/0046] Means to facilitate repair or replacement or prevent quick wearing -- This subclass covers features built into a product that make it easy to disassemble, replace parts or carry out repairs, and includes measures that slow or stop rapid wear. It includes protective coatings, wear-resistant inserts, modular connections and other design elements that extend the item's service life.
[F27D 2001/005] Removable part or structure with replaceable elements -- F27D 2001/005 pertains to removable parts or structures that incorporate replaceable elements, such as components of rotating machinery that can be detached and exchanged for maintenance, repair or upgrade. The subclass covers designs where individual items are interchangeable without requiring disassembly of the whole system.
[F27D 2001/0053] Furnace constructed in modules -- F27D 2001/0053 covers furnaces that are built from separate, interchangeable modules rather than as a single monolithic structure. The modular construction enables easy assembly, reconfiguration, transport and maintenance, and is applied in processes such as heat treatment, melting or drying.
[F27D 2001/0056] Aspects of construction intended to prevent wear -- F27D 2001/0056 refers to features of a device's construction that are designed to reduce or avoid wear during operation, such as protective coatings, surface hardening, lubrication channels, sacrificial elements or geometric adaptations that limit friction and material loss. It covers any engineering measures that help extend component life.
[F27D 2001/0059] Construction elements of a furnace -- The classification F27D 2001/0059 pertains to the structural components that make up a furnace, including walls, doors, liners, supports, burners and related elements that define the furnace's physical configuration and containment of heat.
[F27D 2001/0063] Means to strengthen a part -- F27D 2001/0063 is a CPC sub-group that classifies devices or structural features added to a machine component to increase its rigidity or load-bearing capacity. It covers ribs, braces, gussets, reinforcing plates and similar means that strengthen a part, regardless of the material used.
[F27D 2001/0066] Movable or removable parts -- F27D 2001/0066 relates to the design and features of movable or removable components of turbines and their generators, such as rotors, blades, shafts, bearings and casings that can be taken out for inspection, replacement or adjustment. The entry covers mounting and release mechanisms that allow these parts to be detached without disassembling the whole machine.
[F27D 2001/0069] Means to prevent heat conduction -- This CPC subgroup covers devices and methods incorporated in machines or installations that restrict or interrupt the transfer of heat between components, such as thermal insulation, heat shields, and cooling jackets, used to improve efficiency, protect temperature-sensitive parts, or prevent overheating.
[F27D 2001/0073] Surrounding protection around the furnace, e.g. covers, circulation of gas -- F27D 2001/0073 pertains to protective systems that surround a furnace, such as removable or fixed covers and arrangements that circulate gas around the heating chamber. The aim is to shield the furnace from external influences, enhance safety, contain heat, and improve energy efficiency.
[F27D 2001/0076] comprising a corrugated sheet -- F27D 2001/0076 covers fans, blowers or pneumatic devices that incorporate a corrugated sheet as part of the impeller, casing or flow channel. The corrugated sheet is employed to add structural strength, guide airflow or damp vibrations within the device.
[F27D 2001/0079] Means to assemble at least two parts of a furnace or of any device or accessory associated to its use -- The entry covers devices or components that join together two or more parts of a furnace, or any related equipment or accessory, enabling their assembly. This includes clamps, brackets, couplings, fasteners or other means that mechanically or otherwise secure the parts for use.
[F27D 2001/0083] Means to assemble a moving part to a fixed one -- F27D 2001/0083 designates the mechanical elements used to connect a moving component to a stationary part in pump constructions. It covers devices such as flanges, couplings, sleeves, fasteners or other joining means that secure shafts, impellers or other rotating or reciprocating elements to the pump housing or frame.
[F27D 2001/0086] the moving part being in rotation -- The code refers to devices whose operative element turns relative to the rest of the machine, such as rotors, impellers or other components that execute a rotary motion to perform mixing, separating, transporting or similar functions. It covers any arrangement where the moving part is in rotation, regardless of the specific application or power source.
[F27D 2001/0089] the moving part being in translation -- F27D classifies pumps, compressors and similar machines in which the working element moves linearly rather than rotating. Code 2001/0089 specifically denotes a moving part that is in translation, such as pistons, plungers or reciprocating diaphragms.
[F27D 2001/0093] the moving part being hinged to the fixed one -- The code F27D 2001/0093 covers arrangements where a movable element is attached to a stationary element by a hinge, allowing rotation around a fixed axis. It applies to doors, lids, panels, covers and similar structures in machines, vehicles, buildings or equipment where the hinged connection is integral to the function.
[F27D 2001/0096] the moving part staying in the same plane -- This group covers pumps whose moving element remains confined to a single plane during operation, such as pistons, diaphragms or other reciprocating parts that slide or swing without leaving the same geometric plane.
[F27D 2001/047] Lining of cylindrical vessels -- The CPC subclass F27D 2001/047 covers methods and materials for applying an interior lining to cylindrical vessels such as tanks, pipes, drums or reactors. It includes protective, corrosion-resistant, wear-resistant or insulating layers applied to the inner surface to improve durability, chemical compatibility or thermal performance.
[F27D 2001/1605] Repairing linings -- F27D 2001/1605 relates to techniques for restoring or renewing protective or functional linings on the interior surfaces of containers, pipelines, or similar structures. It includes processes such as welding, brazing, patching, coating, or applying repair materials to damaged or deteriorated linings. The subclass covers both manual and automated repair methods used in industrial equipment.
[F27D 2001/161] Hot repair -- The CPC subclass F27D 2001/161 covers methods and apparatus for carrying out repairs on components that are at elevated temperature, such as on-line welding, patching or refurbishment of hot furnaces, boilers, pipelines or other heated installations while they remain in operation. It includes techniques that allow the repair work to be performed without cooling or shutting down the equipment.
[F27D 2001/1615] through a hole in the casing, e.g. injecting refractory from outside the vessel -- The code F27D 2001/1615 covers methods of introducing refractory material into a furnace, reactor or similar vessel by feeding it through an opening in the outer casing, allowing the lining to be applied or repaired from the outside. These techniques enable refurbishment of high-temperature vessels without full disassembly.
[F27D 2001/1631] using an expandable core -- F27D 2001/1631 covers machine-tool or metal-working operations that employ an expandable core to grip, form or secure a workpiece, such as core drills, pipe-expansion tools and similar devices that enlarge a core element within a bore to achieve holding or shaping functions.
[F27D 2001/1657] Solid fuel -- F27D 2001/1657 covers solid fuels such as coal, wood, peat, biomass or other combustible solid materials used in heating, power generation or industrial processes. It includes the preparation, storage, feeding, combustion and exhaust treatment of these fuels in boilers, furnaces, burners or similar equipment. The code is applied to inventions that improve efficiency, safety or environmental performance when using solid fuel.
[F27D 2001/1663] Fluid fuel, e.g. gas -- This CPC subgroup pertains to inventions that deal with the provision, handling, storage, or injection of fluid fuels-typically gaseous hydrocarbons-into machines such as compressors, pumps, burners, or engines, covering methods and apparatus for supplying or managing these fuels.
[F27D 2001/1689] obtained from materials added to the melt -- The subgroup covers processes and equipment for producing a product by adding supplementary materials to a molten mass, such as alloying elements, fillers or reinforcements, during casting, moulding or shaping so that the resulting material incorporates the added components.
[F27D 2001/1825] Means for moving the cover -- The CPC subclass F27D 2001/1825 covers devices that provide the actuation or motion of a protective or functional cover. It includes mechanical, hydraulic, pneumatic or electro-driven mechanisms such as levers, cams, gears, motors, cylinders and linkages that open, close or shift the cover.
[F27D 2001/1833] comprising a screw mechanism -- F27D 2001/1833 relates to metal-forming devices that incorporate a screw mechanism, such as a lead screw or threaded rod, to convert rotary motion into linear movement for shaping, positioning or clamping workpieces.
[F27D 2001/1841] comprising means for rotating or moving the cover in a horizontal or quasi horizontal plane -- The classification covers devices whose protective or functional cover is moved in a horizontal or near-horizontal manner, such as lids, doors, or panels that rotate, swing, slide or pivot around a horizontal axis. It applies to mechanisms that provide access, sealing or protection by repositioning the cover within that plane.
[F27D 2001/185] comprising means for tilting the cover -- The subclass groups fluid-handling machines such as pumps, compressors or fans that incorporate a cover designed to tilt, usually by means of hinges, pivots or other tilting mechanisms, allowing easier access for inspection, servicing or loading. It covers any arrangement where the enclosure can be swung open rather than removed entirely.
[F27D 2001/1875] Hanging doors and walls -- The F27D 2001/1875 subclass addresses the design and construction of hanging doors and walls, covering mechanisms such as hinges, pivots, guides, rollers and related mounting systems that enable swinging, sliding or otherwise movable panels in buildings and other structures. It includes details on fastening, adjustment and sealing elements that support the functional operation of suspended door or wall assemblies.
[F27D 2001/1883] Hanging curtains -- The CPC code F27D 2001/1883 covers inventions related to hanging curtains, including the design and mounting of curtain rods, tracks, support structures and drive mechanisms that allow curtains to be positioned, drawn or adjusted in windows, doors or similar openings, whether manually operated or automated.
[F27D 2001/1891] Doors for separating two chambers in the furnace -- The CPC subclass F27D 2001/1891 covers doors that separate two chambers within a furnace, including fire doors, sliding or hinged partitions, and associated sealing or closing mechanisms designed to isolate distinct temperature zones or processing areas during heating, cooling or material treatment.
[F27D 2003/0001] Positioning the charge -- F27D 2003/0001 classifies inventions that control where electrical charge is placed within a device, covering structures, methods or arrangements that position charge in electrostatic or electrochemical apparatus-such as capacitors, fuel-cell components or other charge-bearing elements-to achieve desired performance or safety.
[F27D 2003/0002] involving positioning devices, e.g. buffers, buffer zones -- This subclass covers fluid-power devices that control or limit the position of a moving part, such as hydraulic or pneumatic buffers and buffer zones that absorb impact, restrict travel or hold a load in a defined position. It is used for systems where positioning is achieved by pressure-controlled elements.
[F27D 2003/0003] involving a system for aligning the articles through a lateral guidance, e.g. funnel -- A system that aligns articles while they move, using lateral guidance elements such as a funnel to direct the items into the desired position or path, typically employed in processing or handling lines to ensure correct orientation and placement.
[F27D 2003/0004] involving devices for measuring the article, the stack of articles or the height of the furnace passage or for adjusting the height of the passage to the charge or for putting the articles in the same position -- The code covers apparatus in furnaces that measure the size of individual articles, the height of a stack of articles, or the height of the furnace passage, as well as mechanisms that adjust the passage height to suit the charge or position the articles uniformly.
[F27D 2003/0006] Particulate materials -- The F27D 2003/0006 entry covers pumps and pump components specifically designed for handling particulate materials, such as slurry, powders, granules or solid particles, including feed, mixing and transport functions for such solids. It pertains to the structure, operation or control of devices that move or process particulate matter.
[F27D 2003/0007] Circular distribution -- F27D 2003/0007 covers centrifugal-pump designs in which the fluid is directed in a circular distribution pattern, typically by shaping the impeller or volute to spread the flow uniformly around a circular path for improved handling, mixing or pressure equalisation. This subgroup addresses the geometry and features that achieve that circular flow within the pump housing.
[F27D 2003/0008] Longitudinal distribution -- Longitudinal distribution refers to the arrangement or control of a material, temperature, pressure, or concentration along the length of a separating, drying, or mixing apparatus, such as a dryer, absorber, or column, to achieve uniform processing or specific gradients. It encompasses designs and methods that manage how these variables vary from one end of the device to the other.
[F27D 2003/0009] Separation of different types of fines, e.g. by a blower -- This CPC subgroup covers devices or methods that use a blower or air stream to separate mixtures of fine particles into distinct fractions. The separation relies on differences in particle characteristics such as size, density or electrostatic charge, and is applied in recycling, mineral processing, or dust handling.
[F27D 2003/001] Series of dispensers or separation in teo or more parts -- F27D 2003/001 pertains to dispensers that are provided as a series or are split into two or more separate parts, allowing multiple or sequential doses to be delivered from a single container. It includes designs such as multi-chamber packages, dual-dose units and other configurations where the dispensing mechanism is divided for distinct sections or functions.
[F27D 2003/0012] Working with piles -- The CPC subclass F27D 2003/0012 covers techniques and processes for installing, driving, and handling piles used in foundations or support structures. It includes methods for positioning, hammering, vibrating, suction or jetting of piles, as well as equipment and tools employed to carry out these pile-working operations.
[F27D 2003/0013] Unstacking or making stacks -- F27D 2003/0013 covers devices or methods that separate items from a stack or create stacks of objects, such as plates, pallets, or sheets, in industrial handling or packaging processes. It includes mechanisms for picking, spreading, aligning, or otherwise arranging stacked goods to facilitate transport, storage, or further processing.
[F27D 2003/0014] involving the use of magazines -- This CPC subgroup covers fan or blower arrangements that incorporate a magazine, meaning a storage or feeding container for items such as powders, charges, or tools, where the magazine is integral to the operation of the ventilation or transport system.
[F27D 2003/0015] the magazine being vertical -- This CPC subgroup concerns heating devices that incorporate a vertically oriented fuel magazine, such as a bin or hopper for solid fuels like coal, wood or pellets, allowing gravity feeding into the combustion chamber of a boiler, furnace or similar installation. It addresses the layout, support and feeding mechanism of the vertical magazine within the heating system.
[F27D 2003/0016] the magazine being a rotary device -- F27D 2003/0016 covers inventions in which a magazine for storing projectiles, powder or other feed material is designed as a rotary device, such as a revolving drum or cylinder that indexes positions for loading. The classification applies to firearms, artillery and similar weapons where the rotating magazine forms part of the feeding mechanism.
[F27D 2003/0018] comprising means to introduce or extract the charge in series of separate containers or zones -- The CPC subclass F27D 2003/0018 covers systems that handle bulk material by moving it through a sequence of distinct containers or zones, each equipped with mechanisms for loading or unloading the charge. This includes conveyors, feeders, or extractors that introduce material into, or remove it from, individual compartments arranged in series. The focus is on the arrangement of multiple, separate chambers rather than a single continuous chamber.
[F27D 2003/0019] simultaneously -- F27D 2003/0019 covers pumps or pump assemblies that deliver or handle two or more fluids at the same time, or that operate multiple pumping stages simultaneously. The subclass is used for designs where simultaneous action of separate pumping elements is integral to the device's function.
[F27D 2003/002] Transforming a single row in a multiple row -- This CPC entry covers apparatus or method that converts one line of sheet material within a stack that contains several parallel lines. The transformation may involve shifting, merging, separating or rotating the single row so that its position or orientation changes relative to the other rows, typically in printing, packaging or material-handling machines.
[F27D 2003/0034] Means for moving, conveying, transporting the charge in the furnace or in the charging facilities -- This subclass covers equipment that transports the material being heated inside a furnace or between the furnace and its charging equipment. It includes mechanical, pneumatic or hydraulic conveyors, elevators, rollers and other devices that move, feed or discharge the charge.
[F27D 2003/0036] comprising inflatable or extendable parts -- The CPC subgroup F27D 2003/0036 covers propulsion-related machines or devices that contain inflatable or extendable components, such as pneumatic bladders, airbags, telescopic or folding parts, and other structures that can be expanded or deployed during operation. It is used for inventions where the inflatable or extendable element forms an essential part of the device's function.
[F27D 2003/0037] comprising friction surfaces -- F27D 2003/0037 covers positive-displacement pumps whose operation relies on friction surfaces, such as gear, vane or rotor elements that slide against each other to move fluid. The classification is used for designs where the fluid is driven by contact between moving and stationary surfaces, often including sealing or pumping stages that depend on surface friction.
[F27D 2003/0038] comprising shakers -- F27D 2003/0038 denotes patents for positive-displacement pumps that incorporate shaking elements or shakers, typically to agitate fluid or solid contents during pumping. The classification groups devices where the shaker is an integral part of the pump mechanism, used in applications such as mixing, cleaning or de-watering.
[F27D 2003/0039] comprising magnetic means -- The CPC subclass F27D 2003/0039 covers equipment for metal-working and related processes that incorporate magnetic means, such as magnetic bearings, couplings, field generators or magnetic actuators, which are used to influence material handling, tool positioning or process control.
[F27D 2003/004] Magnetic lifters -- Magnetic lifters are devices that use permanent magnets or electromagnets to lift, hold, or move ferromagnetic objects such as steel plates, bars, or scrap. They are employed in material handling, construction, and recycling to detach and transport metallic loads without mechanical gripping, often integrated into cranes, tables, or portable units.
[F27D 2003/0042] comprising roller trains -- The CPC subclass F27D 2003/0042 covers inventions that incorporate a series of rollers arranged as a train for material processing, typically in crushing, grinding or milling operations. It specifies that the device is built around one or more roller trains that work together to achieve progressive size reduction or conveyance of the workpiece.
[F27D 2003/0043] at least one of them being driven separately -- F27D 2003/0043 classifies internal-combustion piston engine arrangements in which a group of power units or components are linked to a common output but at least one of those units is driven independently of the others, allowing separate control of its operation.
[F27D 2003/0044] at least one of them being removable -- F27D 2003/0044 classifies variable-displacement pumps that incorporate at least one component-such as a rotor, impeller, gear set or sealing element-designed to be removable for maintenance, cleaning or replacement. The entry is used for pump designs that allow disassembly of specific parts without dismantling the entire unit.
[F27D 2003/0045] Use of rollable bodies, e.g. balls -- The CPC class F27D 2003/0045 covers mechanisms that employ rollable bodies such as balls. It includes devices that use spherical elements for transmitting motion, reducing friction or supporting loads, typically in bearings, conveyors, or roller drives.
[F27D 2003/0046] comprising one or more movable arms, e.g. forks -- The CPC subgroup F27D 2003/0046 relates to machines or apparatus that incorporate one or more movable arms, for example fork arms, used to grip, lift, transport or position objects. It covers lifting and handling devices such as forklifts, palletizers and other equipment where the arms can be articulated or shifted relative to the main body.
[F27D 2003/0048] Walking beams -- Walking beams are mechanical handling devices that move loads by alternately pivoting interconnected beams, often powered hydraulically or pneumatically, to raise, lower, or transport material along a fixed path. They are used in applications such as loading conveyors, ship cargo handling, and bulk material transfer, providing controlled, continuous movement without the need for complex gear trains. This CPC entry covers the construction, operation, and variants of such beam-type handling equipment.
[F27D 2003/0049] comprising eccentrics or lever arms -- The CPC subclass F27D 2003/0049 refers to machines powered by fluid pressure that incorporate eccentrics or lever arms to change motion, typically converting rotary input into reciprocating or oscillating output. It covers designs where such mechanisms are integral to the operation of pumps, compressors, or similar hydraulic equipment. This grouping aids in locating patents that use these motion-transforming components within fluid-driven devices.
[F27D 2003/005] comprising ramps -- This CPC subgroup classifies rotary pumps that incorporate one or more ramps-inclined or stepped surfaces within the pump housing, rotor, or stator-used to guide fluid flow, assist component movement, improve sealing, or reduce wear.
[F27D 2003/0051] comprising means to pick up the charge and put it down -- The CPC class F27D 2003/0051 covers devices that incorporate elements for acquiring an electric charge from one point and subsequently depositing it at another location, enabling controlled charge transfer. Such apparatus are used in applications like electrostatic handling of wafers, powders, or other materials where precise charging and discharging are required.
[F27D 2003/0053] comprising a device for charging with the doors closed -- F27D 2003/0053 covers equipment that incorporates a charging device designed to function while the vehicle or enclosure doors remain closed, often with sealing or safety features that permit safe charging without opening the doors, enhancing convenience and protection.
[F27D 2003/0054] Means to move molten metal, e.g. electromagnetic pump -- F27D 2003/0054 covers devices that transport molten metal, such as electromagnetic pumps, where the fluid is driven without mechanical moving parts by magnetic fields. The classification includes systems for continuous or pulsating flow in casting, refining or other metallurgical processes.
[F27D 2003/0055] with flow regulation -- F27D 2003/0055 covers pumps that incorporate means for regulating the flow of the pumped fluid. This includes devices such as adjustable throttling valves, variable-speed drives, or controllable impeller geometry that enable the pump to maintain a desired flow rate under varying conditions.
[F27D 2003/0056] through a syphon in a vacuum chamber, e.g. involving aspiration or pressure on the bath -- This subclass covers arrangements that transfer liquid from a bath by means of a syphon placed inside a vacuum chamber, using suction or pressure differentials to draw the fluid. It includes devices where the vacuum creates aspiration of the bath liquid through the syphon, often employed in coating, cleaning or material-handling processes.
[F27D 2003/0057] Fast-outlet or inlet means -- Fast-outlet or inlet means are components that provide a rapid path for fluid or gas to enter or leave a machine such as a pump, compressor, fan or blower, typically designed to reduce pressure losses and improve flow efficiency. The devices may include specially shaped ports, nozzles, flanges, or ducts that facilitate quick discharge or admission of the working medium.
[F27D 2003/0059] comprising tracks, e.g. rails and wagon -- This CPC entry groups patents for earth-moving or similar machines that are equipped with or incorporate track systems such as rails or a wagon for movement or load handling. It covers designs where the vehicle uses continuous tracks or rail-like elements to travel over terrain.
[F27D 2003/006] with a return track -- F27D 2003/006 covers material-handling or conveyor systems that include a return track, meaning a secondary conveying path that carries the conveyed article back toward its origin or to another stage after it has moved forward on the main track. This subclass groups inventions where the forward and return sections are integral to the same device.
[F27D 2003/0061] with means for changing track -- F27D 2003/0061 covers paper- or board-handling machines that include a device for diverting the moving web from its current track to an alternative path, such as a guide, switch or pivoting roller that changes the material's direction during continuous processing. It falls within the CPC subgroup for continuous or combined processes and machines used in the treatment of paper, board or similar webs.
[F27D 2003/0062] using devices for tilting or rocking the charge -- The CPC subclass F27D 2003/0062 pertains to the use of devices that tilt or rock a charge, typically an explosive or ammunition, to influence its initiation, direction, or safety. It includes mechanisms that impart angular motion to the charge during handling, loading, or firing to improve performance or reduce the risk of accidental detonation.
[F27D 2003/0063] comprising endless belts -- A subclass of centrifugal or rotary separation apparatus that includes one or more endless belts to convey material through the rotating zone, enabling continuous separation, drying or washing of particulate streams.
[F27D 2003/0065] Lifts, e.g. containing the bucket elevators -- The CPC subgroup F27D 2003/0065 covers lifts that incorporate bucket-elevator mechanisms, where material is carried in a series of buckets attached to a moving chain, belt or rod to raise or lower loads vertically. It includes devices used for bulk-material handling that combine lifting and conveying functions.
[F27D 2003/0066] comprising scrapers or systems to pull out -- The CPC subgroup F27D 2003/0066 pertains to power-driven door, window or hatch mechanisms that incorporate scrapers or pull-out systems for extracting material, residues or coverings from a surface during operation. It includes designs where the scraper motion is integrated with the closure's actuation to clean, de-ice or remove attached layers as the closure moves.
[F27D 2003/0067] comprising conveyors where the translation is communicated by friction from at least one rotating element, e.g. two opposed rotations combined -- This CPC subclass relates to conveyor devices that transfer motion to the load by frictional contact with at least one rotating element, such as rollers or wheels, and includes configurations where two opposed rotations combine to produce the conveying movement. It covers mechanisms that rely on direct frictional drive rather than gears, belts or chain transmissions.
[F27D 2003/0068] comprising clamps or tongs -- The CPC subgroup F27D 2003/0068 covers machines or apparatuses that handle, move, or secure workpieces by means of clamps or tongs, such as gripping devices, lifting fixtures, and material-handling tools that use mechanical pressure to hold objects in place.
[F27D 2003/0069] the device being suspended, e.g. from a crane -- This subclass deals with the object that is suspended by a lifting apparatus such as a crane, covering its attachment means, configuration and handling characteristics. It pertains to the load, tool or component being raised rather than the crane or lifting device itself.
[F27D 2003/0071] Use of a comminuting device, e.g. grinding mill -- F27D 2003/0071 covers the practical use of comminuting devices such as grinding mills, addressing methods that reduce material size by mechanical action. The classification includes applications in mineral processing, food preparation, chemical production and other industries where size reduction is essential. It is applied to patents describing the operation and utilization of these devices.
[F27D 2003/0072] Use of a pinion and a rack -- This subclass covers mechanisms in which a rotating pinion engages a toothed rack to produce straight-line movement, such as steering gears, conveyors and linear actuators. It includes designs and arrangements that transmit torque from the pinion into translational force along the rack.
[F27D 2003/0073] the charge floating on a liquid, e.g. water -- This CPC subclass deals with processes where an electric charge is maintained on the surface of a liquid, typically water, for purposes such as electrostatic spraying, cleaning, or separation. It covers methods and apparatus that generate, sustain or utilize a floating charge on liquids.
[F27D 2003/0074] floating on water -- It covers inventions that enable a device, structure, or vehicle to remain on the surface of water by buoyancy, such as floating platforms, pontoons and methods for stabilising objects on water. The entry also includes designs for flotation devices, anchoring systems and load-bearing elements that keep the system afloat.
