[F27D 2003/0075] Charging or discharging vertically, e.g. through a bottom opening -- The code refers to devices or methods that introduce or remove material in a vertical direction, typically through an opening at the bottom of a container or vessel. It is used for handling fluids, powders, or other substances where charging or discharging occurs by gravity or pressure from below. This classification applies to systems such as silos, storage tanks, or rechargeable battery packs that employ bottom-entry access.
[F27D 2003/0077] Use of centrifugal devices -- The CPC subclass F27D 2003/0077 covers inventions that employ centrifugal devices to create radial forces for purposes such as separating, mixing, washing, drying or concentrating materials. It includes any process or apparatus where a rotating component generates the needed centrifugal action.
[F27D 2003/0078] comprising jacks, e.g. hydraulic jacks -- F27D 2003/0078 covers devices that lift or support loads by means of jacks, with a particular focus on hydraulic jacks. The class includes the structure, operation and control features of such lifting mechanisms used in construction, maintenance or industrial applications.
[F27D 2003/0079] Use of cams -- The CPC subclass F27D 2003/0079 covers inventions that employ cams to convert rotary motion into a prescribed linear or oscillating motion, including timing, valve actuation and motion control in engines, pumps and automated mechanisms. It encompasses cam-driven devices, cam profile designs and methods of using cams for precise mechanical sequencing.
[F27D 2003/008] comprising cross-beams or gantry cranes -- F27D 2003/008 relates to building or structural devices that incorporate cross-beams or gantry cranes as integral components, typically to support loads, span large openings or facilitate material handling within a construction or industrial setting. The classification covers designs where these beam or crane elements are combined with the primary structure to provide added strength, lifting capability or flexibility.
[F27D 2003/0081] Series of little rams -- The CPC entry gathers patents concerning arrangements of several miniature ram-type propulsion units that are combined in a series to generate thrust, typically for small rockets, model launchers or other compact launch systems where multiple little rams are fired sequentially or simultaneously.
[F27D 2003/0083] Means for stirring the charge -- F27D 2003/0083 classifies devices that agitate the material inside a vessel, such as reactors, furnaces, or kilns. It includes mechanical elements like impellers, paddles, blades, or other rotating, reciprocating or oscillating parts that stir the charge to achieve uniform mixing, heat transfer or reaction control.
[F27D 2003/0085] Movement of the container or support of the charge in the furnace or in the charging facilities -- It covers mechanisms that transport or position the charge container or its support within a furnace or its charging system, including loading, unloading, tilting, rotating or moving the holder during operation; the code also includes devices for moving the container from storage to the furnace and equipment for adjusting the support while the charge is fed.
[F27D 2003/0086] Up or down -- F27D 2003/0086 covers hydraulic or pneumatic mechanisms that raise or lower a load, such as lifts, elevators, jacks and other devices that provide vertical motion by fluid-power actuation.
[F27D 2003/0087] Rotation about a vertical axis -- The CPC subclass F27D 2003/0087 covers machines and apparatuses that generate or utilize motion by rotating around a vertical axis, such as wind-turbine rotors, vertical-axis mixers, and certain centrifuges. It includes designs where the main rotating component is oriented vertically and the classification addresses the mechanical features that enable this type of rotation.
[F27D 2003/0089] Rotation about a horizontal or slightly inclined axis -- This class covers pumps whose driving element rotates about a horizontal or mildly inclined shaft. It includes centrifugal and rotary-type pumps where the axis is essentially horizontal, allowing fluid to be moved by radial or axial flow. The classification is used for designs in which the rotation axis is not vertical.
[F27D 2003/009] Oscillations about a horizontal axis;  Rocking -- The subclass pertains to devices whose motion consists of repetitive swinging or rocking about a horizontal line, including mechanisms that generate, control or utilise such oscillations. It covers springs, dampers, pendulum-type systems and any apparatus where a mass or component repeatedly moves back-and-forth in a horizontal-axis plane.
[F27D 2003/0091] Horizontally -- The CPC subclass F27D 2003/0091 relates to boilers or steam generators in which the principal heating chamber, tubes or other heat-exchange elements are arranged in a horizontal configuration, covering designs such as horizontally fired or water-tube boilers.
[F27D 2003/0092] with a reciprocating movement -- The CPC subclass F27D 2003/0092 falls under the main group for pumps and designates those where the working fluid is moved by a reciprocating motion of the pump elements. It covers devices such as piston, diaphragm and plunger pumps in which the moving parts travel back and forth to generate flow.
[F27D 2003/0093] Movement on a slope -- F27D 2003/0093 covers inventions that provide means for moving vehicles, equipment or loads up or down an inclined surface, including mechanisms for traction, braking, steering or power transmission on slopes.
[F27D 2003/0095] the advancement being step by step -- F27D 2003/0095 covers boilers or steam-generating apparatus in which the heating element or firebed is moved forward in a step-by-step manner, allowing progressive advancement of the combustion zone. This subclass relates to designs that adjust the position of the heat source incrementally to improve heat transfer, control combustion or accommodate varying load conditions.
[F27D 2003/0096] Advancement along the longitudinal axis of the workpiece -- F27D 2003/0096 covers methods and devices that advance a workpiece in the direction of its length during machining, such as longitudinal feed in turning, boring or grinding operations. The classification is used for patents describing mechanisms that control or synchronize this axial movement to achieve precise material removal or shaping.
[F27D 2003/0097] Advancement along an axis perpendicular to the axis of the workpiece -- F27D 2003/0097 covers mechanisms that move a tool or workpiece in a direction perpendicular to the longitudinal axis of the workpiece, such as feeding devices used in machining, forming or cutting operations. It includes cam-driven, hydraulic or screw-type feed systems that provide linear advancement at right angles to the workpiece axis.
[F27D 2003/0098] Workpiece rolling on its support -- The entry covers processes where a workpiece rotates or rolls over a supporting surface while being deformed by rolling operations. It includes arrangements in which the workpiece contacts a backing roll, belt or stationary guide that provides support during reduction or shaping. This classification is applied to patents describing such rolling-on-support techniques in metalworking.
[F27D 2003/045] used to pull the charge -- F27D 2003/045 pertains to devices or methods for extracting stored electrical energy from a battery or similar charge storage element. It includes mechanisms that pull the charge for powering circuits, controlling discharge, or transferring energy to other systems. The classification falls under the CPC group dealing with electric batteries and their charge management.
[F27D 2003/105] using shutters -- F27D 2003/105 relates to heating systems that use shutters-dampers, louvers or other adjustable plates-to control the flow of heated air, gases or liquids. It covers the design, placement and operation of such shutters for regulating temperature distribution, directing airflow or providing safety shut-off in heating installations.
[F27D 2003/121] Band, belt or mesh -- This CPC group concerns flexible continuous elements such as bands, belts and mesh structures that are used to transmit motion or power, to support, guide or convey materials, or to form protective or reinforcing layers. It includes their construction, arrangement, operation and related adaptations.
[F27D 2003/122] Band made from longitudinal wires or bars -- F27D 2003/122 covers bands formed from a series of longitudinal wires or bars that run parallel to each other, creating a continuous strip used for reinforcement or support in structures such as concrete elements, pressure vessels, or mechanical seals. The wires or bars may be wound, welded or otherwise joined to maintain spacing and alignment.
[F27D 2003/124] Sleds;  Transport supports -- The CPC code F27D 2003/124 covers sleds and transport supports, encompassing devices designed to slide or carry loads over a surface, such as sledges, toboggans, drag sleds and similar support structures used for moving goods or equipment. It includes both manual and mechanically powered variants.
[F27D 2003/125] Charging cars, lift trolleys -- This CPC subgroup relates to devices and systems for powering or recharging vehicles such as electric cars, as well as mechanisms for moving lift trolleys that carry loads vertically or horizontally. It includes charging stations, on-board chargers, and trolley-lifting arrangements used in industrial or transport settings.
[F27D 2003/126] with long arms, charging forks -- F27D 2003/126 classifies harvesting and material-handling machines that feature charging forks with extended arms, allowing the device to reach farther when loading, unloading or stacking bulk material. The long-armed forks are used on equipment such as harvesters, loaders or conveyors to improve reach and handling efficiency.
[F27D 2003/127] for carrying pots -- F27D 2003/127 relates to devices designed to transport or support cooking pots, including trays, carriers, carts or frames that hold one or more pots during handling, storage or movement in industrial or domestic settings.
[F27D 2003/128] for carrying stacks of articles, e.g. bricks -- F27D 2003/128 relates to devices that carry stacks of articles, such as bricks, by supporting multiple items arranged in a pile during transport or storage. It includes carriers, pallets, frames or other structures designed to hold and move stacked loads.
[F27D 2003/161] through a porous element -- This subclass groups patents for heating, cooking or drying devices that transfer heat, gas or fluid through a porous element such as a ceramic or metallic matrix, enabling radiant or convective heating.
[F27D 2003/162] the fluid being an oxidant or a fuel -- This CPC subgroup deals with gas-turbine or steam-turbine arrangements in which the working fluid functions as an oxidant or as a fuel. It covers designs where the same stream supplies the combustion oxidizer, the fuel, or both, such as in air-breathing or fuel-rich cycles.
[F27D 2003/163] the fluid being an oxidant -- This CPC subgroup covers combustion or heating devices in which the introduced fluid serves as the oxidizing agent-typically air, oxygen or oxygen-enriched gases-rather than as the fuel, encompassing burners, furnaces and reactors where the oxidant flow is a principal operating parameter.
[F27D 2003/164] Oxygen -- F27D 2003/164 covers inventions dealing with the provision, control or use of oxygen in heating, burning or combustion processes, such as oxygen-enriched burners, oxygen injection systems, or methods for regulating oxygen concentration in furnaces and reactors to improve efficiency, flame stability or emissions.
[F27D 2003/165] the fluid being a fuel -- F27D 2003/165 covers internal-combustion engines in which the working fluid itself serves as the fuel, encompassing designs, operating methods and related components where fuel acts as the fluid driving the cycle.
[F27D 2003/166] the fluid being a treatment gas -- F27D 2003/166 covers fluid-bed processes in which the fluid phase is a treatment gas employed to react with, modify or cleanse solid particles, such as in combustion, gasification, or chemical conditioning of the bed material. It is a subgroup of F27D 2003 for fluidised-bed operations using gases as the treating medium.
[F27D 2003/167] the fluid being a neutral gas -- The CPC subgroup F27D 2003/167 concerns inventions where the working fluid is a neutral (non-ionised) gas, such as air, nitrogen or other inert gases, used in devices like compressors, turbines, fans or ventilation systems, focusing on the handling, flow or processing of these gases in mechanical or thermodynamic applications.
[F27D 2003/168] through a lance -- F27D 2003/168 is a CPC subgroup under "Heating; burning; smelting" that covers processes in which material, fuel or reactive gases are introduced into a furnace, kiln, melt or similar vessel by means of a lance-a tubular conduit that directs the flow for heating, refining or cleaning operations.
[F27D 2003/169] Construction of the lance, e.g. lances for injecting particles -- F27D 2003/169 covers designs and features of lances used to inject particles into a material, including the shape, materials, internal passages, and delivery mechanisms. It addresses construction aspects such as nozzle geometry, sealing, wear protection and attachment to feeding systems for applications in surface treatment, coating or material modification.
[F27D 2003/185] Conveying particles in a conduct using a fluid -- The classification covers devices and methods for moving solid particles through a conduit by means of a fluid, typically air or gas, such as pneumatic or aerated conveying systems. It includes pipelines, ducts, and associated control mechanisms that transport bulk materials from one location to another using fluid pressure or flow. This entry addresses the design and operation of such fluid-driven particle transport arrangements.
[F27D 2005/0075] Pots, e.g. slag pots, ladles -- F27D 2005/0075 covers vessels used in metalworking processes, specifically pots such as slag pots and ladles that hold molten metal, slag, or other high-temperature materials. The classification applies to designs, constructions and features that enable safe handling, transport and pouring of these fluids in furnaces, converters or casting operations. It includes variations in shape, reinforcement, lining and mechanisms for tilting or rotating the vessel.
[F27D 2005/0081] Details -- The CPC code F27D 2005/0081 relates to detailed elements of equipment used in the manufacture of fibrous or non-woven articles, such as specific machine components, attachments or auxiliary devices that enhance or control processing steps. It is a sub-group within the broader class for manufacturing fibrous articles.
[F27D 2005/0087] Means to prevent the adherence of the charge -- F27D 2005/0087 covers devices or methods incorporated in electrostatic precipitators and similar apparatus that keep the collected particulate charge from sticking to the electrodes or collection surfaces, such as vibrating, shaking, conductive cleaning or other anti-adhesion mechanisms.
[F27D 2005/0093] Means to maintain the form of the article -- F27D 2005/0093 relates to devices that keep an article in its intended shape during handling, storage or use, such as supports, brackets, fixtures, moulds or retaining structures. These means preserve dimensional stability and prevent deformation.
[F27D 2007/023] Conduits -- The code F27D 2007/023 designates conduits used in heating, ventilation, air-conditioning and related fluid-distribution systems, covering pipes, ducts and similar channels for transporting air, gas, steam or water, including their construction, dimensions and installation. It falls within CPC section F27 for heating and hot-water supply, subgroup D for building climate control.
[F27D 2007/026] Dampers -- F27D 2007/026 - Dampers covers devices that restrict, absorb or control motion or force in linear actuators, such as hydraulic or pneumatic shock absorbers, vibration dampers, speed-governors and flow-restricting elements used to moderate fluid or mechanical movement.
[F27D 2007/045] Fans -- F27D 2007/045 covers fans, devices that generate a flow of air or gas by imparting kinetic energy to the fluid with rotating blades. It includes axial, centrifugal and mixed-flow fans used for ventilation, cooling, drying or propulsion in industrial, automotive and household applications. The classification groups the mechanical principles, blade arrangements and drive configurations of such air-moving machines.
[F27D 2007/063] Special atmospheres, e.g. high pressure atmospheres -- This CPC subgroup covers inventions dealing with the generation, control and utilization of special gas atmospheres such as high-pressure, low-pressure, inert or reactive environments for processes like material treatment, storage or operation of equipment. It includes apparatus, methods and devices for maintaining these atmospheres under defined conditions.
[F27D 2007/066] Vacuum -- F27D 2007/066 pertains to vacuum technology, covering devices and methods that create, maintain or utilize low-pressure environments such as vacuum pumps, vacuum generators and associated systems, typically applied in industrial, scientific or manufacturing processes.
[F27D 2009/0002] Cooling of furnaces -- The CPC subclass F27D 2009/0002 covers technologies for cooling furnaces, including methods and apparatus that remove heat from the furnace interior or its walls after operation. It encompasses water, air or other fluid cooling systems, heat-exchanger arrangements, and control devices designed to protect furnace components and improve cycle efficiency.
[F27D 2009/0005] the cooling medium being a gas -- The classification covers cooling or refrigeration systems that use a gas as the cooling medium, including apparatus and methods where a gaseous refrigerant circulates through heat exchangers, evaporators, condensers or similar components to absorb and reject heat. It applies to gas-based air-conditioning, refrigeration and cryogenic cooling technologies.
[F27D 2009/0008] Ways to inject gases against surfaces -- F27D 2009/0008 covers methods for directing a gas stream onto a surface, including impinging jets, lances, and other arrangements that force gases against material faces for purposes such as cleaning, cooling, drying or surface treatment. The entry groups patents that disclose the geometry, orientation or flow-control features used to achieve effective gas-surface interaction.
[F27D 2009/001] the cooling medium being a fluid other than a gas -- This subgroup covers refrigeration, heat pump or heat-exchange equipment in which the cooling medium is a liquid or other non-gaseous fluid, such as water, oil, or liquid refrigerants. It includes designs, control methods and apparatuses that exploit the thermodynamic properties of liquid cooling media.
[F27D 2009/0013] the fluid being water -- F27D 2009/0013 is a CPC subgroup within the class for pumps and liquid-moving devices that specifies applications where the conveyed fluid is water. It covers inventions such as water supply, irrigation, fire-fighting and other systems that pump, circulate or treat water, distinguishing them from pumps handling other liquids or gases.
[F27D 2009/0016] Water-spray -- F27D 2009/0016 refers to devices that generate a spray or mist of water, such as sprinklers, foggers and cooling or humidifying systems. It includes mechanisms for distributing water droplets for fire suppression, environmental control, or cleaning.
[F27D 2009/0018] the cooling medium passing through a pattern of tubes -- F27D 2009/0018 covers heat-exchange arrangements where the cooling medium is forced to flow through a pattern of tubes, providing efficient heat removal from a surface or fluid. The classification includes tube-bundle designs, manifold configurations and flow-distribution methods used in refrigeration, air-conditioning and industrial cooling systems.
[F27D 2009/0021] with the parallel tube parts close to each other, e.g. a serpentine -- The CPC subclass F27D concerns lubricating or oil-pumping devices, and the group 2009/0021 specifies designs in which parallel tube elements are positioned closely together, often forming a serpentine path to improve flow characteristics or heat transfer within the pump.
[F27D 2009/0024] with contiguous tubes, which may be separately welded one to the other -- This CPC entry covers heat exchangers that employ a series of adjacent tubes which may be welded together individually, enabling flexible assembly and maintenance. It falls under the F27D subgroup for heat exchangers within the broader class of steam generators and boilers.
[F27D 2009/0027] linked by elements -- F27D 2009/0027 covers arrangements in which two or more mechanical pumps are mechanically linked together by elements such as a common shaft, coupling, gear train, belt or chain drive, or similar devices. The linkage is used to coordinate the operation of the pumps, for example to increase flow, pressure, or to provide redundancy, and includes both series and parallel configurations.
[F27D 2009/0029] fixed, e.g. welded to a supporting surface -- The subgroup F27D 2009/0029 refers to installations where a component is permanently secured to a supporting surface, for example by welding, brazing, soldering or other fixed joining processes. It covers the design and method of attaching structural or mechanical parts that are intended to remain immovable during service.
[F27D 2009/0032] integrated with refractories in a panel -- This CPC subgroup covers panels in which refractory material is integrated as part of the panel structure, providing heat resistance for continuous processing furnaces or high-temperature applications. Such panels may be used as linings, walls or protective layers in industrial ovens and related equipment.
[F27D 2009/0035] arranged for the covering of a cylindrical surface -- F27D 2009/0035 relates to arrangements that provide a covering for a cylindrical surface, such as protective sleeves, coats, linings or claddings applied to pipes, tubes, rollers, cables or similar objects. The classification includes mechanisms and methods for securing, fastening or bonding the covering material to the cylinder, as well as the covering material itself when designed for this purpose.
[F27D 2009/0037] the cooling medium passing a radiator -- F27D 2009/0037 relates to the passage of a cooling medium through a radiator, covering arrangements, devices and methods for directing coolant flow, heat exchange and temperature regulation in systems such as engines, HVAC units or other heat-dissipating equipment.
[F27D 2009/004] the cooling medium passing a waterbox -- The F27D 2009/004 entry covers devices in which a cooling fluid is routed through a waterbox that receives the medium from the rotating part and distributes it to cool components such as blades, stator or housing in fans, wind-turbine or air-conditioning units. It includes the design of channels, manifolds or passages that guide the cooling medium through the waterbox for effective heat removal.
[F27D 2009/0043] Insert type waterbox, e.g. cylindrical or flat type -- An insert type waterbox is a modular water reservoir used in printing or coating equipment to supply water to a dampening system. It is supplied as a separate insert that can be placed inside the machine and may be cylindrical or flat in shape, allowing easy replacement and maintenance.
[F27D 2009/0045] the cooling medium passing a block, e.g. metallic -- F27D 2009/0045 covers cooling arrangements where the coolant is forced to flow through a solid block, typically made of metal, to remove heat from a component such as a piston or cylinder liner. The block acts as a heat-conducting conduit that enhances heat transfer while the fluid circulates within its internal passages.
[F27D 2009/0048] incorporating conduits for the medium -- This subclass relates to pump constructions in which the fluid-conducting channels are built into the pump itself, allowing the medium to be routed through internal ducts, passages or integrated conduits rather than external piping. It includes arrangements where the conduit forms part of the rotating or stationary elements and may serve for supply, discharge or recirculation within the pump assembly.
[F27D 2009/0051] comprising use of studs to transfer heat or retain the liner -- F27D 2009/0051 relates to furnace or boiler designs that employ protruding studs on the heating surface to conduct heat to the protective liner or to secure the liner in place, enhancing heat distribution and liner stability.
[F27D 2009/0054] adapted to retain formed bricks -- The CPC subgroup F27D 2009/0054 addresses devices or modifications that retain bricks after they have been formed, preventing displacement before drying or further handling. It includes retaining plates, guides, supports or other mechanisms integrated with brick-forming machines.
[F27D 2009/0056] Use of high thermoconductive elements -- The entry covers applications of materials or components possessing very high thermal conductivity within heat-exchanger devices. It includes the incorporation of metal fins, plates, inserts or other conductive elements to enhance heat-transfer rates and improve overall efficiency of temperature-control systems.
[F27D 2009/0059] made mostly from graphite -- The CPC entry F27D 2009/0059 covers inventions where the principal constituent is graphite, such as components, structures or devices made primarily from graphite material. It includes methods of producing or using graphite-rich products and applies to both industrial and technical applications.
[F27D 2009/0062] made from copper or copper alloy -- F27D 2009/0062 designates centrifugal pumps or pump components whose primary material is copper or a copper alloy. This subclass groups devices where the housing, impeller or other structural parts are fabricated from copper-based materials, reflecting the specific properties required for corrosion resistance or thermal conductivity.
[F27D 2009/0064] Cooling of furnace arches -- The F27D 2009/0064 entry covers devices or processes for cooling the arches of a furnace, typically by flowing a cooling medium along the arch surface to reduce thermal stresses and protect refractory linings, thereby improving furnace durability and temperature control.
[F27D 2009/0067] Cooling element inlet and outlet tubes -- The CPC subclass F27D 2009/0067 covers tubes that serve as inlet and outlet passages for a cooling element such as a heat exchanger, evaporator or condenser, focusing on their design, arrangement and connection to the fluid circuit. It includes the piping, manifolds and fittings that direct refrigerant or coolant into and out of the device.
[F27D 2009/007] Cooling of charges therein -- The CPC subclass F27D covers devices, methods and arrangements for cooling the refrigerant charge in a refrigeration or heat-pump system, including subcooling, internal heat-exchange and economiser techniques that lower the temperature of the working fluid before it enters the expansion device. It pertains to the control and improvement of system efficiency by reducing charge temperature.
[F27D 2009/0072] the cooling medium being a gas -- CPC F27D 2009/0072 covers refrigeration, heat-pump or heat-recovery devices in which the cooling medium is a gas, describing apparatus and methods that use gaseous refrigerants for heat absorption and transfer.
[F27D 2009/0075] in direct contact with the charge -- F27D 2009/0075 is a CPC subclass for positive-displacement pumps in which the moving element (such as a piston, diaphragm or rotor) contacts the fluid being pumped directly, without a separating barrier, allowing the charge to be moved by mechanical action of the pump itself. This entry groups designs where the pump chamber or sealing surfaces are in direct contact with the pumped material.
[F27D 2009/0078] in indirect contact with the charge -- This subgroup covers apparatus or methods where the material to be processed or the workpiece interacts with an electric charge without being directly touched by the electrode, using an intervening medium such as gas, liquid, or dielectric. It is applied in plasma generators, discharge tubes and similar devices where indirect charge transfer is essential.
[F27D 2009/0081] the cooling medium being a fluid (other than a gas in direct or indirect contact with the charge) -- F27D 2009/0081 covers furnace or metallurgical process cooling where the cooling medium is a non-gas fluid, such as a liquid or molten metal, that contacts the charge either directly or through a separating wall. It excludes any cooling that relies on gases, focusing on fluid-based heat removal systems.
[F27D 2009/0083] the fluid being water -- F27D 2009/0083 is a CPC subgroup relating to machines or devices that use water as the working fluid, typically in hydraulic or fluid power systems. It covers inventions where water is employed for power transmission, actuation, or cooling in equipment such as pumps, cylinders, and hydraulic circuits.
[F27D 2009/0086] applied in spray form -- F27D 2009/0086 covers inventions in the CPC subclass for devices and methods that convey, disperse or mix fluids when the material is applied as a spray. It is used to classify patents where the functional element of the device is employed to deliver a spray onto a target surface, such as spray coating, spray cooling, or spray atomisation.
[F27D 2009/0089] Quenching -- F27D 2009/0089 covers methods and apparatus for quenching, the rapid cooling of metal workpieces after heating to obtain desired hardness or microstructure. It includes various cooling techniques such as immersion, spray, mist or air jet and related control systems for temperature uniformity and distortion reduction. This subdivision falls within the broader CPC class F27D for machining and metal-working processes.
[F27D 2009/0091] in a fluidised bed of cooling elements -- F27D 2009/0091 classifies heat-exchange apparatus that uses a fluidised bed of cooling elements, wherein solid particles are suspended in a gas or liquid to create a highly turbulent medium that enhances heat transfer and provides uniform cooling of the process fluid.
[F27D 2009/0094] making use of already cooled material, e.g. forming a layer -- The CPC code F27D 2009/0094 refers to processes that employ material which has already been cooled, such as depositing or forming a layer of the cooled substance onto a surface. It falls within the heat-exchange and boiler technology subclass, covering methods that reuse or apply pre-cooled media in manufacturing or treatment steps.
[F27D 2009/0097] the charge being used as a coolant -- F27D 2009/0097 covers inventions in which the material being processed-typically a molten metal charge-is also employed as a coolant, for example in casting or metal-working apparatus where the charge absorbs heat from the surrounding tooling or mold. It pertains to designs that integrate the charge's cooling function into the overall process.
[F27D 2013/007] using a rotary furnace -- The CPC entry F27D 2013/007 covers methods or processes that employ a rotary furnace, a cylindrical rotating heating chamber, for treating materials such as calcining, sintering, melting or heat-treating in a continuous or batch operation. It includes any specific use of the furnace's rotation to improve temperature uniformity, material handling or reaction efficiency.
[F27D 2015/0226] Support, fixation of the grate -- F27D 2015/0226 designates inventions that provide a support or fixing arrangement for a grate, including frames, brackets, clamps or mounting structures that hold the grate in a combustion chamber, furnace, incinerator or similar apparatus.
[F27D 2015/0233] with gas, e.g. air, supply to the grate -- This CPC group covers heating or combustion apparatus in which a gas, such as air, is supplied directly to the grate of a furnace, boiler, stove or similar device to provide combustion air or to enhance the burning of solid fuel.
[F27D 2015/024] Multiple grates -- The CPC subclass F27D 2015/024 pertains to heating or combustion apparatus that incorporate multiple grates for supporting fuel or regulating airflow. It covers designs where two or more grates are arranged in series or parallel to improve combustion efficiency, heat distribution, or cleaning access in furnaces, ovens, incinerators and similar devices.
[F27D 2015/0246] Combination of fixed and movable grates -- The CPC subclass F27D 2015/0246 covers devices that combine stationary and adjustable grating elements, typically used in screens, shutters or ventilation systems to allow variable airflow or light transmission while maintaining structural support. This design integrates fixed bars with movable sections that can be opened, closed or angled as needed.
[F27D 2015/0253] Gradin grates -- F27D 2015/0253 covers gradin grates, a type of grate used in heating and heat-exchange apparatus such as furnaces or boilers. The design provides a grid-like support for solid fuel, allowing airflow beneath the fuel bed and facilitating ash removal for efficient combustion.
[F27D 2015/026] Means to discharge the fines -- F27D 2015/026 pertains to mechanisms that remove or discharge fine particles or precipitates from a vessel, tank or separator, such as skimmers, conveyors, pumps or discharge pipes. The claim focuses on structural features that enable the fines to be separated from the process fluid and expelled.
[F27D 2015/0293] including rotating parts -- F27D 2015/0293 refers to pumps that incorporate rotating components such as impellers, rotors or shafts, where the rotation is integral to the fluid-moving function. The subgroup is used to classify inventions that describe the design, operation or arrangement of these rotating parts within pump assemblies.
[F27D 2019/0003] Monitoring the temperature or a characteristic of the charge and using it as a controlling value -- This CPC subclass F27D 2019/0003 covers devices that monitor temperature or other charge characteristics and use that information as a control parameter. It applies to systems where the temperature or a property of a stored energy source is measured and fed back to regulate operation, such as battery management or thermal control.
[F27D 2019/0006] Monitoring the characteristics (composition, quantities, temperature, pressure) of at least one of the gases of the kiln atmosphere and using it as a controlling value -- The entry relates to systems and methods that monitor one or more characteristics of gases in a kiln atmosphere-such as composition, amount, temperature or pressure-and employ the measured values as control inputs for regulating kiln operation.
[F27D 2019/0009] Monitoring the pressure in an enclosure or kiln zone -- The code refers to devices, methods or systems that measure and track the pressure inside a heating enclosure such as a furnace, kiln or similar zone. It covers pressure sensors, transducers, gauges and associated control or safety circuitry used to monitor and regulate internal pressure during operation.
[F27D 2019/0012] Monitoring the composition of the atmosphere or of one of their components -- F27D 2019/0012 covers devices and methods for monitoring the composition of the atmosphere or any individual gas component, such as detecting pollutants, measuring oxygen, carbon-dioxide, or other gases, and providing real-time data for environmental or process control applications. It includes sensors, analysers, sampling systems and associated electronic or software components used in air-quality monitoring, emissions control and related fields.
[F27D 2019/0015] Monitoring the composition of the exhaust gases or of one of its components -- The CPC code F27D 2019/0015 covers techniques for measuring the composition of exhaust gases or individual constituents, typically to monitor engine performance, fuel efficiency, or emissions compliance. It includes sensors, analyzers, and control systems that detect gases such as CO, NOx, CO2, hydrocarbons, or oxygen in the exhaust stream.
[F27D 2019/0018] Monitoring the temperature of the atmosphere of the kiln -- The CPC subclass F27D 2019/0018 covers technologies for measuring and monitoring the temperature of the atmosphere inside a kiln, including sensors, control units and data-processing methods used to ensure uniform heating in processes such as ceramics, metallurgy or material treatment.
[F27D 2019/0021] Monitoring the temperature of the exhaust gases -- The code F27D 2019/0021 covers devices that measure the temperature of exhaust gases in internal-combustion engines or industrial furnaces, providing data for emission control, performance optimisation, or safety monitoring. It includes sensors, thermocouples or infrared detectors integrated into exhaust systems and the associated signal processing or display units. This subclass is part of the broader group for exhaust-gas treatment and monitoring technologies.
[F27D 2019/0025] Monitoring the temperature of a part or of an element of the furnace structure -- The code refers to technologies that sense or record the temperature of a furnace component or any structural element within the furnace. It includes temperature-sensitive devices, sensors, thermocouples, infrared or other measuring means, and the associated data-acquisition or control systems that enable monitoring of the part's thermal condition.
[F27D 2019/0028] Regulation -- F27D 2019/0028 covers patent literature that relates to regulatory matters for rockets, missiles, torpedoes and other projectile propulsion systems, including safety standards, environmental requirements, certification procedures and compliance guidelines governing their design, testing, manufacture and operation.
[F27D 2019/0031] through control of the flow of the exhaust gases -- F27D 2019/0031 covers inventions that regulate the flow of exhaust gases, including devices or methods that adjust flow rate, direction or pressure by means of valves, dampers, variable-geometry components or control circuits, typically to improve combustion efficiency, reduce emissions or protect downstream equipment.
[F27D 2019/0034] through control of a heating quantity such as fuel, oxidant or intensity of current -- F27D 2019/0034 covers devices or methods that regulate the amount of heat generated by adjusting the supply of fuel, oxidant, or the intensity of an electric current. The classification applies to heating systems where precise temperature control is required, such as furnaces, boilers, and electric heaters.
[F27D 2019/0037] Quantity of electric current -- The CPC entry F27D 2019/0037 covers inventions that deal with the quantity of electric current, including devices and methods for measuring, monitoring, regulating or controlling the amount of current flowing in a circuit. Typical subject matter includes current sensors, meters, control circuits and related calibration techniques.
[F27D 2019/004] Fuel quantity -- F27D 2019/004 refers to inventions that determine, measure or regulate the amount of fuel supplied to an internal-combustion engine or other fuel system. It covers sensors, metering devices, control units and related methods used to monitor or adjust fuel quantity for performance, efficiency or emissions management. This subclass is part of the Cooperative Patent Classification system used by the EPO and USPTO.
[F27D 2019/0043] Amount of air or O 2  to the burner -- This subclass covers devices or methods for measuring, controlling, or adjusting the quantity of air or oxygen supplied to a burner in combustion systems. It includes sensors, regulators, feedback loops and metering arrangements used to achieve desired flame characteristics, efficiency or emissions. The classification applies to systems that vary the air-fuel ratio for performance optimization.
[F27D 2019/0046] Amount of secondary air to the burner -- The code F27D 2019/0046 relates to devices or methods that regulate the amount of secondary air introduced into a burner. It covers mechanisms for adjusting airflow to improve combustion efficiency, reduce emissions, and stabilize the flame in heating or power-generation systems.
[F27D 2019/005] Amount of heat given to the charge via a controlled heat exchanger -- This CPC group covers inventions that specify or control the quantity of thermal energy supplied to a material (the charge) by means of a heat exchanger whose heat transfer is regulated. It includes devices, sensors or control strategies that determine or limit the heat given to the charge to achieve desired temperature profiles.
[F27D 2019/0053] involving heating the charge in a fluid or fluidised medium -- F27D 2019/0053 refers to inventions in which the material to be processed is heated while suspended in a fluid or a fluidised bed, the fluid itself providing the thermal energy. The classification is used for processes such as fluid-bed combustion, melting, sintering or drying where the charge receives heat directly from the surrounding fluid medium.
[F27D 2019/0056] Regulation involving cooling -- F27D 2019/0056 pertains to devices and methods that control cooling processes by regulating temperature, such as thermostats, control circuits and feedback systems that adjust cooling output in response to temperature changes in mechanical, hydraulic or electronic equipment. It covers regulation mechanisms for maintaining desired temperature ranges.
[F27D 2019/0059] Regulation involving the control of the conveyor movement, e.g. speed or sequences -- F27D 2019/0059 relates to systems and methods that regulate conveyor motion, including control of speed, acceleration, deceleration and movement sequences, using electronic, pneumatic, hydraulic or mechanical devices to manage the flow of material in handling operations.
[F27D 2019/0062] control of the workpiece stay in different zones -- F27D 2019/0062 relates to metal-working equipment that controls the position of a workpiece by keeping it in separate, defined zones during processing, allowing different forces, tools or treatments to be applied to each zone for precise shaping or forming.
[F27D 2019/0065] Regulation involving controlled section modification -- F27D 2019/0065 classifies inventions that provide a regulation method or device for modifying a defined section of a mechanical system such as an engine, turbine or compressor, where the alteration is actively controlled to achieve desired performance or safety outcomes. The entry covers feedback-based or preset control schemes that adjust geometry, flow or operating conditions of the targeted section.
[F27D 2019/0068] Regulation involving a measured inflow of a particular gas in the enclosure -- The entry covers systems and methods for controlling the measured inflow of a specified gas into an enclosed space, including sensors, regulators and feedback mechanisms that maintain a predetermined flow rate or pressure within the enclosure. It applies to installations where precise gas dosing is required for processes such as combustion, atmosphere control or safety venting.
[F27D 2019/0071] Regulation using position sensors -- This subclass covers methods and devices that control fans, blowers or similar rotating or adjustable components by using sensors that detect the position of shafts, blades, vanes or other moving parts, providing feedback to adjust speed, pitch or flow to maintain a set condition.
[F27D 2019/0075] Regulation of the charge quantity -- F27D 2019/0075 covers methods and devices for controlling the amount of electric charge delivered to a load, including feedback regulation, charge monitoring, and protection circuits in power electronics and battery systems.
[F27D 2019/0078] Regulation of the speed of the gas through the charge -- This CPC subclass concerns the control of gas flow rate within the active region of gas-discharge devices. It includes mechanisms that adjust pressure, restrict apertures, or use magnetic/electric fields to regulate the speed of the gas passing through the charge, thereby influencing discharge behavior and performance.
[F27D 2019/0081] Controlling an air-lock chamber -- This subclass covers methods and devices for regulating the operation of an air-lock chamber in rotating machinery, including control of pressure, flow and sealing to manage material transfer between environments while maintaining system integrity.
[F27D 2019/0084] Controlling closure systems, e.g. doors -- F27D deals with doors, windows and associated closure mechanisms. The sub-entry 2019/0084 specifically covers systems that control the opening, closing or locking of closures such as doors, including actuators, sensors and automated control logic. It addresses both manual and powered solutions for managing access and security.
[F27D 2019/0087] Automatisation of the whole plant or activity -- This subgroup covers systems that automate an entire industrial plant or activity, integrating control, monitoring, data acquisition and operational functions into a unified solution. It includes full-plant automation concepts, central control rooms, and integrated SCADA or PLC architectures that manage all processes of the plant as a whole.
[F27D 2019/009] Use of codes, e.g. bar codes -- A method or device that reads, generates or applies identification codes such as bar codes to items for purposes including sorting, tracking, inventory control or data capture, typically in industrial, retail or logistical environments. This entry covers the use of such codes in automated or manual systems to identify and manage objects.
[F27D 2019/0093] Maintaining a temperature gradient -- The CPC subclass F27D 2019/0093 pertains to devices or methods that create, preserve, or control a temperature difference across a system, such as thermal insulation, heat-pipe arrangements, active cooling or heating elements, and other means of maintaining a prescribed temperature gradient for improved energy efficiency or process control.
[F27D 2019/0096] involving simulation means, e.g. of the treating or charging step -- The CPC subclass F27D 2019/0096 covers inventions that employ simulation means to model or emulate the treating or charging steps of metalworking processes such as heating, melting, or alloying. It includes computer models, virtual prototypes or other digital tools that predict temperature distribution, material flow or energy consumption during these steps.
[F27D 2021/0007] Monitoring the pressure -- F27D 2021/0007 pertains to devices or methods for monitoring pressure in fluid-power or hydraulic systems, covering sensors, transducers and related control units that detect pressure variations to ensure safe operation.
[F27D 2021/0042] Monitoring the level of the solid charge -- F27D 2021/0042 refers to technologies that detect or measure the quantity of solid fuel within a furnace, boiler, or combustion chamber. It includes sensors, probes or monitoring systems used to control feed rates, ensure safe operation and improve combustion efficiency.
[F27D 2021/005] Devices for monitoring thermal expansion -- The CPC subclass F27D 2021/005 covers devices that detect, measure or monitor the thermal expansion of materials or structures. Typical examples include strain gauges, interferometric sensors, displacement transducers and electronic systems that provide real-time data on dimensional changes caused by temperature variations for use in engineering, construction and manufacturing.
[F27D 2021/0057] Security or safety devices, e.g. for protection against heat, noise, pollution or too much duress;  Ergonomic aspects -- The F27D 2021/0057 entry covers devices and methods that enhance personal safety or environmental protection, such as shielding against heat, noise, pollution or excessive stress, with attention to ergonomic design to reduce user strain. It includes protective equipment, barriers and other safety systems that improve comfort and health.
[F27D 2021/0064] against noises -- F27D 2021/0064 covers technical solutions for mitigating noise produced by steam turbines and similar rotating machines, including acoustic enclosures, silencers, dampers, vibration-isolating mounts and other measures that reduce sound emission and protect the surrounding environment.
[F27D 2021/0071] against explosions -- F27D 2021/0071 covers technologies and methods that protect against explosions, including blast-mitigation devices, explosion-suppressing systems, and safety measures designed to prevent or limit the effects of accidental detonations in industrial, transport or storage environments. It encompasses apparatus such as pressure relief valves, rupture discs, blast shields and active suppression mechanisms that detect and neutralise explosive events. This subclass is used for inventions aimed at enhancing safety and reducing damage from unintended explosions.
[F27D 2021/0078] against the presence of an undesirable element in the atmosphere of the furnace -- The subclass covers methods, devices or control measures that prevent or remove unwanted components from the atmosphere inside a furnace during operation, such as inert gas shielding, sealing, scavenging or purification to avoid contaminants that could affect the process or damage equipment. It falls under CPC group F27D for furnaces and relates specifically to managing the furnace atmosphere.
[F27D 2021/0085] against molten metal, e.g. leakage or splashes -- F27D 2021/0085 covers protective arrangements that prevent molten metal from escaping, leaking or splashing during handling, transport or casting. It includes splash guards, curtains, hoods, deflectors and other shielding devices that isolate the molten metal from the surrounding environment or personnel.
[F27D 2021/0092] against a jam in the transport line or a production interruption -- F27D 2021/0092 covers measures and devices for preventing or clearing jams in transport or conveyor lines to avoid production interruptions, including sensors, control systems and mechanical interventions that detect blockages and restore flow.
[F27D 2021/023] Closable inserting openings, e.g. for the introduction of lances, sensors or burners -- This subclass of the CPC covers apparatuses with openings that can be closed after inserting devices such as lances, sensors, or burners, typically found in heating, combustion, or process equipment. The design ensures safe insertion and sealing of the inserted element.
[F27D 2021/026] using a video installation -- The CPC entry F27D 2021/026 falls under subclass F27D for dispensing or feeding of liquids and introduces the specific concept of using a video installation in connection with such apparatuses. It covers technologies that integrate video displays or projection systems to monitor, control, or enhance the operation of liquid dispensing equipment.
[F27D 2027/002] Gas stirring -- F27D 2027/002 covers apparatus or processes that stir a liquid by introducing a gas stream, typically through jets, nozzles or bubbles, to create agitation, mixing or dispersion. It includes designs where the gas flow itself generates the stirring action without moving mechanical parts, such as aerators, gas-induced mixers and similar devices.
[F27D 2099/0008] Resistor heating -- F27D 2099/0008 covers resistor heating, where electrical resistance is deliberately used to produce heat for industrial or domestic purposes. It includes heating elements that convert electric current into thermal energy, such as those in ovens, water heaters, and process-heat equipment, and pertains to the design and operation of such resistive devices.
[F27D 2099/001] the container being the resistor -- F27D 2099/001 covers containers that themselves function as electrical resistors, such as vessels designed to generate heat by passing current through their walls or internal components. The classification includes designs where the container material or structure provides resistance for heating or temperature-control purposes.
[F27D 2099/0011] The resistor heats a radiant tube or surface -- F27D 2099/0011 refers to devices in which an electrical resistor is used to heat a radiant tube or surface, typically for infrared heating or drying applications. The classification covers designs where the resistor directly supplies thermal energy to the tube or surface to generate radiant heat.
[F27D 2099/0013] immersed in the charge -- This subgroup of the Cooperative Patent Classification F27D concerns pumps whose rotating element is placed directly within the fluid being moved, i.e., the impeller or rotor is immersed in the charge. It includes designs where the pump housing acts as the charge container and is relevant for low-speed or high-temperature fluid handling.
[F27D 2099/0015] Induction heating -- F27D 2099/0015 covers the use of electromagnetic induction to generate heat in a workpiece for processes such as melting, forging, or surface treatment. It includes devices, power supplies and control methods that create alternating magnetic fields to heat conductive materials without direct contact. The subclass is part of the broader group for heating by indirect methods.
[F27D 2099/0016] Different magnetic fields, e.g. two coils, different characteristics of the same coil along its length or different parts of the same coil used -- The code refers to rotating electrical machines that use separate or varying magnetic fields, such as employing two distinct coils or a single coil whose magnetic properties change along its length or differ in separate sections, enabling different field characteristics for control, torque distribution or efficiency improvements.
[F27D 2099/0018] Relative movement of the coil and the charge during the heat treatment -- F27D 2099/0018 covers heat-treating operations in which the heating coil and the charge move relative to each other, such as by rotating, oscillating, or transporting either the coil or the material, to obtain uniform temperature distribution or specific heating profiles.
[F27D 2099/002] Core heating -- This subclass covers inventions whose main purpose is to heat the internal core of a boiler, furnace, heat-exchanger or similar thermal device, including methods, arrangements or control means for applying heat to the core to improve temperature uniformity, efficiency or to prevent scale buildup.
[F27D 2099/0021] Arc heating -- F27D 2099/0021 covers the use of electric arcs to generate heat for processing or treating materials. It includes devices in which a high-temperature plasma arc directly transfers energy to a target such as metals, ceramics or liquids, allowing rapid heating without external burners.
[F27D 2099/0023] DC arc heating -- F27D 2099/0023 covers processes that employ a direct-current electric arc to generate heat for melting, cutting, or treating materials, typically in metalworking or waste processing. The classification includes systems where the arc is sustained by a DC power source and applied to a workpiece to achieve localized high temperatures.
[F27D 2099/0025] Currents through the charge -- F27D 2099/0025 covers features related to detecting, measuring or controlling electric currents that pass through a charge element such as a battery, capacitor or similar storage device within an electric machine or power system. It includes apparatus or methods for monitoring the flow of charge to improve performance, safety or efficiency of the overall system.
[F27D 2099/0026] with a generator of electromagnetic radiations -- F27D 2099/0026 covers propulsion or power units that incorporate a generator of electromagnetic radiation, such as microwave, radio-frequency or optical emitters, integrated with turbines, engines, reactors or similar machines for purposes like communication, sensing, or wireless power transfer. It includes arrangements where the radiation source is part of the same assembly that produces mechanical thrust or electrical output.
[F27D 2099/0028] Microwave heating -- The subclass F27D 2099/0028 covers inventions that employ microwave energy to produce heat for processing, drying, cooking, or other treatment of materials. It includes apparatuses, methods and control systems that generate, guide or apply microwaves to achieve the desired heating effect.
[F27D 2099/003] Bombardment heating, e.g. with ions or electrons -- The code refers to heating techniques that employ the bombardment of a workpiece with charged particles such as ions or electrons. This method transfers kinetic energy of the particles to the material, raising its temperature for purposes like surface modification, coating, or material synthesis. It falls under special heating processes in the industrial heating and heat treatment subclass.
[F27D 2099/0031] Plasma-torch heating -- F27D 2099/0031 covers the use of plasma torches as a heating source in industrial furnaces or other heating apparatus, where a high-temperature plasma jet is directed onto a workpiece for melting, surface treatment or other thermal processes.
[F27D 2099/0036] immersed in the charge -- F27D 2099/0036 relates to pumps whose rotating components, such as the impeller or rotor, are situated directly within the fluid being moved, meaning the machinery is immersed in the charge. This sub-category is used for submersible pump designs where the pump operates underwater or in other liquids without external housing.
[F27D 2099/0038] removable -- F27D 2099/0038 falls under CPC section F for mechanical engineering, subclass D dealing with fans and ventilating devices, group 2099 for other fans, and designates fan constructions that incorporate removable parts such as blades, housings, covers or motor assemblies to allow easy disassembly for cleaning, inspection or replacement.
[F27D 2099/004] directed upon the charge, e.g. vertically -- This subclass concerns devices or methods that apply the force directly onto a charge, typically oriented vertically, for initiating or controlling the charge in earth-moving or blasting equipment. It deals with the way the charge is directed rather than the surrounding mechanisms.
[F27D 2099/0041] with a small angle, e.g. almost tangentially -- F27D 2099/0041 covers pump configurations in which the fluid enters or the impeller blades are inclined at a very small angle relative to the rotary axis, essentially almost tangential to the surface. This subclass groups designs that use such shallow angles to influence flow direction, pressure rise, or efficiency, typically found in specialized centrifugal or mixed-flow pumps.
[F27D 2099/0043] Impulse burner -- Impulse burners are combustion devices that generate heat by delivering fuel in short, high-pressure pulses rather than a continuous flow, creating rapid flame ignition and extinguishment. The classification covers the design, operation and control of such intermittent-burning systems for heating, metalworking, waste treatment or power generation.
[F27D 2099/0045] Radiant burner -- F27D 2099/0045 relates to radiant burners, devices that generate heat primarily by radiation rather than convection. These burners are used in industrial, commercial or domestic heating applications where a high-intensity heat source is required, often employing fuels such as gas, oil or coal.
[F27D 2099/0046] with incomplete combustion, e.g. reducing atmosphere -- F27D 2099/0046 covers processes or equipment that operate under incomplete combustion conditions, such as reducing atmospheres, where insufficient oxygen leads to partial oxidation of materials. It includes methods for heat treatment, sintering or material modification that rely on these low-oxygen environments.
[F27D 2099/0048] Post- combustion -- F27D 2099/0048 covers methods and apparatuses applied after the primary combustion stage to treat the resulting gases, such as catalytic or thermal processes that reduce pollutants, recover heat, or modify composition before discharge. It includes post-combustion flue-gas cleaning, selective catalytic reduction, and regenerative thermal oxidisers.
[F27D 2099/005] Catalytic burner -- F27D 2099/005 covers burners in which a catalyst is employed to promote combustion of gaseous or liquid fuels at reduced temperatures, improving efficiency and reducing emissions. The subclass includes designs where the catalyst forms part of the burner assembly for industrial, commercial or residential heating applications.
[F27D 2099/0051] Burning waste as a fuel -- The classification falls under F27D which covers combustion apparatus for solid fuels, sub-group 2099/0051 relates to processes where waste material is incinerated for energy recovery. It includes techniques for using municipal solid waste, industrial residues, or biomass waste as fuel in furnaces, boilers, or kilns, focusing on combustion methods and emission control.
[F27D 2099/0053] Burner fed with preheated gases -- F27D 2099/0053 relates to burners that receive combustion air or other oxidising gases after they have been heated, typically using recuperators, heat exchangers or similar devices to raise flame temperature, improve efficiency or lower emissions. The classification covers the design and operation of such pre-heated-gas burner systems.
[F27D 2099/0055] Fuel -- F27D 2099/0055 covers inventions relating to the supply, handling, storage or control of fuel for pumps, fans, compressors or related fluid-moving devices. It includes fuel-feeding lines, tanks, injectors, metering, and other systems that provide the energy source needed for the operation of these machines.
[F27D 2099/0056] Oxidant -- F27D 2099/0056 classifies devices or methods that incorporate an oxidant into pneumatic conveying, ventilation or fan systems, typically to promote combustion, drying, or chemical treatment of the conveyed material or air stream. It covers the introduction, generation or use of oxidizing agents within such fluid-handling equipment.
[F27D 2099/0058] Means for heating the charge locally -- F27D 2099/0058 relates to apparatus that provides localized heating of the material being processed within a furnace, reactor or similar vessel. It covers devices such as resistance elements, induction coils, burners or other energy-transfer means positioned to raise the temperature of a specific portion of the charge rather than the whole chamber. This enables precise thermal control for processes like metal melting, sintering or waste treatment.
[F27D 2099/006] Auxiliary heating, e.g. in special conditions or at special times -- The CPC subclass F27D 2099/006 covers auxiliary heating arrangements that are employed only under special conditions or at particular times, such as supplemental heating during low ambient temperatures, standby periods, or intermittent operation. It includes devices and control methods that activate secondary heat sources to support the primary heating system when needed.
[F27D 2099/0061] Indirect heating -- The CPC subclass F27D 2099/0061 covers indirect heating techniques used in industrial furnaces and heat-treating equipment, where the material to be processed is heated by a medium such as a hot gas, liquid, or solid that transfers heat without direct contact with the heat source. It includes designs for heat exchangers, radiant panels, and other arrangements that provide controlled temperature rise without exposing the workpiece to the combustion flame or heating element.
[F27D 2099/0063] Liquid -- F27D 2099/0063 is a CPC sub-group that groups patents dealing with the pumping, compression or displacement of liquids using devices such as pumps, compressors, fans or blowers. It includes any technical features that are specific to liquid media rather than gases or solids.
[F27D 2099/0065] Gas -- F27D 2099/0065 covers inventions dealing with the handling, storage or movement of gases in equipment such as compressors, pumps, valves or pipelines, typically applied in refrigeration, air-conditioning, or industrial gas systems.
[F27D 2099/0066] Particles or solid bodies, e.g. bed of particles -- The code covers inventions that involve handling, treating or using particulate solids or solid bodies, such as a bed of particles, within fluidised, mixed or stirred processes. It includes apparatus, methods or arrangements where the particles themselves form a functional medium for reaction, separation, heat transfer or transport.
[F27D 2099/0068] Heating directly by circulation of a fluid other than a gas or by particles -- The code F27D 2099/0068 classifies heating devices that generate heat by directly moving a non-gas fluid-such as liquids or solid particles-through the system to transfer thermal energy. It covers arrangements where the heat source and the heated medium are connected by circulation of the fluid or particles, without intermediate combustion gases, and includes pumps, heat exchangers, and distribution networks for liquid or particulate heat carriers.
[F27D 2099/0071] Partitions used as registers -- Partitions that double as air registers are structural elements designed to both divide spaces and regulate the flow of air or gases through them, often incorporating louvers, flaps or adjustable openings. They are used in ventilation, HVAC and fire-exhaust systems where space-saving integration of airflow control into walls, ceilings or other partitions is required.
[F27D 2099/0078] Means to minimize the leakage of the furnace atmosphere during charging or discharging -- F27D 2099/0078 covers devices or methods that reduce the escape of furnace gases when the furnace is being loaded or emptied. It includes seals, gas curtains, protective atmospheres and loading mechanisms designed to keep the internal environment intact during charging or discharge operations.
[F27D 2099/008] Using an air-lock -- The code F27D 2099/008 classifies fire-extinguishing installations that incorporate an air-lock, a sealed chamber used to isolate a space or control airflow during firefighting. It covers designs that prevent smoke or fire spread by temporarily closing off sections of a building or vessel.
[F27D 2099/0081] Joint -- F27D 2099/0081 covers joints employed in refrigerating and cooling appliances, including connections, seals, couplings and other fastening features that link separate parts of the equipment. The entry is used for inventions that describe or improve such joints in refrigerators, freezers, ice makers and related devices.
[F27D 2099/0083] Drives; Auxiliary drives -- F27D 2099/0083 relates to auxiliary drive arrangements for pumps, covering secondary power-transmission devices, gear or hydraulic assistance, coupling and other supplemental mechanisms that support or control the main pump drive. It is used for inventions that improve start-up, speed regulation, torque multiplication or backup operation of pumping systems.
[F27D 2099/0085] Accessories -- F27D 2099/0085 covers accessories for fans and blowers that are not part of the main moving or rotating components. This includes mounting brackets, protective guards, control panels and other ancillary fittings designed to support, protect or improve the operation of the fan or blower.
[F27D 2099/0086] Filters, e.g. for molten metals -- F27D 2099/0086 covers filters used in handling molten metals, such as screens, porous plates or similar devices that remove slag, oxides, gases or solid inclusions from the melt during casting, pouring or lubrication processes.
[F27D 2099/0088] Apparatus to cut metal, e.g. logs, in billets -- F27D 2099/0088 covers apparatus designed to cut metal pieces such as logs or billets, typically using shears, saws or cutting heads that act on the material while it is held in a fixed or guided position. The subclass includes devices that process metal in bundled or rod form, focusing on the cutting mechanism rather than feeding or handling stages. It falls within the broader F27D group for metal-cutting machines.
[F27D 2099/009] Bumpers -- F27D 2099/009 pertains to protective bumpers used on fans, blowers or similar ventilating equipment, designed to shield rotating blades or housings from impact, reduce vibration and prevent damage during operation or handling. It covers all forms of bumper devices that are attached to or integrated into such mechanical air-movement devices.
[F27D 2099/0091] Means to grease or oil -- The CPC subclass F27D 2099/0091 covers mechanisms and devices that apply grease or oil to moving parts, such as greasing guns, oil dispensers, automatic lubricators and similar means for delivering lubricants during operation or maintenance. It is used to classify inventions that provide the delivery system rather than the lubricant itself.
[F27D 2099/0093] Means to collect ashes or dust, e.g. vessels -- F27D 2099/0093 covers devices that collect ash or dust generated during fire-extinguishing or related processes, such as containers, trays or other vessels designed to capture and hold the residue.
[F27D 2099/0095] Means to collect the slag or spilled metal, e.g. vessels -- This subclass includes devices designed to gather and contain molten metal, slag or other liquid metal waste during casting or forging processes. Typical examples are collection vessels, troughs or channels that direct the material away from the work area for reuse or disposal.
[F27D 2099/0096] Presses -- F27D 2099/0096 - Presses: devices that apply pressure to shape, compress, or form materials, including hydraulic, mechanical, or pneumatic presses used in metalworking, woodworking, plastics, and other manufacturing processes. The class includes full-stroke, incremental, and stamping presses as well as specialized equipment for embossing, forging, or laminate production.
[F27D 2099/0098] Means for moving the furnace -- F27D 2099/0098 covers devices that relocate a furnace during operation or maintenance, such as rollers, tracks, jacks, wheels or other mechanical systems that enable sliding, rolling or lifting of the furnace structure. The classification is applied to inventions that provide means for moving the furnace to facilitate positioning, cleaning, repair or integration with other processes.
[F27D 21/00] Arrangement of monitoring devices -- The CPC class F27D 21/00 covers the layout and integration of sensors, gauges or other monitoring equipment in furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts, focusing on how these devices are positioned, mounted and connected to enable temperature, pressure or atmosphere measurement across different furnace types. It includes designs that are applicable to more than one furnace kind.
[F27D 21/0014] Devices for monitoring temperature -- The entry F27D 21/0014 covers devices used to monitor temperature in various furnace types, including kilns, ovens and retorts, focusing on the arrangement of temperature sensors and related instrumentation. It falls under the CPC group for details or accessories of furnaces that are applicable to more than one kind of furnace.
[F27D 21/0021] Devices for monitoring linings for wear -- F27D 21/0021 refers to devices arranged to monitor the condition of furnace linings against wear. The classification covers any sensing or diagnostic system - such as temperature, thickness or corrosion probes - installed in furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts, where the arrangement is applicable to more than one type of furnace. These devices help detect degradation early to schedule maintenance and prevent failure.
[F27D 21/0028] Devices for monitoring the level of the melt -- The subclass F27D 21/0028 covers devices that detect or indicate the level of molten material inside furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts. It includes sensors, probes, visual or electronic indicators and related systems used to monitor melt height for process control and safety.
[F27D 21/0035] Devices for monitoring the weight of quantities added to the charge -- This subclass covers apparatus that measures the mass of material added to a furnace, kiln, oven or retort during charging, allowing precise control of the charge amount. It includes weigh-scales, load cells, electronic or mechanical sensors and related circuitry that monitor weight in real time.
[F27D 21/02] Observation or illuminating devices -- F27D 21/02 covers the layout and integration of observation or illumination equipment used to monitor the interior of furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts, applicable to any type of furnace where such devices are common. It deals with how these devices are arranged within the heating chamber.
[F27D 21/04] Arrangement of indicators or alarms -- The CPC class F27D 21/04 covers the design and layout of monitoring equipment such as indicators, gauges, or alarm systems that are installed on furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts. It addresses how these devices are positioned, connected and integrated to provide real-time information or warnings about temperature, pressure or other operating conditions. This subclass applies to accessories used across multiple furnace types.
[F27D 2201/00] Manipulation of furnace parts -- F27D 2201/00 covers methods and apparatus for handling, moving, loading, unloading or positioning components inside furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts. It applies to any type of furnace where such manipulation of parts is required during operation.
[F27D 25/00] Devices for removing incrustations -- F27D 25/00 relates to devices for removing incrustations, i.e., scale, slag or other deposits, from the interior surfaces of furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts. These cleaning or stripping mechanisms are applicable to multiple furnace types and are considered accessories or details common across furnace families.
[F27D 25/001] comprising breaking tools, e.g. hammers, drills, scrapers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices for removing incrustations, e.g. slag, metal deposits, dust, comprising breaking tools like hammers, drills, scrapers.
[F27D 25/003] used for punching tuyeres -- F27D 25/003 covers devices that punch or drill tuyeres, the air or fuel openings in furnace walls, to remove scale or other incrustations. The subclass applies to tools used for creating or cleaning these openings in any type of furnace.
[F27D 25/005] used for cleaning the channels of induction furnaces -- The CPC code F27D 25/005 designates devices that remove incrustations and clean the channels of induction furnaces; it falls under the subclass for details or accessories of furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts that are common to multiple furnace types.
[F27D 25/006] using explosives -- F27D 25/006 concerns the use of explosives to remove scale, slag or other incrustations from the interior surfaces or components of furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts. It falls within the CPC group for accessories that are common to more than one type of furnace.
[F27D 25/008] using fluids or gases, e.g. blowers, suction units -- F27D 25/008 covers devices that remove deposits or incrustations from furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts by using fluid or gas flow, such as blowers, suction units or other pneumatic cleaning equipment. It is classified under accessories common to multiple furnace types in the CPC hierarchy.
[F27D 27/00] Stirring devices for molten material -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Charging or discharging liquid or molten material -> F27D3/14
[F27D 27/005] Pumps -- F27D 27/005 covers pumps used as stirring devices for molten material in furnaces, kilns, ovens, or retorts that are common to more than one type of furnace. The class falls under the CPC hierarchy for mechanical engineering, furnaces, and details or accessories of furnaces.
[F27D 27/007] Pulsating pumps -- A pulsating pump is a stirring device for molten material used in furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts, where the pump delivers periodic flow pulses to mix or circulate the melt. It falls under the mechanical-engineering group for accessories common to multiple furnace types.
[F27D 3/00] Charging -- F27D 3/00 pertains to devices and mechanisms that introduce material into furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts, used across various furnace types. It includes charging bins, hoppers, conveyors and other accessories that feed raw material for heating or processing, addressing the handling and delivery of the charge to the furnace interior.
[F27D 3/0021] of ceramic ware -- The code F27D 3/0021 covers components used for charging furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts when the charging material consists of ceramic ware. It refers to accessories such as ladles, trays, bins or other handling devices specifically designed for loading and transporting ceramic pieces into the heating chamber. This subclass applies to any furnace type where ceramic ware is processed.
[F27D 3/0022] Disposition of the charge -- F27D 3/0022 concerns the handling of the material fed into furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts, describing how the charge is positioned, moved, spread or otherwise arranged within the heating chamber.
[F27D 3/0024] of metallic workpieces -- This classification covers devices or components used to introduce metallic workpieces into a furnace, kiln, oven or retort during the charging operation. It includes mechanisms such as charging arms, hoppers, conveyors, or other accessories that handle metal pieces for heating, melting or heat treatment.
[F27D 3/0025] Charging or loading melting furnaces with material in the solid state -- F27D 3/0025 covers methods and devices for introducing solid feedstock into melting furnaces, including the charging and loading of material in solid form. It relates to accessories and procedures used to fill furnaces such as blast, electric-arc, or induction furnaces.
[F27D 3/0026] Introducing additives into the melt -- The classification covers devices or procedures for feeding additives into a molten bath while a furnace is being charged. It includes systems that inject, sprinkle or otherwise introduce supplementary materials such as alloys, slag formers or chemical agents to control the melt's composition, temperature or reaction behaviour.
[F27D 3/0027] Charging vertically with corbs -- The code F27D 3/0027 covers the practice of introducing charge material into a furnace from the top using corbs, which are large lumps or blocks of the material. It relates to vertical charging methods that apply to various furnace types where such bulk handling is required. This falls under accessories and details of furnaces that are common to multiple furnace designs.
[F27D 3/0028] Corbs -- F27D 3/0028 covers corbs, a type of charging accessory for furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts that facilitates the introduction of bulk material into the heating chamber. The entry pertains to the design, structure and use of these devices for moving or holding material during the charging process.
[F27D 3/003] Charging laterally, e.g. with a charging box -- F27D 3/003 designates lateral feeding systems for furnaces, including devices such as charging boxes that introduce material from the side into the furnace cavity. This falls under details and accessories common to multiple furnace types.
[F27D 3/0031] Charging with tiltable dumpers -- F27D 3/0031 covers the use of tiltable dumpers for charging furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts, describing devices that tilt to discharge material into the vessel. The classification applies to such charging equipment when it is employed in more than one type of furnace.
[F27D 3/0032] using an air-lock -- F27D 3/0032 relates to the charging stage of furnaces, kilns, ovens or retorts and specifically covers the use of an air-lock as a detail or accessory. The air-lock enables material to be introduced while minimizing heat loss and preventing the entry of air, and is applicable to a variety of furnace types.
