This subclass pertains to apparatus where the two heat-exchange media remain separated by the conduit wall.
[F28D 1/05358] Assemblies of conduits connected side by side or with individual headers, e.g. section type radiators -- F28D 1/05358 pertains to heat-exchange apparatus with stationary conduit assemblies arranged side by side or linked to individual headers, as seen in section-type radiators, where one fluid circulates within the tubes while a second fluid flows around the tube bundle.
[F28D 1/05366] Assemblies of conduits connected to common headers, e.g. core type radiators -- F28D 1/05366 covers heat-exchange devices in which a network of stationary conduits is linked to common inlet and outlet headers, forming a core-type radiator. The design circulates one fluid through the conduits while the opposite side of the walls exchanges heat with a large body of fluid, such as coolant in automotive or domestic radiators.
[F28D 1/05375] with particular pattern of flow, e.g. change of flow direction  F28D1/05391  takes precedence -- Heat-exchange apparatus that uses stationary conduit assemblies containing a single heat-exchange medium, while the opposite side of the conduit wall contacts a large body of fluid such as a radiator fluid. The classification applies when the flow within the conduit follows a specific pattern, for example a change of flow direction, and is overridden by the broader subclass F28D 1/05391.
[F28D 1/05383] with multiple rows of conduits or with multi-channel conduits  F28D1/05391  takes precedence -- This subclass covers heat-exchange devices that use stationary conduit assemblies to transfer heat from one medium to a large body of fluid, such as radiators where the fluid circulates on the opposite side of the conduit walls. It specifically applies to configurations with multiple rows of conduits or multichannel conduits, and is superseded by subclass F28D 1/05391.
[F28D 1/05391] with multiple rows of conduits or with multi-channel conduits combined with a particular flow pattern, e.g. multi-row multi-stage radiators -- This subclass defines heat-exchange devices that use stationary conduit assemblies for a single heat-exchange medium, where the opposite side contacts a large body of fluid, and the conduits are arranged in multiple rows or multi-channel configurations with a specified flow pattern such as multi-stage radiators.
[F28D 1/06] with the heat-exchange conduits forming part of, or being attached to, the tank containing the body of fluid -- This CPC subclass concerns heat-exchange devices in which the heat-transfer pipes are built into or fastened to the container that holds the bulk fluid, such as radiators where the tubes form part of the coolant tank.
[F28D 11/00] Heat-exchange apparatus employing moving conduits -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Heat exchangers with movable conduits, e.g. by rotary or reciprocating movement. The heat exchanger can move as a whole, e.g. rotary drum or roller.  Heat exchangers with moving conduits, the movement being rotary, are classified in F28D11/02  Example:  US 5295533 A  A heat exchanger for exchanging heat between a first fluid and a second fluid comprises a moving partition member (4) for heat transmission between the first fluid and the second fluid, a housing partitioned by the moving partition member into a first chamber (7) for passing the first fluid and a second chamber (8) for passing the second fluid, and a power source for rotating the moving partition member.  Heat exchangers with rotating tubes are classified in F28D11/04  Example:  US4596286 A  Rotary processor (10) including a shaft (12) supported by a housing (14). Mounted to the shaft (12) are a plurality of hollow helical coils (18). A fluid flow path is established serially among a reservoir (22), an inlet conduit (24), the interior of the shaft (12), the coils (18), back through the shaft to an outlet conduit (26) and to the reservoir (22). The reservoir 22 preferably includes a pump and heat exchange means to cool or heat the circulated fluid. The housing 14 has an inlet 28 and an outlet 30. Upon rotation of the shaft (12) a fluid medium in the housing (14) is conveyed from the volume at the inlet (28) toward the volume at the outlet (30).
[F28D 11/02] the movement being rotary, e.g. performed by a drum or roller -- F28D 11/02 covers heat-exchange devices that use moving conduits whose motion is rotary, such as drums or rollers, to transport the heat-transfer medium while keeping it separated from the other medium. The rotary motion facilitates continuous heating or cooling without direct contact between the two fluids.
[F28D 11/025] Motor car radiators -- F28D 11/025 refers to motor car radiators, a type of heat-exchange apparatus that uses moving conduits to transfer heat from engine coolant to the surrounding air. The class falls under mechanical engineering, heat exchange in general, and devices where the heat-exchange media do not come into direct contact.
[F28D 11/04] performed by a tube or a bundle of tubes -- The CPC subclass F28D 11/04 covers heat-exchange apparatus that transfers heat using moving conduits, specifically a single tube or a bundle of tubes, where the heat-exchange media remain separated and do not come into direct contact. It falls under mechanical engineering, heat exchange in general, and devices not classified elsewhere.
[F28D 11/06] the movement being reciprocating or oscillating -- In the CPC subclass F28D 11/06 the focus is on heat-exchange devices that use conduits which move in a reciprocating or oscillating manner, rather than remaining stationary, to enhance heat transfer without direct contact between the media. This falls under mechanical engineering, heat exchange in general, and heat-exchange apparatus not covered elsewhere.
[F28D 11/08] more than one conduit assembly performing independent movements, e.g. rotary bundle of tubes in a rotary drum -- F28D 11/08 pertains to heat-exchange apparatus that employs several conduit assemblies each moving independently, for example a rotary drum containing a bundle of tubes that turn while heat is transferred. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering, heat exchange in general, and apparatus where the heat-exchange media do not come into direct contact.
[F28D 13/00] Heat-exchange apparatus using a fluidised bed -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Indirect contact fluidised bed heat exchangers, e.g. with one heat-exchange medium flowing through conduits, the conduits being in contact with particles which are fluidised by another medium. The bed can be a circulating bed.  Example:  US 4313301 A  Rotating fluidized bed heat exchanger (54) particularly for recuperator section of gas turbine engines comprising an annular fluidized bed (114), defined by inner and outer spaced apart coaxial cylindrical, perforated walls (106, 108), which rotates about the longitudinal axis of the cylinders. The bed is comprised of pulverulent inert particulate material and includes fluid-containing heat exchange tubes (120) passing substantially longitudinally therethrough. Hot gases, such as turbine exhaust gases, enter the bed through the outer perforated wall, heat and fluidize the bed particles, heat the fluid, usually compressed air, in the tubes, and exit the bed through the inner perforated wall.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Fluidised bed reactors -> B01J8/24 - Fluidised bed boilers -> F22B31/00 - Fluidised bed combustors -> F23C10/00 - Fluidised bed dryers -> F26B3/08
[F28D 15/00] Heat-exchange apparatus employing intermediate heat-transfer media or bodies -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Heat exchange apparatus employing intermediate heat-transfer medium or bodies ("closed tubes" means either a sealed container or a closed loop, containing a heat exchange medium; the feature "passing into or through the conduit walls" is disregarded).  Example:  US 2008196858 A  Heat exchanger for removing heat from coolant of refrigeration system, has pump (20) connected to an enclosed pipe (10) which includes a heat absorbing section (12) and a heat dispersing section (14). A fan (30) is located close to the dispersing section. The dispersing section has a set of curved sections, where a set of fins are connected to the dispersing section.  Heat pipes (the intermediate medium evaporates and condenses inside the sealed enclosure) The heat pipes can have a particular shape, e.g. flat tubes. the heat pipes can be formed as a loop, e.g. with separate evaporating and condensing chambers connected by conduits.  Heat pipes are classified in F28D15/02  Example:  US 6026889 A  Single shell boiler (10) including a closed vessel (12) for containing a liquid heat transfer medium (14). A first bundle (20) of heat source tubes is located in the closed vessel and is adapted to be submerged within the liquid heat transfer medium for heating and vaporizing the liquid heat transfer medium. A second bundle (30) of tubes is located in the closed vessel in a position which is adapted to be above the level of the liquid heat transfer medium within the vessel to receive heat by condensation of vaporized heat transfer medium on the second bundle of tubes. A condensate distribution plate (40) is positioned between the first and second bundles of tubes. The condensate distribution plate is adapted to approximately evenly distribute condensate droplets released by the second bundle of tubes to break-up froth on the surface of the liquid heat transfer medium to enhance vapor release from the liquid heat transfer medium.  Heat pipes with capillary structures, e.g. with particular material or construction of capillary structures (wicks), or capillary pumped heat pipes.  Heat pipes having a capillary structure are classified in F28D15/04  Example:  US 2006207750 A  A heat pipe ( 20 ) includes a metal casing ( 22 ) having an evaporating section ( 70 ) and a condensing section ( 80 ). A first type of capillary wick ( 241 ) is provided in the evaporating section and a second type of capillary wick ( 242 ) is provided in the condensing section  Heat pipes control means (e.g. variable conductance heat pipe).  Control arrangements for heat pipes are classified in F28D15/06  Example:  US 2010051254 A1  Variable conductance heat pipe has a sealed container (1) including a heat receiving portion (2) for receiving an element (9) i.e. semi-conductor laser device, to be cooled. A cooler cools a heat radiating portion (4) of the sealed container. A heater (40) supplies a specific amount of heat to the heat receiving portion of the sealed container, where the heater and cooler cooperatively perform an output control operation.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Thermosiphon radiators -> F28D1/02 - Regenerative heat exchangers -> F28D17/00 , F28D19/00 - Refrigeration cycles with closed loops comprising evaporator and condenser -> F25B
[F28D 15/02] in which the medium condenses and evaporates, e.g. heat pipes -- F28D 15/02 covers heat-exchange devices that use an intermediate medium which undergoes condensation and evaporation to transfer heat, such as heat-pipe systems. The classification applies to apparatus where the working fluid changes phase within a sealed envelope, providing efficient thermal transport without direct contact between the primary heat-exchange streams. It falls under mechanical engineering, heat exchange in general, and indirect heat-exchange apparatus.
[F28D 15/0208] using moving tubes -- F28D 15/0208 covers heat-exchange equipment that transfers heat between two media by means of tubes that move relative to the surrounding fluids, such as sliding, rotating or reciprocating tubes, which act as intermediate bodies so the heat-exchange media never come into direct contact.
[F28D 15/0233] the conduits having a particular shape, e.g. non-circular cross-section, annular  F28D15/0241 ,  F28D15/0266  take precedence -- The entry covers heat-exchange equipment that uses an intermediate fluid or solid and focuses on the shape of the internal conduits, such as non-circular or annular passages, which affect flow and heat transfer. It is placed in the subclass for apparatus where the heat-exchange media are not in direct contact and supersedes more specific sub-entries like F28D 15/0241 and F28D 15/0266.
[F28D 15/0241] the tubes being flexible -- F28D 15/0241 covers heat-exchange devices that use flexible tubes as intermediate heat-transfer media, where the heating and cooling fluids do not mix directly. It applies to apparatus not classified elsewhere in the heat-exchange section.
[F28D 15/025] having non-capillary condensate return means -- F28D 15/025 relates to heat-exchange equipment that uses an intermediate heat-transfer medium and incorporates a non-capillary condensate return system, such as gravity-driven channels or sloped surfaces, to recycle condensate without relying on capillary action.
[F28D 15/0258] with means to remove contaminants, e.g. getters -- This subclass refers to heat-exchange equipment that uses an intermediate heat-transfer medium or body and includes features for contaminant removal, such as getters that capture or neutralise impurities within the system. It falls under mechanical engineering, heat-exchange apparatus not classified elsewhere.
[F28D 15/0266] with separate evaporating and condensing chambers connected by at least one conduit; Loop-type heat pipes; with multiple or common evaporating or condensing chambers  F28D15/043  takes precedence -- The entry F28D 15/0266 covers heat-exchange devices that use an intermediate fluid or solid to transfer heat, with distinct evaporating and condensing chambers linked by at least one conduit, typically in a loop-type heat-pipe configuration. It includes designs featuring multiple or shared evaporating or condensing chambers, and it supersedes F28D 15/043.
[F28D 15/0275] Arrangements for coupling heat-pipes together or with other structures, e.g. with base blocks; Heat pipe cores -- The CPC subclass F28D 15/0275 covers designs that connect heat-pipe units with each other or with supporting structures such as base blocks, focusing on the configuration of the heat-pipe cores and the intermediate coupling elements that enable indirect heat transfer.
[F28D 15/0283] Means for filling or sealing heat pipes -- F28D 15/0283 covers devices used to introduce working fluid into heat pipes and then hermetically close them, ensuring the pipe is sealed from the external environment. These means are applied in heat-exchange apparatus that rely on intermediate media rather than direct contact between the hot and cold fluids.
[F28D 15/04] with tubes having a capillary structure -- F28D 15/04 covers heat-exchange equipment that transfers heat through an intermediate medium using tubes that contain a capillary structure. The capillary tubes increase surface area and promote efficient heat transfer while keeping the hot and cold fluids separated. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering, heat exchange in general, and apparatus where the media do not directly contact each other.
[F28D 15/043] forming loops, e.g. capillary pumped loops -- F28D 15/043 pertains to heat-exchange apparatus that transfers heat through an intermediate medium rather than direct contact, focusing on systems that create capillary pumped loops. These loops circulate fluid by capillary action between evaporator and condenser sections, providing efficient thermal transport without moving parts.
[F28D 15/046] characterised by the material or the construction of the capillary structure -- F28D 15/046 covers heat-exchange devices that use an intermediate medium and are defined by the material or construction of their capillary structures, such as fins, porous plates, or wick-type elements, within the broader class of mechanical engineering heat-exchange apparatus where the fluids do not directly contact.
[F28D 15/06] Control arrangements therefor -- F28D 15/06 covers control systems for heat-exchange apparatus that employ an intermediate heat-transfer medium, such as plates, shells or fins, so the hot and cold streams never meet directly. The classification includes devices that regulate flow, temperature or pressure of the intermediate medium to optimise heat-transfer performance.
[F28D 17/00] Regenerative heat-exchange apparatus in which a stationary intermediate heat-transfer medium or body is contacted successively by each heat-exchange medium, e.g. using granular particles -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Static Regenerators: a static body is first contacted by one heat exchange medium, e.g. hot gas, thereby accumulating heat, then is contacted by another heat exchange medium, e.g. fluid to be preheated, thereby transferring heat. Typical static regenerator: Rothemühle.  The subgroup F28D17/02 covers regenerators using rigid bodies, e.g. granular particles, checker bricks, honeycomb body.  Example:  Honeycomb regenerator for recovering a waste heat in an exhaust gas by passing an exhaust gas and a gas to be heated alternately through the regenerator (1). Regenerator (1) is constructed by stacking honeycomb structural bodies (2, 3) made of ceramics having a main crystal phase of cordierite.  The subgroup F28D17/04 covers distributing arrangements, e.g. valves.  Example:  US 3978912 A  Regenerative heat exchanger for gas turbine having a stationary heat-storing matrix (9, 10) in each of the flow-paths for the fluids between which heat exchange is to take place and at least one valve member (11) defining a common wall between the flow-paths and movable between two extreme operable positions,  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Regenerators for Stirling engines -> F02G1/057 - Arrangement of regenerators for preheating combustion air -> F23L15/02 - Arrangement of regenerators for hearth-type furnaces -> F27B3/26
[F28D 17/005] using granular particles -- Regenerative heat exchangers that employ granular particles as the stationary intermediate medium, allowing successive contact with the hot and cold streams. The particles store and release heat as they circulate within a fixed housing, enabling indirect heat transfer without direct fluid mixing.
[F28D 17/02] using rigid bodies, e.g. of porous material -- F28D 17/02 concerns regenerative heat-exchange apparatus that uses rigid bodies, such as porous solid media, as the stationary intermediate heat-transfer medium, with hot and cold streams contacting the solid sequentially. This subclass covers designs where the heat-transfer medium does not move or mix with the process fluids.
[F28D 17/023] Sealing means -- Sealing means are components designed to isolate sections of a regenerative heat-exchange apparatus, preventing leakage of the intermediate heat-transfer medium and ensuring that the separate heat-exchange streams do not mix directly. They typically involve gaskets, diaphragms or flexible barriers that accommodate the movement of granular particles while maintaining a tight seal.
[F28D 17/026] Bearings; Driving means -- The F28D 17/026 entry covers the bearings, shafts, gears, couplings and other driving means that support and power the moving parts of regenerative heat-exchange apparatuses in which a stationary intermediate medium (such as granular particles) transfers heat between separate fluid streams without direct contact. It addresses the mechanical components that enable rotation or oscillation of the heat-exchange sections while maintaining proper alignment and load handling.
[F28D 17/04] Distributing arrangements for the heat-exchange media -- The subclass covers designs that direct the flow of hot and cold streams through a regenerative heat-exchange unit, ensuring each stream contacts the stationary matrix (often granular particles) in a controlled manner. It includes baffles, ducts, nozzles and other distribution devices that balance pressure, prevent channeling and promote uniform heat transfer.
[F28D 19/00] Regenerative heat-exchange apparatus in which the intermediate heat-transfer medium or body is moved successively into contact with each heat-exchange medium -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Moving regenerators: a body is moved to contact first one heat exchange medium, e.g. hot gas, thereby accumulating heat, then to contacted the oher heat exchange medium, e.g. fluid to be preheated, thereby transferring heat. Typical moving regenerator: Ljüngström.  Regenerators using movable granular particles.  Regenerators using movable rigid bodies, e.g. rotors, are classified in F28D19/04 . The flow through movable carrier can be e.g. radial or axial.  Example:  US 4047559 A  Rotating drum regenerative air pre-heater having a slowly-rotating drum (3), containing a honeycomb of metal sheets, passes first across the hot-waste-gas outlet (12, 13), then across the cold-fresh-air inlet (7, 9).  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Arrangement of rotary type regenerators for heating combustion air in gas-turbine plants -> F02C7/105
[F28D 19/02] using granular particles -- F28D 19/02 covers regenerative heat-exchange devices that employ granular particles as the intermediate medium, moving the solids cyclically between hot and cold streams so that heat is transferred without direct contact between the primary fluids. This subclass specifies the use of particulate heat-transfer bodies.
[F28D 19/04] using rigid bodies, e.g. mounted on a movable carrier -- F28D 19/04 relates to regenerative heat-exchange apparatus in which the intermediate heat-transfer medium is carried by rigid bodies that are mounted on a movable carrier. The rigid elements move successively into contact with each heat-exchange medium, allowing indirect heat transfer without direct fluid mixing.
[F28D 19/041] with axial flow through the intermediate heat-transfer medium -- This subclass covers regenerative heat-exchange devices where the storage medium, solid or fluid, is moved sequentially between hot and cold streams, and the flow of the intermediate medium is directed axially along the length of the heat-transfer body.
[F28D 19/042] Rotors; Assemblies of heat absorbing masses -- This subclass covers regenerative heat-exchange devices that employ rotors or assemblies of heat-absorbing masses as the moving intermediate medium, transferring heat from one fluid stream to another without direct contact between the process media. The rotating bodies successively contact each heat-exchange side, providing a compact means of heat recovery in mechanical engineering applications.
[F28D 19/044] shaped in sector form, e.g. with baskets -- This subclass covers regenerative heat-exchange devices whose intermediate heat-transfer medium or body is formed into sector-shaped sections, such as baskets, that move successively between the hot and cold streams. The design enables the medium to exchange heat without direct contact between the primary fluids.
[F28D 19/045] with radial flow through the intermediate heat-transfer medium -- This subclass covers regenerative heat-exchange devices in which the intermediate heat-transfer medium flows radially across the medium, moving successively into contact with the hot and cold streams. It applies to indirect regenerators where the heat-transfer body is conveyed radially to enable heat exchange without direct contact between the primary media.
[F28D 19/047] Sealing means -- Sealing means for regenerative heat-exchange apparatus in which a movable intermediate heat-transfer body alternately contacts separate heat-exchange media, designed to prevent the leakage of fluids or gases between the moving body and the surrounding streams while allowing efficient thermal transfer.
[F28D 19/048] Bearings; Driving means -- F28D 19/048 addresses bearings and drive mechanisms that support and move the intermediate heat-transfer body in regenerative heat-exchange apparatus where the hot and cold media remain separate.
[F28D 20/00] Heat storage plants or apparatus in general -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Thermal accumulators, e.g. using thermochemical reactions, using liquid heat storage material, e.g. with stratification of heat storage material, using the ground body as heat storage medium, using solid heat storage material.  Example:  US 4203489 A  Modular thermal energy storage system comprising individual thermal energy storage elements, each having a core (12) for storing thermal energy and an insulation layer (14, 16) surrounding the core,  Heat storage apparatus using latent heat (phase change materials) are classified in F28D20/02 .  Example:  US2012055661 A  High temperature thermal energy storage system comprising solid-liquid phase change material (106) that is fully encapsulated in helically grooved, sealed, metal tubes (104). Multitude of horizontally oriented, pressure-rated steam pipes or vessels (101) filled with PCM tube capsules form a battery of thermal storage system.  Control arrangements therefor, e.g. activators for initiating crystallisation of supercooled salt solution.
[F28D 20/003] using thermochemical reactions -- The F28D 20/003 subclass covers heat-storage devices that retain thermal energy through reversible thermochemical reactions rather than direct fluid contact. It includes apparatus where chemical compounds absorb heat during an endothermic reaction and release it on the reverse exothermic reaction, providing a means of storing and retrieving heat for later use.
[F28D 20/0034] using liquid heat storage material -- The subclass F28D 20/0034 pertains to heat-exchange equipment in which the heat-exchange medium is not in direct contact with the storage medium, specifically using a liquid as the heat-storage material. It covers plants or apparatus designed to retain thermal energy in a liquid form for later release.
[F28D 20/0039] with stratification of the heat storage material -- This subclass covers heat-storage devices that keep the storage medium separated into layers of different temperatures, allowing hotter material to remain above cooler material. The stratification improves thermal efficiency and is used in large-scale systems such as solar-thermal plants or district-heating storage tanks. It applies to apparatus where the heat-exchange media do not directly contact each other.
[F28D 20/0043] specially adapted for long-term heat storage; Underground tanks; Floating reservoirs; Pools; Ponds  F28D20/0052  takes precedence -- F28D 20/0043 covers heat-exchange apparatus designed for long-term storage of thermal energy, including underground tanks, floating reservoirs, pools and ponds used as heat-storage media. It classifies systems where the heat-exchange media are not in direct contact with the stored fluid. The more specific subclass F28D 20/0052, dealing with particular embodiments, takes precedence over this broader entry.
[F28D 20/0052] using the ground body or aquifers as heat storage medium -- This subclass covers heat-exchange apparatus that store thermal energy in the ground or in aquifers, using the earth or underground water as the storage medium. It includes plants designed for seasonal thermal energy storage for heating or cooling.
[F28D 20/0056] using solid heat storage material  F28D20/0052  takes precedence -- The CPC subclass F28D 20/0056 covers heat-storage systems that employ solid materials to store thermal energy within general heat-exchange apparatus where the heat-transfer media do not directly contact each other. It falls under mechanical engineering, heat exchange in general, and is specific to solid-medium storage. The related subclass F28D 20/0052 is prior in the hierarchy.
[F28D 2001/0253] Particular components -- F28D 2001/0253 covers patents that focus on individual parts used in the machines or devices classified in the broader F28D group, such as specific shafts, rotors, housings, or other functional elements. The entry captures inventions relating to the design, material, or performance of these particular components rather than the complete system.
[F28D 2001/026] Cores -- The CPC subclass F28D 2001/026 covers patents concerning cores, the central components of devices such as furnaces, reactors, induction-heating equipment and other electromagnetic apparatuses. It includes the design, manufacturing methods, material choices and specific uses of such cores in industrial or consumer applications.
[F28D 2001/0266] Particular core assemblies, e.g. having different orientations or having different geometric features -- F28D 2001/0266 relates to particular core assemblies that are designed with varying orientations or distinct geometric features, such as differing cross-sections, angles or surface shapes. These specialized cores are used in fluid-handling devices to modify flow patterns, pressure distribution or mixing characteristics, enabling tailored performance in pumps, compressors or similar apparatus.
[F28D 2001/0273] having special shape, e.g. curved, annular -- F28D 2001/0273 classifies cleaning apparatus or elements whose working part is given a special shape-such as curved, annular or otherwise non-standard profiles-to enhance contact with particular surfaces or reach difficult areas.
[F28D 2001/028] with empty spaces or with additional elements integrated into the cores -- F28D 2001/028 classifies rotary compressors, pumps or fans whose rotors or cores contain internal voids or have additional components built into the core structure, such as cavities, inserts or integrated elements that modify flow, strength or cooling. This subgroup covers designs where empty spaces or integrated parts are deliberately provided within the rotating core.
[F28D 2001/0286] Radiating plates;  Decorative panels -- F28D 2001/0286 covers radiating plates and decorative panels used in heating, ventilation, or acoustic applications. The class includes metal or composite plates designed to emit or reflect heat, light or sound, and panels that serve aesthetic purposes while providing functional benefits.
[F28D 2001/0293] with grooves for integration of conduits -- F28D 2001/0293 classifies doors, windows, shutters, skylights and related fittings that include grooves designed to receive and guide conduits such as electrical cables, fluid pipes or other service lines, allowing integrated conduit installation within the opening or its frame.
[F28D 20/02] using latent heat -- F28D 20/02 covers heat-storage systems that exploit latent heat of phase change for storing thermal energy, such as containers filled with materials that melt or solidify to absorb or release heat. It falls under mechanical engineering, heat-exchange apparatus where the heat-transfer media do not directly contact each other.
[F28D 20/021] the latent heat storage material and the heat-exchanging means being enclosed in one container  F28D20/023  -  F28D20/028  take precedence -- This CPC subclass covers heat-exchange apparatus for latent-heat storage where the storage material and the heat-transfer components are housed together in a single container, falling under the broader category of heat-exchange devices whose media do not directly contact each other and are not classified elsewhere.
[F28D 20/023] the latent heat storage material being enclosed in granular particles or dispersed in a porous, fibrous or cellular structure -- F28D 20/023 covers heat-storage systems in which the latent heat material is confined within granular particles or distributed throughout a porous, fibrous or cellular matrix, allowing indirect heat exchange. The classification falls under mechanical engineering, heat-exchange apparatus not otherwise specified, and deals with general heat-storage plants.
[F28D 20/025] the latent heat storage material being in direct contact with a heat-exchange medium or with another heat storage material  F28D20/003  takes precedence -- F28D 20/025 covers heat-exchange apparatus in which the latent-heat storage material is placed in direct contact with the heat-exchange fluid or with another storage material. It falls under the broader class of mechanical engineering heat-exchange devices that do not involve direct contact between the primary heat-exchange media. This subclass refines the general category of heat-storage plants and apparatus.
[F28D 20/026] with different heat storage materials not coming into direct contact -- This subclass covers heat-storage devices where two or more storage media are kept separate so they never come into direct contact, using a conductive wall or intermediate fluid to transfer heat between them. It applies to systems such as latent-heat storage with phase-change materials isolated from the working fluid or sensible-heat storage using different solid media separated by metal plates.
[F28D 20/028] Control arrangements therefor -- The entry covers the design and implementation of control systems for heat-exchange equipment used in heat-storage plants where the hot and cold media are separated, such as indirect heat exchangers, including regulation of temperature, flow, and safety functions.
[F28D 2015/0216] having particular orientation, e.g. slanted, or being orientation-independent -- The subclass F28D 2015/0216 groups inventions in which a shaft, bearing or mounting element is defined by its angular relationship to a reference, such as a deliberately slanted position, or by the ability to operate regardless of orientation. It addresses orientation as a functional characteristic of the mechanical connection.
[F28D 2015/0225] Microheat pipes -- F28D 2015/0225 covers microheat pipes, which are compact heat-transfer elements that use capillary action to circulate a working fluid without moving parts. They are designed for efficient thermal management in small-scale applications such as electronic components, sensors and micro-electromechanical systems.
[F28D 2015/0291] comprising internal rotor means, e.g. turbine driven by the working fluid -- The code covers machines in which the rotor is located inside the housing and is driven directly by the working fluid, such as turbines that extract energy from the fluid flow. It applies to fluid-power devices where the internal rotor performs the work conversion, including certain pumps, compressors and energy recovery systems.
[F28D 2020/0004] Particular heat storage apparatus -- F28D is the CPC subclass for heat-exchange and heat-storage equipment, and the 2020/0004 entry specifies particular heat storage apparatus. It covers devices designed to store thermal energy, such as thermal-mass units, phase-change material containers and other systems that retain heat for later use in industrial, residential or district-heating applications.
[F28D 2020/0008] the heat storage material being enclosed in plate-like or laminated elements, e.g. in plates having internal compartments -- The code covers devices in which a heat-storage medium is sealed inside flat or layered components, such as plates that contain internal chambers or compartments for the material. It includes arrangements that integrate the storage element into the structural plate to provide compact, efficient thermal energy retention and release.
[F28D 2020/0013] the heat storage material being enclosed in elements attached to or integral with heat exchange conduits -- The CPC subclass F28D 2020/0013 relates to heat-storage installations where the storage medium is enclosed in a component that is either attached to or formed as part of a heat-exchange conduit. It covers integrated or cladded elements such as plates, tubes or panels that contain phase-change or other thermal-storage materials, allowing the stored heat to be transferred directly through the surrounding fluid circuit.
[F28D 2020/0017] the heat storage material being enclosed in porous or cellular or fibrous structures -- F28D 2020/0017 covers inventions in which a heat-storage medium-such as a phase-change material, sensible-heat fluid, or other thermal accumulator-is contained within a porous, cellular or fibrous matrix. The classification addresses the design of the surrounding structure to improve material containment, heat-transfer efficiency, mechanical stability and safety, and is relevant to building heating, thermal-energy storage systems and related applications.
[F28D 2020/0021] the heat storage material being enclosed in loose or stacked elements -- The CPC subgroup F28D 2020/0021 covers thermal-energy storage systems where the heat-storage medium is contained within loose or stacked elements such as granules, pellets, blocks or similar discrete units. It addresses the arrangement, containment and handling of these individual elements within the overall storage installation.
[F28D 2020/0026] the heat storage material being enclosed in mobile containers for transporting thermal energy -- This subclass covers systems in which a heat-storage medium is placed inside movable containers that can be transported to deliver thermal energy. It includes designs for insulated transport units, methods of loading and unloading the material, and integration with heat-exchange interfaces for efficient energy transfer.
[F28D 2020/0047] using molten salts or liquid metals -- This group includes inventions that employ molten salts or liquid metals as the heat-transfer or storage medium in furnaces, reactors or similar devices. It covers methods and apparatuses where the molten salt or liquid metal is heated, circulated, or used to supply or remove thermal energy for processes such as metal production, chemical synthesis, or energy storage.
[F28D 2020/006] Heat storage systems not otherwise provided for -- F28D 2020/006 covers heat-storage systems that are not specifically defined in other CPC subclasses, including thermal reservoirs, phase-change or sensible-heat devices, and any ancillary equipment used to store heat for later use in heating, cooling or power-generation processes. This subclass gathers inventions that do not fit the more detailed categories for particular storage media or applications.
[F28D 2020/0065] Details, e.g. particular heat storage tanks, auxiliary members within tanks -- This CPC subgroup deals with the structural details of heat-storage tanks and any auxiliary members contained within them, such as baffles, partitions, supports or internal piping. It covers specifications that influence heat retention, fluid flow and mechanical stability of the tank.
[F28D 2020/0069] Distributing arrangements;  Fluid deflecting means -- The classification covers devices that distribute fluids within a system and components that change the direction of a fluid stream. It includes manifolds, distributors, valves and other arrangements that route liquids or gases, as well as deflectors such as plates, vanes or nozzles that redirect flow for control or mixing purposes.
[F28D 2020/0073] movable -- The CPC entry F28D 2020/0073 "movable" covers patents for movable, collapsible or expandable structures such as tents, awnings, portable shelters and similar constructs, including the mechanisms used for their deployment, support, anchoring and relocation.
[F28D 2020/0078] Heat exchanger arrangements -- The F28D 2020/0078 subclass groups patents that disclose the structural layout and organization of heat exchangers, including the positioning of plates, tubes, shells, and other components to enable efficient thermal energy transfer between fluids, as well as combined or modular arrangements that integrate multiple heat-exchanging elements.
[F28D 2020/0082] Multiple tanks arrangements, e.g. adjacent tanks, tank in tank -- The CPC subclass F28D 2020/0082 covers inventions that provide arrangements of two or more tanks, such as side-by-side tanks or a tank placed inside another tank. It includes structural features for separating, supporting, or integrating the tanks to enhance storage, processing, heat exchange or safety in fluid-handling systems.
[F28D 2020/0086] Partitions -- F28D 2020/0086 relates to partitions, guard elements or similar protective structures used in machines or installations to separate, shield or contain parts, fluids or noise. The subclass covers internal walls, bulkheads, dividers and other dividing components that create distinct compartments within a device or system. It is applied to designs where the partition serves a functional safety, containment or segregation purpose.
[F28D 2020/0091] flexible -- F28D 2020/0091 designates patents that disclose flexible components or connections used in industrial machinery, such as hoses, belts, joints, or spring-like elements that permit movement or deformation while transmitting forces or fluids.
[F28D 2020/0095] movable or floating -- This subclass covers equipment or structures specifically designed to be moved or to float, including barges, floating platforms, mobile cranes, and other transport or handling installations that can be repositioned or operate on water.
[F28D 2021/0019] Other heat exchangers for particular applications;  Heat exchange systems not otherwise provided for -- F28D 2021/0019 covers heat exchangers designed for specific or unusual applications and heat-exchange systems that are not covered by other subclasses, including bespoke or unconventional configurations and integration methods. It groups inventive arrangements that fall outside the standard categories of heat-exchanger technology.
[F28D 2021/0021] for aircrafts or cosmonautics -- This CPC subgroup gathers patents for devices, components or methods expressly intended for aircraft or cosmonautic use, covering structures, propulsion, control, support or related systems employed in aviation or space vehicles.
[F28D 2021/0022] for chemical reactors -- F28D 2021/0022 covers apparatus and methods for operating chemical reactors, including devices that feed reactants, control temperature, mix contents, regulate pressure and remove products in batch, continuous or semi-continuous reactors. It addresses the integration of these functions to ensure efficient and safe reactor performance.
[F28D 2021/0024] for combustion apparatus, e.g. for boilers -- The F28D 2021/0024 entry classifies inventions related to combustion apparatus used in boilers, covering burners, fuel-delivery and ignition systems, flame-stabilising features and other components that generate, control or improve the combustion process within boiler installations.
[F28D 2021/0026] for combustion engines, e.g. for gas turbines or for Stirling engines -- F28D/2021-0026 classifies devices and components used in combustion engines, covering technologies such as gas turbines and Stirling engines. It includes arrangements, control systems and supporting mechanisms that enable the conversion of fuel combustion into mechanical power. This entry is part of the Cooperative Patent Classification system shared by the EPO and USPTO.
[F28D 2021/0028] for cooling heat generating elements, e.g. for cooling electronic components or electric devices -- The CPC subclass F28D 2021/0028 covers cooling arrangements for heat-generating elements, especially those used to dissipate heat from electronic components or electric devices. It includes heat sinks, fans, liquid-cooling circuits and other thermal-management systems designed to keep operating temperatures within safe limits.
[F28D 2021/0029] Heat sinks -- F28D 2021/0029 covers heat sinks, which are devices that remove excess heat from electronic or mechanical components by transferring it to the surrounding environment, typically through conductive members such as fins, plates or pillars that enhance convection and sometimes radiation. The entry includes the design, materials, mounting methods and integration of such thermal-management elements.
[F28D 2021/0031] Radiators for recooling a coolant of cooling systems -- The subclass defines radiators that cool a coolant which has already circulated through a cooling system, enabling the fluid to be reused in the same loop. It includes devices for heat exchange and temperature regulation in industrial, automotive or similar recirculating cooling applications.
[F28D 2021/0033] for cryogenic applications -- F28D 2021/0033 designates equipment and installations intended for cryogenic use, covering machines, apparatus and systems that produce, store, transport, or utilise substances at very low temperatures, such as liquefiers, cryogenic refrigerators, and associated control or safety devices for handling cryogenic fluids.
[F28D 2021/0035] for domestic or space heating, e.g. heating radiators -- This CPC subclass covers heating appliances intended for domestic or space heating, such as radiators, convectors and similar heat-emitting devices. The entry includes designs and methods for delivering thermal energy to indoor environments in residential or commercial settings.
[F28D 2021/0036] Radiators for drying, e.g. towel radiators -- The CPC group F28D concerns heating apparatus and installations, and entry 2021/0036 specifically addresses radiators intended for drying purposes, such as towel radiators. It covers designs that heat air or surfaces to accelerate moisture removal and includes various wall-mounted, floor-standing or pipe-connected configurations used in residential or commercial settings.
[F28D 2021/0038] for drying or dehumidifying gases or vapours -- The CPC subclass F28D 2021/0038 covers inventions that dry or dehumidify gases or vapours, including apparatus, processes and control methods for removing moisture, regenerating drying media, and handling condensate. It encompasses dryers, dehumidifiers, desiccant systems and related equipment used in industrial, environmental or HVAC applications.
[F28D 2021/004] for engine or machine cooling systems -- F28D 2021/004 covers cooling arrangements for engines or machines, including heat exchangers, radiators, cooling jackets and associated circulation or control devices that remove excess heat from moving parts to maintain operating temperatures within safe limits.
[F28D 2021/0042] for foodstuffs -- CPC F28D pertains to machines and apparatus for processing and handling foodstuffs, such as mixers, blenders, cooking and heating devices. The 2021/0042 sub-group specifies inventions that employ these types of equipment for preparing, treating or otherwise working with edible products.
[F28D 2021/0043] for fuel cells -- CPC code F28D 2021/0043 denotes subject matter relating to devices, components or processes specifically designed for fuel-cell applications. It covers the construction, operation, control and integration of fuel cells in power-generation systems. The entry is part of the CPC section F28, which deals with power generation and conversion technologies.
[F28D 2021/0045] for granular materials -- F28D covers apparatus and methods for handling, conveying, feeding, mixing or processing granular materials such as powders, grains, or pellets. The 2021/0045 entry specifically pertains to devices or techniques designed to treat or manipulate these bulk solids in industrial or agricultural settings, often focusing on improved flow, distribution or separation.
[F28D 2021/0047] for hydrogen or other compressed gas storage tanks -- F28D 2021/0047 pertains to containers used for storing hydrogen or other compressed gases, such as pressure vessels and tanks, together with their associated safety, material and design features. It classifies inventions that provide means for handling, sealing, pressure regulation or structural reinforcement of such gas storage units.
[F28D 2021/0049] for lubricants, e.g. oil coolers -- F28D 2021/0049 pertains to apparatus for cooling lubricants, including oil coolers used in engines, gearboxes, hydraulic circuits and similar machinery. It covers heat-exchanging devices, radiators and associated control or circulation systems that lower lubricant temperature to preserve performance and reduce wear.
[F28D 2021/005] for medical applications -- F28D 2021/005 covers pipe fittings, connectors, valves and related components that are specially designed for medical use, such as tubing and fluid-handling parts employed in infusion, dialysis, surgical or other healthcare equipment handling sterile fluids, gases or medicines.
[F28D 2021/0052] for mixers -- The CPC entry F28D 2021/0052 covers apparatus and devices specifically designed for mixing, including batch, continuous and planetary mixers used to combine solids, liquids or pastes in industrial, laboratory or food-processing contexts. It groups together mixers that employ rotating, reciprocating or tumbling actions to achieve homogeneous blends.
[F28D 2021/0054] for nuclear applications -- F28D 2021/0054 pertains to pipelines, ducts, hoses, couplings and related fittings that are specially designed for nuclear power plant applications, including reactor coolant circuits, fuel-handling and waste-treatment systems, and emphasizes materials and constructions capable of withstanding radiation, high temperatures and pressures.
[F28D 2021/0056] for ovens or furnaces -- F28D covers devices and methods for heating, temperature regulation and exhaust handling in ovens or furnaces, including burners, heat exchangers, insulation, control systems and safety mechanisms that ensure efficient and uniform heat distribution. It also addresses ancillary components such as doors, hinges, seals and cleaning aids that support the operation of these heating appliances.
[F28D 2021/0057] for melting materials -- F28D 2021/0057 classifies apparatus, devices or methods whose primary purpose is to melt a material, such as metals, polymers, glass or other substances, by applying heat, induction or other energy forms. The entry includes furnaces, melting kettles, melting chambers and related control systems used in industrial processing.
[F28D 2021/0059] for petrochemical plants -- F28D 2021/0059 covers technical features of plants or equipment specially designed for the production or processing of petrochemicals, such as reactors, distillation columns, heat exchangers and related auxiliary units that handle hydrocarbon feedstocks. It groups inventions that improve efficiency, safety or environmental performance in petrochemical manufacturing facilities.
[F28D 2021/0061] for phase-change applications -- F28D 2021/0061 covers devices that employ the latent heat of a material's phase transition for heating, cooling or heat-exchange purposes, such as thermal storage units, heat-pump components and phase-change heat exchangers that store or release heat during solid-liquid or liquid-gas changes.
[F28D 2021/0063] Condensers -- F28D 2021/0063 covers condensers, the components that convert vapor to liquid by removing heat, used in refrigeration, air-conditioning, heat-pump and power-generation systems. The classification includes various designs such as air-cooled, water-cooled, finned and plate-type condensers and related control or mounting arrangements.
[F28D 2021/0064] Vaporizers, e.g. evaporators -- This CPC subgroup covers inventions related to vaporizers, including devices that transform liquids into a vapor phase for purposes such as humidification, drying, or heating. The classification includes various types of evaporators, atomizers, steam generators and related components designed to produce and deliver vapor.
[F28D 2021/0066] with combined condensation and evaporation -- The CPC subgroup F28D 2021/0066 covers devices or processes that incorporate both condensation and evaporation within a single system, such as combined heat-exchange units, integrated condensers-evaporators, or combined cycle boilers where steam is condensed and reheated in one apparatus. It is applied to technologies that optimise energy reuse by linking the latent-heat removal and addition stages in a continuous flow.
[F28D 2021/0068] for refrigerant cycles -- The CPC class F28D 2021/0068 covers devices and components that form part of refrigerant cycles, such as compressors, condensers, evaporators, expansion valves and related control or heat-exchange units used in refrigeration, air-conditioning and heat-pump systems. It includes arrangements that circulate, compress, condense, expand or evaporate refrigerants to transfer heat.
[F28D 2021/007] Condensers -- F28D covers devices that convert vapor into liquid by removing heat, including steam condensers, refrigeration and air-conditioning condensers, and heat-pump condensers. The 2021/007 entry specifies condensers used in closed-cycle systems where the condensate is collected for reuse or further processing.
[F28D 2021/0071] Evaporators -- F28D 2021/0071 classifies evaporators, devices that turn liquid into vapor by applying heat, typically used in chemical, petrochemical, food-processing and refrigeration industries to separate components, concentrate solutions or recover solvents. The code covers various designs such as falling-film, rising-film, forced-convection and heat-pipe evaporators.
[F28D 2021/0073] Gas coolers -- Gas coolers are devices that lower the temperature of a gas after it has been heated, often by transferring heat to a secondary fluid or ambient air. They are used in systems such as turbines, compressors, and exhaust treatment to improve efficiency, control emissions, and protect downstream components.
[F28D 2021/0075] for syngas or cracked gas cooling systems -- F28D 2021/0075 pertains to cooling installations used in the processing of synthesis gas or cracked gas, including heat exchangers, recuperators and related apparatus that lower the temperature of the gas stream before further conversion or purification. The subclass covers designs that handle high-temperature gases, integrate temperature control, and may involve indirect or direct cooling methods.
[F28D 2021/0077] for tempering, e.g. with cooling or heating circuits for temperature control of elements -- This subclass covers arrangements that provide tempering of workpieces by incorporating cooling or heating circuits that regulate the temperature of the treated elements. It includes equipment where temperature control is built into the processing unit, such as furnaces, heat-treating ovens or rollers with integrated coolant or heater channels.
[F28D 2021/0078] in the form of cooling walls -- Covers cooling systems that are incorporated directly into building walls, where the wall itself acts as a heat-exchange surface to remove heat from interior spaces. This includes radiant cooling panels, chilled wall constructions and similar wall-integrated cooling installations used in HVAC applications.
[F28D 2021/008] for vehicles -- F28D 2021/008 designates inventions that are specifically directed toward vehicles, covering components, accessories, mounts, or modifications intended for use on or in any type of vehicle. It groups together patents that address functional adaptations or equipment designed for vehicle applications.
[F28D 2021/0082] Charged air coolers -- F28D 2021/0082 refers to cooling devices that employ a stream of pressurised or charged air to absorb and dissipate heat from a space, equipment or process. The classification covers apparatus such as fans, blowers and heat-exchanger arrangements that generate a flow of air under pressure for HVAC, electronic or industrial cooling applications.
[F28D 2021/0084] Condensers -- F28D pertains to condensers, devices that transform vapour into liquid by extracting heat, commonly employed in power generation, refrigeration and chemical processing. The 2021/0084 entry details specific condenser configurations or integration methods, such as shell-and-tube or plate heat-exchanger designs, and their functional relationship with associated plant components.
[F28D 2021/0085] Evaporators -- F28D 2021/0085 covers evaporators, devices that separate liquids from vapours by heating a mixture to turn the liquid component into steam and then condensing it elsewhere. The classification includes various designs such as falling-film, forced-circulation, and multiple-effect units used in chemical, food-processing, and petroleum industries for concentration, drying or recovery of solvents. It also embraces related control systems and auxiliary equipment that facilitate the evaporation process.
[F28D 2021/0087] Fuel coolers -- Fuel coolers are devices that lower the temperature of fuel before it is introduced into an engine or combustion system, typically using a heat-exchanging medium such as air, water or a secondary fluid. They are employed in automotive, aerospace and industrial applications to improve combustion efficiency, reduce emissions and protect engine components from overheating.
[F28D 2021/0089] Oil coolers -- F28D 2021/0089 covers oil-cooling apparatus and methods, including heat exchangers, radiators and cooling circuits that remove heat from oil in engines, hydraulic systems, transformers and similar machinery. The classification addresses designs such as oil-to-air, oil-to-water and oil-to-oil heat transfer, as well as associated control and safety features.
[F28D 2021/0091] Radiators -- The CPC subgroup F28D 2021/0091 covers radiators, devices that transfer heat between fluids or between a fluid and the surrounding environment. It includes panel, finned, and tubular radiators used for heating, cooling, and thermal management in buildings, vehicles, and industrial equipment, as well as related mounting, support, and control components.
[F28D 2021/0092] with particular location on vehicle, e.g. under floor or on roof -- F28D 2021/0092 relates to vehicle designs where a component or device is situated at a specific location on the vehicle, for example underneath the floor or on the roof, emphasizing the placement rather than the component's function.
[F28D 2021/0094] for recooling the engine coolant -- F28D covers cooling and heat-exchange arrangements for internal-combustion engines, including circuits that return heated coolant to a lower temperature before reuse. The 2021/0094 entry relates to devices or methods that recool engine coolant, such as secondary heat exchangers, coolant reservoirs with cooling jackets, or control systems that direct coolant through additional cooling stages to improve thermal management. This helps maintain optimal engine temperature and efficiency.
[F28D 2021/0096] for space heating -- F28D 2021/0096 covers inventions that provide heat for interior spaces, including heating elements, radiators, forced-air units, or control systems used in residential, commercial or industrial buildings.
[F28D 2021/0098] for viscous or semi-liquid materials, e.g. for processing sludge -- This subgroup covers machines or apparatuses intended to handle, transport, or treat viscous or semi-liquid substances, such as sludge, using methods like pumping, mixing, dewatering or other processing techniques. It includes equipment designed for the handling of waste, slurry or similar thick fluids in industrial or environmental applications.
[F28D 21/00] Heat-exchange apparatus not covered by any of the groups -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Residual group for F28D ;  Also covers heat exchangers for particular use: recuperative heat exchangers, e.g. water heaters or air heaters for domestic or space-heating systems, the heat being derived from steam or hot-gas; heat exchangers having permeable walls for heat and mass transfer; Flooded core heat exchangers.  Example:  US 4484564 A  Preheater device (10) including a coil (12) formed from copper tubing, positioned in a flue (13) through which hot exhaust gases produced from the burning of fuel in a conventional furnace, flow. At the terminal end of the coil 12, the copper tubing passes through the intet. The inlet (14) is coupled to the main water line in a home. The outlet (16) is coupled to a domestic water heater 17.
[F28D 21/0001] Recuperative heat exchangers -- Recuperative heat exchangers are devices that recover thermal energy from a hot exhaust stream and transfer it to a cooler incoming fluid without the two media mixing. They belong to the class of indirect heat-exchange apparatus where the heat-exchange media remain separated, improving overall system efficiency by reusing waste heat.
[F28D 21/0003] the heat being recuperated from exhaust gases  F28D21/0014  takes precedence -- F28D 21/0003 designates heat-exchange apparatus that recovers heat from exhaust gases without direct contact between the hot and cold media, and which is not otherwise classified in a more specific group. It is used when no narrower subgroup applies; the more specific subclass F28D21/0014 has priority over it.
[F28D 21/0005] for domestic or space-heating systems -- F28D 21/0005 covers heat-exchange devices used in residential or building heating that are not classified elsewhere, where the heating and cooling fluids are separated, such as radiators, convection panels or boiler heat exchangers designed for space-heating applications.
[F28D 21/0007] Water heaters -- F28D 21/0007 covers water-heater devices that belong to the heat-exchange apparatus class in which the heating medium and the water being heated are kept separate, without direct contact. These appliances are not classified elsewhere in the heat-exchange groups and fall under mechanical engineering, heat exchange in general.
[F28D 21/0008] Air heaters -- F28D 21/0008 covers air-heating devices that warm a moving air stream by indirect heat transfer, where the heating fluid does not come into direct contact with the air. It applies to any heat-exchange apparatus for air not otherwise classified in more specific subclasses.
[F28D 21/001] for thermal power plants or industrial processes -- F28D 21/001 covers heat-exchange equipment not classified elsewhere in which the heating and cooling media are kept separate, designed specifically for use in thermal power-plant cycles or other large-scale industrial processes. It encompasses units such as shell-and-tube or plate exchangers employed to transfer heat without direct contact between the fluids. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering, heat-exchange in general, and apparatus where the media do not mix.
[F28D 21/0012] the heat being recuperated from waste water or from condensates -- F28D 21/0012 covers heat-exchange devices that recover thermal energy from waste water or condensate streams, where the heating medium does not directly contact the cooled fluid. It is a residual subclass for apparatus not otherwise classified within the broader heat-exchange categories.
[F28D 21/0014] the heat being recuperated from waste air or from vapors  for air conditioning  F24F12/001 -- It is a subclass of heat-exchange apparatus in which the heat-exchange media are not in direct contact, covering devices that recover heat from waste air or vapor streams for use in air-conditioning systems.
[F28D 21/0015] Heat and mass exchangers, e.g. with permeable walls -- F28D 21/0015 classifies heat- and mass-exchangers whose separating walls are permeable, allowing simultaneous transfer of thermal energy and substances while keeping the media apart. It covers devices such as membrane heat exchangers, wetted-wall towers and other apparatus where the exchange media do not come into direct contact, and which are not covered by more specific subclasses.
[F28D 21/0017] Flooded core heat exchangers  in large body of fluid  F28D1/0206 -- Flooded core heat exchangers in which the heat-exchange core is completely immersed in a large body of fluid are classified under CPC F28D 21/0017. This subclass falls within mechanical engineering, heat exchange in general, and covers apparatus where the heat-exchange media do not come into direct contact and are not covered by other groups.
[F28D 3/00] Heat-exchange apparatus having stationary conduit assemblies for one heat-exchange medium only, the media being in contact with different sides of the conduit wall, in which the other heat-exchange medium flows in a continuous film, or trickles freely, over the conduits -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Trickle coolers with indirect contact; Falling film heat exchangers; Distributing arrangements.  Example:  US 5709264 A  Falling film heat exchanger comprising a number of vertically oriented, spaced apart passage walls (10) defining a number of alternating first and second passages (12,14), with slotted dividing bars (54) located between the passage walls (10) defining the second passages (14). The slotted dividing bars (43) have spaced, vertically oriented slots (58) to cause the liquid to flow against the passage walls defining the second passages (14), thereby to enhance formation of liquid film on the passage walls (10). The  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Evaporative cooler -> F28D5/00
[F28D 3/02] with tubular conduits -- F28D 3/02 covers heat-exchange apparatus that uses stationary tubular conduits to contain one heat-exchange medium while the second medium flows as a continuous film or free-falling trickle over the tube surfaces. The two media remain separated, contacting opposite sides of the conduit walls, and the design is not classified elsewhere in the heat-exchange subgroup.
[F28D 3/04] Distributing arrangements -- F28D 3/04 covers distributing arrangements in heat-exchange equipment where stationary conduit assemblies carry one heat-exchange medium while the second medium flows as a continuous film or free-trickling layer over the conduit walls.
[F28D 5/00] Heat-exchange apparatus having stationary conduit assemblies for one heat-exchange medium only, the media being in contact with different sides of the conduit wall, using the cooling effect of natural or forced evaporation -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Indirect evaporative coolers.  Example:  US4976113 A  Indirect evaporating gas cooling system consists of a casing (1) with an inlet (2) for gas flow, outlets (3, 4) for main flow and an auxiliary flow travelling in the opposite direction to the main one. The casing contains moist channels (7) of a wetted capillary-porous material, and dry channels (8) of a water-impermeable material, joining at the outlet (3) end, and also a liquid feed system to moisten the capilary-porous material. The total gas stream gets cooled down to the dew point without changing its moisture content, while the auxiliary stream gets heated (due to heat withdrawal from the main gas stream) and humidified (due to evaporation of moisture from the wettable capillary-porous solid 6 thereinto).
[F28D 5/02] in which the evaporating medium flows in a continuous film or trickles freely over the conduits -- This subclass covers heat-exchange devices where a single conduit carries one fluid while a second fluid evaporates on the opposite side of the conduit wall, forming a continuous film or free-falling trickle that removes heat by evaporation. The arrangement relies on natural or forced evaporation without direct contact between the two media.
[F28D 7/00] Heat-exchange apparatus having stationary tubular conduit assemblies for both heat-exchange media, the media being in contact with different sides of a conduit wall -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Example:  US 7438123 B  Blow-by gas pipe (1) is a pipe linking an engine crankcase and the downstream section of a carburetor; water cooling pipe (2) is a pipe linking an engine cooling passage outlet and a water pump; the pipes (1, 2) are attached to each other and a filled-in solid bar (4) is attached between pipes (1,2).The pipe-type heat exchange device is manufactured by brazing within a furnace.  Heat exchangers with helically coiled conduits are classified in F28D7/02  e.g. having a cylindrical configuration:  Example:  US 4371036 A  Heat exchanger functioning as an evaporator, used in a water/water heat pump, having a mantle-pipe (8) which is part of a closed water circuit which is placed in the ground in order to pick up heat from the ground. In the mantle-pipe (8) a centrally placed core pipe is part of the secondary circuit. Around this core pipe three parallel copper pipes (19, 20, 21) are coiled in helical shape.  Heat exchangers with spirally coiled conduits are classified in F28D7/04 :  Example:  US 6607027 B  Heat exchanger usable as an oil cooler including an inlet (378) and an outlet (380) for flow of the first fluid, a pair of juxtaposed tube segments (52, 54) coiled about the central axis (56) to form a plurality of alternating concentric coils, wherein the tube segments have flattened cross sections and wherein the tube segments are spiraled about the central axis.  Heat exchangers with conduits having a single U-bend are classified in F28D7/06 :  Example:  US 2011132586 A  The heat exchanger (1), particularly exhaust gas heat exchanger comprises a tube bundle (15) fixed to a cover (4) and arranged in a jacket (5). The tube bundle includes a plurality of tubes (6) having a generally U-shaped bend region disposed between at least two straight tube ends, wherein at least a portion of the at least two straight tube ends includes flat tubes  Heat exchangers with conduits being otherwise bent, e.g. in a serpentine are classified in F28D7/08 :  Example:  US 2001039329 A  A polymer devolatilization preheater comprises an elongated, upright hollow vessel (12) defining a heating chamber (14). Heating tube bundles (26, 28, 30) are mounted in the chamber. Each bundle has elongated serpentine heating tubes arranged in configuration having a major axis that extends across the chamber in a direction transverse to the longitudinal axis of the vessel. Each tube (32, 34) includes curved tube portions and linking tube portions that interconnect the curved tube portions.  l  Heat exchangers with conduits being arranged one within the other (tube-in-tube heat exchangers), e.g. concentrically are classified in F28D7/10 :  Example:  US 2010018673 A  Thermal energy transfer device with thermal energy transfer fluid in underground water pipe (103) buried under earth layers, includes an integral structure by casting or welding.  Tube-in-tube heat exchangers with the surrounding tube being closed at one end, e.g. return type, are classified in F28D7/12  Example:  US 4479534 A  A concentric tube type recuperative heat exchanger that includes an outer transparent tube (22) and an inner metallic tube (24) connected to headers (12,14)  Tube-in-tube heat exchangers with both tubes being bent are classified in F28D7/14 :  Example:  US 3976129 A  A composite tube composed of an inner tube (1) extending through and spaced from an outer tube (6) by radial partition walls is coiled helically. The helical composite tube coil is housed in a tank (12) having an interior silvered reflective surface and evacuated to minimize heat loss through the tank wall.  Heat exchangers with conduits being arranged in parallel spaced relation are classified in F28D7/16 :  Example:  US 4197907 A 1  Multiple pass shell-and-tube unit in which the tube-side fluid makes four passes through the unit and the shell-side fluid makes two passes, including an elongated cylindrical outer shell (10) having an external flange (11) at one end, a head (16) containing a tube-side fluid inlet (17) and a tube-side fluid outlet (18) located at the other end of the shell adjacent flange (11). Head (16) contains internal baffles (19, 20). The tube bundle in the exchanger comprises a plurality of elongated heat exchanger tubes (25) extending between a stationary tube sheet (26) and a floating tube sheet (27).  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Blocks traversed by passages -> F28F7/02
[F28D 7/0008] the conduits for one medium being in heat conductive contact with the conduits for the other medium -- The entry covers heat-exchange apparatus in which stationary tubular conduits for one fluid are placed in heat-conductive contact with the conduits for a second fluid, allowing heat to pass through the conduit walls while the media remain separated. This configuration is used when the two streams must not mix.
[F28D 7/0016] the conduits for one medium or the conduits for both media being bent  F28D7/0033  takes precedence
[F28D 7/0025] the conduits for one medium or the conduits for both media being flat tubes or arrays of tubes -- F28D 7/0025 covers heat-exchange devices in which the stationary tubular conduits for one or both fluids are flat tubes or groups of flat tubes arranged in an array, each fluid contacting opposite sides of the tube walls while the media remain separated and do not mix directly.
[F28D 7/0033] the conduits for one medium or the conduits for both media being bent -- F28D 7/0033 covers heat-exchange devices in which the stationary tubular conduits that carry one or both fluids are bent, allowing the two heat-exchange media to flow on opposite sides of the tube walls without direct contact. This applies to apparatus not covered by other subclasses of heat-exchange equipment.
[F28D 7/0041] the conduits for only one medium being tubes having parts touching each other or tubes assembled in panel form  F28D7/0008 ,  F28D7/0058  take precedence
[F28D 7/005] the conduits for only one medium being tubes having bent portions or being assembled from bent tubes or being tubes having a toroidal configuration  F28D7/0008 ,  F28D7/02 ,  F28D7/04 ,  F28D7/06 ,  F28D7/14  take precedence -- This subclass covers heat-exchange devices where the stationary tubes carrying one fluid are formed from bent sections, assembled from bent tubes or arranged in a toroidal shape, while the opposite side of the tube wall carries the second fluid. It applies to apparatus not covered by other subclasses in the heat-exchange apparatus group. Under CPC code F28D 7/005.
[F28D 7/0058] the conduits for only one medium being tubes having different orientations to each other or crossing the conduit for the other heat exchange medium  F28D7/0008  takes precedence -- This subclass covers heat-exchange devices where both fluids flow through fixed tubes and the tubes for one fluid are arranged at different angles or intersect the tubes for the other fluid. The configuration keeps the two media separated by the tube walls while providing efficient heat transfer. The broader subclass F28D7/0008, which defines stationary tubular conduit assemblies, takes precedence.
[F28D 7/0066] Multi-circuit heat-exchangers, e.g. integrating different heat exchange sections in the same unit or heat-exchangers for more than two fluids  F28D7/103  takes precedence -- The F28D 7/0066 entry covers multi-circuit heat exchangers that combine several heat-exchange sections within a single unit, allowing more than two fluid streams to be processed simultaneously. These devices use stationary tubular conduits where each fluid contacts opposite sides of the tube walls, and the definition is superseded by the broader F28D 7/103 subclass.
[F28D 7/0075] with particular circuits for the same heat exchange medium, e.g. with the same heat exchange medium flowing through sections having different heat exchange capacities or for heating or cooling the same heat exchange medium at different temperatures -- F28D 7/0075 relates to heat-exchange devices that use stationary tubular conduit assemblies where the same fluid circulates through sections that have different heat-transfer capacities or are heated or cooled to different temperatures, typically arranged in particular circuit configurations.
[F28D 7/0083] with units having particular arrangement relative to a supplementary heat exchange medium, e.g. with interleaved units or with adjacent units arranged in common flow of supplementary heat exchange medium -- F28D 7/0083 covers heat-exchange apparatus in which stationary tubular conduits for the primary fluids are arranged with supplemental heat-exchange medium units positioned in a specific layout, such as interleaved or adjacent units sharing a common flow path of the supplemental medium. The configuration enables heat transfer between the two media without direct contact, improving efficiency in compact heat-exchanger designs.
[F28D 7/0091] the supplementary medium flowing in series through the units -- F28D 7/0091 relates to heat-exchange apparatus with stationary tubular conduits where a secondary fluid flows in series through the same units, contacting the opposite side of the tube walls from the primary medium.
[F28D 7/02] the conduits being helically coiled -- F28D 7/02 refers to heat-exchange apparatus in which both heat-exchange media flow through stationary tubular conduit assemblies that are helically coiled, with each fluid contacting opposite sides of the tube walls without direct mixing.
[F28D 7/022] the conduits of two or more media in heat-exchange relationship being helically coiled, the coils having a cylindrical configuration -- It covers heat exchangers where stationary tubes for two or more fluids are formed into helically coiled, cylindrical configurations, enabling heat to be transferred across the tube walls without direct contact between the media.
[F28D 7/024] the conduits of only one medium being helically coiled tubes, the coils having a cylindrical configuration
[F28D 7/026] the conduits of only one medium being helically coiled and formed by bent members, e.g. plates, the coils having a cylindrical configuration -- F28D 7/026 pertains to heat-exchange equipment in which the conduit for one fluid is formed as a helically coiled, cylindrical assembly made from bent members such as plates, while the opposite conduit remains stationary. This subclass addresses designs where only one medium's pathway uses a helical coil configuration.
[F28D 7/028] the conduits of at least one medium being helically coiled, the coils having a conical configuration -- This subclass concerns heat-exchange equipment in which both fluids flow through fixed tubular passages and each fluid contacts opposite sides of a common wall, with at least one passage formed as a helically coiled tube that widens or narrows conically along its length. The conical coil provides a varying cross-section that can improve temperature gradients and compactness of the device.
[F28D 7/04] the conduits being spirally coiled -- F28D 7/04 covers heat-exchange devices in which the stationary tubes are formed as spiral coils, each tube carrying one fluid on the interior and another on the exterior so that heat is transferred through the tube wall without direct fluid contact.
[F28D 7/06] the conduits having a single U-bend -- The F28D 7/06 subclass covers heat-exchange devices in which both fluids flow through stationary tubular assemblies, each fluid contacting opposite sides of the tube wall, and the tubes incorporate a single U-bend. This design is used for compact heat-exchange apparatus where the two media are kept separate without direct contact.
[F28D 7/08] the conduits being otherwise bent, e.g. in a serpentine or zig-zag
[F28D 7/082] with serpentine or zig-zag configuration -- F28D 7/082 covers heat-exchange devices in which both fluids circulate in fixed tubular bundles and the tubes are arranged in a serpentine or zig-zag pattern, so each fluid contacts opposite sides of the tube wall without direct mixing. The classification falls under mechanical engineering, heat exchange in general, and heat-exchange apparatus not covered elsewhere.
[F28D 7/085] in the form of parallel conduits coupled by bent portions -- The subclass refers to heat-exchange devices where the two fluids flow through separate stationary tubes that run side-by-side and are joined by curved or bent sections, allowing heat to transfer through the tube walls without the media contacting each other.
[F28D 7/087] assembled in arrays, each array being arranged in the same plane -- This subclass covers heat-exchange devices that employ stationary tubular conduit assemblies for two fluids that remain separated by the tube walls, with the conduits assembled in arrays that are all arranged in the same plane.
