[F28F 2275/085] with snap connection -- The subclass F28F 2275/085 covers heat-exchange or heat-transfer apparatus that are assembled using a snap-type connection, a quick-release interlocking fastening that joins components without separate screws or bolts. This allows rapid disassembly and re-assembly of parts such as plates, tubes or housings in general-application heat-exchange equipment.
[F28F 2275/10] by force joining -- F28F 2275/10 pertains to fastening heat-exchange components by force-joining techniques, such as crimping, pressing, riveting or other mechanical deformation methods that secure parts without adhesives or welding. It is used for assembling general-purpose heat-transfer apparatus where a strong, permanent mechanical bond is required.
[F28F 2275/12] by methods involving deformation of the elements -- The CPC subclass F28F 2275/12 pertains to fastening techniques for heat-exchange or heat-transfer devices in which the components are joined by deforming the elements, such as crimping, swaging, pressing or other mechanical deformation methods. It covers the securing of plates, tubes, fins and similar parts of general-application heat-exchange apparatus through these deformation-based joins.
[F28F 2275/122] by crimping, caulking or clinching -- The code F28F 2275/122 classifies fasteners used in heat-exchange and heat-transfer equipment that are installed by deforming the material to secure parts together, such as crimping, caulking or clinching methods. It falls under mechanical engineering, specifically heat-exchange apparatus details.
[F28F 2275/125] by bringing elements together and expanding -- This CPC subclass deals with fastening techniques for heat-exchange and heat-transfer devices where the connection is achieved by bringing the parts together and then expanding one of them to lock the assembly, such as expanding sleeves, bolts or inserts that secure tubes, plates or fins within a heat-exchanger structure.
[F28F 2275/127] by shrinking -- The code F28F 2275/127 designates fastening methods for heat-exchange or heat-transfer equipment that rely on a shrinking process. It covers devices such as heat-shrink sleeves or other materials that contract when heated to secure components in place.
[F28F 2275/14] by using form fitting connection, e.g. with tongue and groove -- F28F 2275/14 pertains to fastening techniques used in heat-exchange or heat-transfer devices where components are joined by a form-fitting connection such as a tongue-and-groove joint, providing precise alignment and secure attachment without additional fasteners. The subclass falls under Mechanical Engineering - Heat exchange in general - Details of heat-exchange and heat-transfer apparatus, of general application - Fastening.
[F28F 2275/143] with pin and hole connections -- This CPC subgroup refers to fastening arrangements where components of heat-exchange equipment are joined by pins inserted into corresponding holes. It covers designs that rely on pin-and-hole connections to secure tubes, plates or other elements within general-purpose heat-transfer apparatus.
[F28F 2275/146] with bayonet connections -- This subclass covers fastening methods that employ bayonet-style connections, where matching lugs and slots are twisted together to secure components. It is applied to heat-exchange and heat-transfer devices, enabling rapid assembly and disassembly of tubes, plates, or other parts while maintaining reliable mechanical and thermal linkage.
[F28F 2275/16] with toothed elements, e.g. with serrations -- This subclass defines fastening elements on heat-exchange or heat-transfer apparatus that use toothed or serrated features, such as ridges or teeth, to grip and secure adjoining parts.
[F28F 2275/18] by using wedge effect -- F28F 2275/18 covers fastening techniques for heat-exchange and heat-transfer devices that rely on a wedge effect to secure components. It falls within mechanical engineering, specifically the general details of heat-exchange apparatus, and is used when a wedge-based mechanism provides a compact, reliable means of joining parts.
[F28F 2275/20] with threaded elements -- F28F 2275/20 relates to heat-exchange and heat-transfer equipment that is assembled by means of threaded fasteners. It is classified under mechanical engineering, heat exchange in general and details of heat-exchange and heat-transfer apparatus of general application, specifically addressing fastening using threaded elements.
[F28F 2275/205] with of tie-rods -- This CPC subgroup concerns heat-exchange or heat-transfer equipment where the components are fastened together by tie-rods. It falls under mechanical engineering, heat exchange in general, and details of heat-exchange apparatus, specifically addressing fastening methods that employ tie-rods to hold tubes, plates or shells in position.
[F28F 2275/22] by using magnetic effect -- The subclass F28F 2275/22 pertains to fastening arrangements for heat-exchange or heat-transfer apparatus that rely on a magnetic effect to hold components together, enabling attachment and release without conventional mechanical fasteners. This is used in general-purpose heat-exchange equipment where magnetic clamping simplifies assembly, maintenance, or adjustment.
[F28F 2280/00] Mounting arrangements -- The F28F 2280/00 subclass concerns the mounting arrangements used for heat-exchange and heat-transfer apparatus of general application. It includes brackets, frames, clamps, supports and other fixtures that secure heat exchangers to a plant structure, vessel, pipework or chassis, addressing the positioning, attachment and mechanical stability of the equipment.
[F28F 2280/02] Removable elements -- This subclass covers removable components of heat-exchange and heat-transfer equipment, focusing on the mounting arrangements that allow elements such as plates, tubes, coils or similar parts to be detached for cleaning, maintenance, replacement or reconfiguration.
[F28F 2280/04] Means for preventing wrong assembling of parts -- This subclass covers devices incorporated into heat-exchange or heat-transfer units that ensure components are assembled only in the correct orientation or sequence, such as keys, asymmetric fittings, or locking features that guard against mis-placement during installation.
[F28F 2280/06] Adapter frames, e.g. for mounting heat exchanger cores on other structure and for allowing fluidic connections -- The CPC subclass F28F 2280/06 covers adapter frames used to mount heat-exchanger cores onto other structures and to provide fluidic connections. These frames act as mounting arrangements that facilitate integration of the heat-exchange unit with a system, allowing secure attachment and piping interfaces.
[F28F 2280/08] Tolerance compensating means -- Tolerance compensating means are components integrated in mounting arrangements of heat-exchange equipment that absorb dimensional variations caused by thermal expansion, manufacturing tolerances or vibration, ensuring reliable alignment and secure attachment while preventing stress on the heat-transfer elements.
[F28F 2280/10] Movable elements, e.g. being pivotable -- This CPC subclass F28F 2280/10 covers mounting arrangements for heat-exchange equipment that incorporate movable elements such as pivotable brackets, hinges or swivel parts. It is used to classify inventions where adjustable or swiveling components are employed to position, align or secure heat-transfer devices, enhancing flexibility or maintenance.
[F28F 2280/105] with hinged connections -- The subclass F28F 2280/105 addresses heat-exchange or heat-transfer devices of general application whose mounting incorporates hinged connections, enabling components to pivot for assembly, adjustment, cleaning or operation. It falls under mechanical engineering, heat-exchange in general, and details of mounting arrangements.
[F28F 23/00] Features relating to the use of intermediate heat-exchange materials, e.g. selection of compositions -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Particular intermediate heat exchange materials  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Chemical compositions of heat transfer materials -> C09K5/00
[F28F 23/02] Arrangements for obtaining or maintaining same in a liquid state -- This CPC subclass concerns designs and methods that keep an intermediate heat-exchange material in a liquid state, including the selection of suitable compositions and system configurations that prevent solidification during heat-transfer cycles.
[F28F 25/00] Component parts of trickle coolers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Arrangements for distributing, circulation or accumulating liquid  Distributing or accumulator troughs  Spray nozzles  Splashing boards or grids, e.g. for converting liquid sprays into liquid films; Elements or beds for increasing the area of the contact surface, e.g. in the form of spaced elongated bars, laths, horizontal grids, vertical or inclined sheets  Gas or vapour feeding  Ducts; Guide vanes  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Packing elements for chemical reactors -> B01J19/30 , B01J19/32 - Packings; Fillings; Grids in apparatus for biological treatment of water -> C02F3/10
[F28F 25/02] for distributing, circulating, and accumulating liquid -- The CPC subclass F28F 25/02 covers component parts of trickle coolers that are used for distributing, circulating and accumulating the cooling liquid, ensuring uniform flow and temporary storage within the heat-exchange unit.
[F28F 25/04] Distributing or accumulator troughs -- F28F 25/04 covers distributing or accumulator troughs used in trickle coolers, which are component parts that spread liquid coolant or refrigerant over the heat-exchange surface and temporarily store fluid to ensure uniform distribution.
[F28F 25/06] Spray nozzles or spray pipes -- F28F 25/06 pertains to spray nozzles and spray pipes that serve as component parts of trickle coolers, used to distribute liquid in a fine spray for improved heat transfer in heat-exchange equipment such as cooling towers and similar systems.
[F28F 25/08] Splashing boards or grids, e.g. for converting liquid sprays into liquid films -- F28F 25/08 covers splash boards or grids used in trickle coolers and similar heat-exchange devices. These components spread incoming liquid sprays into thin films to improve heat transfer efficiency. They are internal elements designed to distribute fluid uniformly across the heat-transfer surface.
[F28F 25/082] Spaced elongated bars, laths; Supports therefor -- This CPC subclass covers the design of spaced elongated bars or laths and the supports that hold them, used as component parts in trickle cooler heat-exchange devices. It pertains to mechanical engineering applications where the bars provide increased surface area for liquid distribution within the cooler.
[F28F 25/085] Substantially horizontal grids; Blocks -- The code F28F 25/085 covers components for trickle coolers that are essentially horizontal grids or block-type elements used to support and spread the liquid film across the heat-transfer surface. These parts provide a flat, uniformly spaced structure that enhances cooling efficiency.
[F28F 25/087] Vertical or inclined sheets; Supports or spacers -- The subclass defines vertical or inclined sheets that act as flow distributors and the associated supports or spacers that retain them within trickle coolers, a type of heat-exchange apparatus where liquid trickles over surfaces to enhance heat transfer. It covers the design and arrangement of these components for efficient fluid distribution and structural stability.
[F28F 25/10] for feeding gas or vapour -- F28F 25/10 relates to components of trickle coolers that supply gas or vapour to the heat-exchange system, such as inlet manifolds, distribution trays, spray nozzles and other feeding devices used in general-application heat-exchange apparatus.
[F28F 25/12] Ducts -- F28F 25/12 covers ducts that form part of trickle-cooler heat-exchange units. It includes the channels, pipes or passageways that guide fluid flow through the cooling device, focusing on their design, construction and integration within the overall apparatus.
[F28F 27/00] Control arrangements or safety devices specially adapted for heat-exchange or heat-transfer apparatus -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Controlling, monitoring heat exchangers, e.g. temperature control, pressure control, flow control, heat transfer control; Safety means.  The feature "controlling" means influencing a variable in any way e.g. changing its value, maintaining it constant, limiting its range of variation; "regulating" means maintaining a variable automatically at a desired value or within a desired range of values. The desired value may be fixed, manually varied or may vary with time according to a predetermined "programme". Regulation is a form of control.  Controlling the distribution of heat exchange media between different channels, e.g. flow control means in heat exchangers, e.g. valves  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Static flow control means for distributing a heat exchange medium into conduits -> F28F9/02
[F28F 27/003] specially adapted for cooling towers -- This CPC subclass covers control or safety devices that are specially adapted for cooling-tower heat-exchange equipment, addressing regulation of flow, temperature and operational safety. It falls under mechanical engineering, heat exchange in general, and details of heat-exchange apparatus.
[F28F 27/006] specially adapted for regenerative heat-exchange apparatus -- The subclass concerns control or safety devices that are specifically designed for regenerative heat-exchange equipment, covering mechanisms that regulate temperature, fluid flow or protect the apparatus during operation. It falls within mechanical engineering, heat exchange in general, and the detailed design of heat-exchange and heat-transfer apparatus.
[F28F 27/02] for controlling the distribution of heat-exchange media between different channels -- F28F 27/02 relates to control arrangements or safety devices that direct the flow of heat-exchange media between different channels in a heat-exchange or heat-transfer system, ensuring proper distribution and operation of general-purpose heat-exchange apparatus.
[F28F 3/00] Plate-like or laminated elements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Plate-like or laminated elements; assemblies of plate-like or laminated elements,e.g. particular shape, construction, geometry of individual plate-like or laminated element, e.g. arrangements for preventing direct contact between different heat-exchange media.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Elements specially adapted for movement -> F28F5/00 - Fins to facilitate cooling of reflectors for light sources -> F21V29/505 - Fins for nuclear reactor fuel elements -> G21C3/08 - Heat sinks for resistors -> H01C1/084 - Cooling fins for transformers -> H01F27/02 , H01F27/08 - Heat dissipation bodies for electric apparatus -> H05K7/20 - Heat sinks for cooling semiconductor -> H10W40/10 - Cooling facilitated by shape of device, e.g. foil, wire, pin -> H10W40/22
[F28F 3/005] Arrangements for preventing direct contact between different heat-exchange media  F28F3/10  takes precedence -- This subclass of the plate-like or laminated heat-exchange elements covers designs that keep hot and cold fluids from mixing by using physical separators such as double-wall plates, interposing barriers or protective sleeves. It includes arrangements like sealed gaps, insulating layers, or intermediate fluids that prevent direct contact while allowing heat transfer.
[F28F 3/02] Elements or assemblies thereof with means for increasing heat-transfer area, e.g. with fins, with recesses, with corrugations -- F28F 3/02 classifies plate-like or laminated heat-exchange components that incorporate features such as fins, recesses or corrugations to increase the heat-transfer surface area. These elements or assemblies are intended to improve thermal performance in general-purpose heat-exchange devices.
[F28F 3/022] the means being wires or pins -- F28F 3/022 pertains to heat-exchange or heat-transfer devices that use plate-like or laminated elements in which the spacing or support between plates is provided by wires, pins or similar projecting members. It covers arrangements where the conductive or structural elements are formed by such wires or pins to create flow channels or enhance heat transfer.
[F28F 3/025] the means being corrugated, plate-like elements -- F28F 3/025 covers heat-exchange or heat-transfer devices that use corrugated, plate-like elements as the functional means for heat transfer. It falls under the broader group for plate-like or laminated heat-exchange components within mechanical engineering, specifically addressing general-application heat-exchanger designs that employ such corrugated plates.
[F28F 3/027] with openings, e.g. louvered corrugated fins; Assemblies of corrugated strips -- F28F 3/027 covers plate-like heat-exchange elements that incorporate openings such as louvered or corrugated fins, allowing fluid flow through the plates. The classification includes assemblies of corrugated strips used to increase surface area while managing pressure losses in heat-transfer devices.
[F28F 3/04] the means being integral with the element -- This subclass pertains to plate-like or laminated heat-exchange elements whose functional parts-such as flow passages, fins, supports or sealing features-are formed as an integral part of the element rather than being added as separate components. It covers constructions where the means for heat transfer, reinforcement or sealing are built directly into the plate or lamination.
[F28F 3/042] in the form of local deformations of the element -- The subclass F28F 3/042 covers plate-like or laminated heat-exchange elements that achieve improved performance through local deformations of the element, such as corrugations, embossings, dimples or other shaped protuberances. These deformations are applied to the plate surface to promote turbulence, increase surface area or enhance fluid distribution within the heat-transfer device. It is a detailed subcategory under mechanical engineering for general heat-exchange apparatus.
[F28F 3/044] the deformations being pontual, e.g. dimples -- This subclass covers plate-like or laminated heat-exchange elements that feature localized surface deformations such as dimples to promote turbulence, improve fluid distribution, or increase heat-transfer efficiency. The deformations are discrete, point-like rather than continuous across the surface.
[F28F 3/046] the deformations being linear, e.g. corrugations -- F28F 3/046 pertains to plate-like or laminated heat-exchange elements that incorporate linear deformations such as corrugations, which are employed to modify fluid flow, improve heat-transfer performance or increase structural rigidity. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering, heat exchange in general, and details of heat-exchange apparatus of general application.
[F28F 3/048] in the form of ribs integral with the element or local variations in thickness of the element, e.g. grooves, microchannels -- A sub-category of plate or laminated heat-exchange elements that incorporate ribs formed as part of the element or feature local thickness variations such as grooves or micro-channels to enhance heat transfer.
[F28F 3/06] the means being attachable to the element -- F28F 3/06 covers devices or components that can be mounted on plate-like or laminated heat-exchange elements to secure, hold, connect or seal them, such as brackets, clamps, fasteners or mounting frames.
[F28F 3/08] Elements constructed for building-up into stacks, e.g. capable of being taken apart for cleaning -- The code denotes plate-type or laminated heat-exchange components that are built to be stacked together and can be taken apart for cleaning or maintenance. These removable elements are used in heat exchangers where modular assembly aids serviceability and performance.
[F28F 3/083] capable of being taken apart -- A subclass for plate-like or laminated heat-exchange components that are constructed so they can be taken apart for cleaning, repair, or re-assembly, falling under mechanical engineering heat-exchange apparatus.
[F28F 3/086] having one or more openings therein forming tubular heat-exchange passages -- This CPC subclass belongs to mechanical engineering under heat exchange in general, specifically plate-like or laminated heat-exchange elements that incorporate one or more openings forming tubular passages for fluid flow. The design creates internal tubes within the plate structure, allowing heat to be transferred between separate media while maintaining a compact, slab-like form.
[F28F 3/10] Arrangements for sealing the margins -- F28F 3/10 relates to designs that seal the edges of plate-like or laminated heat-exchange elements, detailing gaskets, welds, brazes or other margin-sealing techniques used to prevent fluid leakage from the apparatus.
[F28F 3/12] Elements constructed in the shape of a hollow panel, e.g. with channels -- The F28F 3/12 subclass covers heat-exchange elements formed as hollow panels that incorporate internal channels or passages. These plate-like or laminated structures allow fluid flow within the panel to enhance heat transfer while maintaining a flat, compact shape, and are used in a variety of general-purpose heat-exchange apparatus.
[F28F 3/14] by separating portions of a pair of joined sheets to form channels, e.g. by inflation -- F28F 3/14 covers heat-exchanger designs in which a pair of bonded sheets are partially separated to create internal flow channels, often produced by inflating the space between the sheets. This technique forms plate-like or laminated elements that provide efficient heat transfer while maintaining a compact, lightweight structure.
[F28F 5/00] Elements specially adapted for movement -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Particular type of moving elements, e.g. rotary drums or rollers ( F28F5/02 ), hollow impellers ( F28F5/04 ), hollow screw conveyor s ( F28F5/06 ).  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Movable elements inside conduits, e.g. stirring means -> F28F13/12 - Cooling or heating rolls for rolling of metal -> B21B27/06 , B21B27/08 , B21B27/10 - Cooled casting wheels -> B22D11/06 - Rollers for mixing or kneading devices for preparation of plastic material -> B29B7/62 - Rolls calenders or drums for shaping plastic material -> B29C33/04 - Rollers for thermal treatment of textiles -> D06B23/02 - Drying cylinders for paper-making -> D21F5/02 - Heating or cooling calender rolls for paper-making -> D21G1/02 - Heated or cooled rollers or drums or cylinders for drying fabrics, fibres, yarns, or other material -> F26B13/18 - Movable cleaning elements -> F28G
[F28F 5/02] Rotary drums or rollers -- F28F 5/02 covers rotary drums or rollers that form part of heat-exchange or heat-transfer devices, serving as moving elements that convey material while facilitating heating, cooling or drying. Typical applications include rotating drum heat exchangers, drum boilers and drum dryers where the drum's motion enhances temperature transfer between the material and the heating or cooling medium.
[F28F 5/04] Hollow impellers, e.g. stirring vane -- Hollow impellers are rotating members, such as stirring vanes, that contain internal cavities to reduce weight while maintaining structural strength. They are used in heat-exchange and fluid-mixing equipment to impart motion to liquids or gases, improving heat transfer and agitation efficiency.
[F28F 5/06] Hollow screw conveyors -- The code F28F 5/06 covers hollow screw conveyors, a type of moving element that transports bulk material while permitting fluid to flow through the screw's interior, often in heat-exchange or heat-transfer equipment. These devices combine mechanical conveyance with the ability to heat or cool the conveyed material.
[F28F 7/00] Elements not covered by group -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Elements not covered by group F28F1/00 , F28F3/00 or F28F5/00 , e.g. particular heat-exchange elements not in the form of tubular or plate-like elements.
[F28F 7/02] Blocks traversed by passages for heat-exchange media -- A block-shaped heat-exchange element that contains internal passages through which the heating or cooling fluid flows. The design integrates the fluid channels within a solid body, allowing efficient transfer of heat between media without external tubes or plates, and is used in a wide range of mechanical-engineering applications.
[F28F 9/00] Casings -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Particular construction of casings or shells; particular frame elements, e.g. side plates for radiator cores; auxiliary members within casings, e.g. filling means, sealing means.  Auxiliary supports for elements  Supports for tubes or tube-assemblies, e.g. formed by plates, by slats, by tie-rods, by strips, by wires.  Header boxes (manifold, tank), end plates, e.g. header boxes with inner space divided by partitions or baffles; arrangements for fastening header box or end plate with casing; arrangements for fastening header (cover) with end plate; double end plates or hollow end plates; expansion chambers; header boxes with second heat exchanger disposed inside; floating elements; arrangements for connecting header boxes with flow lines; static flow control, e.g. heat exchange media distributing means.  Arrangements for connecting elements to header boxes or end plates, either by dismountable joints ( F28F9/06 ), or by permanent joints ( F28F9/16 ).  Dismountable joints can be: wedge-type connections, e.g. taper ferrule ( F28F9/08 ), or screw-type connection, e.g. gland, stuffing box ( F28F9/10 ), or flange-type connection, or made by force-joining, e.g. the elements are forced into the end plate, generally with a gasket ( F28F9/14 ).  Permanent joints means: using a working procedure for permanently deforming the elements or using bonding materials, e.g. adhesive, brazing or welding material for permanently joining the elements with the end plate or header box: the subgroup F28F9/18 covers permanent joint by welding.  Arrangement of heat reflectors, e.g. protective walls inside casings.  Arrangement of guide plates inside casing, e.g. longitudinal, transversal or oblique baffles for guiding the heat exchange medium flowing inside the casing  Arrangements for promoting turbulent flow inside casing  Arrangements for connection different heat-exchange sections, e.g. elbows, connecting sleeves, nipples.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Arrangements or mounting of radiators on vehicles -> B60K11/04 - Mounting of radiators on vehicles front or rear portions -> B62D25/08 - Arrangements or mountings of liquid-to-air heat exchangers for cooling engines -> F01P3/18 - Arrangement of fans or blowers for cooling engines -> F01P5/02 - Filling caps, deaeration devices for motor car radiators -> F01P11/02 - Guiding or ducting cooling air to or from liquid-to-air heat exchangers -> F01P11/10 - Arrangement of mountings or supports for central heating radiators -> F04D19/02 - Mounting of ventilators -> F04D29/60 - Supporting arrangements for steam generators -> F22B37/20 - Drums, headers for steam generators -> F22B37/22 - Casings, cover lids or ornamental panels for domestic radiators. -> F24D19/06 - Casings for air-conditioning units; arrangements for mounting of heat exchangers -> F24F13/00 - Casings, cover lids or ornamental panels for fluid heaters having heat generating means -> F24H9/02 - Arrangement of supports for fluid heaters -> F24H9/06 - Connecting heaters to circulation pipes -> F24H9/12
[F28F 9/001] Casings in the form of plate-like arrangements; Frames enclosing a heat exchange core -- F28F 9/001 covers casings that consist of plate-like arrangements or frames that surround the core of a heat-exchange device. These structures provide mechanical support and containment for the heat-transfer elements within general-purpose heat exchangers.
[F28F 9/002] with fastening means for other structures -- This subclass covers casings for heat-exchange or heat-transfer apparatus that incorporate fastening elements enabling the enclosure to be attached directly to other structures or components. It addresses designs where the casing itself includes bolts, brackets, clips or similar devices for secure mounting.
[F28F 9/005] Other auxiliary members within casings, e.g. internal filling means or sealing means -- The code F28F 9/005 refers to auxiliary members that are installed inside the casings of heat-exchange equipment, such as internal fillers, baffles, or sealing devices. These components assist in directing fluid flow, supporting the internal structure, or preventing leakage, and are considered supplemental to the primary heat-transfer elements.
[F28F 9/007] Auxiliary supports for elements -- The code F28F 9/007 covers auxiliary supports that secure or reinforce components inside the casings of heat-exchange or heat-transfer devices. It includes brackets, ribs, stays and similar structures that provide additional mechanical stability to the internal elements of general-purpose heat-exchange apparatus.
[F28F 9/0075] Supports for plates or plate assemblies -- F28F 9/0075 pertains to the structural elements that hold individual plates or plate assemblies in heat-exchange equipment, such as brackets, spacers or mounting ribs that maintain alignment and spacing. The provision may include fixed or adjustable supports designed for general-purpose plate heat exchangers.
[F28F 9/013] for tubes or tube-assemblies -- This subclass covers casings that are specifically adapted to house individual tubes or complete tube assemblies within heat-exchange equipment. It includes shells, frames, or other enclosures that support, protect, and retain the tube bundles during operation.
[F28F 9/0131] formed by plates  F28F9/0138  takes precedence -- F28F 9/0131 classifies heat-exchange casings that are formed by plates, covering plate-constructed housings for general-application heat-transfer equipment. It falls under mechanical engineering, heat exchange in general, details of heat-exchange apparatus, and casings, and is superseded by the broader F28F 9/0138 entry.
[F28F 9/0132] formed by slats, tie-rods, articulated or expandable rods -- The subclass covers heat-exchanger casings that are built from slatted or rod-type structures, such as tie-rods, articulated or expandable rods, which form a framework for supporting tube bundles or plates. These designs allow easy assembly, adjustment or expansion to accommodate thermal movement or cleaning.
[F28F 9/0133] formed by concentric strips -- Casings for heat-exchange or heat-transfer apparatus that are built from a series of concentric strips forming an annular or cylindrical shell, with the strips arranged one inside another to provide structural strength and fluid flow paths. This construction is used to improve heat-transfer efficiency, reduce weight and allow modular assembly of the heat-exchange unit.
[F28F 9/0135] formed by grids having only one tube per closed grid opening  F28F9/0132  and  F28F9/0133  take precedence -- This subgroup covers heat-exchange casings constructed from grid structures in which each closed opening of the grid contains a single tube, a design that is superseded by the more specific grid arrangements defined in F28F9/0132 and F28F9/0133.
[F28F 9/0136] formed by intersecting strips -- F28F 9/0136 covers casings for heat-exchange and heat-transfer apparatus that are formed by intersecting strips, a type of structured metal or composite shell where strip elements cross to create a rigid framework. This design provides increased strength and surface area while allowing fluid flow, used in heat exchangers and related equipment.
[F28F 9/0137] formed by wires, e.g. helically coiled  F28F9/0135  takes precedence -- This subclass covers casings of heat-exchange or heat-transfer devices that are constructed from wire elements, such as helically coiled wires, forming the outer shell of the apparatus. It falls under mechanical engineering > heat exchange in general > details of heat-exchange and heat-transfer apparatus, of general application.
[F28F 9/0138] formed by sleeves for finned tubes -- F28F 9/0138 designates heat-exchange apparatus casings that are formed by placing a sleeve around finned tubes, providing protection or structural support for the tubes within the exchanger. It falls under the CPC section for mechanical engineering, specifically the details of heat-exchange and heat-transfer apparatus of general application.
[F28F 9/02] Header boxes -- Header boxes are integral casings that collect and distribute fluid to multiple heat-exchange tubes or channels within a heat-exchange system. They provide a compact, sealed enclosure to ensure uniform flow and reduce pressure losses. Used in mechanical engineering applications involving general heat-exchange equipment.
[F28F 9/0202] Header boxes having their inner space divided by partitions -- The subclass covers header boxes used in heat-exchange systems whose interior is segmented by internal partitions to separate flow streams. Such partitioned headers are employed to direct fluids into distinct zones, improve temperature control, and prevent mixing within the casing.
[F28F 9/0204] for elongated header box, e.g. with transversal and longitudinal partitions -- This subclass covers elongated header boxes used in heat-exchange equipment, where the box includes both transverse and longitudinal partitions to distribute fluid flow. It pertains to general-application heat-transfer devices within the mechanical engineering hierarchy.
[F28F 9/0207] the longitudinal or transversal partitions being separate elements attached to header boxes  F28F9/0212 ,  F28F9/0217  take precedence -- In the mechanical-engineering heat-exchange hierarchy, this subclass classifies heat-exchange casings whose longitudinal or transversal partitions are separate elements that are attached to the header boxes, rather than being integral to the shell; more detailed sub-classes (F28F 9/0212 and F28F 9/0217) have priority over this definition.
[F28F 9/0209] having only transversal partitions -- This subclass refers to heat-exchange casings that contain only transversal partitions-walls that run across the flow direction to divide the interior into separate channels-without any longitudinal or mixed baffles. It applies to general-purpose heat-transfer apparatus where the partitioning is limited to these crosswise elements.
[F28F 9/0212] the partitions being separate elements attached to header boxes -- This subclass concerns heat-exchanger casings in which the internal partitions are separate components that are fixed to the header boxes, rather than being integral to the shell. It relates to designs that use distinct partition elements to form the flow passages within a general-purpose heat-exchange or heat-transfer apparatus.
[F28F 9/0214] having only longitudinal partitions -- The code covers heat-exchange casings that contain partitions extending only in the longitudinal direction, separating the flow channels without transverse or diagonal dividers. Such designs are used where straight, lengthwise baffles guide fluids through the exchanger, simplifying construction and cleaning.
[F28F 9/0217] the partitions being separate elements attached to header boxes -- This subclass pertains to heat-exchange equipment in which the internal partitions are provided as distinct components that are mounted to the header boxes rather than being integral to the shell. It addresses designs where separate partition members are secured to the headers to form the flow channels within the exchanger.
[F28F 9/0219] Arrangements for sealing end plates into casing or header box; Header box sub-elements  F28F9/0236  takes precedence -- This subclass deals with designs that secure end plates to a heat-exchange casing or header box, describing the sealing methods and associated header-box sub-components. It falls under details of heat-exchange and heat-transfer apparatus of general application and is overridden by subclass F28F 9/0236.
[F28F 9/0221] Header boxes or end plates formed by stacked elements -- This subclass concerns heat-exchanger header boxes or end plates that are fabricated by stacking a series of separate plates, bars or similar thin elements to form a single boxed component. The stacked construction provides the fluid distribution channels and mechanical strength for the unit.
[F28F 9/0224] Header boxes formed by sealing end plates into covers  F28F9/0221  takes precedence -- In the Cooperative Patent Classification, code F28F 9/0224 covers header boxes created by sealing end plates to covers, forming a component that distributes fluid to multiple heat-exchange tubes. These assemblies are used in general-purpose heat-exchange and heat-transfer equipment, providing a compact, sealed inlet or outlet manifold.
[F28F 9/0226] with resilient gaskets -- This subclass covers heat-exchanger casings that incorporate resilient gaskets to provide sealing while accommodating thermal expansion and vibration. It applies to general-purpose heat-transfer equipment where the gasket material yields to maintain a leak-tight joint.
[F28F 9/0229] Double end plates; Single end plates with hollow spaces -- This subclass covers heat-exchanger units whose end plates are either paired (double) or a single plate provided with internal cavities. The design provides structural rigidity, guides fluid flow and can enhance heat transfer by creating additional passages within the plate.
[F28F 9/0231] Header boxes having an expansion chamber -- Header boxes with an expansion chamber are components of heat-exchange equipment that collect or distribute fluid to the individual tubes and incorporate a built-in chamber to absorb thermal expansion and pressure variations, helping to prevent leakage and maintain structural integrity.
[F28F 9/0234] having a second heat exchanger disposed there within, e.g. oil cooler -- The subclass F28F 9/0234 defines heat-exchange devices whose casing incorporates a second heat exchanger inside the primary unit, for example an oil cooler in which a secondary circuit is placed within the main heat-transfer body. It falls under mechanical engineering, heat exchange in general, and details of heat-exchange and heat-transfer apparatus of general application.
[F28F 9/0236] floating elements -- Floating elements are components placed within a heat-exchange casing that are not rigidly attached to the outer shell but are supported so they can move or be buoyed. This arrangement permits thermal expansion, reduces vibration transmission, and can improve fluid distribution across the heat-transfer surfaces. It is used in general-application heat-exchange and heat-transfer apparatus.
[F28F 9/0239] floating header boxes -- Floating header boxes are removable or movable casings that enclose the headers of heat-exchange or heat-transfer equipment, allowing the headers to float relative to the main shell so thermal expansion and vibration are accommodated while simplifying assembly and maintenance. They are used in general-application heat-exchange apparatus where reliable sealing and easy access to the header connections are required.
[F28F 9/0241] floating end plates -- Floating end plates are movable or resilient plates attached to the ends of a heat-exchanger casing that seal and accommodate thermal expansion while maintaining fluid flow. They allow differential movement between the casing and the heat-transfer surfaces, improving reliability in varying temperature conditions.
[F28F 9/0243] Header boxes having a circular cross-section -- A header box is a casing that gathers or distributes fluid among the tubes or channels of a heat-exchange unit, ensuring even flow and simplifying assembly. When the box has a circular cross-section it provides a cylindrical passage that can be welded or bolted to pipework, commonly used in compact heat-exchangers and condensers.
[F28F 9/0246] Arrangements for connecting header boxes with flow lines -- This CPC subgroup concerns the design of connections between header boxes and flow lines in heat-exchange equipment, detailing the geometry, mounting and sealing methods used to join the headers to the distribution piping within a casing. It includes arrangements for attaching, orienting and supporting the headers as part of the overall heat-transfer apparatus.
[F28F 9/0248] Arrangements for sealing connectors to header boxes -- F28F 9/0248 covers the design of sealing arrangements that attach electrical or fluid connectors to the header boxes of heat-exchange equipment. It addresses methods and structures that prevent leakage around the connector interface within the casing of the heat-transfer apparatus.
[F28F 9/0251] Massive connectors, e.g. blocks; Plate-like connectors -- The CPC subclass F28F 9/0251 covers large, block-type or plate-like connectors used to join casings of heat-exchange and heat-transfer equipment. These massive connectors provide structural support and fluid sealing between major sections of a heat-exchange apparatus.
[F28F 9/0253] with multiple channels, e.g. with combined inflow and outflow channels -- The code refers to heat-exchange apparatus having a casing that incorporates several flow passages, such as integrated inlet and outlet channels, allowing fluids to move through separate or combined routes within the device. This classification covers designs where multiple channels are formed in the housing to improve heat transfer efficiency.
[F28F 9/0256] Arrangements for coupling connectors with flow lines -- F28F 9/0256 covers designs for joining connectors to fluid-carrying lines within heat-exchange casings. It includes mechanical arrangements that secure pipe or tube connections, ensuring leak-tight integration of flow lines in heat-transfer equipment. The classification is used for patents dealing with coupling mechanisms in heat-exchange apparatus.
[F28F 9/0258] of quick acting type, e.g. with snap action -- This subclass relates to casings for heat-exchange or heat-transfer apparatus that are designed to operate quickly, such as snap-action or rapid-opening housings, providing fast protection or access to the heat-transfer components.
[F28F 9/026] with static flow control means, e.g. with means for uniformly distributing heat exchange media into conduits -- This subclass defines casings for heat-exchange equipment that incorporate static flow-control features, such as structures that uniformly distribute the heat-transfer fluid into the internal conduits. The static devices achieve even flow distribution without moving parts, improving overall heat-transfer efficiency.
[F28F 9/0263] by varying the geometry or cross-section of header box -- The code covers heat-exchanger designs in which the geometry or cross-section of the header box is intentionally varied to influence flow distribution, pressure drop or heat-transfer efficiency. It applies to any general-purpose heat-exchange apparatus where modifying the header shape improves overall performance.
[F28F 9/0265] by using guiding means or impingement means inside the header box -- This subclass relates to heat-exchange equipment whose casing includes internal guiding or impingement devices inside the header box to direct the flow of the working fluid. The guiding means improve fluid mixing and heat-transfer efficiency within the header. It is used in general-application heat-exchange and heat-transfer apparatus.
[F28F 9/0268] in the form of multiple deflectors for channeling the heat exchange medium -- The subclass F28F 9/0268 covers heat-exchange apparatus whose casings incorporate multiple deflectors that channel the heat-exchange medium, such as baffles or guide plates that direct fluid flow to improve heat transfer efficiency. It falls under mechanical engineering, heat exchange in general, and details of heat-exchange and heat-transfer apparatus of general application.
[F28F 9/027] in the form of distribution pipes -- The F28F 9/027 subclass refers to heat-exchange or heat-transfer devices that are incorporated directly into distribution pipes, where the pipe itself forms part of the exchanger. It includes arrangements in which fluids flow through a pipe that also serves to transfer heat to or from another medium, typically for heating, cooling or thermal-energy recovery in plumbing or utility networks.
[F28F 9/0273] with multiple holes -- The CPC subclass F28F 9/0273 falls under mechanical engineering, heat exchange in general, details of heat-exchange and heat-transfer apparatus of general application, specifically casings. It covers casings that incorporate multiple holes such as perforated tubes, plates, or shell sections designed to enhance fluid flow or heat transfer.
[F28F 9/0275] with multiple branch pipes -- This subclass covers casings for heat-exchange equipment that incorporate multiple branch pipes to feed several secondary circuits or heat-transfer paths. It applies to designs where a main conduit is divided into several branches within the housing, enhancing fluid distribution and compactness.
[F28F 9/0278] in the form of stacked distribution plates or perforated plates arranged over end plates -- It covers heat-exchange casings that use stacked distribution plates or perforated plates arranged over end plates, forming a modular arrangement for directing fluid flow and enhancing heat transfer.
[F28F 9/028] by using inserts for modifying the pattern of flow inside the header box, e.g. by using flow restrictors or permeable bodies or blocks with channels -- This subclass deals with heat-exchange devices whose header box flow is intentionally altered by installing inserts such as flow restrictors, permeable bodies or blocks with channels, thereby changing the fluid pattern to control distribution or improve performance.
[F28F 9/0282] by varying the geometry of conduit ends, e.g. by using inserts or attachments for modifying the pattern of flow at the conduit inlet or outlet -- The classification refers to heat-exchange casings in which the geometry of conduit ends is altered, such as by adding inserts or attachments, to modify the flow pattern at the inlet or outlet and thereby affect heat-transfer performance.
[F28F 9/04] Arrangements for sealing elements into header boxes or end plates -- F28F 9/04 relates to the design of sealing arrangements that secure heat-exchange elements within header boxes or end plates of a heat-exchange apparatus. It includes methods such as gaskets, welding, clamping or other sealing means used to prevent fluid leakage at the connections between the heat-transfer elements and the surrounding casing.
[F28F 9/06] by dismountable joints -- F28F 9/06 relates to casings of heat-exchange and heat-transfer equipment that are assembled with removable or dismountable joints. The code covers designs that allow the casing to be taken apart for maintenance, cleaning or component replacement without destroying the structure. It falls under mechanical engineering, heat exchange in general, and details of heat-exchange and heat-transfer apparatus of general application.
[F28F 9/08] by wedge-type connections, e.g. taper ferrule -- This subclass covers casings of heat-exchange or heat-transfer equipment that are assembled or sealed by wedge-type connections such as taper ferrules. The wedge or taper element creates a tight, self-locking joint that secures the casing components and provides a reliable fluid-tight seal.
[F28F 9/10] by screw-type connections, e.g. gland -- The subclass F28F 9/10 pertains to casings of heat-exchange and heat-transfer devices that are assembled with screw-type connections such as gland fittings, where the housing or shell is joined by threaded or screwed joints. It applies to general-purpose heat-exchange apparatus whose outer enclosure is secured in this manner.
[F28F 9/12] by flange-type connections -- This subclass pertains to the casings of heat-exchange or heat-transfer equipment that are assembled using flange-type connections, where flanged joints secure the shell sections together. It falls under mechanical engineering, heat exchange in general, and details of heat-exchange and heat-transfer apparatus of general application.
[F28F 9/14] by force-joining -- This subclass covers casings of heat-exchange or heat-transfer apparatus that are assembled by force-joining techniques such as crimping, riveting, clinching or other mechanical bonding methods, rather than by welding or adhesive bonding. It applies to general-purpose heat-exchange equipment where the casing sections are secured together through applied pressure or deformation to create a permanent joint.
[F28F 9/16] by permanent joints, e.g. by rolling -- This subgroup covers heat-exchange casings assembled by permanent joints such as rolling, welding or similar processes that create a continuous, non-removable bond, and includes casings where the joint itself contributes to the heat-transfer path.
[F28F 9/162] by using bonding or sealing substances, e.g. adhesives  F28F9/18  takes precedence
[F28F 9/165] by using additional preformed parts, e.g. sleeves, gaskets  F28F9/185  takes precedence -- F28F 9/165 covers heat-exchange apparatus casings that are completed, reinforced or sealed by inserting additional pre-shaped components such as sleeves or gaskets. It refines the general casings category for heat-exchange equipment within mechanical engineering, and is superseded by the more specific subclass F28F 9/185.
[F28F 9/167] the parts being inserted in the heat-exchange conduits
[F28F 9/18] by welding -- The subclass F28F 9/18 covers heat-exchange or heat-transfer apparatus casings that are formed, assembled or attached by welding. It pertains to general-application casings in mechanical engineering heat-exchange equipment where welding is the primary joining technique.
[F28F 9/182] the heat-exchange conduits having ends with a particular shape, e.g. deformed; the heat-exchange conduits or end plates having supplementary joining means, e.g. abutments -- The class covers heat-exchange conduits whose ends are formed with a specific shape, such as a deformation, or are equipped with auxiliary joining features like abutments or end plates. It pertains to the design of conduits and casings used in general-application heat-exchange and heat-transfer equipment.
[F28F 9/185] with additional preformed parts -- The subclass F28F 9/185 covers heat-exchange apparatus whose casing incorporates extra pre-formed components, such as plates, fins or ducts, that are added to the main housing during manufacture. It relates to mechanical-engineering heat-exchange devices and specifies casings built with these additional fabricated parts.
[F28F 9/187] at least one of the parts being non-metallic, e.g. heat-sealing plastic elements -- This subclass concerns heat-exchange or heat-transfer devices whose housing or any component is made from a non-metallic material, for example heat-sealing plastic elements, allowing lighter or corrosion-resistant casings.
[F28F 9/20] Arrangements of heat reflectors, e.g. separately-insertible reflecting walls -- F28F 9/20 concerns the design of heat-exchange casings that incorporate heat-reflecting elements such as separately-insertable reflecting walls. These arrangements aim to enhance thermal performance by redirecting radiant energy within the exchanger, improving overall heat-transfer efficiency.
[F28F 9/22] Arrangements for directing heat-exchange media into successive compartments, e.g. arrangements of guide plates -- F28F 9/22 covers designs that channel a heating or cooling fluid through a series of separate chambers within a heat-exchange casing, typically using internal guide plates or similar structures to direct the flow sequentially and improve temperature uniformity or transfer efficiency. This falls under mechanical engineering, heat exchange, and casing details.
[F28F 9/24] Arrangements for promoting turbulent flow of heat-exchange media, e.g. by plates -- F28F 9/24 pertains to heat-exchange equipment where the flow of the working fluid is deliberately made turbulent to enhance heat transfer. Typical solutions include the insertion of plates, ribs, or other obstacles that disturb the laminar stream and increase mixing within the exchanger.
[F28F 9/26] Arrangements for connecting different sections of heat-exchange elements, e.g. of radiators -- This subclass covers designs for linking separate parts of a heat-exchange device such as radiator sections, including manifolds, junctions, and piping that join multiple tubes or banks to create a continuous fluid path. It is applied to mechanical engineering heat-exchange apparatus.
[F28F 9/262] for radiators  F28D1/0408  takes precedence
[F28F 9/264] by sleeves, nipples -- F28F 9/264 covers casings of heat-exchange or heat-transfer apparatus that are constructed or joined by sleeves or nipples. It includes designs where the outer shell or tube bundles are assembled using these tubular connectors to provide sealing, support or modular extension.
[F28F 9/266] by screw-type connections -- This subgroup covers heat-exchange casings where the individual sections or panels are assembled using screw-type connections or bolts. It includes designs in which the screw joint provides the primary seal or structural link between casing parts, typical for mechanical-engineering heat-exchange equipment.
[F28F 9/268] by permanent joints, e.g. by welding -- F28F 9/268 covers casings of heat-exchange or heat-transfer devices that are assembled using permanent joints, such as welding, brazing or similar processes. The subclass falls under mechanical engineering, heat exchange in general, and the details of heat-exchange apparatus casings.
[F28F 99/00] Subject matter not provided for in other groups of this subclass -- F28F 99/00 designates inventions relating to heat-exchange or heat-transfer apparatus that are not covered by any of the more specific groups in this subclass, serving as a catch-all category for miscellaneous or novel features.
[F28G] CLEANING OF INTERNAL OR EXTERNAL SURFACES OF HEAT-EXCHANGE OR HEAT-TRANSFER CONDUITS, e.g. WATER TUBES OR BOILERS -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatus and methods for removing deposits from surfaces in contact with heat-exchange media.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Cleaning tubes or pipes in general -> B08B9/02 - Removing incrustation in apparatus for thermal non-catalytic cracking of hydrocarbon oils -> C10G9/12 , C10G9/16 - Non-mechanical removal of metallic material from surfaces -> C23F - Cleaning metallic materials by chemical methods -> C23G - Cleaning engines cooling systems -> F01P11/06 - Devices for removing water, salt, or sludge from boilers -> F22B37/48 - Cleaning combustion chambers -> F23J - Cleaning arrangements for water heaters -> F24H9/00 - Removing ice from heat exchanger -> F28F17/00 - Preventing the formation of deposits or corrosion -> F28F19/00
[F28G 1/00] Non-rotary, e.g. reciprocated, appliances -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Cleaning devices comprising a non-rotary cleaning element, e.g. arranged for linear motion or for back and forth movement.
[F28G 1/02] having brushes -- A non-rotary, typically reciprocating cleaning device equipped with brushes for removing deposits from the inside or outside of heat-exchange or heat-transfer conduits such as water tubes, boiler tubes, or similar components. This subclass covers brush-based cleaning tools used in mechanical engineering heat-exchange maintenance.
[F28G 1/04] having articulated tools, e.g. assembled in chain manner -- This subclass covers non-rotary cleaning devices for heat-exchange or heat-transfer conduits, such as water tubes or boilers, that employ articulated tools arranged in a chain-like fashion. The articulated elements may be linked together to reach and scrub internal or external surfaces of the tubes.
[F28G 1/06] having coiled wire tools, i.e. basket type -- This subclass relates to cleaning apparatus for heat-exchange or heat-transfer conduits where the tool is a basket-type made from coiled wire, employed in non-rotary or reciprocating devices to remove deposits from the internal or external surfaces of tubes, boilers and similar equipment.
[F28G 1/08] having scrapers, hammers, or cutters, e.g. rigidly mounted -- This subclass refers to non-rotary cleaning equipment for heat-exchange or heat-transfer conduits that utilizes rigidly mounted scrapers, hammers or cutters to remove deposits from internal or external surfaces such as water-tube or boiler walls. It applies to reciprocating or otherwise fixed-tool mechanisms rather than rotating brushes.
[F28G 1/10] resiliently mounted -- F28G 1/10 covers non-rotary cleaning devices for heat-exchange or heat-transfer conduits such as water tubes or boilers that are resiliently mounted to reduce vibration.
[F28G 11/00] Cleaning by combustion, e.g. using squibs, using travelling burners -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Cleaning devices, performing a cleaning action by contacting the heat exchange surfaces with flames or high temperature fumes for burning the deposits.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Cleaning by detonations -> F28G7/00 - Cleaning by increasing temperature of heat exchange surfaces -> F28G13/00
[F28G 1/12] Fluid-propelled scrapers, bullets, or like solid bodies -- This subclass deals with devices that use a flowing fluid to drive solid cleaning elements such as scrapers, bullets, or similar bodies through or across the interior or exterior of heat-exchange tubes. The cleaning tools are non-rotary, often reciprocating, and are intended for removing deposits from water tubes, boilers and other heat-transfer conduits.
[F28G 1/125] forced back and forth by means of flow reversal -- This subclass deals with apparatus that clean internal or external surfaces of heat exchangers such as tubes or boilers by forcing a fluid to move alternately forward and backward through the conduit, creating a reciprocating flow that dislodges deposits. The cleaning action is achieved without rotating components, using flow reversal to generate a pulsating wash.
[F28G 1/14] Pull-through rods -- Pull-through rods are reciprocating cleaning elements that travel longitudinally inside heat-exchange or heat-transfer tubes, such as water-tube boiler passages, to mechanically dislodge deposits and fouling from the internal surfaces. They are employed in non-rotary cleaning devices that repeatedly push and pull the rod through the conduit, ensuring effective removal of scale without rotating motion.
[F28G 1/16] using jets of fluid for removing debris -- This subclass covers non-rotary cleaning devices, such as reciprocating machines, that employ jets of fluid to dislodge and remove debris from the internal or external surfaces of heat-exchange or heat-transfer conduits like water tubes and boilers.
[F28G 1/163] from internal surfaces of heat exchange conduits -- F28G 1/163 covers non-rotary cleaning methods, such as reciprocating or back-and-forth motions, used to remove deposits from the internal surfaces of heat-exchange or heat-transfer conduits like water tubes or boiler passages. The classification applies to devices and processes designed specifically for cleaning these interior surfaces without rotating the equipment.
[F28G 1/166] from external surfaces of heat exchange conduits -- F28G 1/166 relates to non-rotary cleaning devices, such as reciprocating units, that are used to clean the external surfaces of heat-exchange conduits like water-tube boilers or other heat-transfer elements, removing deposits from the outside of the tubes.
[F28G 13/00] Appliances or processes not covered by groups -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Other cleaning devices or processes, e.g. cleaning by laser, cleaning by oxidation, cleaning by action of bubbles
[F28G 13/005] cleaning by increasing the temperature of heat exchange surfaces  F28G7/005 ,  F28G11/00  take precedence -- F28G 13/005 covers methods for cleaning heat-exchange or heat-transfer surfaces, such as water-tube or boiler interiors, by raising their temperature to remove deposits. It falls under mechanical engineering → heat exchange in general → cleaning of internal or external surfaces of heat-exchange conduits, and is used when the procedure is not covered by the more specific groups F28G 7/005 or F28G 11/00.
[F28G 15/00] Details -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Details of cleaning devices, e.g. control arrangements.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Measuring thickness of deposits -> G01B
[F28G 15/003] Control arrangements -- F28G 15/003 covers control arrangements used in cleaning the internal or external surfaces of heat-exchange or heat-transfer conduits such as water-tube boilers. The classification pertains to mechanisms, sensors, or automatic systems that manage cleaning cycles, fluid flow or chemical delivery to maintain efficient heat transfer. It falls within mechanical engineering's heat-exchange domain.
[F28G 15/02] Supports for cleaning appliances, e.g. frames -- The subclass F28G 15/02 covers structures that hold or mount cleaning devices used on heat-exchange or heat-transfer conduits, such as frames, brackets or other support elements that position brushes, scrapers or other cleaning tools while removing fouling from water tubes, boilers or similar equipment.
[F28G 15/04] Feeding and driving arrangements, e.g. power operation -- The subclass covers systems that supply cleaning media and provide the mechanical power needed to move or drive cleaning apparatus within heat-exchange tubes or boilers. It includes pumps, motors, drive shafts and related control devices that enable the cleaning process.
[F28G 15/06] Automatic reversing devices -- Automatic reversing devices are mechanisms that periodically change the direction of fluid or cleaning media flow within heat-exchange or heat-transfer conduits, such as water tubes or boiler tubes, to enhance the removal of deposits from interior or exterior surfaces during cleaning operations.
[F28G 15/08] Locating position of cleaning appliances within conduits -- F28G 15/08 covers techniques for determining where cleaning devices are positioned inside heat-exchange or heat-transfer conduits such as water tubes or boiler passages. It includes methods, sensors or arrangements that locate the cleaning appliance relative to the conduit walls to ensure effective surface cleaning.
[F28G 15/10] Masks for delimiting area to be cleaned -- F28G 15/10 relates to masks that delimit a defined area of a heat-exchange or heat-transfer conduit during cleaning, enabling targeted removal of deposits from internal or external surfaces such as water-tubes or boiler walls.
[F28G 2015/006] Arrangements for processing a cleaning fluid after use, e.g. filtering and recycling -- F28G 2015/006 relates to systems and methods that treat a cleaning fluid after it has been used, including filtering, contaminant separation and recycling of the fluid for reuse. The entry covers arrangements that recover, purify or otherwise process spent cleaning solutions in industrial or domestic equipment.
[F28G 3/00] Rotary appliances -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Cleaning devices comprising rotatable cleaning elements, e.g. arranged for turning arount at least one axis; Cleaning devices comprising movable cleaning elements, wherein the movement is a combination of a non-rotary movement and a rotary movement.
[F28G 3/02] having abrasive tools -- F28G 3/02 relates to rotary cleaning appliances for heat-exchange or heat-transfer conduits that employ abrasive tools to remove deposits from interior or exterior surfaces, such as water-tube boilers. It includes devices like rotating brushes, scrapers or similar abrasive implements designed specifically for tube cleaning.
[F28G 3/04] having brushes -- The CPC subclass F28G 3/04 covers rotary cleaning devices for heat exchangers that use brushes to scrub internal or external surfaces of tubes, boilers or similar conduits. It is situated under mechanical engineering, heat-exchange cleaning and specifically rotary appliances employing brushes.
[F28G 3/06] having articulated tools, e.g. assembled in chain manner -- This subclass defines rotary cleaning apparatus for heat-exchange or heat-transfer conduits that employ articulated tools, such as a chain-like series of linked elements, to scrub internal or external surfaces of tubes, boilers and similar equipment. It falls under mechanical engineering, heat-exchange cleaning, and specifically rotary appliances.
[F28G 3/08] having coiled wire tools, i.e. basket type -- This subclass covers rotary cleaning machines for heat-exchange equipment that employ a coiled-wire basket as the scrubbing element. The basket is rotated to remove deposits from the interior of tubes, boiler shells or other heat-transfer conduits.
[F28G 3/10] having scrapers, hammers, or cutters, e.g. rigidly mounted -- The F28G 3/10 subclass concerns rotary cleaning appliances for heat-exchange or heat-transfer conduits that employ scrapers, hammers or cutters, usually mounted rigidly on the rotor. It addresses devices that mechanically remove deposits from the internal or external surfaces of water tubes, boilers and similar heat-exchange equipment.
[F28G 3/12] resiliently mounted -- The F28G 3/12 subclass covers rotary cleaning appliances that are resiliently mounted, such as brushes, scrapers or paddles mounted on springs, dampers or other vibration-isolating supports, used to clean the internal or external surfaces of heat-exchange or heat-transfer conduits like water tubes or boilers.
[F28G 3/14] thrown into working position by centrifugal force -- F28G 3/14 covers rotary cleaning appliances for heat-exchange or heat-transfer conduits in which the cleaning element is thrown into its working position by centrifugal force, such as devices that clean water-tube or boiler interiors using a spinning motion.
[F28G 3/16] using jets of fluid for removing debris -- The code F28G 3/16 covers rotary cleaning devices that employ streams of fluid directed at the interior or exterior of heat-exchange tubes, such as water-tube boilers, to dislodge and remove accumulated debris. It falls under mechanical engineering, heat exchange, and cleaning of heat-transfer conduits.
[F28G 3/163] from internal surfaces of heat exchange conduits -- F28G 3/163 relates to rotary appliances used to clean the internal surfaces of heat-exchange or heat-transfer conduits such as water tubes or boiler tubes. The class covers mechanical cleaning actions that remove deposits from the inside of the tubes without dismantling the system.
[F28G 3/166] from external surfaces of heat exchange conduits -- F28G 3/166 covers rotary appliances used to clean the external surfaces of heat-exchange or heat-transfer conduits such as water-tube boiler shells, condensers or other heat-exchange equipment. The classification is part of the mechanical-engineering hierarchy, specifically under heat-exchange cleaning methods that address external surfaces. It includes devices that employ rotating brushes, scrapers or similar rotary tools to remove fouling, scale or deposits from the outer walls of the conduits.
[F28G 5/00] Cleaning by distortion -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Cleaning devices performing a cleaning action by deforming the heat exchange surfaces.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Cleaning by vibration -> F28G7/00
[F28G 7/00] Cleaning by vibration -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Cleaning devices performing a cleaning action by producing vibrations on the heat exchange surfaces, e.g. by using knocking devices, or by using pressure pulses, or by producing detonations or explosions.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Cleaning by distortion -> F28G5/00
[F28G 7/005] by explosions or detonations; by pressure waves generated by combustion processes -- Cleaning of heat-exchange or heat-transfer conduits that employs the rapid pressure pulses from explosions, detonations or combustion-driven shock waves to generate vibrations and dislodge deposits from internal or external surfaces, particularly in water tubes and boiler components.
[F28G 9/00] Cleaning by flushing or washing, e.g. with chemical solvents -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Cleaning devices performing a cleaning action by contacting the heat exchange surfaces with a solvent and/or by flowing a washing fluid through the heat exchange conduits.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Cleaning by using appliances using jets of fluids for removing debris -> F28G1/16 , F28G3/16
[F28G 9/005] of regenerative heat exchanger -- This subclass covers methods for cleaning the internal or external surfaces of regenerative heat exchangers, such as water tubes or boiler passages, by flushing or washing them with liquids, often employing chemical solvents to remove deposits and restore heat-transfer efficiency.
